events.id: Come Together 2026--Wellington NZ
events.name: Come Together 2026
events.long_description:
New Zealand’s most successful rock series ever returns in 2026, focusing on an electrifying trio of landmark albums. Come Together is bringing three iconic albums to Wellington. This year, they take on Bruce Springsteen’s Born to Run, Radiohead’s OK Computer and Talking Heads’ Stop Making Sense. Each concert will faithfully recreate an album and follow up with a set of classics and deep cuts that span each artist’s catalogue. 
The group is made up of a rotating roster of Aotearoa’s most acclaimed rock musicians. What began as a bold celebration of legendary rock albums has since exploded into one of New Zealand’s most electrifying live concert experiences. The collective includes Jon Toogood, Tami Neilson, Liam Finn, Delaney Davidson, Reb Fountain, Julia Deans, Dianne Swann, Mel Parsons, Ed Knowles, Arahi, Jazmine Mary, Deva Mahal, and many more. Their endeavours have drawn thousands of fans, cementing Come Together as a must-see event on the music calendar.

Each concert will faithfully recreate an album and follow up with a set of classics and deep cuts that span each artist’s catalogue.
The group is made up of a rotating roster of Aotearoa’s most acclaimed rock musicians.

events.id: Orchestra Wellington-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Orchestra Wellington
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
With the overarching theme of ‘collaborations’, Orchestra Wellington is looking forward to bringing people together with their 2026 concerts. A dazzling array of musicians, all at the top of their game, will collaborate with the local orchestra. These include the New Zealand String Quartet, NZTrio, Ballet Collective Aotearoa, four brass bands, and many choirs and soloists. 
The orchestra is not just going big with the number of performers, but with the sheer scale of the works presented. Wellington audiences will get to hear Leonard Bernstein’s Chichester Psalms, Hector Berlioz’s Requiem Grand Messe des Morts, and Carl Nielsen’s Symphony 4.

events.name: New Zealand Symphony Orchestra 2026 Season
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The NZSO’s reputation for artistic excellence attracts many of the world’s leading musicians, conductors, and soloists. In 2026, some of the music-world luminaries include violinists María Dueñas and James Ehnes, trombonist Jörgen van Rijen, pianist Isata Kanneh-Mason, and New Zealand piano prodigy Shan Liu.   
Wellington shows are hosted in the Michael Fowler Centre, home of the NZSO. The touring programme will see the orchestra perform over 100 concerts in over 30 communities. Be among over 100,000 people across the motu who experience live orchestra music.

events.id: Circa Theatre Season 2026-Circa Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Circa Theatre Season 2026
events.venue: Circa Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Inside Circa Theatre’s waterfront venue, you’ll find two performance spaces. Both offer a smorgasbord of professional theatre throughout the year. Playwrights from international heavy hitters to brilliant Kiwi voices are represented. It also includes programming from comedies and cabarets to contemporary dance and pantomime. 
Before or after your show, visit the on-site café, brasserie and restaurant, Chouchou. It opens out onto a deck, where you can take in views across the harbour. Look up to admire the art installation above. Called ‘Velarium’, it undulates in the wind, mirroring the water in the harbour. 
Circa Theatre began its life in 1976. A breakaway group of actors and theatre practitioners decided that Aotearoa needed a new and innovative approach to theatre. Five decades later, it is still pushing boundaries, making people laugh and cry, and creating theatrical memories. 
If you don’t have your own Circa memory yet, there’s an opportunity to create one nearly every night of the week.

events.id: Mills Albert Wellington Saints 2026 Season-TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Mills Albert Wellington Saints 2026 Season
events.venue: TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Mills Albert Wellington Saints take to the court for what is sure to be a massive 2026 season. After taking out the 2025 season with a nail-biting win over the Southland Sharks, can they defend their championship title? Kiwi coach Aaron Young takes to the helm again after winning coach of the year. Hutt Valley local Jordan Hunt returns to the team after representing New Zealand in the Tall Blacks, and Melbourne’s Akech Aliir joins the Saints, bringing 6’9” of power and athleticism. 
The 2026 regular season features nine home games at the TSB Arena. Saints’ fans can expect high-energy basketball and a family-friendly atmosphere. Tip-off times have been optimised to make it easier for young fans to witness their heroes in action. 
Join the game court-side and put your lungs to the test as you cheer on your favourite Wellington side.

events.id: Ice Rink-Queen's Wharf, Queen's Wharf, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Ice Rink
events.venue: Queen's Wharf, Queen's Wharf, Wellington
events.long_description:
Wellington Ice Rink at Queens Wharf

Paradice EVENTS returns to Wellington this winter with the Wellington Ice Rink at Queens Wharf. Located on Wellington’s waterfront, the pop-up ice rink transforms Queens Wharf into a seasonal skating destination for visitors of all ages.

Now in its seventh year in Wellington, the event offers a winter skating experience in the heart of the city, featuring music, lighting effects, and sessions suitable for both experienced skaters and first-time visitors.

The rink measures 30 metres by 15 metres and provides approximately 400 pairs of ice skates for hire.
Throughout the season, a range of themed sessions will be held, including:
• Student Wednesdays – Midweek skating sessions with student-focused pricing
• Skate & Date Thursdays – Evening sessions with music and ambient lighting
• Groovy Fridays – Top 40 music sessions to start the weekend

Wellington Ice Rink is suitable for families, groups, students, corporate outings, and casual visitors looking for a winter activity on the waterfront.


Whether visiting for a daytime skate or an evening session under the lights, Wellington Ice Rink offers a seasonal experience in one of the city’s most iconic locations.

Wellington
20 June – 19 July
Location: Queens Wharf
10 am – 10 pm daily
Ice Skating Prices
Adults $26 (13 years plus)
Children $21 (5-12 years)
Pre schoolers $16 (3 and 4 year olds)
Family pass $70
Student night Wednesday 7pm – 10pm $18 Student ID required
Please note: all skaters are required to wear a helmet or Paradice safety approved headband when skating.

Risk Disclosure
Please bear in mind that ice skating and ice sliding is an extreme sport which can result in harm or injury. The risks and hazards include, but are not limited to tripping, falling, collisions and cold exposure. By participating in the activities offered by the Wairakei Estate Ice Rink and MB Century Ice Slide you acknowledge these risks could result in injury and you skate/slide at your own risk.

Keep Yourself Safe
The wearing of a helmet or Paradice approved safety headband is compulsory and we recommend the wearing of gloves. Children 6 years and under only have the option of wearing a helmet not the safety headband. Please follow our rink rules and the advice of staff
Please note minimum age for ice skating is 3 years old.

Tickets are available inside the rink and online www.paradiceevents.co.nz
For more information
Contact Paradice Events
09 281 5645 or 027 233 0972 | events@paradice.co.nz

events.id: Monday Eve 8 Week Block - Mixed Levels-Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Monday Eve 8 Week Block - Mixed Levels
events.venue: Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington
events.long_description:
Our popular weeknight block classes are the best way for keen makers to get into jewellery making in a lively, warm and friendly environment. This class will be held at our new waterfront location, Market Lane!

This class is a mixed-level class, allowing beginners to work alongside advanced students. It is the best way for beginners to get into jewellery-making in a lively, warm and friendly environment.

Beginners start with basic ring-making and stone-setting in copper or brass. Students will learn measuring, texturing, shaping, cutting and soldering. Then we move on to making a practice-ring set with a Cabochon stone (cabochon means it has a flat bottom and a smooth domed top) in a Bezel Setting. As you work through your practice ring and setting, you can move on to working in silver and produce another ring with a cabochon stone that you really love.

Level 2 students learn to solder more competently on their own using chainmaking as a way to develop their fine soldering skills. They may create a simple stone setting or ring alongside this challenge project so they have something to hone their skills that they are familiar with, as well as taking on a new skill.

Level 3 and above students are encouraged to work on their design skills and try new and challenging techniques. They should prepare to have one or two simple projects they can work on self-directed in class, alongside a challenge project.

This class fee does NOT include materials for any student.

Beginners should expect to spend at least $60 on materials for their ring making project.
All metals and stones as well as quality findings and chains are available for purchase each week in the studio. Students are encouraged to keep their silver scrap and find ways to use it thriftily in future projects!

All metals and stones as well as quality findings and chains are available for purchase each week in the studio. Students are encouraged to keep their silver scrap and find ways to use it thriftily in future projects!

A note for young makers: those over 12 years are welcome in this class, but anyone under 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.

events.name: Advances Yoga Class Level 3 - 4
events.venue: Yoga in Daily Life Centre, 23 Jessie St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Level 3 - 4 continues with more advanced postures that build on all the previous exercises learnt, if you have systematically followed them you will find these Asanas easy and energising.

The previous excercise have increased the flexibility of the spine, improved the body's physical condition and heightened awareness to the importance of relaxation. It has been emphasised that each exercise be practised slowly, coordinating movement with the breath, pausing motionless in each position and always with full concentration. Ideal for those who would like to learn the levels of the System YIDL.
Mondays 6 - 7.15pm, $20 or concession card or Monthly pass drop in class, no booking required, for experiences practitioners.

events.id: Adult Class - LED Neon Sign-Evans Bay Intermediate School, 14A Kemp St, Kilbirnie, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Adult Class - LED Neon Sign
events.venue: Evans Bay Intermediate School, 14A Kemp St, Kilbirnie, Wellington
events.long_description:
4 Weeks
Mon 18th May 7pm-9pm
Mon 25th May 7pm-9pm
Mon 8th June 7pm-9pm
Mon 15th June 7pm-9pm

OR

4 Weeks
Mon 22nd June 7pm-9pm
Mon 29th June 7pm-9pm
Mon 6th July 7pm-9pm
Mon 13th July 7pm-9pm

During this course everyone will get to design and build their own awesome LED Neon Sign. The sign can be up to 50cm x 40cm and there are a range of colours available.

The design for the sign will be completed using the open source program 'Inkscape'. The frame will then be cut out of clear acrylic using our laser cutter.

The neon strip can be cut and soldered together to make sharp corners or bent into reasonably tight curves as shown in the images. Once all the neon pieces are positioned they will be glued then soldered together and powered by a standard 12 Volt wall adaptor (which is provided). There is also the option of incorporating a small dimmer circuit that allows you to control the brightness of your sign.

Only 6 Students Per Class.

Some prior soldering experience would be beneficial.

events.id: Marlene Dietrich in 'The Blue Angel' - Koha Screening with the Wellington Film Society-10 Kent Ter, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Marlene Dietrich in 'The Blue Angel' - Koha Screening with the Wellington Film Society
events.venue: 10 Kent Ter, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
In cooperation with the Goethe-Institut, Wellington Film Society is delighted to invite members of the public to attend our screening of Josef von Sternberg's 1930 masterpiece 'The Blue Angel'.
This film marked the beginning of Marlene Dietrich's international stardom. Emil Jannings plays a high school teacher who punishes his students for their infatuation with the local cabaret headliner, Lola (Dietrich). Unfortunately, the professor finds himself just as susceptible to the siren song of Lola as his students are, and he has a lot more to lose than they do...
This tale of lust and jealousy was so successful that Dietrich was hired for Hollywood productions before 'The Blue Angel' even made it to American shores. Few can forget her musical numbers in this film, most famously her crooning performance of 'Falling in Love Again'.

events.name: Soul in Motion
events.venue: Khandallah Presbyterian Church,33 Ganges Road, Khandallah, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Soul in Motion
This fortnightly session is a little different from a traditional yoga class. It’s designed to help you feel good inside and out through a blend of movement, soothing sound, breath, and deep relaxation.

You’ll experience movement sequences that gently challenge your coordination, strength, and flexibility whilst leaving you feeling more mobile and free in your body. Breath and sound help to calm your nervous system, and the session finishes with deep relaxation so you can carry that sense of ease into your week.

events.id: Beehive to Business with Chlöe Swarbrick-Wellington Chamber Of CommerceWellington-Event Brite
events.name: Beehive to Business with Chlöe Swarbrick
events.venue: Wellington Chamber Of CommerceWellington
events.long_description:
Beehive to Business with Chlöe Swarbrick
With the election approaching, understanding how different political perspectives shape policy and direction has never been more important for business.
We’re pleased to welcome the next speaker in our Business to Beehive Leaders Series: Chlöe Swarbrick, Co-leader of the Green Party.
This series brings party leaders to our members for open conversations about their priorities, perspectives, and policy direction.
Join us on 23 June where Chlöe Swarbrick will share her views on the forces shaping New Zealand’s future, and the role business can play within a changing economic, environmental, and social landscape.
Hear about:
The Green Party’s priorities heading into the election
Key themes shaping their policy approach
Their perspective on the opportunities and challenges facing New Zealand businesses
How they see the role of business in building a more sustainable and resilient economy
This is the third in our series of high-value discussions designed to give our members direct access to political leaders and a broader understanding of the landscape ahead.
Join us for a thoughtful and engaging discussion as you navigate the lead-up to election day.
Agenda:
7:15am- Standing breakfast, networking
7:45am- Address
8:10am- Q&A
8:30am- Event concludes
Can't make it? Click here to register for the free livestream. Sign up and watch at a time convenient to you.
Part of the ongoing Beehive to Business series, this event is proudly made possible through our partnership with Wellington-based consulting firm Allen + Clarke.

events.id: Karori Friendship Club-St Ninian's Uniting Church, 208 Karori Road, Karori, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Karori Friendship Club
events.venue: St Ninian's Uniting Church, 208 Karori Road, Karori, Wellington
events.long_description:
Tuesday mornings just got better!

If you enjoy a good chat, a decent cuppa, and the kind of laughter that makes you forget the time, the Karori Friendship Club is for you. We meet at St Ninian’s, 208 Karori Road, Karori, from 10 am to 12 pm, and for a koha you’ll be treated to morning tea and genuinely friendly company.

It’s a wonderful fit for seniors, people new to Karori, or any ESOL learners who would like a relaxed place to practise their language skills and settle into the community. Some weeks, there’s a board game or a puzzle on the go. Most weeks, it’s simply good conversation, shared stories, and the kind of connection that brightens your whole day.

No pressure, no fuss — just a warm welcome and a seat at the table. Come and see what makes this little Tuesday gathering such a special part of our community. You don't have to live in Karori to come; we have members from all over Wellington.

Ph: 476 7137 for more info or check out our website.

events.name: Art n Social for Autistic Adults
events.venue: UHAG HQ in G16, 6 Railway Ave, New Zealand 5018,6 Railway Ave, Maidstone, Upper Hutt 5018, New Zealand, Upper Hutt
events.long_description:
Art n Social for Autistic Adults
Tomorrow is Art n Social, an art group for autistic adults to come and do art for themselves, it's not a class, it doesn't need to be art that someone else wants you to do. There are options but they are there to create your own art. Support people are welcome, art options available are: colouring (for when creating is simply picking colours), painting (acrylic and watercolours), felting, jewelry making, something with wool (we have 1 crochet hook), pens, pencils, air dry clay.
There are even some DnD trees to decorate for the DnD sessions.

events.id: Between Dreams: Resistance and Representation in Asian Aotearoa-Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, 65 Victoria St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Between Dreams: Resistance and Representation in Asian Aotearoa
events.venue: Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, 65 Victoria St, Wellington
events.long_description:
This panel discussion will present fresh, progressive perspectives on what it means to be Asian in Aotearoa New Zealand. Te Papa curator and editor of ‘Between Dreams’ Grace Gassin will be joined by contributors Serena Lal, Keith Ng and Sapna Samant to discuss experiences of living between cultures and to explore the legacies of New Zealand’s diverse Asian diaspora histories.

Tue 23 June, 5.30-6.30pm
Ngā Pou Ruahine (Lvl 2), Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui Central Library
Free event, all welcome.

About the Speakers:
Dr Grace Gassin is Curator Asian New Zealand Histories at Te Papa. Raised in her mother’s Malaysian Chinese family, Grace proudly identifies as a second-generation Chinese (Hokkien) New Zealander of mixed heritage. Grace’s overarching focus is on highlighting the diverse historical and contemporary experiences of New Zealand’s many Asian diaspora communities. Her wide-ranging interests also encompass the politics of inclusion and interpretation that frame our understandings of Asian diaspora histories, and transnational dimensions of Asian Australasian diaspora experiences. She is also a committed delegate for E tū union.

Keith Ng is a Hong Kong New Zealander based in Pōneke. He has been a freelance writer, journalist and data-visualisation specialist for most of his career, and a data journalist for the NZ Herald until 2022. His work has ranged from reporting on politics, public finances and cybersecurity to investigations of companies and hidden assets. He currently works as a data scientist in the public sector, and occasionally as a freelance digital investigator. He was in Hong Kong during the summer of 2019, and he still misses the city’s Museum of History.

Dr Sapna Samant. is a writer, filmmaker, radio producer, activist, GP and single mum. A migrant herself, Sapna tells migrant and refugee stories through her company Holy Cow Media. She has made award-winning content for Radio New Zealand and produced The Asian Radio Show, which played on Radio Live from 2008 to 2012. While her initial filmmaking mahi was through a pan- Asian lens, her activism has led her to focus on making visible the many Indian and other South Asian stories in Aotearoa.

Serena Lal is a liberation led innovator and Fijian Indian girmitya descendant, raised as a 1.5 generation migrant in Tāmaki Makaurau and now living in Pōneke. A long serving member of Asians Supporting Tino Rangatiratanga, she works across cross cultural partnerships, mental health, governance, and community led innovation, and leads Kites Trust, a peer led Tāngata Whaiora organisation grounded in lived experience.
events.description:
Te Papa curator and editor of ‘Between Dreams’ Grace Gassin will be joined by contributors Serena Lal, Keith Ng and Sapna Samant to discuss experiences of living between cultures and to explore the legacies of New Zealand’s diverse Asian diaspora histories.
Grace’s overarching focus is on highlighting the diverse historical and contemporary experiences of New Zealand’s many Asian diaspora communities.
Keith Ng is a Hong Kong New Zealander based in Pōneke.
She has made award-winning content for Radio New Zealand and produced The Asian Radio Show, which played on Radio Live from 2008 to 2012.
Serena Lal is a liberation led innovator and Fijian Indian girmitya descendant, raised as a 1.5 generation migrant in Tāmaki Makaurau and now living in Pōneke.

events.id: Jewellery-making in 8 weeks for Beginners-Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Jewellery-making in 8 weeks for Beginners
events.venue: Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington
events.long_description:
This class is beginner level only, with each student following the class project.

It starts Tue 23 June and runs for 8 weeks ill Tue 11 August, 5.30pm - 8pm.

Our popular weeknight classes are the best way for beginners to get into jewellery-making in a lively, warm and friendly environment.

The stone-setting process teaches you many fundamental techniques in one project!

We start with basic ring-making and stone-setting in copper or brass. Students will learn measuring, texturing, shaping, cutting and soldering.

Then we move on to making a practice ring set with a Cabochon stone (a cabochon has a flat bottom and a smooth, domed top) in a Bezel Setting.

As you work through your practice ring and setting, you can move on to working in silver and produce another ring with a cabochon stone that you really love. This way, you will learn the basics as you go on your practice ring, before taking the next step in costly silver.

This class has all materials included in the class fee!

Each student receives a carefully curated materials pack including copper or brass, sterling and fine silver for your ring-making projects, as well as two stones from our supply so you get a good stone for your level of experience.

We have loads of gorgeous stones of a range of colours, and they are all appropriate for beginner settings.

Our booking system asks for payment to be made via credit card, Afterpay or PayPal on booking. If you do not have access to these payment methods or need to pay your course fee over time, please email us to arrange this ;)

events.name: Awareness through Movement Classes on Mt Vic
events.venue: Quaker Meeting Room, 8 Moncrieff St, Mt Victoria, Wellington
events.long_description:
Want to reduce pain and tension, improve posture, in a friendly and supportive atmosphere?

These lessons use principles of neuroplasticity, biomechanics, and human development to help you improve long-term habits of movement and posture.

They are based on the pioneering work of physicist and engineer Dr Moshe Feldenkrais. He developed his unique system to help manage his own knee injuries. His method is now used worldwide.

First class is free.
Sue 0274667123

events.id: Cooking Classes-Sheila's Indian Kitchen, Ashton Fitchett Dr, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Cooking Classes
events.venue: Sheila's Indian Kitchen, Ashton Fitchett Dr, Wellington
events.long_description:
Authentic Indian cooking classes in Wellington with panaromic views over Wellington Harbour.

In each class, you will learn 4-5 recipes like: potato stuff roti, roasted eggplant curry, wholemeal, roti, naan; shahi paneer; dhal, samosa; palak paneer; gourmet chickpeas; vegetable korma; gulab jamun; kheer; mango lassi; masala chai, and many more...

The classes start at 6 pm or 10:30 am on weekends and weekdays with a 2-hour session followed by a dinner or lunch afterwards. The ingredients, copies of recipes, organic karma drink will be provided with a full course meal in a banquet setting. Custom made classes available for gluten free, dairy free and any other food intolerance and dietary requirements.The ingredients, recipes, complimentary organic karma drinks will be provided with a full course meal.

There are 2 to 8 people in each class in purpose built cooking studio. Also a private cooking classes available.

The menu for the class can be:

1) Spicy potato stuffed paratha and roti, rajasthani tomato relish, aalu matter or spicy peas- potato curry, roasted cumin yogurt raita and fresh mint- coriander chutney.

2) Shahi Paneer (cottage cheese cubes with spices and rich tomato-cashew gravy), garlic coriander naan, cumin jeera basmati rice and sweet fragrant rice pudding with cardamom. Also I will teach how to make fresh paneer.

3) Roasted eggplant curry, plain whole wheat chapatti or roti, jeera aaloo or potato curry, Tuar dahl (split yellow lentil) and Basmati rice with homemade pickle.

4) Palak paneer (creamy spinach curry with cubes of paneer), jeera rice (basmati rice with fragrant spices, panjabi chhole or spicy chickpeas curry and mango lassi. Also fresh paneer making.

5) Khadi (chickpeas fritters in yogurt and chickpeas flour soupy gravy), Aloo-Gobhi (cauliflower and potato curry),  savoury wraps with herbs and spices, fresh pickle and basmati rice.

The total cost is $130 per person, this includes ingredient cost. (As an alternative we also sell cooking class gift vouchers.The food options are unlimited and the class provides fresh and best ingredients)
For booking a class please contact indiancooking@xtra.co.nz

Just bring your taste buds! and Enjoy!!

events.name: GREAT EXPECTATIONS
events.venue: Nayland CollegeNelson, Nelson
events.long_description:
Level 3 Drama’s production brings to life the glorious and unforgettable characters of this Dickens classic.
A terrifying encounter with an escaped convict forever changes the life of the orphaned Pip. Turning his back on his humble beginnings as a blacksmith’s apprentice, he strives to better himself and become a gentleman, unaware of the hidden dangers that await him.
Level 3 Drama’s production brings to life the glorious and unforgettable characters of Dickens’ classic including the jilted bride Miss Havisham, icy heart-breaker Estella and Wopsle, who might be the worst actor to ever grace the stage of a
Join us for a fresh telling of this timeless classic that investigates what is means to see others who have more than you and what you can lose if you try to become like them – a message as relevant as ever in the age of social media.
PLEASE NOTE - SEATING IS LIMITED AND NO ADDITIONAL TICKETS WILL BE RELEASED – BOOK EARLY TO AVOID DISAPPOINTMENT.

- No double ups of lines across types i.e. no fairy Mimikyu as well as a psychic Mimikyu.
- If a Pokémon evolution line contains 2 types, that line will count towards your type limit for BOTH. i.e. a Scyther- Scizor line will count as both your Grass & Steel.
- Dual type Pokémon rules apply as normal except when in a evolution line count as the same type as the basic Pokémon as long as it shares a type i.e. Marill (Primal Crash)- Azumarill (Steam Seige) will count as a Fairy Evolution line
Example: a deck could include a Gyarados, Magikarp line for Water, a Torterra, Grotle, Turtwig line for Grass, and a Entei for Fire.
Apart from that the usual GLC Restriction will still apply
- Only one of each card with the same name allowed in a deck,
except for Basic Energy.
- Cards with a Rule Box are not allowed.
- ACE SPEC cards are not allowed.
- Legal Cards: Black & White – onwards, and a short ban list
- 60 card decks are built & 6 Prize Card games are played with
the most current Pokémon TCG rules.
Okidogi - Adrena-Power - is BANNED
Regieleki - Evolving Skies 060 - Teraspark - is BANNED
Additional format details, including the GLC banned list, can be found at the following link:
https://gymleaderchallenge.com/
Here are the event details:
Start Time: 6:00pm
Entry Fee: $10
Format: 60 card GLC (3-4 Rounds Swiss)
A Pokémon account is required to play. You can create on for free here: https://www.pokemon.com/

events.name: Gauntlet Prom - The Musical
events.venue: BATS Theatre,1 Kent Terrace, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Gauntlet Prom - The Musical
Gauntlet Prom is a campy, high-energy comedy musical about the pressures of superhero high school; who to team up with, and how to find your one true nemesis.
At Pinnacle Academy, future heroes and villains prepare for epic downtown battles. To secure their top choice of sponsor, they’ll need a compelling rivalry. And what better place to throw down the gauntlet and secure the nemesis of their dreams than prom?
Will the school power rivalry finally throw the gauntlet and make it official? Will the lone hero ever find a nemesis? Will the reputation of the academy survive its graduating class?

events.id: Hadestown: Teen Edition-Te Raukura ki Kapiti, Coastlands Theatre, 34a Raumati Rd, Paraparaumu-Event Finder
events.name: Hadestown: Teen Edition
events.venue: Te Raukura ki Kapiti, Coastlands Theatre, 34a Raumati Rd, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Kapiti College is excited to present Hadestown: Teen Edition, giving local audiences the opportunity to experience this internationally acclaimed musical live on stage.

Hadestown: Teen Edition blends modern folk and jazz-inspired music with ancient Greek mythology. It follows Orpheus and Eurydice, a young couple trying to make things work in a tough world, alongside Hades and Persephone, who rule over a dark, industrial underworld. When Eurydice is pulled into that world, Orpheus risks everything to bring her back, relying on nothing but his music and his belief in her. It’s a story about love, hope, and the choices we make.

The show is a huge team effort, with around 100 students involved. While many take the stage, others are working behind the scenes on lighting, sound, set design, and stage management. This helps to bring the entire production to life and gives students valuable experience both on and off stage.

--

For accessibility seating information and wheelchair space bookings please email kiaora@teraukura.nz or call 04)9740535 during office hours.

events.id: Julia-Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Julia
events.venue: Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
In 2012, Julia Gillard delivered a speech that ricocheted across Australia, New Zealand, and around the world. Now, the woman and the moment is brought to the stage in Julia. This gripping one-woman show puts us inside a very public storm, and reveals the life and career that led to it.

From idealistic young advocate to leader under relentless scrutiny, Julia takes us behind the microphone of the landmark “misogyny speech.” It steps into the personal, political and socio-cultural forces that forged it. Blending real excerpts with scenes inspired by true events, this is more than one leader’s story. It’s a sharp, human look at how we judge power, how we listen to women, and what we demand of those who lead us.

Against a backdrop of progress and pushback on gender equality, Julia takes on fresh resonance here in Aotearoa. It draws the line between conviction and compromise, private life and public duty. And it asks what it costs to stand your ground and say “not now, not ever” in a world that still questions women who lead.

From idealistic young advocate to leader under relentless scrutiny, Julia takes us behind the microphone of the landmark “misogyny speech.” It steps into the personal, political and socio-cultural forces that forged it.

events.id: OWE-1 Kent Terrace, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: OWE
events.venue: 1 Kent Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
A MUST-SEE new Aotearoa play written by Jack McGee and directed by Campbell Wright!
Adria’s transmission is shot, and she can’t afford to fix it. Her girlfriend, Mel, offers to pay. Mel doesn’t have the money. She asks her Aunt Jennifer. Jennifer is squeezed with the mortgage. Her son Steven owes her a few grand. Maybe she can get that back?
Loosely inspired by La Ronde, Arthur Schnitzler’s 19th century satire about interlinking lovers, Owe follows twelve individuals down a chain of debt and difficult conversations. With crackling dialogue, a sense of humour, and one hell of an ensemble, Squash Co’s newest work casts a wide lens on the cost of living crisis.
The play weaves a sincere tapestry of human interdependence in a world of shrinking wallets, narrowing worldviews, and closing borders. With twelve, short, eclectic scenes, Owe moves fast and keeps you wanting more.
️"Pōneke's brightest young theatre-makers - and honestly, thank god they're here" - Theatreview (on previous work)

events.name: The Story That Won't Go Away - Justice, Peace Sharing, Cooperating
events.venue: Kāpiti College Library Room, Margaret Rd, Raumati
events.long_description:
The Story that won't go away - how can we build a world
based on Justice & Peace, Sharing & Cooperating?


At the start of every new age from the beginning of civilisation on Planet Earth, new ideas and insights have been discovered, and introduced through advanced human beings.

Teachers have come into the world to inaugurate an age set in motion the ideas which would uplift and galvanise humanity bringing it forward in its evolution.

According to the Ageless Wisdom Teachings, an age or cycle is around 2,350 years. Teachers who have come into such a particular age were for example the Buddha, and other teachers. The Ageless Wisdom Teachings represent an ancient body of spiritual teachings involved with the development of humanity.

At the present time, humanity is going through a great spiritual crisis which is concentrated in our current political and economic structures.

For this current age, the Age of Aquarius, a teacher – Maitreya The World Teacher – is here to inspire humanity that the way forward is through sharing, cooperating, not competing, with enough food and resources for everyone – for a total transformation of every aspect of our lives – and systems – political, economic, social, cultural, scientific, educational, religious.

Humanity decides and implements the plan; realising humanity is one (based on humanity’s free will).

It is our choice – to decide (or not) – to create a beautiful new world, based on justice and peace.


~ followed by a short Transmission Meditation session ~

Share International New Zealand.
Contact (021) 261 2749

Access - entrance from Raumati Road/ walk - drive past Te Raukura ki Kāpiti / Kāpiti Performing Arts Centre

events.id: Stereolab tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Stereolab tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Banished Music & Strange News
The panic is over! Anglo-French avant-pop group Stereolab are indeed returning to Aotearoa in June, hot on the mechanical heels of last year’s brilliant album Instant Holograms on Metal Film. This was the band’s first studio album in fifteen years, and followed their celebrated reunion in 2019. The Guardian hailed the album as “a radiant return”, and Rolling Stone fizzed over the band’s “gliding grooves, wowing-and-fluttering synthesizers, and lyrics that elegantly pine for more”. They’ve got a cult following for a very good reason, and we’re beyond astonished to have them as part of our winter line-up!

events.id: Regional Business Briefings Winter 2026 - Wellington-Wellington ANZ CentreWellington, Wellington-Event Brite
events.name: Regional Business Briefings Winter 2026 - Wellington
events.venue: Wellington ANZ CentreWellington, Wellington
events.long_description:
Regional Business Briefings Winter 2026 - Wellington

Join us at our Winter Business Briefing in Wellington, in partnership with ANZ & Strategic pay.
A practical view of the economic, employment and policy forces shaping business in your region.
There’s a lot of information coming at businesses in New Zealand right now, but it’s not always clear how it connects with your day-to-day operations, or what it means in practice.
Our Regional Business Briefings bring together information on what’s happening in the economy, the latest employment relations updates, and policy context in a way that is directly relevant to your region and business.
These in-person sessions are designed to be interactive, providing space to ask questions, share perspectives, and engage directly with the people sharing your environment.
A clear view of what’s shaping your business environment.

Each briefing is structured to reflect what matters most to businesses right now:
Regional economic update
A real-world view of the economic environment, tailored to what’s happening in your region.
Employment relations insights
Practical updates drawn from real casework, highlighting the patterns, risks, and opportunities emerging across workplaces.
Policy and advocacy update
An overview of the key issues being worked on, and how they connect to your business.
Business Central NZ update
What we are seeing, what we are hearing, and how we are representing your interests.

Who should attend
These briefings are designed for business owners, senior leaders, and managers who want to stay informed, make better decisions, and remain connected to what is happening across their region.
If you are leading people, making decisions, or working through change in your business, this is a chance to step back, sense-check what you are seeing, and engage directly with those working across these issues every day.
Stay informed. Stay connected.
In a fast-moving environment, the businesses that stay close to what’s happening, and to each other, are the ones best placed to respond.
Agenda
8am-8.150am - Arrival networking
8.20am-10.00am - Briefing (including Q&A)
We look forward to seeing you there.

events.id: Uhi Tai: Te Reo Māori in Business 2026-Te Papa Tongarewa,55 Cable St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Uhi Tai: Te Reo Māori in Business 2026
events.venue: Te Papa Tongarewa,55 Cable St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Uhi Tai: Te Reo Māori in Business 2026
Uhi Tai brings together leaders, innovators and practitioners from across Aotearoa to explore how te reo Māori is shaping the future of business, technology, sport, tourism and brand identity. Over two days, speakers from across sectors will share practical insights into how organisations are embedding te reo Māori into strategy, customer experience, innovation and everyday operations.

Whether you are beginning your reo journey or are already integrating te reo Māori into your work, Uhi Tai offers an opportunity to learn, connect and be inspired by those leading this kaupapa.

The theme for Uhi Tai: Te Reo Māori in Business 2026 is ‘Ihu ki te kei, we all have a part to play’, speaking to the idea that there's room for everyone on a canoe, and there are opportunities for everyone to contribute to the journey.

Registrations are now open, get your tickets now e hoa mā.
Kia Kaha te Reo Māori | Te Taura Whiri i te Reo Māori

events.id: Self-Guided Tour of Stonehenge Aotearoa-Stonehenge Aotearoa, 51 Ahiaruhe Rd, Carterton-Event Finder
events.name: Self-Guided Tour of Stonehenge Aotearoa
events.venue: Stonehenge Aotearoa, 51 Ahiaruhe Rd, Carterton
events.long_description:
Explore Stonehenge Aotearoa at Your Own Pace

Our self-guided tour includes a map and a 12-minute audio-visual presentation to help you uncover the secrets of the henge. Afterward, head down to the stone circle and try out its unique acoustics — clap your hands and hear the echo respond, or whisper and see how far your voice carries.

A self-guided tour typically takes around 30 minutes, but many visitors stay longer simply because it’s so much fun. The bigger your group, the better the experience!

Please arrive at least 30 minutes before closing time.
Available during our opening hours: 10 AM – 4 PM.

events.name: Tahatū Careers Expo NZ - Wellington
events.venue: TSB Bank Arena,Queen's Wharf, Wellington, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Tahatū Careers Expo NZ - Wellington
The Tahatū Careers Expo NZ is Coming to Wellington!
Wednesday 24 June (10am-3pm + 5pm-7pm)

New Zealand’s longest-running careers event is back for 2026.
Whether you’re a student, jobseeker, or figuring out your next move, the NZ Careers Expo is your chance to explore exciting career pathways, study options, and training opportunities across Aotearoa.

What you can expect:
- Connect with employers, tertiary providers, employers, industry groups, and government organisations
- Discover a wide range of career and training pathways
- Get advice and inspiration to help plan your future - all under one roof!

And it’s completely FREE to attend!

Bring your questions, curiosity and an open mind - you never know what career path could be waiting for you.

For more info go to https://careersexpo.org.nz/

events.id: Shared 90-Minute Guided Tour-Stonehenge Aotearoa, 51 Ahiaruhe Rd, Carterton-Event Finder
events.name: Shared 90-Minute Guided Tour
events.venue: Stonehenge Aotearoa, 51 Ahiaruhe Rd, Carterton
events.long_description:
Join a Shared Guided Tour and experience Stonehenge Aotearoa through the eyes of an expert. In this 90-minute journey, you’ll uncover how ancient cultures measured time, tracked the Sun and Moon, and connected the heavens with life on Earth.

Your experience begins with a captivating introduction and a 15-minute audio-visual presentation explaining how the Henge works. Then, step outside with your guide for a detailed tour of the stone circle, where astronomy, archaeology, and mythology come together in one unforgettable setting.

Perfect for visitors seeking a deeper understanding of the stars and stories that shaped human history.

events.id: Sunset Guided Tour-Stonehenge Aotearoa, 51 Ahiaruhe Rd, Carterton-Event Finder
events.name: Sunset Guided Tour
events.venue: Stonehenge Aotearoa, 51 Ahiaruhe Rd, Carterton
events.long_description:
Experience Stonehenge Aotearoa at its most breathtaking — as the sun dips below the Wairarapa horizon.
Our Sunset Guided Tour blends astronomy, storytelling, and the natural beauty of twilight for an evening you will never forget.

Led by one of our knowledgeable guides, this 90-minute experience explores how ancient cultures used the Sun to track time and mark the turning of the seasons. You’ll discover the scientific and cultural thinking behind the Henge’s unique Southern Hemisphere design before watching the sunset alignment from inside the stone circle.

Weather permitting, you’ll witness how the setting Sun interacts with the structure — a striking moment that links ancient knowledge with the rhythms of the natural world.

Duration and Schedule
• Allow: Approximately 90 minutes
• Start Time: Begins one hour before sunset
• Availability: Offered any day with advance booking

events.id: LEANZ AGM + Keynote by Hon Judith Collins KC-Buddle Findlay Wellington & on ZoomWellington, Wellington-Event Brite
events.name: LEANZ AGM + Keynote by Hon Judith Collins KC
events.venue: Buddle Findlay Wellington & on ZoomWellington, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join us for the 2026 LEANZ AGM, featuring a keynote address by The Hon Judith Collins KC.
The Law and Economics Association of New Zealand (LEANZ) and Buddle Findlay invite you to the LEANZ AGM and a seminar (in-person and online) where the Hon Judith Collins KC will present on the challenges and opportunities for local and central governments with technological advances.

Wednesday 24 June 2026
Buddle Findlay, AON Building, Level 17, 1 Willis Street, Wellington CentralOnline attendance also available
Attendees are welcome to arrive from 5:00 pm, with refreshments available from then until the AGM commences at 5:30 pm until 6:00pm followed by the seminar from Hon Judith Collins KC.

Speaker: Hon Judith Collins KC
Topic: Reflections on challenges and opportunities for local and central governments with technological advances
Hon Judith Collins KC – bio
Prior to being elected as a Member of Parliament, Judith Collins practised law in Auckland for 21 years, primarily in the areas of commercial, property, tax and employment law. She undertook governance positions with both private and public entities.
Judith was elected as a Member of Parliament for 24 years and held 18 portfolios as a Minister of the Crown, including Justice, Police, Corrections, Defence, Energy and Resources, Space, Science, Innovation and Technology, Digitising Government, the Public Service, Revenue, and New Zealand's intelligence and security agencies. Judith is a former Attorney-General of New Zealand and has a strong record in advocating for the Rule of Law and comity.
Judith is a past President of the Auckland District Law Society (now The Law Association of New Zealand), and a Vice-President of the New Zealand Law Society. She is an honorary life member of The Law Association of New Zealand.
She is a Vice-Patron of the Vietnam Veterans Association Children’s & Grandchildren’s Trust, an honorary life member of the Long-Range Desert Group, and an honorary member of the Sappers’ Association (NZ).
With an interest in AI, Judith was pleased to have been an Advisory Road Member of the NAOInstitute (Natural, Artificial, and Organisation Intelligence Institute) of the University of Auckland.
Judith holds a Bachelor of Laws, a Master of Laws (Honours) and a Master of Taxation Studies from the University of Auckland, and a Graduate Diploma in Occupational Health & Safety from Massey University.
Judith is a barrister and was appointed a King’s Counsel in 2023. Judith is also an Adjunct Professor of Law at Western Sydney University.

CPD Hours

events.id: LEANZ AGM + Keynote by Hon Judith Collins KC-Buddle Findlay Wellington & on Zoom,1 Willis Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: LEANZ AGM + Keynote by Hon Judith Collins KC
events.venue: Buddle Findlay Wellington & on Zoom,1 Willis Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
LEANZ AGM + Keynote by Hon Judith Collins KC
Join us for the 2026 LEANZ AGM, featuring a keynote address by The Hon Judith Collins KC.

About this Event



The Law and Economics Association of New Zealand (LEANZ) and Buddle Findlay invite you to the LEANZ AGM and a seminar (in-person and online) where the Hon Judith Collins KC will present on the challenges and opportunities for local and central governments with technological advances.

Wednesday 24 June 2026
Buddle Findlay, AON Building, Level 17, 1 Willis Street, Wellington CentralOnline attendance also available
Attendees are welcome to arrive from 5:00 pm, with refreshments available from then until the AGM commences at 5:30 pm until 6:00pm followed by the seminar from Hon Judith Collins KC.

Speaker: Hon Judith Collins KC
Topic: Reflections on challenges and opportunities for local and central governments with technological advances
Hon Judith Collins KC – bio
Prior to being elected as a Member of Parliament, Judith Collins practised law in Auckland for 21 years, primarily in the areas of commercial, property, tax and employment law. She undertook governance positions with both private and public entities.
Judith was elected as a Member of Parliament for 24 years and held 18 portfolios as a Minister of the Crown, including Justice, Police, Corrections, Defence, Energy and Resources, Space, Science, Innovation and Technology, Digitising Government, the Public Service, Revenue, and New Zealand's intelligence and security agencies. Judith is a former Attorney-General of New Zealand and has a strong record in advocating for the Rule of Law and comity.
Judith is a past President of the Auckland District Law Society (now The Law Association of New Zealand), and a Vice-President of the New Zealand Law Society. She is an honorary life member of The Law Association of New Zealand.
She is a Vice-Patron of the Vietnam Veterans Association Children’s & Grandchildren’s Trust, an honorary life member of the Long-Range Desert Group, and an honorary member of the Sappers’ Association (NZ).
With an interest in AI, Judith was pleased to have been an Advisory Road Member of the NAOInstitute (Natural, Artificial, and Organisation Intelligence Institute) of the University of Auckland.
Judith holds a Bachelor of Laws, a Master of Laws (Honours) and a Master of Taxation Studies from the University of Auckland, and a Graduate Diploma in Occupational Health & Safety from Massey University.
Judith is a barrister and was appointed a King’s Counsel in 2023. Judith is also an Adjunct Professor of Law at Western Sydney University.

CPD Hours
Lawyers may note that if this seminar is part of your learning objectives in your CPDPR, then attendance at this event can count for one hour of your 10 CPD hours. Please contact a LEANZ representative at the event to confirm your attendance.

Online attendance (Zoom): from 5:30pm onwards

events.id: Lost Wax Casting - 6 Weeks Course-Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Lost Wax Casting - 6 Weeks Course
events.venue: Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington
events.long_description:
A fantastic course that's full of fun and inspiration!

It starts on Wed 25 March and runs for 6 weeks till Wed 29 April, 5.30pm - 8pm.

Create interesting designs in wax, then send them off to be professionally cast in metal. Once your pieces are back, you will be taught how to cut, file and shape them into wearable pieces.

Level 1 students: Learn to work in both soft and hard jewellers' wax and send your waxes away to be cast. When they return, you will learn how to clean them up into finished jewellery and/or incorporate them into jewellery in multiple ways. We start with rings, but encourage students to think outside the box!

Level 2 students: Advance your carving skills and learn to set stones in your waxes. This can be done either by creating claws and other settings or trying the cast-in-place technique - an organic, exciting process.

Included in the class fee are all your different types of waxes, sandpaper and courier costs.

Not included are the casting and metal costs, which vary from student to student depending on what they want to make. Small castings can cost around $7 - $10, and metals are between $0.35 per gram for bronze - $4 per gram for silver. Please see details on our Regular Classes page.

Our booking system asks for payment to be made via credit card, PayPal or Afterpay on booking. If you do not have access to these payment methods or need to pay your course fee over time, please email us to arrange this ;)

events.name: Mo Bros x Padel House
events.venue: Padel House - Wellington,48A Kemp Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Mo Bros x Padel House
Join the Mo Bros at Padel House for a fun, sweaty session of pade, laughs, and good vibes!

About this Event


Mo Bros x Padel House - $10 payable on the night at the venue
Join us for our next Movember Connection Event at Padel.
This event is all about bringing men together through movement, connection and conversation. Whether you are already active, looking to get moving again, or simply keen to meet some new people, this is a great opportunity to do something positive for your mind and body.
The evening will include time to play padel, connect with other men, have a laugh, and be part of a community that is focused on improving men’s health and wellbeing.
With June being Men’s Health Month, we want to keep creating spaces where men can show up, move, talk and connect in a relaxed environment.

events.id: Garden to Kimjar- Sauerkraut & kimchi Workshop-27 Chelsea St, Miramar, New Zealand 6022,Motu Kairangi Te Kohanga Reo, 27 Chelsea St, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Garden to Kimjar- Sauerkraut & kimchi Workshop
events.venue: 27 Chelsea St, Miramar, New Zealand 6022,Motu Kairangi Te Kohanga Reo, 27 Chelsea St, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Garden to Kimjar- Sauerkraut & kimchi Workshop
"From Garden to Kimjar"

In March we had a Relish bash and Cheeky Chutney with the Seeds to Feeds team. We're excited that the Seeds to feeds to be Leading our Garden to Kimjar - Sauerkraut & Kimchi workshop.

We warmly invite likeminded Kimchi beginners to "From the Garden to Kimjar- Sauerkraut & kimchi Workshop", Lead by the Seeds to feeds team and hosted by Miramar Maupuia Community Centre on 24th June.

As the growing season winds down, it's the perfect time to preserve the last of the summer harvest and transform fresh vegetables into delicious, pro-biotic rich foods to nourish us through the colder months. These tasty ferments should be ready to enjoy around Matariki and will continue to develop beautifully throughout the winter.

Wednesday 24th June 6.30-9.30pm Miramar & Maupuia Community Centre

Are you interested in learning?
please email Miramar Maupuia Community Centre.

You will need to bring:

1 x 1 litre clean glass jar to take home your ferments
any surplus vegetables from your garden that you'd like to ferment (optional)
Koha if you can

Limited to 16 persons (5 booked)

Irihaapeti Te Aho


#SeedsToFeeds #wethrivetogether❤️ #CommunityKindness

events.id: Paint and Wine Night - Banksy Balloon Girl (first Drink Included)-Hey Coastie, Wellington, 42 Tutere Street, Waikanae-Event Finder
events.name: Paint and Wine Night - Banksy Balloon Girl (first Drink Included)
events.venue: Hey Coastie, Wellington, 42 Tutere Street, Waikanae
events.long_description:
The concept is simple - take a brush in one hand, a drink in the other and let’s paint a masterpiece!

Join us for a creative night of drinking and painting in the heart of Waikanae Beach! No previous painting experience necessary. Get your creative juices flowing and your brush strokes going!

Drinks: First drink is included in the ticket price

Food: A delicious menu will be available to purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website.

events.id: Showquest Wellington (Junior Event)-St James Theatre Wellington, Wellington, NZ-Ticket Master
events.name: Showquest Wellington (Junior Event)
events.venue: St James Theatre Wellington, Wellington, NZ
events.long_description:
RQP presents Showquest & TOI, a night celebrating creative rangatahi as they bring their ideas to life on stage.

Showquest is a nationwide performing arts programme and a platform for youth voices to be heard. Be amazed by original performances combining Art, Music, Dance, Drama, Culture & Technology, as students design, produce and perform their stories in celebration and recognition by their community.

Following the Showquest stage performances, prepare to be WOWed with TOI, a show-stopping wearable art showcase featuring bold, imaginative garments by emerging student designers. 

Watch friendly inter-school rivalries come to life as students explore the issues that shape their world. Join friends and wh?nau for an unforgettable night supporting local talent on the big stage.

Showquest and TOI are proudly presented by RQP, champions for young creators and producers of Smokefreerockquest, Smokefree Tangata Beats, Rockshop Bandquest, OnScreen and Stills.

events.id: Waenga-Toi Pōneke Arts Centre, 3-6 Market Lane, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Waenga
events.venue: Toi Pōneke Arts Centre, 3-6 Market Lane, Wellington
events.long_description:
Created by Hariata & Tamati Moriarty and directed by Jim Moriarty, 'Waenga' centres on a young wahine Māori who is arrested and finds herself in a police holding cell. Alongside her journey is Peyton, who is coming to understand what a Tangata Tiriti partnership is and Grayson, a young Māori lawyer navigating his role in the system.

Performed by an ensemble cast, including well known rangatahi Hariata and Tamati Moriarty, the play is presented in Te Rākau’s unique Theatre Marae framework, creating space for collective reflection and shared experience.

Te Rākau is the longest running Māori theatre company in Aotearoa founded in 1989.

Recommended age 12+ contains swearing and depicts some violence. Children under 12 can accompany for free with a paying adult.

events.id: Wānaga Matariki-Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa, 55 Cable St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Wānaga Matariki
events.venue: Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa, 55 Cable St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Wānanga Matariki: Matariki Herenga Waka is a special Matariki evening kōrero exploring the traditions, knowledge and future of waka voyaging in Aotearoa and across the Pacific.

Presented by the crew of Ngahiraka-Mai-Tawhiti and Te Kura Waka, this free public wānanga brings together experienced navigators, cultural leaders and storytellers to share insights into traditional celestial navigation, ocean knowledge, waka hourua traditions, and the intergenerational revival of indigenous voyaging practices.

Through personal stories, cultural perspectives and discussion, speakers will reflect on the past, present and future of waka navigation, and the role of mātauranga Māori in reconnecting communities with whakapapa, te ao Māori and the moana.

The evening features respected speakers and facilitators including Tainui Stephens, Manihera Forbes, Hinerapa Rupuha and Tamahau Tangitu, each bringing extensive experience in Māori storytelling, cultural leadership, traditional navigation and Pacific voyaging.

Suitable for adults, educators, cultural practitioners and anyone interested in Māori knowledge systems, navigation traditions and contemporary indigenous storytelling.

Booking is recommended.

This event is wheelchair accessible. Please note that variable lighting may be used during the event.

events.name: Secret Cinema "Big-Screen" - June 2026
events.venue: Massey University Theatrette, Block 10 (Old Museum Building), Buckle Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
AroVideo proudly presents our fifth Secret Cinema 'Big-Screen' event for the 2026 Season at our new state-of-the-art venue at Massey University's National Academy of Screen Arts.

Secret Cinema is a "blind date with a movie" curated each month by Andrew at AroVideo, who presents an introduction to the mystery film and hosts a brief forum for discussion after the screening.

The film we have in store for you for the June screening is a delightfully off-beat indie comedy (rated M - contains offensive language).
You'll find more clues published on AroVision here: https://ondemand.arovideo.co.nz/film/secret-cinema-at-home-black/

The snack bar opens at 6.30pm, with Garage Project TINY non-alcoholic beer our beverage of choice ($10 cash at the door). And it's free parking for all patrons.

The Big-Screen event is held on the last Wednesday of every month, supported by a Secret Cinema 'At Home' on-demand version, available on our sister streaming site, AroVision.

events.id: AGM with talk by Gábor Tóth-Wellington-Facebook
events.name: AGM with talk by Gábor Tóth
events.venue: Wellington
events.long_description:
Everyone is warmly invited to the Thorndon Society’s AGM, featuring special guest speaker Gábor Tóth - you don’t need to be a member or a Thorndon resident to attend!
So please come along, sit through our brief AGM business, enjoy Gábor’s excellent illustrated talk, and stay awhile for a convivial chat. We look forward to seeing you there!

The Butcher, the Baker and the Beauchamps
Katherine Mansfield convincingly evoked the very particular setting of her childhood home, at 75 Tinakori Road, to examine late Victorian class interactions, in her 1922 work of short fiction ‘The Garden Party’.
Local and NZ History Specialist Gábor Tóth has twitched the historical lace curtain to give us the facts about her Beauchamp family’s neighbours and neighbourhood - who really was just over the palings or the pickets? And what was that terrible stench?
Gábor’s entertaining talk, outlining his detailed investigations into the Tinakori Road area’s residents and their occupations in 1900, will not only give you fascinating insights into demography and social history but possibly a newfound appreciation of your neighbours!

events.id: Stereolab (UK) w/ Glass Vaults-Meow Nui, 92 Vivian Street, Te Aro, Wellington, 6011, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Stereolab (UK) w/ Glass Vaults
events.venue: Meow Nui, 92 Vivian Street, Te Aro, Wellington, 6011, Wellington
events.long_description:
The panic is over! Banished Music and Strange News are impossibly excited to be hosting iconic Anglo-French avant-pop group Stereolab for two very special shows in Aotearoa this June.

Fans who saw the Strange Universe: Winter series announcement yesterday will have seen the band confirmed to play Powerstation on Thursday 25 June, but Wellington fans can breathe a sigh of relief with the announcement of a standalone Stereolab show at Meow Nui, Wednesday 24 June.

‍Returning on the mechanical heels of last year’s brilliant album Instant Holograms on Metal Film. This was the band’s first studio album in fifteen years, and followed their celebrated reunion in 2019. The Guardian hailed the album as “a radiant return”, and Rolling Stone fizzed over the band’s “gliding grooves, wowing-and-fluttering synthesizers, and lyrics that elegantly pine for more”.
‍
“..hailed as an inspiration by everyone from Deerhoof to Tyler, the Creator” - The Guardian

Stereolab have a cult following for a very good reason, with Banished and Strange News beyond astonished to have secured one of their all time favourite acts for these two hotly anticipated shows.

events.id: Awareness through Movement in Karori-Karori Community Centre, 7 Beauchamp St, Karori, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Awareness through Movement in Karori
events.venue: Karori Community Centre, 7 Beauchamp St, Karori, Wellington
events.long_description:
Maintain mobility and balance as you go through life.

Want to reduce pain and tension, improve posture, in a friendly and supportive atmosphere?

These lessons use principles of neuroplasticity, biomechanics, and human development to help you improve long-term habits of movement and posture.

They are based on the pioneering work of physicist and engineer Dr Moshe Feldenkrais. He developed his unique system to help manage his own knee injuries. His method is now used worldwide.

First class free.
Sue 0274667123

events.name: Hindi Language Classes
events.venue: Sheila's Indian Kitchen, Ashton Fitchett Dr, Wellington
events.long_description:
Learn Hindi language in 8 to 12 weeks. Travelling to India? Hindi will help you explore and give you invaluable experience of incredible India.

The lesson is approximately 90 mins long one on one teaching, and I teach anyone according to your ability. Can you please let me know how much do you know about the Hindi language and why do you want to learn.

Example: are you planning to travel to India? Or just interested in learning a new language?
We teach basic writing, reading and speaking in 6 sessions, and also teach more advanced levels if anyone wants to learn more. Classes are available everyday. Day and time can be arranged according to our availability.

If the time and cost of these lessons are prohibiting you from learning the language then please let me know and I can definitely work something out for keen Hindi learners.

Email: hindiclass@xtra.co.nz
Txt: 02102697038

events.id: Understanding women’s health across the lifespan: A talk by Lynda Lovatt-Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui Central Library, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011,Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Understanding women’s health across the lifespan: A talk by Lynda Lovatt
events.venue: Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui Central Library, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011,Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Understanding women’s health across the lifespan: A talk by Lynda Lovatt
In this presentation Wellington-based Menopause specialist nurse, personal trainer, clinician and author Lynda Lovatt asks "What if the conditions we associate with 'old age' actually begin decades earlier?" Through one woman’s story — from age 20 to 80 — this talk explores how hormones, muscle, bone health, pelvic floor changes, stress, lifestyle, pregnancy, menopause, and chronic disease risk shape a woman’s future health outcomes. This is not a talk about fear or perfection. It’s about understanding the female body earlier, recognising the warning signs sooner, and learning what women can do at every age to stay stronger, healthier, independent, and well for longer. This will be a powerful and practical conversation for women of all ages — and for anyone who cares for them.

Location: Ngā Pou Ruahine space (Level 2)

About the presenter/author:

Lynda Lovatt is a New Zealand Registered Nurse, Exercise and Pelvic Health Professional, and Lifestyle Medicine Practitioner. She is a successful businesswoman with a gift for creating outstanding exercise programmes for women to promote safe exercise during pregnancy, prepare them for birth and recover them afterwards. Her Postnatal Recovery Programme won Programme Excellence in 2017, and her Postnatal Recovery Programme for Personal Trainers was awarded finalist for the Innovation award in 2020. Both awards were received at the NZ Exercise Industry Awards. Lynda’s earlier nursing career was rich and diverse, and saw her looking after burn victims and disfigurement, cancer and chronic disease. After Lynda sold her fitness business, she had a strong desire to return to nursing to focus on women’s health aging from Menopause onwards. Driven to prevent the disease she had seen in New Zealand and British hospitals, Lynda started up her own Wellington Clinic. Her mission is to help women navigate menopause, keep healthy as they age and to provide assessment and treatment for incontinence and the genitourinary changes in Menopause.

Lynda’s superpower is her empathy for her patients and her drive to ease the conversation in taboo topics in women’s health to improve health outcomes. Increasingly, Lynda is being invited to speak at corporate events, webinars and women’s business groups on topics such as menopause, healthy aging and pelvic floor dysfunction from fifty years onwards.

Lynda is the author of: 'Lady Talk, A personal pursuit with practice pearls'. Copies of the book will be available for purchase and signing after the event.

The Hutt City Night Market is the place to be - a vibrant international celebration of food, packed with incredible flavours from around the world!

events.id: Street Eats and Beats-Masterton Club, 98 Chapel Street, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: Street Eats and Beats
events.venue: Masterton Club, 98 Chapel Street, Masterton
events.long_description:
Masterton Street Eats & Beats

Formerly known as Thursday Night Food Trucks, Masterton Street Eats & Beats is Masterton's favourite weekly food truck and community event, bringing together great food, live music, and a relaxed family-friendly atmosphere every Thursday evening.

With a rotating lineup of food trucks and street food vendors, no two weeks are ever quite the same. From gourmet burgers and international cuisine to sweet treats, coffee, and local favourites, there's something to satisfy every craving. Add live music, plenty of seating, and a welcoming community vibe, and you've got the perfect way to kick off your weekend early.

Whether you're catching up with friends, bringing the family out for dinner, or simply looking for an easy meal after work, Masterton Street Eats & Beats offers a fun and affordable night out for all ages.

Event Details
Every Thursday
4:30pm – 7:00pm (rain or shine)
Outside the Masterton Club, Chapel Street (SH2), Masterton
Free Entry

What to Expect
• rotating food and beverage vendors throughout the season
• Live music and entertainment
• Family-friendly atmosphere
• outdoor seating available or bring a picnic blanket
• New vendors regularly joining the lineup
• EFTPOS and cash accepted by most vendors

Food truck and street food vendors are always welcome to enquire about joining the event.

Come hungry, bring your friends, and enjoy one of the Wairarapa's favourite weekly community gatherings.

events.id: Between the Lines: Private Lives with New Zealand String Quartet-Ngā Pou Ruahine, Level 2, Te Matapihi, 65 Victoria Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Between the Lines: Private Lives with New Zealand String Quartet
events.venue: Ngā Pou Ruahine, Level 2, Te Matapihi, 65 Victoria Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
This event will take place in Ngā Pou Ruahine on Level 2 of Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, Wellington Central Library.

The theme of our June musical book club is private lives.

Between the Lines is a monthly early-evening chamber music series and book club that brings music and literature into conversation. Presented in the beautiful setting of Ngā Pou Ruahine in Te Matapihi, the series invites audiences to listen, think, and talk. No prior knowledge of music or literature is required, just a curious mind.

Taking place monthly from April to October on the final Thursday of each month, Between the Lines has been co-created by New Zealand String Quartet, Orchestra Wellington, and New Zealand School of Music. Each month these organisations will take turns presenting a talented group of musicians to perform music and host discussion on a different literary theme. From devil’s bargains to language games, the obsessive tortured artist to the intimate private lives of writers and composers, this book club will delve deep into themes that span across artforms.

Each session will feature carefully selected music and a reading list highlighting the theme of the month. So, arrive solo or bring your book club to Te Matapihi for a new kind of conversation.


The theme of our June musical book club is private lives.

Music to be performed:
Amadeus Mozart | String quartet Kv. 465 “Dissonance”, 1st movement
Leoš Janáček | Intimate Letters, 3rd movement
Dmitri Shostakovich | String Quartet No. 8, 1st & 2nd movement
Felix Mendelssohn | String Quartet No.6, Op.80, 1st movement
Pyotr Tchaikovsky | String Quartet No. 1, Op. 11, 2nd movement
Florence Price | "Adoration"

Optional reading list is available on the New Zealand String Quartet website.

Music and literature both have the power to transport an audience deep into the interior private lives of the composer and author. For our June musical book club, Between the Lines, which will take place on the anniversary of the publication of Anne Frank’s diaries in 1947, the New Zealand String Quartet have devised a programme of music that explores the ‘Private Lives’, secret thoughts, and hidden desires of four composers. Mozart, Janáček, Shostakovich, Mendelssohn, Tchaikovsky and Price each encoded their music with secrets, imbuing their music with a personal significance often at odds with their public personas. Now, by reading their letters, alongside performing their music, we can learn more about these composers’ personalities and appreciate their thoughts, fears, and hopes.

The reading list for ‘Private Lives’ brings together a variety of primary sources – the letters and diaries of artists, writers, composers and people living through extraordinary and difficult times – alongside fiction that utilises diary and letter forms.


Explore the rest of the series:

Thursday 30 April, 5:30pm - Orchestra Wellington - The Art of Conversation (first & second sessions to take place on Ngā Paiaka/ Bleachers on Te Papa Whenua/ Ground Floor)
Thursday 28 May, 5:30pm - New Zealand String Quartet - Obsession (first & second sessions to take place on Ngā Paiaka/ Bleachers on Te Papa Whenua/ Ground Floor)
Thursday 25 June, 5:30pm - New Zealand String Quartet - Private Lives
Thursday 30 July, 5:30pm - Orchestra Wellington - The Devil's Bargain
Thursday 27 August, 5:30pm - New Zealand String Quartet - Outsider
Thursday 24 September, 5:30pm - NZSM - Te Kōkī - Theme to be announced
Thursday 29 October, 5:30pm - Orchestra Wellington - Language Games

events.id: Jewellery-making on Thursdays, 8-week block, Level 2+-Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Jewellery-making on Thursdays, 8-week block, Level 2+
events.venue: Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington
events.long_description:
Our popular weeknight block classes are the best way for keen makers to get into jewellery making in a lively, warm and friendly environment. This class will be held at our new waterfront location, Market Lane!

The course starts on Thursday 25 June and runs for 8 weeks till Thursday 13 August, 5.30pm-8pm.

This class is designed for students who already have some skills and are ready to develop their craftsmanship in a lively, warm, and supportive environment. Guided by our highly skilled tutors, you’ll build confidence, refine your techniques, and gain the satisfaction of making jewellery that reflects your personal style.

If you’re brand new to jewellery making, we recommend beginning with our Tuesday Evening 8-Week Beginners’ Block, which gives a solid foundation in essential techniques before moving on to this class.

At Level 2, the focus is on precision and technique. Students explore the art of chain making, a classic jewellery making project that develops fine soldering skills and accuracy in construction. This project is an excellent way to build confidence with the torch and create a piece of jewellery that is both functional and beautiful. The finished chain can become a pendant, bracelet, or necklace, or you may choose to adapt it into a design of your own.

At Level 3 and above, the emphasis shifts toward creativity and design development. Students at this stage are encouraged to take on more complex projects, experiment with new and challenging techniques, and bring their own design ideas to life. With the guidance of our skilled and supportive tutors, you’ll learn how to push your skills further, problem-solve during the making process, and refine your eye for detail and design.

Evening classes are not just about the technical side of jewellery making - they are also a chance to slow down, enjoy the process, and be part of a creative community. Many students return term after term, finding the rhythm of regular making both mindful and rewarding.

Please note: this class does not include a materials allowance, giving you the flexibility to choose your own metals, stones, or other materials. You only pay for what you use, making it easier to customise your projects and work at your own pace.

Our booking system asks for full payment at the time of registration (via credit card, PayPal, or AfterPay). If you don’t have access to these options or would like to pay in instalments, please email us and we’ll happily arrange an alternative.
