events.id: Tanzanian Dinner-Le Ciel Bakery, 100 Tory Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Tanzanian Dinner
events.venue: Le Ciel Bakery, 100 Tory Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Tanzanian Cuisine Dinner – Returning for a Limited Season

After winning Best Event at last year’s WOAP, our celebrated Tanzanian Cuisine Dinner is back — for two weeks only.

Experience an evening designed for discovery, comfort, and generous sharing. Guests are invited to order as many dishes as they wish from a specially curated menu showcasing classic Tanzanian flavours, traditions, and vibrant spices. This is not a set menu, but a relaxed dining experience that encourages exploration at your own pace.

To complement the food, an optional pairing menu featuring wines, beers, and thoughtfully crafted non-alcoholic beverages will be available each evening.

Event Dates:
May 7th, 8th, 9th
May 14th, 15th, 16th
June dates added due to high demand!

Time:
6:00 PM – 8:30 PM

Join us for a warm, flavour-driven dining experience that quickly became one of our most loved and awarded events.

Seats are limited.

We are offering a tasting menu with 5 tasting plates for $62 per person, or the main event, a 5-course dinner for $82 per person.

OPTION 1: TASTING MENU
5 Tasting Plates — $62 per person
Option to order extra plates at additional cost!

Course 1: Mshikaki ya Ng’ombe
Charcoal-grilled beef skewers
Marinated in garlic, ginger, lemon and pili pili.
Served with fresh kachumbari (tomato–onion salad).

Course 2: Viazi Karai
Spiced potato fritters (Zanzibar style)
Golden fried, dusted with masala,
served with tamarind-coconut chutney.

Course 3: Maharage ya Nazi
Slow-simmered red kidney beans in coconut cream
Fragrant with turmeric and cardamom.
Served with turmeric basmati rice.

Course 4: Kuku wa Kupaka
Grilled chicken in rich coconut curry sauce
Charred first, then simmered in spiced coconut gravy.
Served with chapati.

Course 5: Mandazi
Traditional doughnut
with mango passion curd.

OPTION 2: FIVE-COURSE DINNER
Plated Culinary Experience — $82 per person

This version is more structured and elevated — designed like a progressive tasting journey from coast to mainland.

Amuse-Bouche
Mini Mandazi
Lightly spiced coconut doughnut
with mango and passionfruit curd.

Entrée
Zanzibar Seafood Supu
Prawns and fish in coconut broth
with lime leaf and coriander.

(Vegetarian alternative: Zanzibar mix)

Intermezzo
Tamarind & Lime Sorbet
A bright palate cleanser.

Main Course

Nyama Choma (Chef’s Selection)
Charcoal-grilled lamb or beef
served with fragrant Swahili pilau rice,
kachumbari and pili pili sauce.

(Vegetarian option: Mchuzi wa Mboga – seasonal vegetable skewers)

Course 4: Kuku wa Kupaka
Grilled chicken in rich coconut curry sauce
Charred first, then simmered in spiced coconut gravy.
Served with chapati.

Dessert
Spiced Chocolate & Cardamom Mousse
with candied coconut and sesame shard.

Served with turmeric basmati rice.
Course 4: Kuku wa Kupaka Grilled chicken in rich coconut curry sauce Charred first, then simmered in spiced coconut gravy.

events.name: The Brent Wong Fan Club
events.venue: 20 Mahara Place,18 Mahara Pl, Waikanae 5036, New Zealand, Waikanae
events.long_description:
The Brent Wong Fan Club
Join The Brent Wong Fan Club and enjoy a night of live music and conversation about his distinctive work.

Guitarist, vocalist and multi-instrumentalist Steve Poulton will present a specially curated ambient set designed to drift between painting, space and atmosphere. Artist and photographer Jack Penman has created an immersive projection to accompany the music.

A conversation about Brent Wong’s work will follow between art writer Megan Dunn and art dealer James Blackie.

Cash bar and snacks available onsite.

This event is seated. Book now via the link above to secure your spot or visit our website for more info: https://toimahara.nz/events/the-brent-wong-fan-club/

Tickets are $25pp.

Funds raised will support Toi MAHARA to purchase free-standing gallery stools to be used in our floor talks, exhibition openings and other events.

Part of our winter Live Art @ Toi MAHARA series. Visit www.toimahara.nz for the full programme.

events.long_description:
Yoga Nidra: Winter Solstice
Yoga Nidra: Winter Solstice

On this threshold moment, as nature gently calls us inward, we join together to honour the longest night of the year.

In this special Yoga Nidra, we respect the beauty of the pause. You’ll be guided into a deeply restful state between waking and sleeping, where the body can fully release, the nervous system can unwind, and the mind can settle into spacious awareness.

Through breath, body sensing, and subtle imagery, we’ll rest in the richness of the dark - not as something to move through,
but as something to be held by.

This is a practice of deep nourishment, quiet reflection, and inner listening

Come wrapped in warmth, bring anything that helps you feel held, and allow yourself to be guided into profound rest.

There will be an amazing plant-based dinner & dessert afterwards - all included in your ticket!



Meet Nicola, your host
Nicola of Namaste Nicola is a dedicated yoga teacher with over 20 years of experience teaching across Australia and New Zealand. With more than 300 hours of training with international teachers, her approach is gentle, soulful, and deeply grounded in yoga philosophy. Nicola creates a calm and nurturing space where students can slow down, rest deeply, and reconnect with themselves through practices such as yoga nidra, hatha, and yin yoga.

events.id: Cultivating Calm: Positive Psychology & Mindfulness-175 Vivian Street, New Zealand 6011,175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Cultivating Calm: Positive Psychology & Mindfulness
events.venue: 175 Vivian Street, New Zealand 6011,175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cultivating Calm: Positive Psychology & Mindfulness
Cultivating Calm: Positive Psychology & Mindfulness

Develop the tools you need for navigating stress, anxiety, and overwhelm.

Mary Rose Dias - Positive Psychology Practitioner (MSc), Yoga Therapist, and Certified iRest®️ Meditation Teacher - will be leading a nourishing and practical evening exploring how mindfulness and positive psychology can help us cultivate greater calm, resilience, and inner steadiness.

Life can be demanding, and when stress builds in our system, it can become difficult to access clarity, ease, and perspective. While we can’t always remove life’s challenges, we can learn to shift our relationship with stress and develop tools that help us meet it with greater awareness and self-compassion.

Through guided meditation, reflection, and experiential practices, you’ll be introduced to accessible tools to help you:
•⁠ ⁠Regulate stress and settle anxiety
•⁠ ⁠Connect with a deeper sense of inner safety and steadiness
•⁠ ⁠Clarify intentions and reconnect with what matters most
•⁠ ⁠Access practical resources for greater calm and wellbeing in everyday life

Together, we’ll explore grounding practices drawn from mindfulness, meditation, and positive psychology, beginning and ending with guided meditation to support integration and deep rest.

This is more than just developing a mindset, you’ll be leaving with something practical to draw upon long after the evening ends.

Open to all - no prior meditation experience needed.

Plus - there is an amazing plant-based dinner & dessert included in your ticket! Stay back for the good food & great company.

Meet Mary Rose
Mary is a Wellington-based holistic therapist, meditation teacher and yoga practitioner whose work gently weaves together evidence-based psychology, ancient yogic wisdom, meditation practices and a whole lot of unconditional love and respect for each person’s unique journey. With over 15 years of experience in wellbeing, counselling, coaching, yoga and meditation, she is deeply passionate about creating nurturing spaces that support healing, self-discovery and inner calm.

Her heart-felt calling is to support others in accessing greater health, healing and happiness. While based in her birthplace of Aotearoa New Zealand, Mary’s dedication to wellness has taken her around the world, including Hawaii, Australia, Ireland, the Czech Republic and the UK, where she has trained with internationally recognised teachers in yoga, meditation and positive psychology. Through private sessions, workshops, courses and retreats, she offers a grounded, compassionate and holistic approach that honours both modern psychology and timeless wisdom traditions.

events.id: Forest & Bird talk - 100 Years Old and Counting ...-The Garden Room at St Peter's on Willis, 170 Victoria St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Forest & Bird talk - 100 Years Old and Counting ...
events.venue: The Garden Room at St Peter's on Willis, 170 Victoria St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Come and hear from … Anthea McClelland (past Chair of Forest & Bird Manawatū branch) who will guide you through 100 years of Forest & Bird - how it started and how it grew over the years, with a few anecdotes popped in along the way.

Anthea joined Forest & Bird Manawatū in early 2002 and in 2007 was voted onto the Branch Committee as Secretary and then in 2014 as Chair. She stepped down as Chair in 2024 and now serves on the Branch Committee – that’s over 24 years involvement with the Society!

Our Wellington Branch Chair, Kate Littin will also give a brief on what’s happening presently and what’s coming, and delve into the important role that Wellington and Wellingtonians played in Forest & Bird's first steps and how you can carry the legacy forward. We look forward to seeing you there!

- ADMISSION FREE - KOHA WELCOME -

events.long_description:
RQP presents Showquest & TOI, a night celebrating creative rangatahi as they bring their ideas to life on stage.

Showquest is a nationwide performing arts programme and a platform for youth voices to be heard. Be amazed by original performances combining Art, Music, Dance, Drama, Culture & Technology, as students design, produce and perform their stories in celebration and recognition by their community.

Following the Showquest stage performances, prepare to be WOWed with TOI, a show-stopping wearable art showcase featuring bold, imaginative garments by emerging student designers. 

Watch friendly inter-school rivalries come to life as students explore the issues that shape their world. Join friends and wh?nau for an unforgettable night supporting local talent on the big stage.

Showquest and TOI are proudly presented by RQP, champions for young creators and producers of Smokefreerockquest, Smokefree Tangata Beats, Rockshop Bandquest, OnScreen and Stills.

Haiming blends hypnosis and comedy to entertain the audience while showcasing the power and benefits of hypnosis through live audience participation. With his unique cultural background and deep understanding of both Western and Oriental cultures, this is a must-see event for anyone curious about hypnosis and keen for a good laugh.

Come and discover how this talented Chinese hypnotist uses hypnosis to both entertain crowds and deeply relax participants.
Experience the hilarious Comedy Hypnosis Show – where you could become the star of the night! (Only if you’re keen – no pressure!)

events.id: Wellington Speed Dating For Ages 26-44 by Cheeky Events New Zealand-The Arborist Rooftop Bar,166 WILLIS STREET, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Wellington Speed Dating For Ages 26-44 by Cheeky Events New Zealand
events.venue: The Arborist Rooftop Bar,166 WILLIS STREET, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Wellington Speed Dating For Ages 26-44 by Cheeky Events New Zealand
Get ready for a night of fun and connection at Wellington's hottest speed dating event!

About this Event

Let’s ditch the apps together and embrace real connections. Say goodbye to the dating apps and hello to your very own Cheeky Love Story! 💖
🍸 What’s included: Your first drink is on us—let’s toast to great company!
👇How It Works:
Before the event:Make sure to download the Cheeky Events App (free on Google Play & the App Store) to set up your profile and upload a photo.
P.S. Don’t worry—it’s not a dating app! We’ve skipped the chat feature because we’re all about real, in-person connections.
During the event:
Check in to grab your name tag and free drink token. 🍻
Arrive on time to enjoy 15 minutes of casual pre-mingling.
Ladies stay seated while men rotate every 5 minutes—don’t forget to rate your matches in the app! 👍👎
Take a quick 15-minute break before the next round.
After the rotations, feel free to stick around for some extra socializing!
After the event: Stay for extra mingling and check the app for your cheeky matches at 9am the next day! 📱💖
For more details, check out our blog: What to expect
Who knows? You might just find your next spark! ✨ Our IRL singles events (single mixers and speed dating events) are happening across Australia, New Zealand, London, and NYC! 🌏

About Cheeky Events
We host fun, inclusive singles events across Australia, New Zealand, London, and NYC! Whether you’re in your 20s, over 40, looking for love, or just looking to meet new people, our events are designed to bring people together. Whether you’re seeking romance, new friendships, or even networking, we’ve got something for everyone!
👉 Follow us on Instagram:@cheekyeventsaustralia @cheekyeventsukandireland @cheekyeventsnewzealand @cheekyeventsukandireland @cheekeventsus
Visit our website: www.cheekyevents.net or download the Cheeky Events app to get started.
We can’t wait to see you at one of our events soon! 🕺💃

events.id: Engelbert Humperdinck — The Celebration Tour-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Engelbert Humperdinck — The Celebration Tour
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
He has a unique voice that has charmed millions of fans around the world. Engelbert Humperdinck returns to Wellington in June 2026 for The Celebration Tour. After 60 extraordinary years, the legend continues to woo with crowd favourites. Audiences can expect hits like Release Me, The Last Waltz, A Man Without Love, Can’t Take My Eyes Off of You, and Quando, Quando, Quando. 
Over the past six decades, Engelbert has sold over 140 million records. His achievements include 64 gold albums, 35 platinum albums, four Grammy nominations, a Golden Globe, and stars on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, Las Vegas Walk of Fame, and Leicester Walk of Fame. Recordings span everything from romantic ballads to movie theme songs, disco, rock, and even gospel.

events.id: Te Wehi — I’m Home NZ Album Tour-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Te Wehi — I’m Home NZ Album Tour
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
A debut album and a first-ever headline tour of New Zealand and Australia make 2026 a huge year for Te Wehi. The Kiwi singer has found fame for his mix of reggae, soul, and country. He’s carved out his own sound and, in the process, connected with a genuine fan base across the country. All 15 songs on the album will feature in the concert. They are rooted in real-life stories, cultural pride, and deep whakapapa . His down-to-earth approach and humble outlook make his songs resonate with emotional honesty and authenticity.   
Some would argue that his self-produced sound and sincere approach have propelled him to the number one place on the Aotearoa Music Charts throughout 2025. This is on top of taking his live performances to the main stages at Homegrown and Rhythm & Vines, and supporting L.A.B on their recent AU and NZ tour.

events.id: Ruapehu Mountaineer-Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Ruapehu Mountaineer
events.venue: Wellington
events.long_description:
After a sold-out run in February, the Ruapehu Mountaineer is back. If you’ve been thinking about it, now’s the time to lock in a seat.
This is a full-day steam-hauled rail journey through the Central North Island to Mt Ruapehu. You’ll travel in comfortable heritage carriages with large viewing windows, an open-air viewing platform, and a café carriage onboard. It’s an easy, relaxed way to take in some of the best scenery on the network, from the Kapiti Coast through the Manawatū and across the Mangaweka viaducts.
At Ohakune, you’ll transfer by coach up to Whakapapa Village, where you’ve got a few hours to do your own thing. Grab lunch, take in the alpine views, or add on the Sky Waka gondola for a trip up the mountain.
It’s a long but rewarding day, and a pretty unique way to experience Mt Ruapehu. Ideal for families, groups, or anyone keen on a proper steam day out.
⸻
FARES
Plimmerton, Paraparaumu
Adult $219.00 | Child $179.00
Ōtaki, Levin
Adult $199.00 | Child $159.00
Palmerston North, Feilding
Adult $179.00 | Child $139.00
Taihape (limited availability)
Adult $159.00 | Child $129.00
Sky Waka Gondola add-on
$29.00
⸻
TIMINGS (Northbound)
Plimmerton 5:20am
Paraparaumu 6:00am
Ōtaki 6:20am
Levin 7:05am
Palmerston North 8:10am
Feilding 8:30am
Taihape 10:40am
Ohakune 12:15pm
RETURN (Southbound)
National Park 4:10pm
Taihape 6:05pm
Feilding 7:55pm
Palmerston North 8:10pm
Levin 9:20pm
Ōtaki 9:45pm
Paraparaumu 10:10pm
Plimmerton 10:45pm
⸻
Seats are limited and expected to sell quickly.
Operated by Mainline Steam Heritage Trust

If you’ve been thinking about it, now’s the time to lock in a seat.
This is a full-day steam-hauled rail journey through the Central North Island to Mt Ruapehu.
It’s an easy, relaxed way to take in some of the best scenery on the network, from the Kapiti Coast through the Manawatū and across the Mangaweka viaducts.
Grab lunch, take in the alpine views, or add on the Sky Waka gondola for a trip up the mountain.
It’s a long but rewarding day, and a pretty unique way to experience Mt Ruapehu.

events.id: Matariki Sunrise Swim-231 Marine Pde, Seatoun, Wellington 6022, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Matariki Sunrise Swim
events.venue: 231 Marine Pde, Seatoun, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Let us know if you're coming so we know how many cups of tea to brew! Follow the link above to register for FREE!
Celebrate Matariki with a joyful splash into the moana — a chance to connect, reset, and support mental wellbeing — followed by warming kai and hot drinks.
Hear from the Kura Kelpers from Te Kura Kaupapa Māori o Ngā Mokopuna about their involvement in the Love Rimurimu project, and chat with the team about how our baby seaweeds are growing.
We’ll be staying close to shore — come for a dip, or simply relax on the beach. Everyone is welcome.
What to bring:
Please arrive wearing your togs/swimmers with plenty of warm layers over the top. Bring a towel and warm clothes to change into afterwards before heading up to the Worser Bay Boating Clubrooms for hot drinks and kai.
Safety reminders:
- This is a quick dip, not a long-distance ocean swim
- You are responsible for your own safety in the water — please swim within your comfort zone
-Stay within the designated swim zone, marked by dive flags and buoys
- Swim with a buddy and stay together at all times
- Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian both in and out of the water
- Please look after yourself and exit the water before you get too cold

events.id: Matariki: Bringing our stars to life — A hands-on learning experience-National Library of New Zealand, 70 Molesworth Street, Thorndon, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Matariki: Bringing our stars to life — A hands-on learning experience
events.venue: National Library of New Zealand, 70 Molesworth Street, Thorndon, Wellington
events.long_description:
Nau mai, te whetū o te tau! Join us for our special Matariki event.

Using a whāriki star map and special glowing pīataata (star globes), come and share the magic of Matariki mā Puanga this Hōtoke season. We will sit around a giant 7 metre mat, learning about the different qualities of each whetū (star) and what they relate to in nature.

It is beautiful to make the star globes glow in different colours, respresenting the Matariki star cluster. We’ll mihi, sing and tuku karakia to our loved ones who have passed and make wishes to Hiwa-i-te-rangi for the year ahead!

Booking is not required — you can just turn up on the day.

As determined at the Hui held 10 a.m, March 17th, 2026 in Masterton and online, a hui has been locked in for the Pirinoa land blocks for July 4th, 2026 to be hosted at Kohunui Marae with a carry over lease for the time being to not commit to any timeframes for leases.
Further matters of discussion planned for the July 4th Hui include:

The agenda for the hui includes:
The agenda for the hui includes:
Change administration of the land from the Māori Trustee to back to whānau as Responsible Trustees.
Appoint whānau as Responsible Trustees.
Discuss the difference between amalgamation and aggregation and whether this is a pathway to consider and follow.
Review and amend Trust Order to reflect whānau whakaaro.
Confirm access to ALL whenua.
Meet with all Advisory Trustees prior to hui.

events.id: Kintsugi Workshop in Wellington // Hutt Art Society Inc-Hutt Art Society Inc,11 Myrtle Street, Lower Hutt, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Kintsugi Workshop in Wellington // Hutt Art Society Inc
events.venue: Hutt Art Society Inc,11 Myrtle Street, Lower Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Kintsugi Workshop in Wellington // Hutt Art Society Inc
Re-use, Re-Purpose, Beautify with Gold!! Come along and learn the magical art of Kintsugi with Artist Ema Frost in this new Workshop.

About this Event

After several SOLD OUT workshops in Wellington here is the next one... June 2026
Learn the magical art of Kintsugi with Artist Ema Frost in this new Workshop. You can bring along a piece of your own pottery to restore in this beautiful style. (I'll also have a few as back up if you don't have your own piece) (All Levels)
Saturday 27th June - 10:30am - 12:30pm or 2 - 4pm

Ema Frost is a very clever Kiwi Artist, who has been teaching Kintsugi for 5+ years and regularly travels to Japan to study and learn new techniques. Ema's illustrations and ceramic works sit in many of our countries best galleries, and whose products and merchandise sell in the most stylish design stores and gallery shops. Her style is enchanting - quirky, feminine and colourful, and clearly influenced by her travels throughout the world, usually off the beaten track.
Have you ever wanted to try Kintsugi.... It's an ancient Japanese art form where you fix broken pottery with gold - making its imperfections its beauty. - A truly wonderful philosophy to live by. Nothing is ever really broken but new life and meaning is brought back making your piece a work of art and truly something special. You can also apply this technique on various materials like glass, wood, greenstone and shells.
KintsugiIt's called kintsugi (金継ぎ), or kintsukuroi (金繕い), literally golden (“kin”) and repair (“tsugi”). This traditional Japanese art uses a precious metal – liquid gold, liquid silver or lacquer dusted with powdered gold – to bring together the pieces of a broken pottery item and at the same time enhance the breaks.You will take away your wonderful finished work, plus your knew skills which will enable you to further your own Kintsugi in your own time.
What you will take away:A finished artwork pieceA understanding of KintsugiKnowledge and History of this beautiful art form.

events.id: Waltz of My Heart-Carterton Events Centre, 50 Holloway Street, Carterton-Event Finder
events.name: Waltz of My Heart
events.venue: Carterton Events Centre, 50 Holloway Street, Carterton
events.long_description:
Step into a world of sweeping melodies, soaring voices, and the graceful rhythm of the waltz in this unforgettable concert Waltz of My Heart, a celebration of one of the most beloved musical forms in history.

From the iconic Viennese waltzes of Johann Strauss to modern interpretations such as The Anniversary Waltz and The Tennessee Waltz, this enchanting programme showcases the enduring beauty and emotional power of the waltz across generations and genres. Whether heard in grand ballrooms or intimate theatres, this music continues to captivate and enchant.

Taking centre stage are our four magnificent tenors whose rich, expressive voices breathe new life into classic and contemporary waltzes alike. Joining them is fabulous baritone John Bayne plus stunning Amanda Atlas, whose powerful soprano and magnetic presence bring depth and drama to every note.

This is a concert of melody, magic, and masterful singing — where every phrase is infused with passion and every performance is unforgettable. Let yourself be swept away by the romance, elegance, and enduring charm of the waltz.

Featuring:
Bonaventure Allan-Moetaua
Benjamin Makisi
Derek Hill
Kalauni Pouvalu
Amanda Atlas
John Bayne
Paul Carnegie-Jones

NZ Tour 02 June 2026 - 03 July 2026
Concerts Start 11am
General Admission Seating
Doors Open 10am – Complimentary morning tea included.

Phone: 0508 266 237
Email: bookings@operatunity.co.nz

events.id: Mid-Winter Christmas (Lunch/Dinner) 2026-Tirohana Estate Vineyard, 42 Puruatanga Rd, Martinborough-Event Finder
events.name: Mid-Winter Christmas (Lunch/Dinner) 2026
events.venue: Tirohana Estate Vineyard, 42 Puruatanga Rd, Martinborough
events.long_description:
An annual tradition in Martinborough and the Wairarapa, Tirohana Cellars' Restaurant will be transformed into a magical Christmas winter wonderland, with twinkling fairy lights, mulled wine, Christmas trees, evocative music, the warmth of the open fire - and who knows, we might even get some snow in the vineyard!? :)

There is a festive menu where Christmas turkey and all the trimmings will be on offer (with options for vegetarians/special diets). But there are plenty of other options on the menu available for those who do not want to indulge in Turkey and ham off the bone, plum pudding and other favourites.

Lunch is three courses (starter, main, dessert) for $69 pp and Dinner is $75 pp for five courses (amuse bouche, starter, main, dessert, petit fours)

A wonderful lunch or dinner for a couple or a group, this Tirohana institution is something you won't want to miss. If you're feeling particularly festive, why not dress to suit the occasion - we'll be running a competition on our Facebook page for the best Midwinter photo.

Reservations are essential to secure a table for lunch or dinner.

To book a table please call 06 306 9933 or email makeabooking@tirohanaestate.com or book online https://www.tirohanaestate.com/restaurant/book-a-table/.

events.id: Lunchtime Awareness through Movement Lessons-St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Lunchtime Awareness through Movement Lessons
events.venue: St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
Want to reduce pain and tension, improve posture, in a friendly and supportive atmosphere ?
These lessons use principles of neuroplasticity, biomechanics, and human development, to help you improve long-term habits of movement and posture.
They are based on the pioneering work of physicist and engineer Dr. Moshe Feldenkrais. He developed his unique system to help manage his own knee injuries. His method is now used world wide.

First class free.
Sue 0274667123

Winter Ales Festival 2026
Winter Ales Fest is BACK for 2026!

Enjoy a unique selection of brand new winter beers, from NZ's best small and micro scale brewers.

Winter Ales Fest 2026 will again be held at the amazing Whisky & Wood event space, conveniently located at 60 Cambridge Terrace (above Sweet Axe Throwing Co).

Winter Ales Fest is a small, cozy beer festival with only 120 tickets available per session. We'll be pouring 15-20 brand new wintery (and not so wintery) beers from small and very small brewers. This is a beer festival for beer geeks!

Mata
Mean Doses

Plus more to come!

WAF runs on a token system: $6 per token, each token gets you a half glass of any beer.

events.id: Ferris Bueller's Day Off (1986): 40th Anniversary Dinner & Movie Event-Regent 3 Cinemas, 230 Queen Street, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: Ferris Bueller's Day Off (1986): 40th Anniversary Dinner & Movie Event
events.venue: Regent 3 Cinemas, 230 Queen Street, Masterton
events.long_description:
Celebrate the 40th Anniversary of Ferris Bueller's Day Off (1986) with a themed menu and a special big-screen screening. For One Night Only! Friday 26 June 2026!

Movie Menu served from 5:30pm at Cafe Strada, Masterton.
Movie starts at 7:30pm at Regent 3 Cinemas, Masterton

Movie Information:
One spring day in suburban Chicago, charismatic high school student Ferris Bueller decides to take a day off school. Along with his girlfriend Sloane and best friend Cameron, he embarks on an unforgettable adventure through the city while trying to stay one step ahead of his suspicious principal and frustrated sister.

Written and directed by John Hughes, Ferris Bueller's Day Off remains one of the most beloved comedies of the 1980s. Funny, warm-hearted and endlessly quotable, it is a celebration of friendship, freedom and making the most of life's opportunities.

Themed Menu:
Make a night of it with our specially created Ferris Bueller-themed dining experience at Cafe Strada, Masterton. Inspired by the film and its Chicago setting, the menu features a selection of themed mains and desserts created especially for this event.

Ticket Options:
• $15 movie tickets
• $35 ticket includes movie, main meal and a soft drink, tea or coffee.
• $45 ticket includes movie, main meal, dessert and a soft drink, tea or coffee.

Whether you're revisiting a favourite classic or discovering it for the first time, join us for a memorable evening of great food, laughter and one of cinema's most iconic coming-of-age adventures.

Movie Meal Deal Menu
Guests with a Movie + Meal ticket can choose one main and one dessert from the options below.

Main Options
Save Ferris Chicago Hot Dog
Classic Chicago beef hot dog with yellow mustard, onion, relish, tomato, pickle spear, jalapeños and fries.

Cameron's Chicago Club Stack (GFO, DFO)
House spice-rubbed grilled chicken, bacon, lettuce, tomato and aioli on toasted ciabatta, served with fries.

Sloane's Winter Wellness Bowl (GF, DFO, VO)
Warm quinoa and brown rice, roasted kumara, pumpkin, carrot, pickled onion, toasted seeds, silver beet and lemon tahini dressing.
Optional add: chicken, halloumi or tofu.

Day Off Street Tacos
Slow-cooked smoky BBQ pulled pork tacos with lettuce, slaw, corn salsa and BBQ sauce.

Dessert Options
Lemon Meringue Pie

events.venue: The Bond Store,21 Birmingham Street, Paraparaumu, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Kintsugi Workshop in Wellington // The Bond Store - Distillery
Re-use, Re-Purpose, Beautify with Gold!! Come along and learn the magical art of Kintsugi with Artist Ema Frost in this new Sip & Kintsugi.

About this Event

Come and experience this unique Sip & Kintsugi workshop at the lovely Bond Store Distillery. You will get a gin tasting tray on arrival with three different gins with tonic. And why not come a little earlier and grab a bite to eat beforehand. (Must be 18+ yrs of age)
Learn the magical art of Kintsugi with Artist Ema Frost in this new Workshop. You can bring along a piece of your own pottery to restore in this beautiful style. (I'll also have a few as back up if you don't have your own piece) (All Levels)
Friday 26th June - 5:30pm - 7:30pm
Kintsugi
It's called kintsugi (金継ぎ), or kintsukuroi (金繕い), literally golden (“kin”) and repair (“tsugi”). This traditional Japanese art uses a precious metal – liquid gold, liquid silver or lacquer dusted with powdered gold – to bring together the pieces of a broken pottery item and at the same time enhance the breaks.
You will take away your wonderful finished work, plus your knew skills which will enable you to further your own Kintsugi in your own time.
This class is not just for budding or accomplished artists. It is for everyone to enjoy a relaxing day of art and design with Ema.
What you will take away:
A finished artwork piece
A understanding of Kintsugi
Knowledge and History of this beautiful art form.

Where: The Bond Store, 21 Birmingham St, Paraparaumu, Kapiti Coast 5032. NEW ZEALAND
When: Friday 26th June
5:30 - 7:30pm

events.id: Lost Wax in 3 - Soft Wax - Friday Nights-Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Lost Wax in 3 - Soft Wax - Friday Nights
events.venue: Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington
events.long_description:
Over three sessions, two weeks apart, create stunning jewellery using the age-old lost wax technique. This is an excellent opportunity for a best friends' outing or a self-care date with yourself!

In the first and second session, you'll learn how to work with jeweller's soft wax to create impressions of natural textures and creatures like seashells, leaves and feathers. We can also use moulds created using Fimo, and press soft wax into them, which will result in amazing pieces!

Then there’s a week's break before the third session, to allow time for everyone's waxes to be cast. The third session covers how to trim, clean and finish your casts, turning them into original jewellery ready to wear!

More advanced students can use these sessions to complete projects in a class setting.

Included in the class fee are all your different types of waxes, sandpaper and courier costs.

Not included are the casting and metal costs which vary from student to student depending on what sized piece they want to make and the type of metal they choose. Small castings typically cost around $6.65–$12, with metal costs ranging from approximately $7 to $30, depending on whether you use brass, bronze, or silver.

events.id: Lost Wax in Three - Mitsuro Hikime Wax-Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Lost Wax in Three - Mitsuro Hikime Wax
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Discover the magic of Mitsuro Hikime — the beautiful Japanese technique that uses a special stretchy wax to create flowing, organic textures unlike anything else in jewellery-making.

Over three inspiring sessions, you’ll learn how to transform this fascinating material into stunning wearable pieces using the traditional lost wax casting process.

Mitsuro Hikime wax behaves very differently from standard jeweller’s wax. Warm and pliable, it can be stretched, twisted and pulled into delicate ribbons and natural textures that resemble flowing water, bark, roots or molten metal. The results are beautifully organic and wonderfully unpredictable, making every piece completely unique.

During the first two sessions, you’ll learn how to handle and shape this traditional Japanese wax, exploring different techniques to create rings, pendants or small sculptural objects full of movement and texture. Once your designs are complete, your waxes will be professionally cast in the metal of your choice.

There is then a one-week break before the final session to allow time for casting. In the third class, you’ll learn how to cut, file, sand and finish your cast pieces, transforming them into polished jewellery ready to wear, gift or treasure.

This course is suitable for complete beginners as well as more experienced makers wanting to experiment with this exciting and expressive technique. More advanced students are also welcome to use the sessions to develop independent projects in a supportive studio environment.

Included in the class fee are all Mitsuro Hikime waxes, consumables, sandpaper and courier costs.

Please note that casting and metal costs are not included, as these vary depending on the size of your piece and the metal selected. Small castings generally cost around $6.65–$12, with metal costs ranging approximately from $8–$30 depending on whether you choose red bronze, white bronze or silver.

This class is great for young folks aged around 12 and up. Anyone under 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.

events.name: Sip & Kintsugi Workshop in Wellington // The Bond Store Distillery
events.venue: The Bond Store,3C Magrath Ave, Paraparaumu Beach, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Sip & Kintsugi Workshop in Wellington // The Bond Store Distillery
Come and experience this unique Sip & Kintsugi workshop at the lovely Bond Store Distillery. You will get a gin tasting tray on arrival with three different gins with tonic. And why not come a little earlier and grab a bite to eat beforehand. (Must be 18years +)

Learn the magical art of Kintsugi with Artist Ema Frost in this new Workshop. You can bring along a piece of your own pottery to restore in this beautiful style. (I'll also have a few as back up if you don't have your own piece) (All Levels)

Friday 26th June - 5:30pm - 7:30pm


Ema has been teaching Kintsugi for 5+ years and regularly travels to Japan to study and learn new techniques. Ema's illustrations and ceramic works sit in many of our countries best galleries, and whose products and merchandise sell in the most stylish design stores and gallery shops. Her style is enchanting - quirky, feminine and colourful, and clearly influenced by her travels throughout the world, usually off the beaten track.

Have you ever wanted to try Kintsugi.... It's an ancient Japanese art form where you fix broken pottery with gold - making its imperfections its beauty. - A truly wonderful philosophy to live by. Nothing is ever really broken but new life and meaning is brought back making your piece a work of art and truly something special. You can also apply this technique on various materials like glass, wood, greenstone and shells.

Kintsugi
It's called kintsugi (金継ぎ), or kintsukuroi (金繕い), literally golden (“kin”) and repair (“tsugi”). This traditional Japanese art uses a precious metal – liquid gold, liquid silver or lacquer dusted with powdered gold – to bring together the pieces of a broken pottery item and at the same time enhance the breaks.

You will take away your wonderful finished work, plus your new skills which will enable you to further your own Kintsugi in your own time.

What you will take away:
A finished artwork piece
A understanding of Kintsugi
Knowledge and History of this beautiful art form.

events.id: June Full Moon Drumming-Wellington-Facebook
events.name: June Full Moon Drumming
events.venue: Wellington
events.long_description:
Please remember this is a ZERO WASTE and *NO ALCOHOL* community gathering.
Kia ora!
Full Moon Drumming Wellington is a registered public event with the Wellington City Council and its been running for over 10 years. We are a group of humans from all walks of life who gather under the full moon each month to connect through drumming and dance around the fire. This gathering is co-creative and participatory, that means if you made it to the circle you are part of the circle. There is no audience, everyone is invited to participate as part of the ‘chorus’ (singing/clapping), dancing, or drumming.
The event is weather permitting, we check the forecast a few days prior and aim to make a decision the day before the event, although this is not possible and sometimes we make the decision on the day. All updates are posted on the event page.
This gathering is entirely run by volunteers, if you'd like to contribute in any way please get in touch.
There are a few things that we need help with:
- FIREWOOD: Please bring dry firewood as a koha, no wood no fire! Please NOTE we DO NOT burn pallets (due to nails) or treated timber. Feel free to post or reach out if you have a vehicle and are keen to help gather firewood on the day of the event.
- BEAUTIFYING THE CIRCLE SPACE: Anyone has fairy lights, oil burners, or is keen to make some signs?
NOTE: We hold a fire permit for this event. For safety reasons we don't allow participants to feed the fire, and while is generally okay to roast some marshmallows early in the evening we don't allow the use the fire for cooking or heating food.
This is an **ALCOHOL FREE EVENT**, please help us uphold this kaupapa and remind those who are unaware and might be new to the event that alcohol is not permitted. As hosts we are responsible for your safety and we take that responsibility seriously, especially given that the event is authorised through both fire and event permits. Large, public, all ages gatherings require administration and logistics so please respect this and the guidelines provided.
Children are welcome at the fire, though, especially after dark, please make sure that all children have adult supervision, that the supervisors are aware of the children they are looking after, and that the children are aware of who is looking after them.
Feel free to bring:
- Your friends.
- Drums or any percussive instrument.
- Warm clothing; jackets and wool blankets are good.
- Something to seat on (otherwise sand is often wet)
- Drinking water.
- Food, tea to share.
- Your lovely selves.
- Fire spinning kit and fuel.
FIRE SPINNERS: Please take all precautionary safety measures and make sure to set up fire-spinning downwind from the fire circle.
CARPOOLING: If you don't have a way to get to Princess Bay, or if you have spare seats in your car, feel free to post it on the event page. The less cars driving the better.
Feel free to share the event page and send an event invite to whomever you feel would be interested, not everyone sees the notifications and advertising

We are a group of humans from all walks of life who gather under the full moon each month to connect through drumming and dance around the fire.
This gathering is co-creative and participatory, that means if you made it to the circle you are part of the circle.
Feel free to post or reach out if you have a vehicle and are keen to help gather firewood on the day of the event.
As hosts we are responsible for your safety and we take that responsibility seriously, especially given that the event is authorised through both fire and event permits.
CARPOOLING: If you don't have a way to get to Princess Bay, or if you have spare seats in your car, feel free to post it on the event page.

events.name: NYO La Mer - Wellington
events.venue: 111 Wakefield St, Wellington
events.long_description:
The NZSO National Youth Orchestra brings together Aotearoa New Zealand’s best young musicians to perform four exceptional works.
Programme:
Jack Bewley New Commission (World Premiere)
Britten Four Sea Interludes From Peter Grimes
Michael-Thomas Foumai Raise Hawaiki: Kealaikahiki (NZ Premiere)
Debussy La Mer
In 2026, the NZSO National Youth Orchestra brings together Aotearoa New Zealand’s best young musicians to perform Britten’s breathtaking Four Sea Interludes; Raise Hawaiki: Kealaikahiki, commemorating the trans-Pacific voyage of the Hōkūle’a waka from Hawai’i to Tahiti; a new commission by the 2026 NYO Composer-in-Residence Jack Bewley; and Debussy’s La Mer, one of the most influential masterpieces of the 20th century.
NZSO Members receive two complimentary tickets to the NYO concert of their choice.

events.venue: Lotus Yoga Centre,91 Ruapehu St, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Transformational Breathwork Workshop
🌿 Transformational Breathwork Journey 🌿

Join me for an evening of learning, healing, release, and reconnection through the power of your breath.

This experience is designed to teach you practical breathing techniques that you can use in everyday life to reduce stress, regulate your nervous system, increase focus, and support your overall wellbeing. We will also journey into a deeper breathwork session for relaxation, release, and inner connection.

✨ What to expect:
• Learn simple breathing techniques for daily life
• Tools to help manage stress and anxiety
• Nervous system regulation practices
• Guided transformational breathwork release session
• Deep relaxation and emotional release
• Increased clarity, energy, and inner connection

📍 Location: Lotus Yoga Centre, 91 Ruapehu Street, Paraparaumu
💰 Investment: $35
🎟 Door sales available
📩 Contact me to book your spot

Please arrive 10 minutes early so we can begin on time.

What to bring:
• Yoga mat
• Blanket
• Cushion
• Water bottle

Wear comfortable clothing.

It’s recommended to eat only a light meal beforehand and enjoy your main meal afterwards.

I would also like to acknowledge my mentor, Marcel Hoff, whose teachings and guidance have inspired and supported my own breathwork journey.

Spaces are limited — book now to secure your place 💙

Belinda Seamark
✨ Guidance to Your Inner Wisdom ✨


#breathwork #healingjourney #emotionalhealing #innerhealing #mindbodyspirit #breathworkjourney

events.long_description:
Get ready to blast away the winter blues with Crash Bandihoot's biggest ever indoor home show, opened by the incredible Cumbia Blazera!
Linda (trumpet) from Crash will soon be heading overseas so this is her farewell Welly show, let's send her off in style with a bang (or a hoot...)
Crash Bandihoot - 8 piece colourful raucous New Orleans street style hornfunk explosion. Soaring horns, driving dancing rhythms and plenty of smiles! 3 International Tours and counting, thousands at Gardens Magic, parades at Newtown Festivals and Cuba Dupas, Waiheke Jazz Festival, National Jazz Festival, tours across the motu....... + Special Guests.
Cumbia Blazera - ahead of their eagerly awaited 2nd album release. After a tour of Colombia and several trips around Aotearoa, they have established themselves as a much loved part of the Wellington music scene, lighting up dancefloors with infectious South American rhythms.

events.id: New Zealand School of Dance - Choreographic Season 2026-Te Whaea: National Dance and Drama Centre, 11 Hutchison Road, Newtown, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: New Zealand School of Dance - Choreographic Season 2026
events.venue: Te Whaea: National Dance and Drama Centre, 11 Hutchison Road, Newtown, Wellington
events.long_description:
The season lives in the space between who we are, who we think we are, and who we become when witnessed.

NOTE TO SELF is a full-length choreographed performance, created from original short works, devised by NZSD 3rd-year contemporary dance students. Each work has been independently created, then thoughtfully woven together to form one shared performance experience.

Together, these works sit inside the tender and uneasy space of being seen. They ask what it means to be viewed, to be perceived, and how that gaze can shape, distort, reveal, or challenge our sense of self. The season explores the body not simply as something to look at, but as something that knows, remembers, senses, absorbs, and reveals.

Running through the work is a deep humanness - vulnerability, discomfort, identity, observation and the quiet negotiation between who we are privately and who we become when witnessed.

events.id: Virtuosi-111 Wakefield St, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Virtuosi
events.venue: 111 Wakefield St, Wellington
events.long_description:
In keeping with our season’s theme of COLLABORATIONS, this programme showcases many of the fabulous string-instrument performers in Wellington.
We invite the New Zealand String Quartet to lead our string sections in Edward Elgar’s "Introduction and Allegro," a piece which opens the concert with a big Romantic flourish and a touch of Baroque fugue playfulness.
The orchestra continues its Elgar theme with his cello concerto. Cellist Inbal Meggido, who frequently leads the Orchestra Wellington cello section, steps up to take the solo. This work of emotional virtuosity is a poignant response to the tragedies of the First World War. The music’s emotional intensity holds nothing back, and the cello’s deep, autumnal tone is the perfect vehicle for conveying a sense of both beauty and loss.
The orchestra’s collective virtuosity is on display in Bartok’s "Concerto for Orchestra," a work that gives the audience a peek under the bonnet of how orchestral players collaborate. It breaks the ensemble into solos and pairs and unusual groupings so one can truly appreciate how an orchestra creates the incredible palette of sounds of which it is capable. There are haunting fragments from different solo instruments or groups, soft brass chorales, and elements of Bartók’s mysterious “night music” style. This is one of Bartók’s most accessible works yet it demonstrates his genius, progressing from darkness to light and ending with a formidably complex fugue.
This programme is proudly supported by concert partner The Adam Foundation.
Read more at https://orchestrawellington.co.nz/event/virtuosi/

events.id: A Night at the Beat Bach-17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: A Night at the Beat Bach
events.venue: 17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Aotearoa festival scene is in crisis. With rising costs and falling ticket sales, many independent festivals are struggling to stay afloat. With the recent announcement that financial pressures mean the next iteration of Twisted Frequency may be its last, we knew we wanted to help, so we’re throwing an epic gig with all profits going to our favourite festival so that we all may party for many more years to come.
Tickets will start at $20 and will be sold on a “pay what you can afford” basis which means that those who can afford a little more can send a few extra dollars Twisted’s way. All of us (artists and crew) are donating our time and expertise so that we can raise maximum funds for our favourite 5-day long, non-stop, fiercely-independent festival.
The lineup is huge and tickets will move fast so don’t wait around!

// SET TIMES //
8:00pm // Doors
8:30pm // The Temple
9:40pm // Weka
10:50pm // Grains
12:00am // Clova
1:00am // Ohimesama Breaks

Tickets on sale Monday 22nd June from meow.nz

// THE TEMPLE // [Alternative Ambient]
The Temple is a Pōneke-based trio comprised of producer Jack Woodbury, drummer/percussionist Nathan Carter (aka Alter Natural), and saxophonist Peter Liley. The Temple’s broad palette of influences reaches into minimalism, electronica, ambient, funk, doom metal, new age, sound art, and jungle, creating a beguiling, kaleidoscopic sound.

// WEKA // [Live Electronic]
Based in Te Whanganui-a-Tara Wellington, Weka draw inspiration from acts like Bonobo, The Prodigy, Chemical Brothers, and Massive Attack, weaving live drums, sax, guitar, percussion and analog synths. Weka will take you on a journey from downbeat and rock, through to techno and world inspired music.

// GRAINS // [Progressive Electronica]
Grains were sowed in 2019 as an ambient sound project of synth kids Peregrin Hyde and Calum Turner. Utilising a pair of Wellington-made Synthstrom Deluges, the pair worked in co-op mode on a number of ambient music explorations. After some early releases, they teamed up with their friends Jared Reynolds (Drums), Jed Prickett (Guitar) and Fredd Marshall (Vocals) to form a 5-piece live band, retaining much of their signature synth-duo sound, but dialing it up to maximum crunch. In 2024, they released their self-titled debut album, Grains.

// CLOVA // [UK Garage // Jungle]
Clova is a 21 year old producer and multi-instrumentalist based in Pōneke, Aotearoa. Her live sets are 100% original productions that blend her inspirations from 90s rave culture and genres like UK Garage and Jungle, with her own take on dance music; live processing her violin and vocals to create new sonics using Ableton live.

// OHIMESAMA BREAKS // [Jungle]
Originally hailing from Tamaki Makaurau, as the soundsystem as her homing beacon, she's found her way to share the love of heavy breaks, deep basslines and serious grooves with like minded people here in Te Whanganui o Tara.
A selector and dance floor groover, Ohimesama Breaks combines nostalgic sounds from the 90's with fresh beats, encouraging you to move the way you feel. She's a Drop Bass NZ regular, supporting the international sounds of Coco Bryce, Break, Sully, and Breakage, finding her way around Poneke. Expect your daily serving of bass, breaks, beats and bounce from your new local.

events.id: WBS Society night featuring Charlotte Yates-Abandoned Taproom Petone, 38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: WBS Society night featuring Charlotte Yates
events.venue: Abandoned Taproom Petone, 38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
In a professional musical career of nearly three decades, Charlotte Yates has worn a number of hats, both literally and figuratively. She was part of the comic-musical trio When The Cat’s Been Spayed, with their fruit-festooned headgear and high-camp renditions of Kiwiana classics such as ‘Taumarunui On the Main Trunk Line’.

She conceived and directed the large-scale recording and stage projects Baxter, Tuwhare and Ihimaera, based around the works of three of this country’s major literary figures. She has managed festivals and worked in different roles as an arts professional.

Yet looking back, writing and performing original material has been the thread that runs through her work. “I never thought, ‘I’m going to be a singer-songwriter for the rest of my life’,” says Yates. “I didn’t have the grand master plan.” In 2017 she released her seventh solo album, Then the Stars Start Singing.

events.name: Weekend Laughs with James Mustapic and Sameena Zehra
events.venue: The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Weekend Laughs at Fringe Bar is a two hour comedy showcase with some of the country's favourite performers. Each month's line up will feature a guest MC, plus comedians from around the capital and the country, doing their best to bring the big laughs!

With MC James Mustapic
James is an award-winning comedian known for Winning Celebrity Treasure Island, as well as his hit TVNZ+ series Abandonment Issues. James made his start in comedy with his online show Shorty Street Scandal back in 2014, and started stand up comedy in 2015. Since then he's been a regular performer across the NZ comedy circuit, and has also appeared on hit TV shows such as Have You Been Paying Attention NZ, The Comedy Gala, and Guy Mont's Spelling Bee.

and Sameena Zehra
Sameena is an award-winning storyteller, comedian, writer, director, political satirist, intersectional feminist and humanist with a unique take on the world; equally at home exploring the individual experiences of daily life and the larger issues of the world we live in. She is also a blues singer-songwriter, extreme doodler and Level 2 chocolate taster who was once headbutted by a llama while climbing Machu Picchu.

more acts to be announced!

This event is helping fundraise for KidsCan -supporting Kiwi kids in hardship so they can learn, participate, and thrive

BODY OCEAN — WELLINGTON - 26th June
Where: Meow 🐾
Tickets from $25 +BF
Wellington! We heard you… Something's been missing, you want more house music in this beautiful city?! 🌊 so here it is, Body Ocean!

Fronted by Jeremy Glenn (one half of The Upbeats) with creative input from Nik Roos (formerly of Noisia), the project has quickly carved out its own lane, blending deep production with a strong live presence that hits just as hard in the club as it does on a system.

With releases across Night Bass, Black Book Records, STMPD RCRDS, Dirtybird, Mad Decent and more, Body Ocean has earned support from Jamie Jones, Martin Garrix, Oliver Heldens and Claptone 🎶 Their 2025 run saw appearances at Snow Machine, Rabbit Eats Lettuce, and Slander’s Star Base Festival in LA.

Now heading into 2026, they arrive off the back of new music on Night Bass, including the upcoming single “Listen to Your Teacher” with Benni Ola 🚀

events.long_description:
Ferris Bueller's Day Off (1986): 40th Anniversary Dinner & Movie Event
Celebrate the 40th Anniversary of Ferris Bueller's Day Off (1986) with a special big-screen screening at Regent 3 Cinemas, Masterton.

For One Night Only! Friday 26 June 2026!

Movie Information:
One spring day in suburban Chicago, charismatic high school student Ferris Bueller decides to take a day off school. Along with his girlfriend Sloane and best friend Cameron, he embarks on an unforgettable adventure through the city while trying to stay one step ahead of his suspicious principal and frustrated sister.

Written and directed by John Hughes, Ferris Bueller's Day Off remains one of the most beloved comedies of the 1980s. Funny, warm-hearted and endlessly quotable, it is a celebration of friendship, freedom and making the most of life's opportunities.

Themed Menu:
Make a night of it with our specially created Ferris Bueller-themed dining experience at Cafe Strada, Masterton. Inspired by the film and its Chicago setting, the menu features a selection of themed mains and desserts created especially for this event.

Ticket Options:
• $15 movie tickets
• $35 ticket includes movie, main meal and a soft drink, tea or coffee.
• $45 ticket includes movie, main meal, dessert and a soft drink, tea or coffee.

Whether you're revisiting a favourite classic or discovering it for the first time, join us for a memorable evening of great food, laughter and one of cinema's most iconic coming-of-age adventures.

Movie Meal Deal Menu
Guests with a Movie + Meal ticket can choose one main and one dessert from the options below.

Main Options

Save Ferris Chicago Hot Dog
Classic Chicago beef hot dog with yellow mustard, onion, relish, tomato, pickle spear, jalapeños and fries.

Cameron's Chicago Club Stack (GFO, DFO)
House spice-rubbed grilled chicken, bacon, lettuce, tomato and aioli on toasted ciabatta, served with fries.

Sloane's Winter Wellness Bowl (GF, DFO, VO)
Warm quinoa and brown rice, roasted kumara, pumpkin, carrot, pickled onion, toasted seeds, silver beet and lemon tahini dressing.
Optional add: chicken, halloumi or tofu.

Day Off Street Tacos
Slow-cooked smoky BBQ pulled pork tacos with lettuce, slaw, corn salsa and BBQ sauce.

Dessert Options

Lemon Meringue Pie
House-made lemon meringue pie served with whipped cream.

Chicago Cheesecake
Vanilla white chocolate cheesecake with berry compote and whipped cream.

Twist & Shout Skillet Cookie
Triple chocolate skillet cookie topped with ice cream and chocolate sauce.

events.name: polymer clay workshop - jules + the wildflowers craft club
events.venue: Colombo Street &, Rintoul Street, Newtown, Wellington 6021,7A Rintoul St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
polymer clay workshop - jules + the wildflowers craft club
polymer clay workshop: slow down. make something.

Join me for a fun, beginner-friendly polymer clay workshop designed for adults who want to learn something new and enjoy a creative evening together.

In this 2.5 hour session, we’ll play with colour combinations, I will show you a simple technique to make your own earrings or small accessories.

I’ll guide you step-by-step as you create your slab and cut your pieces, with plenty of time to experiment and enjoy the process. Think relaxed vibes, and the simple joy of making something with your hands.

You’ll head home with some seriously cute, handmade pieces!

I will bake your creations after 1.5h of making and you can either sit with a tea/coffee and snacks for some chats or keep playing with polymer clay's million possibilities. Then we'll assemble your pieces to take home.



April 22nd: abstract canes

June 26th: floral slab


Soft drinks and snacks will also be available to keep our creative energy flowing.

No experience is needed — just come along and make something cute!

events.id: Virtuosi-Michael Fowler Centre,111 Wakefield St, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Virtuosi
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre,111 Wakefield St, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Virtuosi
In keeping with our season’s theme of COLLABORATIONS, this programme showcases many of the fabulous string-instrument performers in Wellington.

We invite the New Zealand String Quartet to lead our string sections in Edward Elgar’s "Introduction and Allegro," a piece which opens the concert with a big Romantic flourish and a touch of Baroque fugue playfulness.

The orchestra continues its Elgar theme with his cello concerto. Cellist Inbal Meggido, who frequently leads the Orchestra Wellington cello section, steps up to take the solo. This work of emotional virtuosity is a poignant response to the tragedies of the First World War. The music’s emotional intensity holds nothing back, and the cello’s deep, autumnal tone is the perfect vehicle for conveying a sense of both beauty and loss.

The orchestra’s collective virtuosity is on display in Bartok’s "Concerto for Orchestra," a work that gives the audience a peek under the bonnet of how orchestral players collaborate. It breaks the ensemble into solos and pairs and unusual groupings so one can truly appreciate how an orchestra creates the incredible palette of sounds of which it is capable. There are haunting fragments from different solo instruments or groups, soft brass chorales, and elements of Bartók’s mysterious “night music” style. This is one of Bartók’s most accessible works yet it demonstrates his genius, progressing from darkness to light and ending with a formidably complex fugue.

This programme is proudly supported by concert partner The Adam Foundation.

Read more at https://orchestrawellington.co.nz/event/virtuosi/

events.id: Porirua Parkrun-Bothamley Park, Champion Street, Porirua - Mana-Event Finder
events.name: Porirua Parkrun
events.venue: Bothamley Park, Champion Street, Porirua - Mana
events.long_description:
What is Porirua parkrun? It is a free 5km walk or run event - It’s not about coming first or winning. You can go at your own pace and just enjoy being with other people. It is inclusive and the perfect way to start your weekend in the outdoors chatting to people.

The start occurs just upstream of the Bothamley Park carpark. This car park is off Champion street, opposite the intersection with Windley Street. Head to end of carpark where the concrete ends and there is a gate. For location see https://goo.gl/maps/wEohfp9bRu82.

The course is following the old coach road (circa 1870's) established for the early farms in the area. Today this historic track is located within a park, away from cars and now used by walkers, runners and cyclists.

The track is generally 3 metres wide with a gentle gradient and a compacted gravel surface beside the Kenepuru Stream through a picturesque wooded wild valley. (Note there is a pinch point for a short section after the 2km marker where participants need to go single file each direction for approx 30 metres)

The valley runs east-west with mature trees which means this has to be one of the most sheltered places, out of the wind to exercise in Wellington.

What does it cost to join in? Nothing - it's free! but please register before your first run or walk, so you can get your timing results. You only need to register with parkrun once, to participate at any of the parkrun events around the world. Don't forget to bring a copy of your barcode. If you forget it, you won't get a time.

How fast do I have to be? Walking is fine as long as you can finish the 5km. Please come along and join in whatever your pace!

One dog per adult is welcome, as long as it is on a short hand held lead.

Children welcome but those 10 years and under need to be kept within arms reach of a supervising adult.

Note there is no toilets in the park, so go before you go.

We're friendly! Every week we grab a post park run/walk coffee/tea at Get Fixed within Kai Tahi at 20 Parumoana Street, to mix exercise with socialising.

There are volunteering opportunities even week. So those doing Duke of Edinburgh or similar challenges can get volunteer credits. Ensure you contact us well in advance at "porirua@parkrun.co.nz"

events.id: Negotiation Skills for Successful Outcomes-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Negotiation Skills for Successful Outcomes
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Negotiation Skills for Successful Outcomes
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
What’s the Goal?
To arm you with the strategies, skills, and mindset to walk into any negotiation and leave with the outcome you desire.
What You'll Discover:
Crack the Code on People: Spot different types of negotiators and tailor your approach using the DISC methodology.
Gear Up for Success: Build a killer prep list with intel on your opponent and a clear inventory of your strengths.
Own the Room: Master the art of negotiation strategy, use space to your advantage, and know the unspoken rules.
Discover Your Edge: Tap into your natural negotiation style and make it work for you.
Learn from the Masters: Unlock game-changing tips and theories from the biggest “gurus” in the field.
Practice Makes Perfect: Get hands-on with exercises that turn theory into action.
Ready to negotiate like a pro? This workshop will show you how to make your case, win people over, and seal the deal.
You must be a NZ citizen or resident to register in this course for free and you must provide photo ID when you attend. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Jewellery-making On Saturday Mornings - 6 Weeks, Level 2+-Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Jewellery-making On Saturday Mornings - 6 Weeks, Level 2+
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Our popular weekend block classes are the best way for keen makers to get into jewellery making in a lively, warm and friendly environment.

This class is for regular students at Ōtaki who have already completed at least a beginner's 6 week block as a minimum.

The block starts Sat 27 June and runs for 6 weeks till Sat 1 August, 10am-1pm.

Students will be supported by Vaune and the occasional guest tutor to further their jewellery making skills in the areas that interest them.

This class is a Fabrication focus. Students who have already taken a class in enamelling or wax work can continue their work in this class but the format does not allow for beginner lessons in these things.

You can purchase metals and materials for your projects as you go - in the studio.
Advanced students are encouraged to retain their silver scrap and learn to work thriftily with materials, using scrap for future projects.

Our booking system asks for payment to be made via credit card, Afterpay or PayPal on booking. If you do not have access to these payment methods or need to pay your course fee over time please email us to arrange this ;)

events.id: The Royal New Zealand Ballet presents Whānau Day 2026-St James Theatre, 77-87 Courtenay Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Royal New Zealand Ballet presents Whānau Day 2026
events.venue: St James Theatre, 77-87 Courtenay Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Bring your whānau and spend the day exploring creativity, discovering the world of dance, and celebrating the joy of being uniquely you at St James.

Join us for a vibrant and inspiring Whānau Day at St. James, a special event designed to bring families together through dance, storytelling, and creativity. For the first time, this much-loved day will take over Theatre’s multiple spaces across Levels 1 and 2, the stage, and the Hospitality Suite, offering a wide range of interactive experiences for all ages.

This year’s event is inspired by the beautiful children’s ballet The Bomb, creating a day full of imagination, movement, and self-expression.

About The Bomb

Based on the award-winning book by Sacha Cotter, illustrated by Josh Morgan, The Bomb tells a powerful and uplifting story about finding your own voice. It follows a young boy determined to master the perfect “bomb” dive. With the help of his Nan, a former champion, and through learning to trust himself, he discovers that true success comes from embracing what makes him unique.

This meaningful story has captured the hearts of readers across New Zealand and has received multiple prestigious awards in 2019, including the Margaret Mahy Book of the Year.

https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=389888618397391

A Day for the Whole Whānau

With most activities completely free and something to engage every age group, Whānau Day is a welcoming and inclusive event for the whole community. Whether your child is trying dance for the first time, getting creative with crafts, or watching live rehearsals, there is something to inspire everyone.

events.id: Truffle Hunt and Taste Experience-Le Gra Vineyard, 42 Loopline RD 1, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: Truffle Hunt and Taste Experience
events.venue: Le Gra Vineyard, 42 Loopline RD 1, Masterton
events.long_description:
The winter truffle season runs from June through August, making it the ideal time to join and watch skilled dogs in action and savour the rich, earthy flavours of freshly unearthed truffles.

This Truffle hunting experience has you enjoying a relaxed mid-morning hunt through Fiona and John’s truffle orchard of Oak and Hazel trees - Akura Truffles. Accompanied by their trained truffle dogs, they want to share this short but wonderful season and experience with you.

When you've found some of these treasured delicacies, pop around the corner to Le Gra Vineyard and Winery for a fun and interactive lunch inspired by these little black beauties.

You'll learn how best to infuse the truffle flavour into your food, taste some of the Opaki's finest and freshly hunted in delectable dishes, and, if you so choose pair them with local wines.

There are only 10 spots per hunt, ensuring a truly intimate and real hunting and tasting experience. Don't miss out; choose the date to suit your diary now.

events.id: Ōtari Raranga 2026 - Pihinga (Beginners)-Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Ōtari Raranga 2026 - Pihinga (Beginners)
events.venue: Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington
events.long_description:
Kua raranga tahi tātou he whariki mō āpōpō
Together we weave the mat for future generations

Nau mai haere mai, all are welcome to attend Ōtari Raranga wānanga (classes).

These classes run approximately fortnightly.

No experience required and all materials provided. You will walk away with woven taonga at the end of every class.

During the first 2 classes, beginner students will complete a variety of putiputi (flowers).

We provide harakeke for you if it is your FIRST class and we will teach you how to harvest for yourself during this time and what to harvest for your next class.

After that, if you would like us to harvest for you, purchase the add-on - "Please harvest for me" for an additional fee.

Moving forward, you will start making kōnae (2 corner kete - basket), kono (4-sided kete), tapawhāroa and more.
You will need to harvest 8-10 rau for yourself and start building your own tool kit.

After completing 6 or more beginner classes (close together), you may be ready to move up to our intermediate classes. Please talk with our kaiako and kaiāwhina if you are interested.

With regular attendance, you will:
- Learn tikanga (protocols) and karakia for hauhake (harvesting) and preparing harakeke
- Build your mātauranga (knowledge)
- Use kupu Māori appropriate to raranga practice
- Develop your pūkenga (practical skills)
- Create taonga (treasures) for your whānau
- Build hononga (connections) within our raranga rōpū (group)
- Have the skills to practice at home.

What’s included in your beginner's ticket?
- Three-hour weaving class with experienced kaiako
- Harakeke is provided for your first class, and then you should hauhake (harvest) for yourself or click the "please harvest for me" option in addition to your ticket at least two days before class.
- Light refreshments are provided, and we encourage you to bring kai to share

Led by Irihāpeti Te Aho (Ngāti Porou, Ngāti Kahungunu, Ngāti Pauhawera) , Linda Lee (Ngāti Huia, Ngāti Raukawa, Ngāti Kurī, Te Rarawa, Te Aupōuri), and/or Anna Lees (Te Āti Awa, Kāi Tahu).
On occasion our raranga rangatira, Frank Topia (Ngā Puhi, Ngāti Porou, Ngāti Haua), will teach and we often have support from our kaiāwhina.


Important information
- It is important that everyone turns up on time for karakia
- For tikanga purposes, beginner ticket sales close two days before wānanga, giving us time to hauhake (harvest) for you
- If class numbers are too low, we may have to cancel (and will offer a refund or transfer), so please check your emails before class
- We offer concession tickets for students, unwaged, seniors, and those with disabilities.
- It is important to us that our classes are accessible to all. If ticket costs are a barrier for you, please visit our website to enquire about our reduced-cost or free tickets
- We welcome children aged 10-14, but they should be accompanied by an adult with a ticket

Follow us on Facebook or Instagram: Otari Raranga Weavers
For more information about what to expect and what to bring, visit our website: www.otariraranga.co.nz

We mihi to our funders and sponsors, Wellington City Council, Wellington Gardens and the Ōtari-Wilton Bush Kaitiaki, Maverick Creative, Good Fortune Coffee and Wellington Apothecary.

Donations, Tax Credits and Corporate Gifting
- We accept koha to support Ōtari Raranga Weavers via Shared Lines Trust. This enables us to continue to offer subsidised and free events to the community
- Shared Lines Trust is registered with the Charities Service; Shared Lines Trust (CC61762)
- For corporate gifting or individuals requesting tax credits, please email SharedLinesTrust@gmail.com or visit www.sharedlines.org.nz for more information
