events.id: WELLINGTON BREATHWORK EVENT-Courtenay Creative,49 Courtenay Place, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: WELLINGTON BREATHWORK EVENT
events.venue: Courtenay Creative,49 Courtenay Place, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
WELLINGTON BREATHWORK EVENT
On Saturday June 27, three of New Zealand’s OWAKEN-trained facilitators are coming together to lead a powerful breathwork experience.

About this Event


WELLINGTON BREATHWORK EXPERIENCE

On Saturday June 27, step into a rare breathwork portal with three of New Zealand's OWAKEN trained facilitators - Jacqui, Hayley and Brigette.

This experience is designed to bring you HOME to yourself. Within this immersive afternoon, the girls will guide you through a breathwork journey that will help you to release stored stress and emotion, regulate your nervous system, reconnect to your body, and access deeper clarity, power, peace and aliveness.
If you’ve been overwhelmed, stuck in your head, emotionally shut down, or constantly running on pressure, this experience is an invitation to move what’s been trapped beneath the surface and come back into contact with your full self.

This is a powerful inner experience designed to help you break through emotional holding patterns, shift stagnant energy, and reconnect to the part of you that is clear, grounded, alive and deeply powerful.

So many people are living with years of suppressed emotion, unresolved stress, internal pressure and nervous system overload in their bodies. We get used to coping. Used to pushing through. Used to overthinking, overriding ourselves, staying busy, staying strong, and disconnecting from what we actually feel.
But there is a cost to living that way. It drains your energy. It clouds your clarity. It dulls your aliveness. It disconnects you from your power.
Breathwork changes that.



Breathwork is a powerful active practice that works directly with the body and nervous system to create emotional, physical, mental and energetic shifts. Your breath is one of the fastest ways to influence your internal state. It can unlock held emotion, shift stress patterns, restore regulation, increase presence, and open access to deeper clarity, truth and vitality.
Because insight alone is not always enough.
You can understand your patterns intellectually and still feel blocked in your body.
You can know what needs to change and still feel unable to move it.
That’s because the body holds what the mind cannot think its way through.
Breathwork offers a direct pathway into that deeper layer. It creates space to release what has been suppressed, process what has been stored, and move energy that has been keeping you stuck. What opens on the other side is often relief, freedom, clarity, self-trust, emotional power and reconnection to life force.
During this experience, people may move through emotional release, physical sensation, energetic activation, nervous system recalibration, grief release, insight, breakthrough, clarity, peace and profound reconnection to self. Every experience is unique, but many people leave feeling lighter, clearer, stronger, more open, more grounded and more fully alive.



This experience is intentionally guided in a grounded, supportive and trauma-aware way.

We’ll begin by sharing cacao and coming together in community, creating space to arrive, connect, and gently open into the experience together.
Then, we move into grounding and nervous system preparation so your body feels supported and ready for the journey. From there, you’ll be guided through a powerful active breathwork experience held collectively by three trained facilitators through breath, music, emotional release and live support in the room. Afterwards, we move into integration, grounding and reflection so your system can land the experience and fully receive what has shifted.
This event may deeply support you if you’ve been:
carrying stress, emotion or pressure for too long
feeling stuck, flat, disconnected or shut down
trapped in overthinking and disconnected from your body
navigating change, grief, burnout or inner tension
craving clarity, release, reset or reconnection
doing inner work but still feeling blocked in your body or patterns
No prior breathwork experience is necessary. Beginners are absolutely welcome.



People often leave feeling lighter, clearer, emotionally released, physically grounded, more energised, more open-hearted and more deeply connected to themselves. Sometimes transformation is not about becoming someone new. Sometimes it’s about releasing what has been sitting on top of who you already are.

IMPORTANT:
Please bring a yoga mat, blanket, pillow or cushion, water bottle, journal, and comfortable clothing you can fully relax in. Creating physical comfort helps the nervous system feel safe enough to open and go deeper into the work.
This is your invitation to breathe, release, move what’s been stuck, & come fully home ot yourself with more clarity, more power and more life inside you.
We can’t wait to breathe with you.


Please note that breathwork may not be suitable for people who are pregnant or experiencing certain medical or psychiatric conditions including epilepsy, severe cardiovascular conditions, schizophrenia, psychosis or recent major surgery. If you have questions about whether this experience is suitable for you, please reach out privately. Your safety is deeply important to us.

events.long_description:
Stephanie Murray Mortgages Winter Ladies Day Fashion in the Field
Wellington Steeplechase is back bringing with it a fantastic afternoon of winter racing, socialising and style. Alongside a great card of racing, we’re excited to introduce the Stephanie Murray Mortgages Winter Ladies Day Fashion in the Field.

Fashion in the Field is all about embracing winter racewear and showcasing your raceday style trackside. Whether your look is classic and elegant, modern and tailored, or bold with statement winter pieces and accessories, this is your chance to dress up and be part of the fun.

This year’s categories are:
Best Dressed Lady, Best Dressed Gent and Best Winter Ensemble

So gather your friends, plan your raceday outfits and enjoy a stylish winter day out at Trentham Racecourse. Whether you’re entering Fashion in the Field or simply coming along to enjoy the atmosphere, Wellington Steeplechase is the perfect excuse for a fun afternoon out this winter.

We can’t wait to see everyone’s winter raceday style this June.

events.id: 80s Day Disco: Wellington 🪩-Eva Pub - Wellington-All Events In
events.name: 80s Day Disco: Wellington 🪩
events.venue: Eva Pub - Wellington
events.long_description:
80s Day Disco: Wellington 🪩
Get Ready to Groove at the Ultimate 80s Day Disco! 🎉
Step into a time machine and relive the magic of the 80s with a day filled with the biggest disco anthems and synth-powered hits of the decade. Whether you’re a true 80s fanatic or just here for the nostalgia, this party is all about electric vibes, funky beats, and non-stop dancing! 🎧🕺✨
What to Expect:
🎶 Nonstop 80s Classics: Dance to the timeless hits that defined the decade—from disco grooves to rock anthems and synth-pop bangers.
🕺 Retro Dance Floor Energy: Show off your best moves under the dazzling glow of neon lights and shimmering disco balls.
🌟 Feel-Good Vibes All Day Long: Celebrate the music, the energy, and the nostalgia with great people and iconic tunes.
Let’s make it a day to remember—80s style! 🎶💿✨Expect to hear music from:Michael Jackson ⭐ Madonna ⭐ Prince ⭐ Whitney Houston ⭐ Queen ⭐ George Michael ⭐ David Bowie ⭐ Tina Turner ⭐ Elton John ⭐ Phil Collins ⭐ Cyndi Lauper ⭐ Bon Jovi ⭐ The Police ⭐ Bruce Springsteen ⭐ Duran Duran ⭐ Journey ⭐ Billy Joel ⭐ Lionel Richie ⭐ Eurythmics ⭐ Depeche Mode ⭐ Aerosmith ⭐ U2 ⭐ The Cure ⭐ Van Halen ⭐ Hall & Oates ⭐ AC/DC ⭐ The Smiths ⭐ New Order ⭐ Fleetwood Mac ⭐ The Bangles ⭐ Rick Astley ⭐ Spandau Ballet ⭐ Roxette ⭐ Simple Minds ⭐ Guns N’ Roses ⭐ Billy Idol ⭐ Tears for Fears ⭐ Bryan Adams ⭐ A-haFAQ’S🗣️ Why do the tickets come in different price tiers?Whilst there is no physical difference. There is only a limited amount of tickets we can sell. Therefore we operate a festival style ticketing model. The earlier you buy your ticket the cheaper it will be.🗣️ Do you do group discount? Stag/Hen etcYes, we offer a discounted ticket price for groups of 10 or more. These tickets can be found listed as the “Big Group Package!” above, orders for these tickets are restricted to a minimum of 10.🗣️ Will there be more tickets available if the event is sold out?Once this event is sold out, there will not be any additional tickets released. In addiction to this we will be unable to sell them at the door due to maximum capacity and health and safety regulations.🗣️ Is the venue accessible?Our events take place at a variety of locations across the UK and Ireland. As each varies please ensure to check the specific venue's website for accessibility information.🗣️ Can I resell my tickets?Yes, if you are unable to attend an event you are welcome to sell your tickets on. We do not use names on tickets as a condition for entry.🗣️ Are your events 18+?All of our events are strictly 18+. A valid photographic ID may be requested on entry.🗣️ What do I do if I cant find my tickets or confirmation email?
We use Fatsoma to sell tickets for our events. If you’re struggling to find your ticket, you can access their support channels via  https://support.fatsoma.com/support/home
🗣️ Are there tickets available on the door?We always recommend booking in advance as we cannot guarantee on the door entry. However, in certain instances there will be a limited number of tickets on a first come first served basis for sale on the door.🗣️ I have more questions?If you have additional questions and wish to contact us directly, don’t hesitate to drop us a DM on either Facebook (Daytime Disco) or Instagram (@daytimediscoevents)Refund Policy:In-line with normal consumer standards for ticket purchases, tickets for Daytime Disco events are non-refundable, on the condition that we not moved the event or canceled it. If you are no longer be able to attend the event, you are of course welcome to gift or sell your tickets onwards as we do not use names on the tickets as a condition of entry.

Disclaimer:
The event listed here is purely for informational purposes. All the details such as venue, date/time, artist information, etc., are subject to change.
This event is not associated with or sponsored by AllEvents, but it may be of interest to you. You can explore more such events with us at AllEvents.

events.id: Bike Repair Workshop for Beginners - No Experience Needed-Kilbirnie/Lyall Bay Community Centre, 56/58 Bay Road, Kilbirnie, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Bike Repair Workshop for Beginners - No Experience Needed
events.venue: Kilbirnie/Lyall Bay Community Centre, 56/58 Bay Road, Kilbirnie, Wellington
events.long_description:
Learn to fix common issues and keep your bike running smoothly!

Got a bike but not much experience with repairs?
Keen to learn how to look after it and fix common issues?

Bikespace’s friendly bike mechanics are coming to Kilbirnie to guide you through the basics in a relaxed, supportive environment.

• Perfect for beginners and those with a little knowledge
• Learn how to look after tyres, brakes, chains, gears, and more
• Friendly, experienced, patient community mechanics
• First part of the session: Step-by-step explanation of how things work and how to fix or adjust them
• Second part of the session: Hands-on time to work on your own bike with support available, or watch and learn from others
• No equipment or technical know-how needed – just bring yourself. Also bring your bike if you’d like to apply what you’ve learned during the second part of the evening
• Tea and biscuits provided

Recommended from 13 years and older. Younger participants may benefit from parent support – please note that the first 45mins are observing and learning and require a level of focus and patience.

Cost: Registration is $15. At the end of the workshop, you’ll receive a $15 voucher to spend at your local bike shop, Burke Cycles at Kilbirnie. Perfect incentive to stock up on what you need to keep your bike running smoothly!

Find us in the hall at Kilbirnie Lyall Bay Community Centre.
We look forward to seeing you!

Questions? Email bikespace@wcc.govt.nz

Dance Workshops

Our dance workshops offer an exciting opportunity for children to get moving and experience the joy of dance firsthand. Led by experienced dance educators, these sessions introduce basic ballet techniques in a fun, supportive environment. Students will explore movement inspired by The Bomb, helping them connect storytelling with physical expression.

events.id: GrandPrix Landmower Racing-Molewood Orchard, 17 Mole Street, Greytown, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: GrandPrix Landmower Racing
events.venue: Molewood Orchard, 17 Mole Street, Greytown, Greytown
events.long_description:
Start your engines, it’s time for one of the Festival of Christmas’s newest iconic events, the Lawnmower Grand Prix!

Watch as brave (and slightly competitive) speedsters take to the track in a high-speed showdown of modified mowers, questionable tactics, and plenty of festive spirit. From souped-up speed machines to wildly twisty chicanes, anything can happen once the flag drops.

Expect:
• Fast laps and fierce competition
• Noise
• Plenty of laughs (and maybe a few breakdowns)
• Serious bragging rights up for grabs

Whether you’re racing or just cheering from the sidelines, it’s guaranteed chaos, camaraderie, and classic small-town fun.
Don’t miss it — this is lawnmowing like you’ve never seen before.

Head to the Farley Ave door, follow the noise, grab a hot drink, and witness some racing before the Festival Big Switch.

Many thanks to the North Island Mower Racing Association for this event.

events.id: Learn the Tarot and Guide Yourself-Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, 65 Victoria St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Learn the Tarot and Guide Yourself
events.venue: Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, 65 Victoria St, Wellington
events.long_description:
This 2-hour Tarot workshop, guided by TripAdvisor Hall of Fame recipient Tarot Zamm, is designed for everyone from beginners to experienced readers seeking to deepen their practice. The session explores the Major Arcana, Minor Arcana, Court Cards, practical self-reading techniques, and includes a guided practice session. Seats are limited to 11 participants to ensure an intimate and focused learning experience. RSVP essential.

The registration fee is NZD $90, offered at a special rate from the standard fee NZD $180. Early registration is recommended. Register for the waitlist once spaces are filled.

Bring your own tarot deck and a notebook if you would like to take notes.

events.id: Old School Beers In Wellington-Rumours Rum Bar, 64 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Old School Beers In Wellington
events.venue: Rumours Rum Bar, 64 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
The legends that launched a thousand meaningless conversations and just as many handovers! You have heard all the boring stories now try the beers that inspired them.

Welcome to the 1960's and 70's when beer was big. Big bottles drank by big people. In big pubs with big tables. The only things little were the prices and the glasses

A Wellington Beer Tasting, celebrating classic brown bottle beers.

Served with the food that made the All Blacks unbeatable

Sample 6 Ales, have some Kiwiana snacks, listen to old school Kiwi music and be bored or entertained by your Host with fascinating stories and insights of the time.

events.id: Polymer Clay Earring Making Workshop-Coopers Loft, 117 East Street, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Polymer Clay Earring Making Workshop
events.venue: Coopers Loft, 117 East Street, Greytown
events.long_description:
Escape the winter chill and step into the cosy light filled studio at the home Coopers Loft in Greytown!

Every Saturday during the festival, Coopers Loft welcome adults and children aged 8+, making this a wonderful activity to share with friends, family, or visitors to the region.

You’ll be crafting handmade clay jewellery or accessories in an inspiring and fun workshop.

With five-star reviews, previous customers have said this was a highlight of their trip to Greytown.

Coopers Loft workshops are thoughtfully timed to fit around the festival programme, so you can enjoy the wider celebrations while making time for a creative activity.

With limited spaces, you’ll have to book fast! These seats fill up quickly! Craft stunning earrings or accessories, whether it’s chic and stylish or novelty. We will have a range of pieces on display to bring you inspiration on the day!

Book now via https://www.coopersloftgreytown.com/

Each participant will make 2-3 pieces during the session.

events.id: Textured Bracelets-Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Textured Bracelets
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
This lively, hands-on workshop is designed for complete beginners, but it’s also a great opportunity for more experienced students to enjoy a creative session making their own unique bracelet.

Everyone will leave with a beautifully finished bracelet of their own preferred texture.

We’ll begin by experimenting on a sample strip of copper, where you’ll practice creating textures using hammers, punches and stamps. Once you’re confident you’ll move on to your main project: a 3mm wide flat strip bracelet in either sterling silver or copper, crafted entirely by you from start to finish. Along the way, you’ll learn essential jewellery making skills including measuring, texturing, soldering, shaping, and finishing.

Students are free to personalise their bangle with textures or stamped designs. This could be a simple hammered finish, decorative patterns, or meaningful words, names, dates, or poetry lines - making your bracelet truly one of a kind.

Beginners will work with the 3mm strip provided, ensuring a rewarding project that’s manageable to complete. Wider metal is much harder to form and solder, so we are learning to walk before we can run ;)

Copper is included in the course fee, if you wish to work in silver you can purchase it on the day.

This workshop is a taster class: perfect for anyone seeking a fun, creative experience with a beautiful result, or for those looking to dip their toes into jewellery making and build foundational skills.

Important Note for Young Makers: Kids aged 12 and over are welcome to attend, but anyone under the age of 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver. Extra metals are available for purchase in the studio if you wish to make more.

events.id: Vintage cake masterclass-Le Ciel Bakery, 100 Tory Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Vintage cake masterclass
events.venue: Le Ciel Bakery, 100 Tory Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Step into the charm of classic pâtisserie and learn the art of timeless cake design in our Vintage Cake Masterclass.

In this guided, hands-on session, each guest will receive a 10cm mini chocolate cake — beautifully prepared and ready for decorating. You’ll begin with a live demonstration by Chef Shuchi Bhardwaj, who will walk you through the techniques and piping styles that define elegant vintage cakes.

Then, with expert guidance, you’ll recreate the design on your own cake, icing and piping step by step to achieve a stunning, nostalgic finish.

All materials are thoughtfully provided, including:

• Freshly baked mini chocolate cake
• Buttercream icing
• Decorations and piping tools
• Cake box to take your creation home

Whether you’re a beginner or a baking enthusiast, this interactive class is designed to be relaxed, creative, and enjoyable.

Gluten-free options are available.
$70 per person, including a hot beverage of your choice.

Come for the experience, leave with new skills — and a cake that looks straight out of a vintage bakery window.

events.id: 80's Day Disco: Wellington-33 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: 80's Day Disco: Wellington
events.venue: 33 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Tickets selling fast → https://www.fatsoma.com/e/vb6j5pqr/la/qwmu
Get Ready to Groove at the Ultimate 80s Day Disco!
Step into a time machine and relive the magic of the 80s with a day filled with the biggest disco anthems and synth-powered hits of the decade. Whether you’re a true 80s fanatic or just here for the nostalgia, this party is all about electric vibes, funky beats, and non-stop dancing!
What to Expect:
Nonstop 80s Classics: Dance to the timeless hits that defined the decade—from disco grooves to rock anthems and synth-pop bangers.
Retro Dance Floor Energy: Show off your best moves under the dazzling glow of neon lights and shimmering disco balls.
Totally Rad Photo Ops: Capture the moment with throwback props, vintage backdrops, and all the 80s glam.
Feel-Good Vibes All Day Long: Celebrate the music, the energy, and the nostalgia with great people and iconic tunes.
Let’s make it a day to remember—80s style!
Expect to hear music from:
Michael Jackson
Madonna
Prince
Whitney Houston
Queen
George Michael
David Bowie
Tina Turner
Elton John
Phil Collins
Cyndi Lauper
Bon Jovi
The Police
Bruce Springsteen
Duran Duran
Journey
Billy Joel
Lionel Richie
Eurythmics
Depeche Mode
Aerosmith
U2
The Cure
Van Halen
Hall & Oates
AC/DC
The Smiths
New Order
Fleetwood Mac
The Bangles
Rick Astley
Spandau Ballet
Roxette
Simple Minds
Guns N’ Roses
Billy Idol
Tears for Fears
Bryan Adams
A-ha
FAQ’S
Why do the tickets come in different price tiers?
Whilst there is no physical difference. There is only a limited amount of tickets we can sell. Therefore we operate a festival style ticketing model. The earlier you buy your ticket the cheaper it will be.
Do you do group discount? Stag/Hen etc
Yes, we offer a discounted ticket price for groups of 10 or more. These tickets can be found listed as the “Big Group Package!” above, orders for these tickets are restricted to a minimum of 10.
Will there be more tickets available if the event is sold out?
Once this event is sold out, there will not be any additional tickets released. In addiction to this we will be unable to sell them at the door due to maximum capacity and health and safety regulations.
Is the venue accessible?
Our events take place at a variety of locations across the UK and Ireland. As each varies please ensure to check the specific venue's website for accessibility information.
Can I resell my tickets?
Yes, if you are unable to attend an event you are welcome to sell your tickets on. We do not use names on tickets as a condition for entry.
Are your events 18+?
All of our events are strictly 18+. A valid photographic ID may be requested on entry.
I have more questions?
If you have additional questions and wish to contact us directly, don’t hesitate to drop us a DM on either Facebook (Daytime Disco) or Instagram (@daytimediscoevents)
Refund Policy:
In-line with normal consumer standards for ticket purchases, tickets for Daytime Disco events are non-refundable, on the condition that we not moved the event or canceled it. If you are no longer be able to attend the event, you are of course welcome to gift or sell your tickets onwards as we do not use names on the tickets as a condition of entry.

events.id: Greytown Lands Trust Big Switch Opening Night-Greytown Town Hall, 89 Main St, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Greytown Lands Trust Big Switch Opening Night
events.venue: Greytown Town Hall, 89 Main St, Greytown
events.long_description:
Kick off the festivities on Saturday 27 June with a spectacular rural celebration. Main Street turns into a Town and Country show of sorts, where you can cheer on your favourite backyard hero in our Ride on Lawnmower Grand Prix, play old school games, and once it gets dark, see the magical lights get switched on for the first time, and enjoy the much-loved Light Up Tractor Parade as it rolls through the village.

What to expect:
- Entertainers throughout the village
- Artisan Night Markets with arts, crafts, food, and festive treats
- Outdoor dining at Truckstop, The White Swan Garden Bar and Food Truck Central
- The first switch-on of magical Christmas lighting
- The beautiful Community Christmas Tree, featuring adorable Ruru Morepork's decorated by local children. Thanks to Greytown Lands Trust.
- Snowfall returns — by popular demand - thanks FreshChoice Greytown! Every 30 mins from 6pm - 8pm
- spin the wheel to win a meat pack

Browse the Night Markets and sample delicious food truck treats, grab a hot chocolate or mulled wine, enjoy the entertainment, the snowfall and festive music through the streets.

Main Street will be closed to traffic so you can roam freely and soak up the atmosphere. Throw on your winter coat or your Christmas jumper and join us for an opening night to remember, thanks to Greytown Lands Trust.

IMPORTANT INFORMATION
Some Festival of Christmas events involve flashing lights and light projections, which may affect people with light sensitivity. Please stay within designated safe zones and follow the directions of our traffic marshals.

events.id: Greytown School Country Food Fair-Greytown School, 73 East St, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Greytown School Country Food Fair
events.venue: Greytown School, 73 East St, Greytown
events.long_description:
Join us at Greytown School for a country-themed food fair on Opening Night! Just a few steps away from Main St (follow signs through Stella Bull Park), the school children will have delicious, family-friendly food, drinks and sweet treats for sale with proceeds going towards a Shade Solution for the Greytown School Playground.

The event will kick off at 3pm with games, vintage machinery and tractors that kids can sit on - so head into town early to nab a carpark and relax while your kids enjoy their safe, fenced school grounds. The school will have seating, toilets, pram parking for the entire event (if you don’t wish to navigate Main St with it) and even a cosy parents’ room for feeding babies.

Grab a yummy dinner from the stalls, then head to Main Street to watch the Light Up Tractor Parade at 6pm.

events.name: Oyster Saloon with Martin Bosley
events.venue: Aperitif Wine Bar, 101 Main Street, Greytown, Greytown
events.long_description:
Martin Bosley is back in the Aperitif courtyard for two delicious nights only to bookend the Festival. Join the team at Aperitif Wine Bar & Eatery as the legend himself returns to work his oyster-shucking magic while you sip on crisp Champagne or a warm mulled wine.

Rug up, gather your crew, and stay toasty by our outdoor heaters as you soak up the festive atmosphere and live music in the courtyard, right amongst the action. No bookings required – just turn up and enjoy!

3pm - 7pm (unless sold out prior) Saturday 27 June and Saturday 25 July only.

events.id: Sarah A Parker: Book Signing - Ballad of Falling Dragons-Hedleys Bookshop, 150 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: Sarah A Parker: Book Signing - Ballad of Falling Dragons
events.venue: Hedleys Bookshop, 150 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
We are so excited to host internationally bestselling author, Sarah A Parker to celebrate the release of her latest book, The Ballad of Falling Dragons!

Sarah is the author of the epic fantasy romance series: Moonfall and Crystal Bloom which have sold millions of copies internationally and are New York Times Bestsellers.

Sarah is currently touring the world for the release of Ballad of Falling Dragons – the second book in the Moonfall series, and we are incredibly lucky to be included as a stop on her tour. This event will be particularly special as Sarah grew up in Masterton!

All of Sarah's books, including The Ballad of Falling Dragons and some special editions, will be available to purchase and have signed on the day.
**To keep things fair for everyone, we kindly ask that only books pre-ordered from Hedley's or purchased on the day are brought in for signing.**

This is a free event but bookings are required.

events.long_description:
Truckstop Winter Edition is back on Friday and Saturday nights throughout the festival.
This little hub of tasty goodness gives great dining for the whole family, tucked away from the hustle and bustle of Main Street. Every weekend, Dale Keith will be serving up his famous smashed burgers, Mao & Co will bring the zing with their delicious Asian street food, and Goodtimes really do make times good with flash flatbreads that pack a flavour punch. This year, in the ‘sweet spot’ corner, Mrs Blackwell will be selling New York-style cookies.
Truckstop will also be the official festival central station for mulled wine! Grab a bite, a mulled wine and cosy up next to the brazier, trucker style!

events.id: Wairarapa Youth Orchestra: Fate & Hope Symphonies-Wairarapa Events Centre, Holloway St, Carterton-Event Finder
events.name: Wairarapa Youth Orchestra: Fate & Hope Symphonies
events.venue: Wairarapa Events Centre, Holloway St, Carterton
events.long_description:
Join the Wairarapa Youth Orchestra for Fate & Hope: Symphonies, an inspiring afternoon of live orchestral music and the orchestra’s annual fundraising concert. Featuring music by Beethoven, Tchaikovsky, Dvořák and Holst, this programme explores themes of resilience, drama and optimism.

Programme highlights include:

Holst – Mars, the Bringer of War

Beethoven – Symphony No. 5 “the Fate symphony”

Dvořák – Symphony No. 9 “from the New World”

Tchaikovsky – Symphony No. 5

Including a special performance from the orchestra’s chamber ensemble, showcasing smaller-scale works and the versatility of these talented young musicians.

The orchestra provides a unique, inclusive opportunity for young musicians to learn orchestral music at no cost, supported throughout the year by expert tutoring from respected musicians, including players from the New Zealand Symphony Orchestra. By attending, you help sustain this programme and ensure that young musicians across the region can continue to access high-quality orchestral training and performance opportunities in the Wairarapa.

Tickets
This concert is the orchestra’s primary fundraiser. Every ticket purchased helps keep participation free for young musicians and supports specialist tuition from experienced orchestral musicians.

Choosing a Flexi ticket is a simple way to give more and directly invest in the future of these performers.

For school, rest home, or other group bookings, please phone for tailored pricing options.

We're coming in hot with our first ever Hot Wing Competition!
Think you're elite with heat? Come prove it at the Top Wings Academy!
Coast through 10 missions of hot wings of increasing levels of heat, and brace for impact as every encounter is going to get more and more difficult. We're going to test your taste buds right to the edge of the world, and send you higher - and more dangerous - than you've ever flown before.
The most elite will receive a Bar Tab, Spicy Boys & DVB merch, and earn a place on the Wall of Flame for ultimate glory.
Shoutout to our mates over at @spicyboys_limited for supplying the 10 levels of hot sauces!

events.id: Food Truck Central-Main Street, Greytown, Main Street, Greytown, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Food Truck Central
events.venue: Main Street, Greytown, Main Street, Greytown, Greytown
events.long_description:
Treat your taste buds every Friday and Saturday night during the 2026 festival, with a vibrant lineup of food trucks serving up crowd favourites and new delights. An extra night added for Thursday 9 July, before Matariki!

From authentic tacos and freshly made crepes to Japanese sushi bowls and slow-cooked meats, there’s something for every craving. If you’re leaning sweet, expect cakes, doughnuts, churros and plenty of decadent treats - plus the return of glow-in-the-dark candy floss for a little festival magic.

Find the action under the festoon lights outside the Town Hall, in Stella Bull Park, and at Truckstop.

events.id: Paint and Wine Weekend Afternoon in Kāpiti - Banksy Balloon Girl-The Bond Store, 21 Birmingham Street, Paraparaumu-Event Finder
events.name: Paint and Wine Weekend Afternoon in Kāpiti - Banksy Balloon Girl
events.venue: The Bond Store, 21 Birmingham Street, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Join us for a fun adults’ painting class at The Bond Store – a vibrant distillery in the heart of Paraparaumu, known for its award-winning New Zealand craft spirits. No painting experience is needed – your friendly artist will guide you step by step in a relaxed, judgement-free environment.

This event is all about creativity, conversation, and having a great time while unleashing your inner artist.

Your ticket includes a gin tasting tray, giving you the chance to sample some of The Bond Store’s specialty gins while you paint.

Whether you’re coming with friends, planning a date night, or flying solo, this is your chance to unwind, get creative, and experience the joy of painting with Paintvine.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website ⇒ paintvine.co.nz

events.venue: Greytown Town Hall, 89 Main St, Greytown
events.long_description:
Glorious snow will be falling once again outside the Town Hall, thanks to FreshChoice Greytown.

A firm festival favourite, this magical moment arrives right on cue every Friday and Saturday night throughout the festival (plus Thursday 9 July, the day before the Matariki public holiday). Who knew the forecast could be this reliable?

Set to music and lights, enjoy a little northern hemisphere White Christmas magic right here in Aotearoa. Strike your best snowy selfie pose, grab something delicious from the food trucks, keep an eye out for Ruru and his friends and get ready for a flurry of fun.

The forecast is looking very convincing. Snow will fall every half hour from 6pm to 8pm, like clockwork.

events.name: Songwriters' Saturday
events.venue: The Realm Bar & Bistro, 7 Moxham Avenue, Hataitai, Wellington
events.long_description:
Welcome to June Songwriters' Saturday at https://therealm.co.nz/

We have another exciting evening of homegrown music planned for your enjoyment. Things kick off at 6.30 p.m. with the first of four acts. Your host venue, The Realm, is fully seated, with Bar and Restaurant facilities available and is close to bus services. Reservations can be made online or by phone.

Parking is free and, on the street, with easy access from the front of the building. The music ends around 9 o'clock.

About the Artists:
My name is Aurelia Humphreys. I love singing and writing music. I am exploring, learning and experimenting with acoustic originals, where I go where the music takes me. I play the guitar.
https://www.instagram.com/songsfromaureliahumphreys/

Jo Sheffield plays a selection of original songs and loves collaborating with other musicians. She is joined by John Broadbent, guitar virtuoso and songwriter in his own right. Jo released her first Album “Gypsy Mind”, in 2022. A collection of 11 original songs, this was inspired by the music, people and places directing her journey through life. Expect to hear contemporary acoustic originals influenced by traditional folk and blues styles.
https://josheffield.bandcamp.com/track/gypsy-mind-single

South Taranaki duo Pete Norris and Farley Hokopaura met at a jam session four years ago and discovered they both enjoyed playing and singing each other's songs. Backed by their guitar and bass, they present their folk/country-flavoured compositions and an occasional cover, complemented by their melodic harmonies
https://farleyhokopaura.bandcamp.com/
https://traceyhaskellteweratosing.bandcamp.com/.../family...

Meet Robyn Lynn & the TGs. Born from a story of breaking up and moving on, these tracks have been perfected over three years. The TGs spin original chords into cool, fresh beats behind Robyn Lynn’s soulful vocals. Hear them live before the official release of the upcoming album!
https://robynlynn777.bandcamp.com/album/reflections
Organized by Rachel Hird
https://rachelhird.bandcamp.com/

events.id: Condenser Plays The Cure and Wire-46a Victoria St Alicetown,46 Victoria St, Alicetown, Lower Hutt 5010, New Zealand, Lower Hutt-All Events In
events.name: Condenser Plays The Cure and Wire
events.venue: 46a Victoria St Alicetown,46 Victoria St, Alicetown, Lower Hutt 5010, New Zealand, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Condenser Plays The Cure and Wire
Last year they delivered Joy Division’s Unknown Pleasures to rave reviews.

This year they’re back to perform two more landmark post-punk albums - The Cure - Boys Don’t Cry and Pink Flag by Wire.

Condenser, Wellington’s punk rock stalwarts, have been unleashing sonic assaults and kicking against the pricks since 1980. This trio, composed of James on drums, PJ on bass, and Nigel on guitar, delivers a raw and energetic performance that harks back to the genre’s incendiary origins. But Condenser isn't just a band; it's a culmination of Wellington's rich punk history. James, Nigel, and PJ are all veterans of the scene, having cut their teeth in noted Wellington bands including Condemned Sector, Neoteric Tribesmen, Columbian Necktie and Compos Mentis. These groups, active in the early days of punk, were instrumental in shaping the Wellington sound and fostering a spirit of rebellion that continues to resonate.

Condenser's music isn't just a performance; it's a living history lesson, connecting the dots between Wellington's punk past and its present. The experience gained in their wild youth has coalesced in Condenser, creating a potent force that keeps the flame of punk rock burning bright in Aotearoa.

But Condenser isn't just a band; it's a culmination of Wellington's rich punk history.

events.long_description:
FULLY BOOKED Workshop: Darn Good Fun
FULLY BOOKED

Saturday 27 June 2026
10:00am – 1:00pm (Half Day)
Karori Arts & Crafts Centre, 7 Beauchamp Street, Karori

Cost: $30 for members
$45 for non-members

Taught by: Anna Hicks

This workshop is now FULLY BOOKED but check out the other workshops here: https://weg.org.nz/whats-happening/#solstice-2026

Discover the joy of darning! This beginners aimed class blends traditional techniques with contemporary methods to teach you how to repair and enhance your favourite garments with darning. You’ll learn to combine colours, create patterns, and watch a demonstration on using a ‘speed weave’ darning loom. Whether you’re new to darning or looking to refine your skills, join us to see how fun and creative darning can be.

This class has 12 spaces available.
There will be an optional purchase for stabilisers during the class.

The timing of this class allows you to have a full day of stitching when you sign up for both Darn Good Fun in the morning followed by Mindful Moss in the afternoon!

https://weg.org.nz/

events.name: Healing Cafe - Petone community healing June 27
events.venue: The Petone Depot,5 Britannia St, Petone, Lower Hutt 5012, New Zealand, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Healing Cafe - Petone community healing June 27

✨ The First Healing Café of the Year is Back! ✨

We are excited to welcome you on 27 June for another beautiful evening of connection, healing, and community.

We are currently confirming our practitioners, so the full list of healers and readers will be announced soon.


☕ What is Healing Café?

Healing Café is an intimate, accessible, affordable, and safe space to explore your spiritual path and wellbeing.

Each event brings together 3–5 practitioners from different healing and spiritual modalities, creating a cosy environment where you can experience a range of offerings in a more personal setting.

Whether you'd like to have a kōrero with a practitioner, learn about different healing approaches, or book yourself a mini session, you are warmly invited to join us.


💛 Entry by Koha

This is a koha-entry event. All koha donations go directly to support the venue, which has been serving the Petone community for decades.


✨ Mini Sessions Available

Healing and reading sessions will be available throughout the day. Each practitioner will offer discounted mini-sessions on-site. This is a separate charge from the koha entry.

To avoid disappointment, please get in touch with practitioners directly to book your session once the practitioner list is released.



✨ Meet Our Practitioners ✨

🔮 Lynne O'Brien – Tarot Reader & Medium

Lynne is a highly gifted spiritual channel with over a decade of experience supporting people through intuitive guidance and spiritual connection. Having walked her own profound healing journey, she brings clarity, compassion, and authenticity to her readings. If you're seeking insight, guidance, or connection with spirit, Lynne's sessions are not to be missed.

📧


💙 Kath Lys – Blue Star Energy Healer

Kath is a well-loved healer known throughout spiritual fairs and healing events for her gentle presence and genuine desire to help others thrive. Working as a clear spiritual channel, she brings through energies of unconditional love, healing, and empowerment. Her sessions offer a nurturing space to reconnect with your true self and inner potential.

🌐 https://www.zenwithkath.com/


🌿 Fery Qiu – Shamanic & Reiki Healer, Tarot Reader & Astrologer

Fery is a Shamanic Healing Practitioner, Reiki Healer, Tarot Reader, and Astrologer who combines ancient wisdom with practical guidance. events.id: Pruning Workshop with Jon Muller!-99 St Andrews Road, Plimmerton, New Zealand 5026,99-109 St Andrews Rd, Plimmerton, Porirua 5026, New Zealand, Porirua-All Events In
events.name: Pruning Workshop with Jon Muller!
events.venue: 99 St Andrews Road, Plimmerton, New Zealand 5026,99-109 St Andrews Rd, Plimmerton, Porirua 5026, New Zealand, Porirua
events.long_description:
Pruning Workshop with Jon Muller!
Our very popular workshop is back!

Click the link to register/ limited 25 people, $10 each:
https://www.palmers.co.nz/products/998166824?_pos=1&_sid=54d1e674b&_ss=r

Join us in-store for a practical pruning workshop designed to help you feel more confident caring for your garden. Whether you’re a beginner or confident home gardener, this session will give you simple, useful techniques for pruning and training common ornamental and fruiting plants.

What you’ll learn:

· Why pruning is important for plant health, shape and productivity
· When, where and how to prune common garden plants
· How to prune and train roses, hedges, fruiting plants and other ornamentals
· Practical techniques for shaping plants and encouraging healthy growth
· How to use pruning tools safely and correctly
· Simple plant-care tips, including fertilising and pest and disease control

About Jon Muller:
Jon Muller is a highly experienced Wellington-based horticulturist who has lived in the region for over 50 years. He holds a Dip. Hort. and NDH Hons, and has worked across the industry as a nurseryman, landscape consultant and lecturer. Jon has also appeared on radio and TV, lectured at Victoria University School of Design and various polytechnics, and written six gardening books.

events.id: WellyFest Committee Meet & Greet-The Welsh Dragon Bar,Middle of the Road, Cambridge Terrace, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: WellyFest Committee Meet & Greet
events.venue: The Welsh Dragon Bar,Middle of the Road, Cambridge Terrace, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
WellyFest Committee Meet & Greet
Whether you're a long-time festival regular, a musician, a volunteer, or just folk-curious, come along to our Meet & Greet and share a drink with the crew behind the Wellington Folk Festival.

When: Saturday 27 June, 4pm–6pm (and onwards for those keen to stay)
Where: Welsh Dragon Bar, Wellington 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁷󠁬󠁳󠁿

It's a relaxed, no-pressure catch-up — a chance to put faces to names, hear what's brewing for the festival, and connect with others who love folk music as much as we do. We would also love to hear from you about what you want your 2026 WellyFest to look like.

Bring your instrument, bring your stories, and we'll bring the good company. Bar snacks will be provided. The Welsh Dragon is a dog friendly pub and children are welcome if accompanied by a parent or legal guardian.

We can't wait to see you there!

Ngā mihi nui,
The WellyFest Committee

events.long_description:
San Pedro’s Jazz Collective @ The RSC
🌵San Pedro’s Jazz Collective🌵

Inspired by the iconic cactus landscapes and the vast beauty of Chile’s Atacama Desert, San Pedro’s Jazz Collective emerges from Jazz Shed Studio with a fresh and vibrant musical journey.

Join us for a laid-back evening featuring a rich selection of Jazz classics, Latin grooves, Bossa Nova favourites, Funk rhythms, and timeless Standards. Like an oasis in the desert, this new local collective brings together creativity, passion, and world-class musicianship.

Come relax, unwind, and enjoy the sounds of this exciting new ensemble.

#SanPedrosJazzCollective #JazzShedStudio #LiveJazz #LatinJazz #BossaNova #FunkMusic #JazzStandards #LiveMusicNZ

events.id: Car Boot at the Otaki Market-Otaki Market, 58 Main Highway, Ōtaki, Kapiti Coast, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Car Boot at the Otaki Market
events.venue: Otaki Market, 58 Main Highway, Ōtaki, Kapiti Coast, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Bring a fresh, fun twist to your market day with our new Car Boot Sale at the Ōtaki Market! This is a great opportunity to clear out pre-loved items, share hidden treasures, and connect with bargain hunters in a relaxed, community-focused space.

Whether it’s clothing, toys, books, household goods, furniture, tools, garden tools or bric-a-brac, if it’s in good condition and ready for a new home, it’s welcome at the car boot. It’s all about giving items a second life while creating a vibrant, accessible way for locals to get involved in the market.
Bookings: Will be made on our website through the booking enquiry form and then will follow the same process of general bookings.

KEY DETAILS
Location: South end of the carpark
Spaces available: Five car spaces
Cost: $10 per car
10am-2pm

events.name: Community Sundays at the Tav
events.venue: 172 Cuba Street Te Aro, New Zealand 6011,172 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Community Sundays at the Tav
Community Sundays at the Tav ❤️

Good food. Good people. Doing some good while we're at it.

We're launching a new monthly series at Cuba St Tavern, bringing people together over a great Sunday roast while supporting local organisations doing important work in our city.

For our first Community Sundays series, we'll be supporting Wellington City Mission across June, July and August - donating a portion of proceeds from each event to support the amazing work they do in our community.

Bookings are $40pp and include a two-course Sunday roast (main and dessert), with a vegetarian option available. Simply book your spot via our online booking portal, pull up a chair, and spend your Sunday afternoon enjoying good food and good company.

Whether you're a regular at the Tav or joining us for the first time, everyone is welcome!

📅 Last Sunday of every month
🍽️ Two-course Sunday roast
🥕 Vegetarian option available
❤️ June-August supporting Wellington City Mission

🎟️ Bookings via the Cuba St Tavern website
(Contact Us → select your date → choose Community Sundays – Sunday Roast)

Because good food has always had a way of bringing people together.

events.id: Otaki Market - June-Otaki Market, 58 Main Highway, Ōtaki, Kapiti Coast, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Otaki Market - June
events.venue: Otaki Market, 58 Main Highway, Ōtaki, Kapiti Coast, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Nau Mai Haere Mai, meet you at the Market.
Discover a vibrant outdoor market nestled on State Highway One in Ōtaki, just across from New World. Join us to greet our regular stallholders and special guests from the community.
---
PARKING
There is plenty of free parking available at the Otaki train station.
Main Highway is a perfect spot for customers to the market
60 Minute Parking zone behind Riverstone Cafe.

CASH / ATM
Some vendors have eftpos, and all take cash. There is an ATM located on the main highway.

FOOD VENDORS
And when hunger strikes, our lineup of food trucks and stalls is ready to tantalize your taste buds. From waffles to hangi, seafood chowder to fry bread, sausage sizzles, and a delectable assortment of rice, noodles, and home baking – a feast awaits.

BUSKER
We often have an awesome busker, who'll be playing chilled out middle of the road tunes.

- Art & Craft
- Native trees, flowers and plants
- Candles, soaps and wax melts
- Antiques and collectables
- Produce, including yummy honey!
- Pre-love clothing, shoes and accessories
- Tools for the home and garden
- Toys and books for tamariki

---
STALL HOLDERS
Would you like to be a stall holder?
Join our market family, fill out the enquiry form: www.otakiwomensclub.org/stall-holder

events.id: Sunday Morning Jewellery-making In 4 Weeks - Mixed Level-Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Sunday Morning Jewellery-making In 4 Weeks - Mixed Level
events.venue: Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington
events.long_description:
Spend your Sunday mornings immersed in creativity, learning the timeless craft of jewellery-making in our warm, welcoming studio.

It runs from Sun 28 June for 4 weeks till Sun 19 July, 10am-1pm.

This short course is the perfect introduction for complete beginners, guiding you step-by-step through the essential techniques used by jewellers. You’ll start by working in copper or brass, building confidence as you learn to cut, heat, shape and texture metal. From there, you can choose to move into silver and bring your ideas to life in more refined pieces.

A beautiful textured ring band is an ideal first project, but once you’ve mastered the basics, the possibilities really open up—create earrings, pendants, or small bespoke pieces that reflect your personal style. You’ll learn how to solder using a jeweller’s torch, refine your finishing techniques, and polish your work to a professional shine. It’s a hands-on, deeply satisfying process, and there’s nothing quite like wearing something you’ve made yourself.

These Sunday sessions are also perfect for returning students and more experienced makers who want dedicated studio time, guidance on more ambitious projects, and the chance to be part of a friendly, creative community.

Materials are purchased as you go, so you can tailor your projects to your budget and ideas. Beginners typically spend around $50–$70 on metals over the course.

This block is ideal for teens and young creatives aged 12 and up. Please note: anyone under 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.
events.description:
Spend your Sunday mornings immersed in creativity, learning the timeless craft of jewellery-making in our warm, welcoming studio.
It runs from Sun 28 June for 4 weeks till Sun 19 July, 10am-1pm.
This short course is the perfect introduction for complete beginners, guiding you step-by-step through the essential techniques used by jewellers.
From there, you can choose to move into silver and bring your ideas to life in more refined pieces.
You’ll learn how to solder using a jeweller’s torch, refine your finishing techniques, and polish your work to a professional shine.

events.id: Glass Bead-Making Taster-Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Glass Bead-Making Taster
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Join us for this fabulous introduction to working with colourful molten glass!

This class is a great way for absolute beginners to get a taste of working with glass to produce beautiful beads that can be used in a myriad of ways, including making a unique gift for your loved one (or yourself!).

As one of the earliest forms of human adornment, beads have long functioned as a way to express individuality, and what better way to do that than to learn to make your own unique beads?

Students are shown correct lampworking techniques to produce several decorative beads in just 3 hours! How many beads you make is up to you; some students make up to 10 while others make fewer using trickier techniques.

Kids over 12 (or so) are welcome to take the class, but anyone under 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.

events.long_description:
Improv Workouts- Undisputed Champions w. PopRox Improv
Work your improv muscles with monthly "workouts" hosted by PopRox. Each month's workout will have a specific focus, with an emphasis on reps over theory. Our "Undisputed Champions" sessions are aimed at highly skilled improvisers with strong performance experience.

If you're not sure which workout is best for you, take our wee quiz linked below or get in touch. We're happy to offer some guidance.
https://opinionstage.com/page/d55e4c73-8faf-4d9c-9910-320b2dd00d6e

OPEN SESSION DATES:
Sunday 25 Jan
Sunday 22 Feb
Sunday 22 March
Sunday 26 April
Sunday 24 May
Sunday 28 June
Sunday 26 July
Sunday 23 August
Note: No September session due to NZIF
Sunday 25 October
Sunday 22 November

SEASON PASS:
Book a season pass to get access to all 10 Undisputed Champion sessions for the whole year at a reduced rate. Please note these are paid out straight away so refunds are not available for season passes. We'll reduce the pricing of these as the year goes on, so feel free to try out a session with a standard pass first.

STAND-BY LIST
PopRox firmly believes that improv is for everyone, especially when it comes to sharing skills and knowledge. So we're offering "the stand-by list" for all our workshops in 2026. We'll giveaway any spots remaining on the Friday before each Workout, on a first-come-first-serve basis. No need to provide a reason, no questions asked. If the entry fee is a barrier for you to come along, then simply email and we'll pop you on the SBL. Everyone on the list will be notified whether there was a place available for them 2 days before the relevant workshop.

events.id: Silver Clay Taster-Toi Pōneke Arts Centre, 3-6 Market Lane, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Silver Clay Taster
events.venue: Toi Pōneke Arts Centre, 3-6 Market Lane, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join us to try out this amazing technique and make a gift for yourself or your loved ones :-)

Precious Metal Clay is an incredible product made from metal particles mixed with an organic binder. When your pieces are shaped - just like a modelling clay - they are then dried and fused in the kiln. That process takes only a few minutes so there is plenty of time in this 3-hour class to complete your projects.

Beginners start with simple pendants/earrings. It is much easier to start with flat projects so we leave the rings for the advanced class!

Jewellery pieces can be textured and shaped however you like, and if you don't like your piece simply squish it and start again - Precious Metal Clay is the ultimate recyclable material!

Included in the course fee is 5 grams of fine silver clay, enough to make a pendant or earrings, or a collection of charms - it's up to you.

Extra findings such as earring hooks and chains will be available to purchase (you can also make your earring hooks if you are feeling adventurous).

This class is great for kids also but an enrolled parent or caregiver must accompany any person under the age of 16.

events.id: Stories Behind Some of Aotearoa’s Most Interesting Plants-Tāne Whakapiripiri - Ōtari Visitors Centre, 150 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Stories Behind Some of Aotearoa’s Most Interesting Plants
events.venue: Tāne Whakapiripiri - Ōtari Visitors Centre, 150 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington, Wellington
events.long_description:
Why is speargrass so sharp? Why does the parapara have such sticky seeds (hint, little birds beware). Join Te Papa science researcher and evolutionary biologist Lara Shepherd on an exploration around Ōtari’s plant collection, she’ll reveal all sorts of fascinating facts about our native species.

Meet at Tāne Whakapiripiri, Ōtari Visitor Centre. It is easy walking and family groups are welcome. We ask that dogs stay at home for everyones comfort and a smooth tour experience.

This is part of the Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush Trust’s annual guided walks programme. This year, they are on the fourth Sunday of the month, March – November, to celebrate 100 years of Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush.
Join the Trust for only $20 for an individual or $30 for a double/family for a year and all the walks will be free, as well as other great benefits. www.owbt.nz/memberships-legacies/

Ōtari 100: Celebrating 100 years of native plants, conservation, and community. 1926-2026.

events.name: Pottery Social: Raise a Glaze
events.venue: Duncan's Brewing Company,4B Sheffield Street, Paraparaumu, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Pottery Social: Raise a Glaze
Join us for Pottery Social: Raise a Glaze, where you can sip, glaze and decorate your own custom Tumbler

About this Event


Pottery Social: Raise a Glaze
Join us in person for a fun and laid-back afternoon of creativity and good vibes. Whether you're a pottery pro or just starting out, this social is all about raising a glass and enjoying some creativity together. Bring your friends, get your your creativety flowing, and let's make some unique pieces while sharing laughs and good company. Don't miss out on this awesome chance to connect and create!
You'll be glazing your very own a handmade tumbler made my Mud + Woman Pottery.





Pottery Class Terms & Conditions


Bookings: All class bookings are non-refundable 7 days prior to event.
Changes & credits: If something comes up and you can’t make it, just reach out — we’re happy to offer a voucher so you can join another class instead.
The nature of clay: Working with clay is a beautiful but imperfect process. Each piece goes through drying and firing, and sometimes cracking, warping, or breakage can happen despite our best care. It’s all part of the magic (and mystery) of pottery.
Finished pieces: While we do everything we can to look after your creations, we’re unable to guarantee the final result or offer refunds or remakes for pieces that don’t make it through the process.

You'll want to be able to say you were there to see it!
Bookings essential to 02102361951 or buy your tickets here.

events.id: Paekākāriki Film Festival 2026-St Peters Village Hall, Cnr Beach Rd and Ames St, Paekākāriki-Event Finder
events.name: Paekākāriki Film Festival 2026
events.venue: St Peters Village Hall, Cnr Beach Rd and Ames St, Paekākāriki
events.long_description:
The Paekākāriki Community Trust would like to warmly welcome you, your family and friends to our Film Festival in the St. Peters Village Hall Paekākāriki.

June 14: "My Favourite Cake" (NZIFF 2024) is a delightful, bittersweet Iranian romantic comedy-drama about a seventy-year-old widow who is inspired to have a bit of excitement after years of duty and repression. (Cake treats in house too for a few extra coins)

June 21: "Put Your Soul on Your Hand and Walk" (NZIFF 2025) is a heart-breaking but essential portrait of life in Gaza during the Israeli military campaign, centred on courageous Palestinian photojournalist Fatima Hassouna.

June 28: "Thelma and Louise" (1991) is a landmark film and one of the very best films of the 1990s. It's a fierce feminist celebration packaged into a wildly entertaining road movie starring Geena Davis and Susan Sarandon.

events.id: Paint & Sip with Mandy Emerson-The Offering, 65 Main St, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Paint & Sip with Mandy Emerson
events.venue: The Offering, 65 Main St, Greytown
events.long_description:
Enjoy a fabulous night of creativity and great company paired with delicious food & refreshments!

Mandy Emerson is an established NZ artist from Riversdale Beach in the Wairarapa. She started her paint workshops over a year ago and they have been such a success she continues to be fully booked out.

It is The Offering's absolute pleasure to host her here in Greytown. Enjoy a glass of bubbles on arrival, select a delicious dish from the curated menu and settle in for some creativity. The workshop will then pause for dinner.

Mandy's work is mixed media, feminine with florals and she has won awards for her works and exhibits all over New Zealand.

Her biggest success came from exhibiting at the NZ ART SHOW in Wellington which she still continues to be a part of and also attending ARTMELBOURNE in Australia. Visit her website www.mandyemerson.com

events.id: The Snow Supper at The White Swan-The White Swan, 109 Main Street, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: The Snow Supper at The White Swan
events.venue: The White Swan, 109 Main Street, Greytown
events.long_description:
This winter, step into something truly magical.

In the heart of Greytown, experience The Snow Supper, an enchanting outdoor dining experience on the deck of The White Swan, where soft snowfall, glowing lights, and award-winning food and drink come together beneath the Wairarapa’s famed dark skies.

Set on the front deck of this much-loved local, you will dine under drifting flurries at the iconic starlit hotel, with festive feel-good music as the backdrop. Grab a cosy blanket, and enjoy a beautifully curated menu, all delivered with the warm, welcoming hospitality The White Swan is known for. Inclues a perfectly spiced mulled gin to complete the mid-winter experience.

Running Sunday to Thursday throughout the New Zealand Festival of Christmas, this is a distinctly local take on mid-winter Christmas, elevated, atmospheric, and designed to be shared.
Available: Sunday – Thursday evenings only between 28 June - 23 July 2026

Location: The Captain’s Dining Room, or outdoor deck (dependent on weather)

Menu: Set menu – $99 per person

Choice of 2 entrées / 2 mains / 2 desserts

Includes a warming mulled gin on arrival

Payment: Pay on arrival

Please note: Snow Supper is a special experience and only available on selected nights with limited capacity.

Tickets available soon via The White Swan: https://thewhiteswanhotel.co.nz/table-booking

events.id: PADI Rescue Diver Course-Lower Hutt-Facebook
events.name: PADI Rescue Diver Course
events.venue: Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Wellington is renowned for its ever changing weather and it is easy to get caught out under prepared. You need to know your limits and what to do when things don't go to plan on your dive.
A little knowledge can help avoid many of the common dive incidents and being familiar with rescue diving skills means you are ready to act if required.
With a day in the pool to learn the skills and a day at the sea putting them into practice with various rescue scenarios.
Pool Sessions - Monday 21st & Wednesday 23rd from 6:15pm
Sea Scenarios - Saturday 27th or Sunday 28th from 9:45am
Pre-requisites:
Advanced Open Water Diver Certification
Current First Aid Certification
$500 + gear hire
Add the PADI Emergency Oxygen Provider Specialty and/or PADI Emergency First Responder (first aid) & save!

events.id: AUDITIONS for Lettice and Lovage-9 Sunset Parade, Plimmerton,9 Sunset Pde, Plimmerton, Porirua 5026, New Zealand, Porirua-All Events In
events.name: AUDITIONS for Lettice and Lovage
events.venue: 9 Sunset Parade, Plimmerton,9 Sunset Pde, Plimmerton, Porirua 5026, New Zealand, Porirua
events.long_description:
AUDITIONS for Lettice and Lovage
Lettice and Lovage by Peter Shaffer is a hilarious, satirical comedy about Lettice Douffet, an eccentric, history-obsessed tour guide who embellishes facts for tourists. When a strict preservationist, Lotte Schoen, fires her for lying, the two clash—only to form an unlikely bond over a shared disdain for the modern world.

· Lettice Douffet
An extravagant, imaginative, and highly theatrical former tour guide whose mother travelled France acting in Shakespearean plays. An expert on Elizabethan cuisine and medieval weaponry.

Requires extremely strong comedic acting. Maggie Smith won a Tony for this role!

Stage age 30-60.

· Lotte Schoen
A stern, strait-laced, and unbending inspector from the Preservation Trust. Underneath her brisk, bureaucratic exterior lies a deeply disappointed idealist and secret champion of radical architecture preservation.

Stage age 30-60.

· Mr. Bardolph/Visitor to Fustian House
Stiff-upper-lip, buttoned-down British attorney who is reluctantly dragged into one of Lettice’s extreme historical reenactments.

Requires excellent comedic acting.

Stage age 25+.

· Miss Framer/ Visitor to Fustian House
Lotte’s timid, and obedient secretary.

Stage age 30+.

· Surly man/ Visitor to Fustian House
Hostile history buff and visitor to Faustian House.

Stage age 25+.

· Visitors to Fustian House

Email for the audition sides.

Show run 2-12 September
Rehearsals will be 3 times a week in July and August.

AUDITIONS for Lettice and Lovage Lettice and Lovage by Peter Shaffer is a hilarious, satirical comedy about Lettice Douffet, an eccentric, history-obsessed tour guide who embellishes facts for tourists.
When a strict preservationist, Lotte Schoen, fires her for lying, the two clash—only to form an unlikely bond over a shared disdain for the modern world.
An expert on Elizabethan cuisine and medieval weaponry.

events.venue: 150 Queen St, New Zealand 5888,150 Queen St, Masterton 5810, New Zealand, Masterton
events.long_description:
Author James Russell is Coming to Town!
Join award-winning children’s author James Russell at this FREE celebration to launch the first book in his brand new series for junior fiction readers – Marlon Foundling and the Circus of Lost Children!

James will read the first chapter, followed by an informal chat and book signing.

Kids will no doubt remember James as the awesome author behind the very popular, Dragon Defenders and Children of the Rush series. He was an absolute hit with the kids during our Yarns in Barns ‘Inspire a Child to Read’ programme in 2025 so we're stoked to have him coming back to town!

10am, Sunday 28th June 2026
Hedley’s Books, Masterton

Free event – all welcome – please let us know you’re coming by emailing if you can!

events.long_description:
Property Brokers Jets vs Gilas Pilipinas
The Philippines National Team, Gilas Pilipinas, are coming to Palmerston North to play an exhibition against the Manawatu Jets.

This is a unique opportunity to see international basketball in Palmerston North against your very own Manawatu Jets. This is a warm-up game for Gilas Pilipinas before they go to play the Tall Blacks in Auckland.


⏰ Doors open 12.00pm (noon)
📍 Fly Palmy Arena @ The Hangar
✈️ Tip Off 1.00pm


Whether you’re a long-time Jets supporter, a member of the Filipino community, or simply a basketball fan, this is a game you will want to experience.

Game Timing & Entry:

Gates will open at 12:00 pm for a 1:00 pm tip-off
Doors will open to the general public 1 hour prior to the advertised start time.

Ticketing Information:

Tickets purchased at the door are $2.50 more than pre-purchased tickets. We encourage supporters to secure their tickets in advance to avoid the additional charge. Courtside seats are available by pre-purchase only and will not be sold on game day.

*As this is a special international exhibition fixture outside the Sal’s NBL season, all attendees, including season members and corporate guests, will require a separate ticket for entry.

events.name: 2026 Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance
events.venue: Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
2026 Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance
The Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance celebrates contemporary dance in the capital.

For one week each winter, we’re bringing together the freshest artists from across Aotearoa and beyond to heat things up and push the boundaries. Expect performance seasons, artists in residence, free events, parties, debates, and good vibes.

The Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance is here to ensure a vibrant future for Pōneke's dance scene, fostering a passionate community around this captivating art form.

The programme for the 2026 festival is now live!

/Hannah Playhouse
/Toi Pōneke
/Footnote Dance Studios

The festival is a joint initiative curated by Footnote New Zealand Dance and presented with The Hannah Playhouse and Toi Pōneke, supported by Wellington City Council.

events.venue: PATAKA Art + Museum, Cnr Parumoana and Norrie St, Porirua - Mana
events.long_description:
Join Infometrics Chief Executive and leading economist Brad Olsen for an engaging look at the economic trends shaping Porirua, Wellington, and New Zealand.

Brad will break down the latest data, explore the opportunities and challenges facing local businesses, and share practical insights into what may lie ahead for our city.

Whether you’re a business owner, leader, investor or simply interested in Porirua’s future, this is a conversation you won’t want to miss.

This event is free to attend, but registration is essential.

Stay for Q&A and networking with other local businesses.

events.id: Waltz of My Heart-Hutt City Church, 22 Marsden St, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: Waltz of My Heart
events.venue: Hutt City Church, 22 Marsden St, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Step into a world of sweeping melodies, soaring voices, and the graceful rhythm of the waltz in this unforgettable concert Waltz of My Heart, a celebration of one of the most beloved musical forms in history.

From the iconic Viennese waltzes of Johann Strauss to modern interpretations such as The Anniversary Waltz and The Tennessee Waltz, this enchanting programme showcases the enduring beauty and emotional power of the waltz across generations and genres. Whether heard in grand ballrooms or intimate theatres, this music continues to captivate and enchant.

Taking centre stage are our four magnificent tenors whose rich, expressive voices breathe new life into classic and contemporary waltzes alike. Joining them is fabulous baritone John Bayne plus stunning Amanda Atlas, whose powerful soprano and magnetic presence bring depth and drama to every note.

This is a concert of melody, magic, and masterful singing — where every phrase is infused with passion and every performance is unforgettable. Let yourself be swept away by the romance, elegance, and enduring charm of the waltz.

Featuring:
Bonaventure Allan-Moetaua
Benjamin Makisi
Derek Hill
Kalauni Pouvalu
Amanda Atlas
John Bayne
Paul Carnegie-Jones

NZ Tour 02 June 2026 - 03 July 2026
Concerts Start 11am
General Admission Seating
Doors Open 10am – Complimentary morning tea included.

Phone: 0508 266 237
Email: bookings@operatunity.co.nz

events.id: Founder Lecture: A thousand words: Photographic archives in Aotearoa-National Library of New Zealand, 70 Molesworth Street, Thorndon, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Founder Lecture: A thousand words: Photographic archives in Aotearoa
events.venue: National Library of New Zealand, 70 Molesworth Street, Thorndon, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join us for the annual Friends of the Turnbull Library Founder Lecture with Auckland Museum’s Catherine Hammond, who will share insights from her work with major photographic archives across Aotearoa.

Catherine's Founder Lecture will discuss the role and impact of photographic archives as complex repositories of memory and research.

Currently Director of Collections and Research at Auckland War Memorial Museum Tāmaki Paenga Hira, Catherine has more than 20-years’ experience working with artists’ archives in major cultural institutions in Aotearoa.

She will discuss the recent book and touring exhibition, 'A Different Light: First Photographs of Aotearoa', and reflect on archives and meaning-making in examples ranging from Marti Friedlander to Frances Hodgkins, feminist photographic practice and whānau portraiture.

Light refreshments will follow the lecture.

Seats are limited, book your spot here: https://events.humanitix.com/catherine-hammond-founder-lecture-2026-a-thousand-words-photographic-archives-in-aotearoa

events.venue: O'Malleys Bar & Kitchen, 612 Marine Parade, Eastbourne, Lower Hutt 501, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
The concept is simple - take a brush in one hand, a drink in the other and let’s paint a masterpiece!

Join us for a creative night of drinking and painting in the heart of Eastbourne, Wellington! No previous painting experience necessary. Get your creative juices flowing and your brush strokes going!

Drinks: No drinks are included in the ticket price, but drinks are available for purchase.

Food: A delicious menu will be available to purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website.

events.long_description:
OPEN DANCE
OPEN
DANCE

The Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance opens with a collective gathering of movement at The Hannah Playhouse. An open invitation to participate, witness and begin the festival together.

This improvised movement sharing brings together dancers, artists and movement practitioners from across Pōneke. Part performance, part meeting place, this open event celebrates coming together through movement, participation and exchange. Audiences are welcome to witness the unfolding improvisation, or join the space themselves.

Featuring live music by Dream Chambers! Dream Chambers is a multifaceted musical artist and composer for film and television. She works primarily with modular synthesizers and voice to craft evocative, immersive sound worlds. Her music has been described as visceral and transcendent, carrying an almost spiritual fervour, where patterns of melody refract and brim over toward the ineffable.

30th June 7pm
Free to participate. Free to attend.
No registration required, just turn up!

events.id: Treasury Guest Lecture with Vangelis Vitalis (MFAT): IN-PERSON attendance-The Treasury, level 3, 1 The Terrace,1 The Terrace, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Treasury Guest Lecture with Vangelis Vitalis (MFAT): IN-PERSON attendance
events.venue: The Treasury, level 3, 1 The Terrace,1 The Terrace, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Treasury Guest Lecture with Vangelis Vitalis (MFAT): IN-PERSON attendance
The international context for New Zealand trade policy – where we were and where we are – and how do we respond?

About this Event

Join us for the next lecture in our new seminar series
The golden weather for New Zealand trade policy - from 1995 to roughly 2017 - is well and truly over. The world trading system is more turbulent, uncertain and challenging, but New Zealand is well placed to navigate this new world trade disorder.
We will, however, need to double-down on three inter-connected ‘pillars’ of trade policy: rules; resilience and innovation.
This presentation - by MFAT’s Deputy Secretary Vangelis Vitalis - will explain what this means and how it can help us prevent the jungle from growing back.
About this event
This will be a hybrid event. Please use this site to register if you plan to attend it in person.
If you would like to attend it virtually, please use THIS LINK to register.
About the presenter
Vangelis Vitalis is Deputy Secretary, Trade and Economic at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade. He is New Zealand’s Chief Negotiator for the New Zealand-India Free Trade Agreement and has led or helped conclude several major trade agreements, including those with the European Union, CPTPP, ASEAN-Australia-New Zealand, China, and Malaysia.
He previously served as New Zealand’s Ambassador and Permanent Representative to the World Trade Organisation in Geneva, and has also been Ambassador to the European Union and NATO. He has held key international leadership roles through APEC and CPTPP host years, bringing deep experience in trade policy and global economic diplomacy.
About this seminar series
New ideas, robust evidence, and insights from leading thinkers are crucial to strengthening the Treasury’s economic analysis and policy advice. Our new guest lecture theme — Navigating Global Shifts: Resilience, Challenges and Opportunities for New Zealand — invites us to reflect on how Aotearoa New Zealand can navigate an increasingly complex and interconnected global environment.
This series explores the major global forces that shape our economic landscape, including evolving trade relationships, geopolitical developments, and the global movement of people, capital, and ideas. Through these discussions, we aim to deepen our understanding of how New Zealand can build economic resilience, respond effectively to uncertainty, and harness our regional and international partnerships to support economic performance, productivity, and our living standards.
Our speakers explore strategies for mitigating the impacts of global disruptions — from pandemics and climate‑related events to geopolitical tensions and supply‑chain shocks – while also examining potential opportunities presented by rapid technological shifts, digital economy, and global innovations, to name a few. The series considers the role of government in supporting economic transitions, strengthening national resilience, and ensuring that New Zealand remains well‑positioned to meet emerging global opportunities. By bringing together diverse perspectives, this series seeks to inspire new ways of thinking about New Zealand’s economic future and to support informed, forward‑looking policymaking.
Please note that the views, opinions, findings, and conclusions expressed in the Treasury Guest Lecture Series are those of the individual presenters. They do not necessarily reflect the views of the New Zealand Treasury or the New Zealand Government.
For updates and our latest research visit: www.treasury.govt.nz
Keep in touch
If you would like to be the first to hear about our upcoming events in the Treasury Guest Lecture Series, please SUBSCRIBE to stay up to date.
