events.id: Waltz of My Heart-Southward Theatre, Otaihanga Rd, Otaihanga, Paraparaumu-Event Finder
events.name: Waltz of My Heart
events.venue: Southward Theatre, Otaihanga Rd, Otaihanga, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Step into a world of sweeping melodies, soaring voices, and the graceful rhythm of the waltz in this unforgettable concert Waltz of My Heart, a celebration of one of the most beloved musical forms in history.

From the iconic Viennese waltzes of Johann Strauss to modern interpretations such as The Anniversary Waltz and The Tennessee Waltz, this enchanting programme showcases the enduring beauty and emotional power of the waltz across generations and genres. Whether heard in grand ballrooms or intimate theatres, this music continues to captivate and enchant.

Taking centre stage are our four magnificent tenors whose rich, expressive voices breathe new life into classic and contemporary waltzes alike. Joining them is fabulous baritone John Bayne plus stunning Amanda Atlas, whose powerful soprano and magnetic presence bring depth and drama to every note.

This is a concert of melody, magic, and masterful singing — where every phrase is infused with passion and every performance is unforgettable. Let yourself be swept away by the romance, elegance, and enduring charm of the waltz.

Featuring:
Bonaventure Allan-Moetaua
Benjamin Makisi
Derek Hill
Kalauni Pouvalu
Amanda Atlas
John Bayne
Paul Carnegie-Jones

NZ Tour 02 June 2026 - 03 July 2026
Concerts Start 11am
General Admission Seating
Doors Open 10am – Complimentary morning tea included.

Phone: 0508 266 237
Email: bookings@operatunity.co.nz

Explore how to move from anxiety to alignment. Discover nervous system insights and yoga wisdom for inner peace in an overstimulated world.

Tuesday Wellbeing Seminars:
From Anxiety to Alignment: The Nervous System & the Search for Happiness

In a world of constant stimulation, anxiety and inner restlessness have become increasingly common. Explore the nervous system, the mind, and the deeper human search for happiness and fulfilment. events.id: Pukerua Bay Coastal Walk-Porirua Pa-Facebook
events.name: Pukerua Bay Coastal Walk
events.venue: Porirua Pa
events.long_description:
Another coastal walk but this time at the other end of Wellington. Parking at the end of Ocean Parade known as Pukerua Bay Climbing Parking. We will set off towards the climbing rocks which is also the pinch point at high tide.
You will definitely need a good head torch or light to light your own path. Please do not rely on others to light your way. If you are bringing a dog or children we do ask that you keep up with the walking group or stay close to the start of the walk.
This track is rocky and will have a uneven surface so study shoes are recommended.
If we make it to the rocks in time we will be able to hang out and get some cool photos of the rock tunnel that is there.
If you are going please mark this event letting us know so we can manage numbers and help if needed to ensure this is a fun event for you all.
There is a image of where to park in this event if needed as well

We look forward to seeing you all there

events.id: Gates to Memory - Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance: CANCELLED-Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Gates to Memory - Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance: CANCELLED
events.venue: Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
*TICKETS TO THIS EVENT ARE CURRENTLY ON HOLD*

Gates to Memory is a contemporary dance work that premiered at the Kia Mau Festival 2025. The work explores the connection with entities and embodies the messages, wants, and connection with our physical world.

Gates to Memory invites audiences to witness the intimate act of remembering—of reclaiming lost stories and forging new ones through embodied performance. It is a call to honour those who came before, to hold space for those yet to come, and to respect the ones who will always be.

The strength of this work lies in the unspoken but strongly felt spiritual presence that comes from openness to access messages that are not ours but from ancestors from spirit worlds. To the creators, these messages come in the form of music, movement, and intuition.

Gates to Memory is performed and directed by Caribbean and Brazilian afro-descendant artists Ella Williams and Stela Dara, lighting designer Elekis Poblete and CreaTech sound designers Kevin Licorish and Jeanelle Frontin . All who wish to connect with their spirituality and the cultural intertwining of traditions and entities.

Presented as part of the Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance 2026.

events.id: Paintvine Uncanvased™- Nude Life Drawing-Southern Cross Garden Bar Restaurant, 39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Paintvine Uncanvased™- Nude Life Drawing
events.venue: Southern Cross Garden Bar Restaurant, 39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Undress your creativity in this fun, relaxed and easy to follow beginners nude life drawing workshop.

Over the course of two hours, our expert Paintvine artists will guide you through a series of life drawing exercises designed to introduce you to different methods of drawing, all while you and your friends enjoy a vino or brew. You’ll have access to a wide range of drawing tools and materials to explore your creative side in this experimental workshop.

After a couple of drinks, a few cheeky winks and a good laugh or three you and your friends will bid adieu to your model and head home with a series of unique and fun drawings to remember this creatively curious night out!

This is a popular event, so get your tickets before they sell out!

Drinks & Food

No drinks are included in the ticket price, but drinks and food are available for purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?

It’s a fun social art class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own artwork in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends.

We provide all the materials you need. All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). All our events are in licensed venues, so you can purchase drinks to sip as you create! At the end of the night, you will leave with your own collection of unique drawings.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website ⇒ paintvine.co.nz

No drinks are included in the ticket price, but drinks and food are available for purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?

It’s a fun social art class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own artwork in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends.

We provide all the materials you need. All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). All our events are in licensed venues, so you can purchase drinks to sip as you create! At the end of the night, you will leave with your own collection of unique drawings.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to ➡️ [[ https://paintvine.co.nz/]
events.description:
Over the course of two hours, our expert Paintvine artists will guide you through a series of life drawing exercises designed to introduce you to different methods of drawing, all while you and your friends enjoy a vino or brew.
You’ll have access to a wide range of drawing tools and materials to explore your creative side in this experimental workshop.
After a couple of drinks, a few cheeky winks and a good laugh or three you and your friends will bid adieu to your model and head home with a series of unique and fun drawings to remember this creatively curious night out!
Drinks & Food No drinks are included in the ticket price, but drinks and food are available for purchase throughout the event.
All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass).

Building a Plan to Gain Results Like You've Never Had | Q3
What if the reason you're not getting different results isn't because you're not working hard enough?
What if it's because you're planning the same way you've always planned?
Most business planning days focus on goals, budgets, forecasts, and action lists.
The Anti Plan is different.
This interactive planning day is designed for business owners and leaders who are ready to challenge conventional thinking, break through limitations, and create meaningful momentum for the second half of 2026.
You'll step away from the daily demands of your business and spend the day working on your business, not in it.

events.name: Welly Silent Book Club
events.venue: Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, 65 Victoria St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Silent Book Club is a global community of readers with 2,000 chapters in 60+ countries. There's no assigned reading — it's bring your own book.

More than a million members gather in person, online, and in destinations around the world to read together and swap stories. All readers are welcome!

A typical SBC meetup looks something like this:

5:00-5:15pm - People arrive, order drinks/food, share what they're reading
5:15-6:45pm - Quiet reading
6:45-7:00pm - Optional socialising, or just keep reading

The club will be reading on the second floor of Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, under the Earth Mothers (the three large panel art pieces across the ceiling and down the wall that faces the entrance of Ngā Pou Ruahine).

events.id: YEPN Wellington - Show and Tell with Beca-85 Molesworth StreetWellington, Wellington Region-Event Brite
events.name: YEPN Wellington - Show and Tell with Beca
events.venue: 85 Molesworth StreetWellington, Wellington Region
events.long_description:
Join YEPN Wellington for a presentation night with the spotlight on the network faithful!
YEPN Wellington is pleased to invite you to something a little different for our July event - a community presentation night, proudly hosted by Beca at their Molesworth Street offices.
This evening is all about the network sharing with the network. A handful of YEPN members from across the energy sector - spanning engineering, economics, policy and beyond - will take the floor for 5–10 minutes to talk about something energy-related they are genuinely excited about. Whether it is a work project, a personal interest, or something they have been thinking about, we are giving the spotlight to those who aren't always front page news!
It is a great chance to hear what your peers are working on, discover corners of the industry you might not have come across, and connect with people who share your interest and passion for the energy sector.
Drinks and nibbles will be provided courtesy of Beca. Spaces are limited - register below to secure your spot.

events.id: Weds Eve Jewellery Making - Mixed Level-Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Weds Eve Jewellery Making - Mixed Level
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Spend your Wednesday evenings immersed in creativity, learning the timeless craft of jewellery-making in our warm, welcoming studio.

This short course is the perfect introduction for complete beginners, guiding you step-by-step through the essential techniques used by jewellers. You’ll start by working in copper or brass, building confidence as you learn to cut, heat, shape and texture metal. From there, you can choose to move into silver and bring your ideas to life in more refined pieces.

A beautiful textured ring band is an ideal first project, but once you’ve mastered the basics, the possibilities really open up—create earrings, pendants, or small bespoke pieces that reflect your personal style. You’ll learn how to solder using a jeweller’s torch, refine your finishing techniques, and polish your work to a professional shine. It’s a hands-on, deeply satisfying process, and there’s nothing quite like wearing something you’ve made yourself.

These sessions are also perfect for returning students and more experienced makers who want dedicated studio time, guidance on more ambitious projects, and the chance to be part of a friendly, creative community.

Materials are purchased as you go, so you can tailor your projects to your budget and ideas. Beginners typically spend around $50–$70 on metals over the course.

This block is ideal for teens and young creatives aged 12 and up.
Please note: anyone under 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.

events.name: Pubble - Escape Room meets Pub Quiz
events.venue: The Old Bailey, 101 Lambton Quay, Wellington
events.long_description:
Pubbles have the goal of reconnecting people. One screen, your squad and an evening of social adventure!

A mix between the puzzles and lateral thinking of an escape room, mixed with the social, free experience of a pub quiz, Pubbles are designed to have something for everyone. They allow everyone to shine in a game where teamwork wins out over general knowledge and 'either you know it or you don't' traditional quizzes. Each Pubble is set to a different theme, so you could be a group of pirates one month, and astronauts the next. It’s not about what you know, it’s about what you see. Decode images, crack pattern, and beat the timer (and the other teams)

Perfect for meeting people, reconnecting, building bonds and having a laugh - Pubbles are free so it's never been easier to enjoy a midweek catch-up with mates!

To learn more, visit pubble.co.nz/players to learn why you might just love this brand new concept

events.name: Stand Up, Sing Back: People Have the Power
events.venue: Two/Fiftyseven, Level 2, 57 Willis Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Come along for an evening of creativity, community, politics, irreverence and, most of all, singing! Join local Wellington artists Ru Mundy and For Future's Sake Singers, and visiting Palmerston North group the Brazen Hussies in a journey through protest singing together. Sing some well-known protest songs, some new adaptations of old classics, and some original songs, all with a political message. Come ready to sing with us!

Ticket sales cover artists' and event costs, and additional proceeds go to https://triplethevote.nz - supporting 'getting out the vote' for the upcoming election in November.

NOTE: Venue is wheelchair accessible, gender neutral toilets and wheelchair accessible toilets available. Attendees will need to enter via Victoria Street doors as Willis Street doors are closed after hours.

A reminder: You need to be enrolled to vote (and have up to date details!) before October 25th this year. Remind your friends, family, colleagues, neighbours, acquaintances and everyone you can to enrol and update their details early at www.vote.nz

Wednesday 3 June
From 7pm

$65 per person

events.id: You, Me, Everybody - Midnight Album Tour-Whisky & Wood, 60 Cambridge Terrace, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: You, Me, Everybody - Midnight Album Tour
events.venue: Whisky & Wood, 60 Cambridge Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
You, Me, Everybody hit the road in 2026 for a national tour celebrating Midnight, their new album exploring the moment where one day ends and the next begins. Recognised as finalists for Best NZ Folk Artist at the 2021 Aotearoa Music Awards, the group has earned widespread acclaim, including a global audience after their song “Stranger” featured in Netflix’s Sweet Tooth.

Founded by brothers Laurence (vocals and guitar) and Sam Frangos-Rhodes (mandolin, fiddle and vocals) and featuring Nat Torkington on Banjo, Rob Henderson on double bass and Kim Bonnington on vocals and guitar, You, Me, Everybody has built a reputation for pushing bluegrass beyond its borders, blending virtuosic musicianship with contemporary folk and modern string-band innovation. Midnight captures the tension and possibility of life’s turning points, brought to life onstage through rich harmonies, bold instrumental interplay, and great storytelling.

“Acoustic, drumless, genius” - Roger Bowie

The Midnight Tour sees the band bring their new album to Whāngarei as part of a 12-date run across Aotearoa.

events.long_description:
Triple Threat | Monthly Comedy at BATS Theatre
Three of Wellington's sharpest comedians come together for a night of laughs that "stands out...in Wellington's crowded comedy calendar" (theatreview).

Triple Threat is Wellington's premiere comedy night curated for quality over quantity and professional production values- guaranteeing a great night out, every time.

JUNE CAST:

ALAYNE DICK- An icon for so many reasons. Alayne has been brought berets and belly laughs all over Aotearoa, to the Adelaide Fringe and Melbourne International Comedy Fest. She's featured on the NZ Comedy Gala (Wellington) and Best Comedy Show on Earth (TV3). You don't want to miss her action!

GABBY ANDERSON- Fresh off her run at the NZ Comedy Fest, Gabby is back at Triple Threat with her trademark goofs and sharp punchlines. She's a Billy T nominee, a national raw quest champion and all round delight.

GINGE & MINGE- You know 'em, you love 'em. Wellington's most beloved unhinged sketch comedy duo are making their Triple Threat debut!! They've been cleaning up awards around the country including Best Show at the Wellington Comedy Awards 2025, Best Comedy at Dunedin Fringe 2026 and "Comic Originality" at the 2025 National Comedy Guild Awards. They're so hot right now, and we're stoked to have them on the killer line up.

events.long_description:
All students and their whānau are warmly invited to celebrate Matariki on Thursday 2nd July, 5pm–7pm.
This is a cash-only event.
Tickets are available from the school office for $6 in the week leading up to the event, or on the night. Your ticket includes a sausage sizzle, hot soup + bread, and a toasted marshmallow (kids cook their own at the firepit outside!).
Extra sausages, and cinnamon sugar doughnuts will also be available to purchase on the night. Note: doughnuts sold out early last year, so get in quick!
New this year – Dan's delicious makhanwala vegetable curry is available for pre-order, with pick-up at the event. This curry sold out at the carnival, so pre-order now and get dinner sorted! A pre-order sheet will be available at the office until Friday 26th June.
This is a low-waste event – please bring your own soup cup or bowl.
If the weather is wet, the event will go ahead inside the hall.
We hope to see you all there!

events.id: Growth Lab | BDM Performance Mindset Day-Willeston Conference Centre,15 Willeston Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Growth Lab | BDM Performance Mindset Day
events.venue: Willeston Conference Centre,15 Willeston Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Growth Lab | BDM Performance Mindset Day
Build the mindset, confidence and communication skills that drive consistent BDM performance and long-term growth.

About this Event


Build the mindset, confidence and consistency that drive long-term business success
Success in business development is never just about strategy — it’s about mindset, communication, resilience, and the habits you build every day.
This immersive Growth Lab workshop is designed specifically for BDMs and business owners who want to elevate their performance without sacrificing their energy, confidence, or wellbeing.
In an industry where pressure, inconsistency and burnout are common, this day focuses on the personal growth that directly impacts professional results.
You’ll leave with practical tools, renewed clarity, and a personalised action plan to help you perform at a higher level — sustainably.

Who should attend?
This workshop is ideal for:
Business Development Managers
Property management business owners
Growth-focused leaders
Anyone wanting to improve confidence, consistency, communication and emotional resilience

What you’ll walk away with
Every delegate will complete a personalised one-page Personal Performance Plan, including:
Strengths to lean into
Mindset shifts to work on
Confidence and communication goals
Daily and weekly success habits
Energy and motivation management strategies

events.id: Business Wairarapa Summit-Wairarapa Events Centre, Holloway St, Carterton-Event Finder
events.name: Business Wairarapa Summit
events.venue: Wairarapa Events Centre, Holloway St, Carterton
events.long_description:
Create your future!

We’re bringing together some of new zealand’s sharpest minds, most inspiring entrepreneurs, and most provocative thinkers for a full day of keynotes, practical workshops, and real conversations — all designed to help you build the future you want for your business, your career, and your community.

Your takeaways for the day >>>

This isn’t a day of death-by-PowerPoint. Every session has been designed to leave you with something — a fresh perspective, a new skill, or a renewed sense of what’s possible. Here’s a taste of what’s on the programme:


The confidence to disrupt. Meet the environmentalist and social entrepreneur who created the world’s first full range beauty and lifestyle bar brand, sold it globally, and is now shaking up the drinks industry all over again. If she can do it, what’s stopping you? (Brianne West)

Networking skills you’ll actually use. Walk away from a practical, fun session knowing how to work a room, build real connections, and make networking work for your business — not against it. (Catherine Jones)

A reason to be optimistic. Forget the doom and gloom. One of New Zealand’s most respected economic commentators is here to show you why the next 30 years could be the best we’ve ever seen — and he says he can prove it. (Sam Stubbs)

AI that works for your business. Cut through the noise and learn how to use artificial intelligence as a practical tool for your business — starting tomorrow. (Mike Burke)

Your local economy, brought to life. Understand where Wairarapa has been, where it’s heading, and where the unexpected opportunities are hiding — so you can plan your next move with confidence. (Brad Olsen)

The mindset to embrace change. A street-smart futurist will reset your thinking on risk, disruption, and innovation — and leave you ready to act. (Melissa Clark-Reynolds)

Every session has been designed to leave you with something — a fresh perspective, a new skill, or a renewed sense of what’s possible.
Here’s a taste of what’s on the programme: The confidence to disrupt.
Meet the environmentalist and social entrepreneur who created the world’s first full range beauty and lifestyle bar brand, sold it globally, and is now shaking up the drinks industry all over again.
(Brianne West) Networking skills you’ll actually use.

events.id: Adapting to Change-7 Hartham Place SouthPorirua, Wellington Region-Event Brite
events.name: Adapting to Change
events.venue: 7 Hartham Place SouthPorirua, Wellington Region
events.long_description:
Change is inevitable - but your business can be ready for it. Join us for this free workshop for NZ business owners.
Change is a constant part of running a business - but with the right tools and strategies, businesses can adapt, respond, and thrive.
The Adapting to Change workshop is designed to support NZ business owners and teams to anticipate, respond to, and adapt to change through practical tools, guided reflection, and business planning strategies.
This interactive 3 hour workshop will help you better understand your current business environment, identify risks and opportunities, and build greater resilience and agility into your business moving forward.

What you’ll learn
How to recognise and respond to change in your business environment
Practical tools for business resilience and adaptability
Strategies for pivoting and responding to challenges
Identifying risks and uncovering new opportunities
Building agility into your business planning and decision-making

Who should attend?
Small business owners
Entrepreneurs
Business managers and leadership teams
Businesses experiencing growth, uncertainty, or change

Whether your business is navigating challenges or preparing for future opportunities, this workshop will provide practical insights to help you move forward with confidence.

Workshop details
📅 Thursday 2 July 2026
⏰ 1pm to 5pm
📍 Bizhub (7 Hartham Place, Porirua)
🚀 Provided by Wellington NZ in partnership with Wellington Pasifika Business Network and Business Central

Afternoon tea and refreshments will be provided by Wellington Pasifika Business Network, for all guests.

Venue and parking details
Bizhub is located at 7 Hartham Place, Porirua, in the far corner (down at the bottom, by the ramp up to the mall doors, next door the New World).
Get free parking for 4 hours - underneath the new world car park at Porirua North City Mall.
Take the ramp/ stairs up to Bizhub.

Spaces are limited so register fast to secure your spot!

events.venue: Northland
events.long_description:
Northland Wing-Foil & Windsurf Surfari Holiday
Northland Wing-Foil & Windsurf Surfari Holiday is in Doubtless Bay of Northland, New Zealand. Our most popular Winterless North Surfari Trip!
Choose a three night or five night Northland Wing-Foil and Windsurf Holiday to the stunning Doubtless Bay and Karikari Peninsula. The Karikari Peninsula with its white sand beaches and crystal clear water provides opportunities to foil in any wind direction. Northland is known as the ‘winterless north’ with mild temperatures and reliable wind conditions to suit all wing foil and windsurf skill levels. The perfect winter break to give yourself a welcome vacation.
Our Surfari Trips are all inclusive, meaning you don’t need to do a thing except book the trip and turn up!
Quality accommodation
Single, double or twin share available. Limited to 6 guests.
Meals
Fully catered throughout your stay - Breakfast + Lunch + Dinner.
Local knowledge
We ensure you will be at the right spot at the right time.
Self Drive
You have the freedom to travel as it suits you.
Fly in?
We offer gear hire of the latest premium equipment.
Photos
Professional photos taken of you on the water.

events.name: Barnardos Foundation Gala Dinner
events.venue: Tākina Wellington Convention Centre, 50 Cable Street, Te Aro, Wellington, Wellington
events.long_description:
Be part of an extraordinary evening at the inaugural Barnardos Foundation Gala Dinner, proudly presented by Willis Bond and LT McGuinness, where generosity, leadership, and purpose come together to create brighter futures for children and families across Aotearoa.

This special event will bring together passionate supporters, business and community leaders, and advocates for change for an evening of fine dining, inspiring guest speakers, and exclusive live and silent auction experiences.

Every ticket, bid, and connection made on the night supports the Barnardos Foundation and the wider work of Barnardos Aotearoa, helping fund innovation, research, and services that shift the dial for tamariki and whānau where the need is greatest. By investing in bold ideas and long-term solutions, we help create lasting change for generations to come.

Join us to connect, be inspired, and help every child shine bright.

events.id: Giant Crepe Wallflower-Main Street Deli Cafe, 88 Main Street, Greytown, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Giant Crepe Wallflower
events.venue: Main Street Deli Cafe, 88 Main Street, Greytown, Greytown
events.long_description:
Learn to create your very own voluminous, oversized crepé paper flower. A sculptural statement piece that’s equal parts art and joy.

In this immersive workshop, you’ll be guided through every step of the process:
Cutting, curling, shaping, and assembling each petal by hand. It’s a meditative, rewarding craft that requires patience, but with the right techniques (and a few of my favourite tricks!), you’ll be amazed at what you can create.

What’s included:
– A selection of curated colour options where you choose your favourite in advance, and I’ll prepare your paper for you
– All materials provided
– Step-by-step guidance in a relaxed, welcoming setting
– Your own stunning floral sculpture to take home
- Cocktail on arrival
- Canapès to enjoy while creating
- Enjoy a cuppa and sweets after the workshop!

Book now through https://www.ventana.co.nz/upcoming-workshops/p/giant-crepe-flower-greytown-msd

events.id: Kitchen Alchemy: Cook Well, Live Well-Bhakti Lounge Wellington,175 Vivian Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Kitchen Alchemy: Cook Well, Live Well
events.venue: Bhakti Lounge Wellington,175 Vivian Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Kitchen Alchemy: Cook Well, Live Well
Discover Kitchen Alchemy through hands-on organic cooking that connects you with food, nourishes your body and deepens wellness.

About this Event


Kitchen Alchemy: Cook Well, Live Well

Fuel your mind and stimulate your senses through a practical, hands-on experience. We provide organic produce from our own farm in Shannon, and you are invited to help prepare it! You will get to experience integration of culinary arts and nutrition through different cooking methods and ingredients that can enhance different parts of foods.  
This isn't your typical cooking class focused on recipes or perfecting a particular dish. Instead, it's an opportunity to connect with food, ask questions, experiment, and learn through doing. Working with in-season organic vegetables, we'll share ideas, insights, and plenty of good conversation along the way.  Ground yourself and your senses, no experience is required, just try something out. 
If you want food to be a means in your life to better your health and wellness, this is the workshop for you!




Your ticket also includes a plant based dinner and dessert at the end!



Meet your host, Balarama. 

Balarama is a Medical Science graduate with a masters from Melbourne, and is highly knowledgeable in epidemiology and public health. With qualifications in plant-based nutrition and a global education, they thrive in the midst of cooking, sciences, health and agriculture. 



Agenda


🕑: 05:45 PM - 06:00 PM
Arrive to Bhakti Lounge

🕑: 06:00 PM - 07:00 PM
Kitchen Alchemy: Cook Well, Live Well
Host: Hosted by Balarama

🕑: 07:00 PM - 08:15 PM
Plant-based dinner and dessert!

events.venue: Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
AWAKENING THE TANIWHA, HONOURING THE RECLAMATION. MATA MATA is an offering through the act of solo dance. A summoning of the ancestral spirits that reside deep within the awa. As the taniwha stirs just beneath the surface, memory begins to flow from skin into whenua. Awakening the blood memory that is present here. Becoming a sacred ancestral ritual, allow yourself to slip into this journey of returning. Acknowledging the physical body as a vessel to conjure spirit, mana, and memory. MATA MATA is the honouring of Whakapapa and identity and the holder of space to witness the sacred act of awakening this reclamation.

Presented as part of the Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance 2026.

About the artist:
Harrison Cook is a contemporary dancer, choreographer, and writer of Māori (Kāi Tahu) and Pākehā descent, born and raised on Ngāti Hauā Whenua in the Waikato region of Aotearoa. He is currently based on the lands of the Whadjuk Noongar people in Boorloo (Perth). Harrison is a graduate of the Western Australian Academy of Performing Arts, completing his Bachelor of Arts Dance (2023) and Bachelor of Performing Arts Honours (Awarded a First Class Honours) (2024). During his honours research year, working under the mentorship of Jo Pollitt, he developed and produced his thesis work ’Te Wakahuia: Coming to Know Lineage through Dance Improvisation and Archival Poetics’ which included a body of movement and film work under the name ‘HōtokeWynter ~ ComingToKnow.’ This experience of producing this written and movement practice left Harrison feeling pulled to continue this ongoing artistic enquiry into whakapapa, ancestral memory, and the uncovering of the body as a living archive. His developing choreographic practice centres on research-based improvisation, exploring relationships between land and water, ancestral voice, and embodied lineage. Harrison’s solo work MATA MATA has become a sacred part of his practice and artistic offerings and has been developed and supported through the PILOT Choreographic Program with Enneagon Movement and will premiere as part of The Blue Room Theatre’s 2026 Annual Season through the EVOLVE Program with STRUT Dance. His artistic development has been supported through residencies with The Blue Room Theatre and PICA (Perth Institute of Contemporary Arts), where Harrison was given time and space to continue developing his ongoing practice and project 'THE RETURN'. Through his evolving practice, Harrison continues to explore and understand the connecting to dance becoming a sacred site for remembering, reconnecting, and embodying the stories of our past.

events.id: No One Rides Alone - Film Screening with Q and A-Penthouse Cinema, 205 Ohiro Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: No One Rides Alone - Film Screening with Q and A
events.venue: Penthouse Cinema, 205 Ohiro Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington
events.long_description:
Special screening of No One Rides Alone the latest documentary from Silvereye Films.

Can someone with Multifocal Motor Neuropathy (MMN) ride the route of the Tour de France? That’s what Clive Phillips and four fellow veterans set out to discover during the Tour de MMN 2025. Following the 1955 Tour de France route, the team set out to see if they could ride in the wheel tracks of the first British Tour de France team

Would they overcome the challenges of the Alps, Mont Ventoux and the Pyrenees? Or would the impacts of this rare disease on Clive’s motor function and strength bring him and the team to a halt?

From Making the Most of Now and the team at Silvereye films behind the award winning “One Day Ahead”. No One Rides Alone, follows the team as they ride around France, connect with the rare disease community and learn more about MMN and the impacts it and conditions like it have.

Featuring double Paralympic gold medalist Ben Watson and Italian para-triathlete Ilaria Brugnoli, No One Rides Alone is a journey of discovery and an intimate portrait of how with team work, positivity and a willingness to be vulnerable we can achieve the seemingly impossible.

Set against the majesty of the French landscape and a remarkable historical achievement. No One Rides Alone tells an inspiring story that has lessons for us all whatever challenges we face. The film aims to raise awareness of MMN, inspire the rare disease community and raise funds for MMN Research.

events.name: Konpa Gouya 101
events.venue: Footnote Dance, 60 Dixon Street, New Zealand 6011,60 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Konpa Gouya 101
Konpa, or also know as Compas or Kompa is a carribean dance from Haiti.
Two styles Dirèk and Gouya.
Dirèk music often a little faster and with more steps.
Gouya is slow and often dance with little steps.

Here will will be learning Konpa Gouya due to it's similar nature to Tarraxinha and as the music if often played at Kizomba Festivals overseas.

events.id: Pub Sing-The Empire, 175 Jackson St, Petone, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: Pub Sing
events.venue: The Empire, 175 Jackson St, Petone, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Pub Sing - where the audience (you) turns into the choir at the pub, with a live rock band.

No sheet music, no experience needed, and absolutely no solos.

Katy reveals three songs on the night (usually a crowd-pleaser, a ballad, and a banger), and even if you don’t know them, you’ll pick them up fast with colour-coded lyrics on screen.

You learn different harmony options, sing along with the band, and blend into a room of up to 80 voices, so no one’s listening to you; they’re singing with you.

There’s banter, laughs, a bit of silliness, and a whole lot of feel-good energy. It’s not karaoke (shudder), it’s everyone singing together just for the fun of it, and it’s the perfect end-of-week lift you needed.

*Please check the booking link for updates or changes to dates*

*Tickets are $10pp, and all proceeds go directly to the band!

events.id: Andrew London Album launch - Set Me Up By The Bar-Kapiti Playhouse, 7 Ruahine St, Paraparaumu-Event Finder
events.name: Andrew London Album launch - Set Me Up By The Bar
events.venue: Kapiti Playhouse, 7 Ruahine St, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
‘Set Me Up By The Bar’, the long-awaited new album from Andrew London and an army of collaborators loosely dubbed the Swing Voters, will be officially launched at the Kapiti Playhouse on 2nd July. The band will play all twelve tracks from the new album which includes several of Andrew’s signature satirical offerings about supermarket etiquette, unintentionally falling out with neighbours and the pitfalls of playing in restaurants; some poignant country/folk tunes, and a handful of rollicking western swing-inspired songs that give opportunity for jazz-style improvisation to pianist Wayne Mason, saxophone/clarinet player Oscar Laven and Andrew of course on his trademark 1959 Hofner guitar.

With his ever-reliable partner Kirsten on bass, and the metronomic Ryan Watson on drums, the band will also play a selection of jazz, blues and swing standards from their 2019 album ’Standard Deviations’. There will be joyous swing, satirical mirth, foot-tapping country and even a little beard-stroking jazz.

events.long_description:
Celebrate Matariki with an adults-only celebration of the stories, traditions, and mātauranga shared between Aotearoa New Zealand and Te Moananui-a-Kiwa, the wider Pacific.

Ngā ringatoi me ngā kaikōrero‌ | Artists and speakers:
* R&B, hip‑hop blended beats with KOREKASH
* Dance the night away with our Cook Island whanaunga/whānau through performances by Inano Dance
* Learn about the practice of tīvaevaefrom the Tīpurepure ‘Au Vaine
* Weave and wānanga with the Pacific Climate Warriors
* Learn about te reo o te waka from Te Moananui-a-Kiwa, with experienced kaumoana from Ngahiraka Mai Tawhiti. This kōrero will be in te reo Māori.
* Te Papa curators and special guest speakers kōrero about waka and collection highlights
* Explore our Mānawatia a Matariki hub, relax with some Māori Mermaid colouring in, play kēmu with friends, or chat around the fire
* Cash bar and seasonal kai available

R16

This event will have NZSL interpretation.

A full schedule, including information for Deaf whānau, will be shared soon at tepapa.nz/afterdark

events.id: The 25th Annual Putnam County Spelling Bee-Niblick Hall, Niblick Lane,, Porirua - Mana-Event Finder
events.name: The 25th Annual Putnam County Spelling Bee
events.venue: Niblick Hall, Niblick Lane,, Porirua - Mana
events.long_description:
Welcome to “The Bee!”

A hilarious take on the high-octane pressure of competitive spelling.

Six “tween-agers” battle it out for the glorious title of champion or perhaps a consolation juice box. Each with their own worries and pressures, they seek comfort amongst the complexities of perfectly arranged letters.

With its rapid-fire witty script and spellbinding music, “The Bee” hums out a beautiful coming-of-age story with more meaning than the word run*

The 25th Annual Putnam County Spelling Bee is a musical comedy and winner of 7 awards for its off-Broadway production and nominated for six Tony Awards, (winning two, including Best Book)

The Bee also invites selected audience members to take part in the spelling competition creating totally unpredictable, one show only, performances.

Come see what the buzz is all about!

Run time: 2 hours plus 20 min intermission
Contains sexual references, moments of bright lighting and haze effects
Recommended for mature audiences
Licenced Bar

(*Run has 645 distinct but related uses as a verb alone according to the Oxford English Dictionary)

events.id: A Person Shaped Thing - Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance-Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: A Person Shaped Thing - Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance
events.venue: Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
A Person Shaped Thing is a new solo contemporary dance work by Trinity Maydon, exploring the fragile architecture of Self Perception/Presentation - the process of strategically concealing or revealing information in order to influence others' and our own perceptions on ourselves. Even the person you believe yourself to be only exists for you.

We consciously select different versions of ourselves in different rooms. Each version is carefully calibrated. Each gesture considered. A quiet choreography of revealing and concealing.

Drawing on sociologist Erving Goffman’s dramaturgical theories of human interaction, the work examines life as a performance: frontstage and backstage, audience and actor and the constant maintenance of expression control where we learn to play roles before we even realise we are performing them.

The attentive pupil performs attentiveness
until the performance consumes the listening.

In A Person Shaped Thing, identity is not something created, but selected — assembled from external influences, expectations, and the environments we move through. We shapeshift to fit the spaces we enter, adjusting the mask, refining the image, editing the self.

But the performance is exhausting.

What happens when the masks begin to slip?
What remains when the roles fall away?

As the work unfolds, the body becomes a site of tension — caught between authenticity and artifice, between who we are, who we think we are, and who we believe we should be. In the search to “fit into one’s own skin,” we risk becoming estranged from it entirely.

There is no singular, stable self.
At the crux, we are something harder to name —
An unidentifiable, faceless creature.

Blurring the boundaries between performer and performed

Does the performance ever really end?

Presented as part of the Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance.

About the artist:
Trinity Maydon is a contemporary dance artist originally from Ōtepoti and a graduate of the New Zealand School of Dance. Since graduating in 2024, she has been taking her first steps as a freelance artist, exploring and shaping her unique voice within the art form of dance.

Trinity has always been drawn to the ways in which we use our bodies to tell stories, how we can shape ourselves to physicalize our ideas and express them to the world. She is deeply interested in the lived experience of movement and the connection between mind and body, constantly exploring how embodiment can be a tool for both personal expression and collective storytelling.

Since graduating, Trinity has had the privilege of collaborating on two rave installations with Untitled Warehouse Project— 'Entry' and 'Omnia' (2025). She has also worked alongside Jeremy Beck, teaching throughout Dunedin and performing in Company Beck’s 'Commentary of Dreaming' as part of the 2025 Dunedin Arts Festival.
In 2026, Trinity was immersed in the Fringe Festival, performing in Lyndon Foley’s 'Our Dreaming Bodies' and and was a choreographer in Wellington Community Ballet Theatre's 'Four Questions' in her piece 'Is Santa Claus Real'. She also performed as a guest artist at the Performance Arcade in 'Four Strings – Four Limbs' with Alex Cooper.

A Person Shaped Thing is Trinity’s first full-length work and she is excited to be debuting it as part of this year’s festival.

A Person Shaped Thing is a new solo contemporary dance work by Trinity Maydon, exploring the fragile architecture of Self Perception/Presentation - the process of strategically concealing or revealing information in order to influence others' and our own perceptions on ourselves.
As the work unfolds, the body becomes a site of tension — caught between authenticity and artifice, between who we are, who we think we are, and who we believe we should be.

events.id: Deafheaven & Nothing-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Deafheaven & Nothing
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
Deafhaven return to NZ with special guests Nothing
American black-metal trailblazers Deafhaven announce their return to New Zealand this July, presented by Banished Music. The band will perform two exclusive headline shows in Auckland and Wellington, joined by special guests Nothing.
Formed in the San Francisco Bay Area in 2010, Deafhaven have spent the past decade and a half pushing at the emotional and sonic boundaries of modern metal. From their debut Roads to Judah, to the genre-defining breakthrough Sunbather, and subsequent records like New Bermuda, Ordinary Corrupt Human Love, and Infinite Granite, Deafhaven have continually redefined the limits of heavy music while confronting themes of alienation, addiction, family history, and self-perseverance.
The tour follows the release of their 2025 album Lonely People With Power. Their sixth album and first for Roadrunner Records arrives as both a culmination and a reckoning , an album that embraces the band’s full identity after years of evolution. Produced by Justin Meldal-Johnsen (Beck collaborator and producer of Infinite Granite), the record captures Deafhaven’s innate ability to merge melody with brutality, and pain with poetry. Through reflections on masculinity, inherited trauma, family, and self-perception, the album grapples with the idea that while we can’t fully escape our past or our nature, we can choose how we move forward. Ultimately, Lonely People With Power stands as one of Deafhaven’s most expansive and human statements.
The album has drawn praise from Pitchfork, Rolling Stone, The Line Of Best Fit, Kerrang and more, and features collaborations with Interpol’s Paul Banks and Boy Harsher’s Jae Matthews.
Revered for their transcendental live shows, Deafhaven have toured the world over, performing at festivals such as Coachella and Primavera Sound, and sharing stages with artists ranging from Slipknot and Knocked Loose to Chelsea Wolfe and Mono. They’ve received a Grammy Award nomination for Best Metal Performance and continue to command a reputation as one of the most compelling live acts in heavy music.
Joining them are American shoegaze outfit Nothing, whose latest release A Short History of Decay has been described by Pitchfork as “fresh and exciting.” Their fifth album and first for Run For Cover Records further expands the band’s sonic scope, delivering the most refined and immersive iteration of Nothing to date.

San Fran holds a Supervised License. Minors are welcome to attend events at San Fran so long as they are accompanied by a legal guardian at all times whilst at the venue.

events.id: The 2026 Wellington Raw Comedy Quest - Heats-The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The 2026 Wellington Raw Comedy Quest - Heats
events.venue: The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Presented by Cover Yours Ltd and Wellington Comedy

The capital's premier comedy competition. Witness the rising stars in stand-up comedy before they hit the big time.

The stepping stone for many New Zealand comedians, the Raw Comedy Quest has brought forth new comedy talent from across the country, with heats in Christchurch, Wellington, Palmerston North and Auckland.

From six heats, the top 24 go to the semifinals, and the top 10 to the Wellington final. 2024 Wellington finalist Chen Wang came runner up in the 2024 National Raw Comedy Quest, and Lydia Sainsbury runner up in 2025, can the capital win it this time?

Each heat is MC'd by a pro comedian, kick your weekend off with a rollercoaster of comedy

Thanks to support from the Wellington City Council, One Foundation, and Cover Yours Ltd

To enter email contact@humorous.co.nz, all you need is 6min of original material, and the guts to give it a go!

events.id: Pōneke Youth Theatresports-BATS Theatre,1 Kent Terrace, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Pōneke Youth Theatresports
events.venue: BATS Theatre,1 Kent Terrace, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Pōneke Youth Theatresports
Welcome back to the return of the Youth Theatresports™ programme in Wellington!

Our teams of teens have been honing their skills in spontaneity and are ready to take to the stage in the traditional, world-famous-in-New-Zealand-but-also-in-the-world show THEATRESPORTS™

In Theatresports™, two teams fearlessly take the stage and with no scripts, they take turns performing scenes, playing games, telling stories and creating characters entirely spontaneously.

Who will be victorious?
Who will be ridiculous?
Who will receive the first 5 point score from the judges?

Thursday and Friday are our heats, with the grand final on Saturday night.

Come support your team, have a great time, and discover the future of improvised comedy.


Performed by youth from Wellington High and Wellington Girls, and supported by Locomotive.

(Image description: An orange splodgy smiley face on a yellow background)

events.venue: Mama’s Sizzler Restaurant and Bar,3/33 johnsonville road, Johnsonville, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Quiz Night with Mama's Sizzler!
Come join us for Johnsonville's newest weekly ritual on a Thursday night, where you and your crew will negotiate five rounds of amazing trivia with Gee Quiz!

Your team's street cred, life smarts and common sense will go a long way to ensuring success on the night.

Add to the mix delicious food and an incredible vibe, it's the place to be on a Thursday night with plenty of time for laughter, banter and merrymaking!

The quiz kicks off at 6.30pm and runs for roughly 2 hours. So, grab your buddies and come on down and join us for one of J-ville's best quiz nights!

events.id: Free Marshmallows at Staglands for July School Holidays!-Staglands Wildlife Reserve, 2362 Akatarawa Rd, Upper Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: Free Marshmallows at Staglands for July School Holidays!
events.venue: Staglands Wildlife Reserve, 2362 Akatarawa Rd, Upper Hutt
events.long_description:
Our yummy winter marshmallows are back and for a limited period only!* During the July School Holidays, we will be giving them away FREE to all visitors!
Toast your own on the cosy Barn campfire and enjoy some wild cooking in the great outdoors. Then hop on the free off-road tractor-trailer ride, perfect for adventurous explorers of all ages!

If the weather takes a turn, there's no need to worry. Take shelter in their stunning café or warm up in the heated undercover barn, complete with free BBQ facilities - ideal for a winter picnic.

Don’t miss the chance to get up close with the friendly animals! Join their Ranger-led feeding tour daily at 12:30pm and experience the magic of feeding time.

As the saying goes: "There’s no such thing as bad weather, only the wrong clothes." So, dress for the season and come make some sweet memories!
Admission: Adults: $29, Children (4–16 years): $12, Under 3s: FREE

*Offer available 4th - 19th July only.

events.name: Massage (By appointment only)
events.venue: 12 King Street, Upper Hutt
events.long_description:
Oasis Centre offers Massage by appointment. During your spiritual Massage session you will feel your body, mind and soul nurtured in the most spiritual, unwinding and relaxing way. Mindful healing touch and care is given so that you can receive the full benefit of this experience.
The benefits of Massage Therapy are many and varied, and include:
•Body realignment
•Clarity for the mind
•Motivation
•Pain relief
•Rejuvenation
•Relaxation
•Relief of stress and tension
•Sense of calmness
•Therapeutic tension release
Massage is an excellent therapy for relieving pain and stiffness from the muscles of the face, scalp, neck, body, legs, upper back to the shoulders. We often use hot stones to promote deeper relaxation and muscle release.
Massage/Spiritual Healing is also an effective method to bring about a deep relaxing, enjoyable and harmonious healing. In some cases it will deal with the emotional release that is required at the time.
Come and enjoy the healing atmosphere. Have a Massage session and experience the benefits it gives to your mind, body, soul and spirit.
Each Massage session usually takes about an hour. Bookings are required as spaces are limited.
*Bookings Only*
Cost: Unfortunately we have had to set a $25 minimum on the Koha/Donation.
Phone Anne on 027 6404968

events.id: Adults Only Schoc Chocolate Making Class-Schoc Chocolate, 177 Main Street, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Adults Only Schoc Chocolate Making Class
events.venue: Schoc Chocolate, 177 Main Street, Greytown
events.long_description:
Indulge in a hands-on chocolate experience at the Festival of Christmas, featuring delicious tastings and expert guidance throughout. You’ll learn two different methods of tempering chocolate, create and decorate three of your own chocolate tablets, and explore the art of painting Santa with cocoa butter.

To top it all off, take home your very own chocolate Santa, along with a 10% discount to enjoy while shopping in-store during the festival. This workshop is strictly for the grown ups! No need to share your delights with the kids.

events.id: ILLUMINATION Night Walk at Cobblestones-Cobblestones Heritage Village, 169 Main Street, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: ILLUMINATION Night Walk at Cobblestones
events.venue: Cobblestones Heritage Village, 169 Main Street, Greytown
events.long_description:
Step into a world of wonder with your family at our enchanting interactive light show walk, where the beautiful grounds of Cobblestones comes alive with dazzling lights, magical soundscapes, and surprises around every corner! Watch your child's face light up as they step on colour-changing pathways, and discover hidden stories told through beams of light.

Perfect for all ages, this unforgettable nighttime adventure combines the outdoors with cutting-edge technology to create a one-of-a-kind experience that sparks imagination, laughter, and memories. Don’t miss your chance to walk through the magic—grab your tickets now! Open for ten nights only, as part of the NZ Festival of Christmas.

Session One: Gates open 5.30pm. Last entry is 6pm
Session Two: Gates open 6.30pm. Last entry is 7pm
Session Three: Gates open 7.30pm. Last entry is 8pm

2026 Sparkle Pack Presale - on sale 1-7 April, exclusively for Festival Sparkle Pack holders
2026 General Public - on sale from 8 April

Illumination Night Glow is designed to go ahead in typical winter weather conditions. Please dress warmly and come prepared to enjoy the experience, rain or shine.

In the unlikely event that your selected session is cancelled due to extreme weather, your ticket will be transferred to the next available date, so you won’t miss out.

Sorry, no dogs allowed.

events.id: Canadian Film Screening - Tous Toqués!-Alliance Française Wellington,78 Victoria Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Canadian Film Screening - Tous Toqués!
events.venue: Alliance Française Wellington,78 Victoria Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Canadian Film Screening - Tous Toqués!
Celebrating Canada Day with a little taste of Québec!

About this Event

🎬 Join us for a special Canada Day screening of TOUS TOQUÉS! (2024), a warm and joyful Québécois comedy by Manon Briand that celebrates community, family, and the unexpected bonds that bring people together.
The story 👉 In a small village on the Quebec-US border, Sonia a no-nonsense border officer, reluctantly turns to Victor, a talented but down-on-his-luck chef she once refused entry to the country, when her daughter Lili-Beth signs up for a kids' cooking competition with little chance of winning. What starts as an unlikely alliance becomes something much bigger, uniting an entire village around what truly matters.
The trailer 👉 Watch here
This film is rated M – suitable for mature audiences (contains offensive language & adult references). The film is provided free of charge by the High Commission of Canada.

📅 Friday, 03 July 2026 - 6pm
📍 Alliance Française, 78 Victoria Street, Wellington
🎥 French VO with English Subtitles
🎟️ Free entry with koha donation

FEVER|DREAM
BCA presents FEVER|DREAM, a fresh programme of dance and music featuring four newly created works. This mixed bill takes audiences on a journey from intense, feverish moments to dreamlike states, reflecting on today's fast-paced life and the subconscious ways we process ourselves and our experiences within it.

Presented as part of the Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance.

events.id: FEVER|DREAM - Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance-Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: FEVER|DREAM - Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance
events.venue: Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
BCA presents FEVER|DREAM, a fresh programme of dance and music featuring four newly created works. This mixed bill takes audiences on a journey from intense, feverish moments to dreamlike states, reflecting on today's fast-paced life and the subconscious ways we process ourselves and our experiences within it.

FEVER
FEVER is a high-energy dance work exploring burnout, competitiveness, and the relentless pressure to keep going. Driven by fast-paced, dynamic movement, this piece captures the sensation of being trapped in cycles of productivity and competition — where stopping feels impossible.
PATHS CROSS
In the unpredictability of life, paths cross and intersect, but a singular focus may result in missed opportunities towards an intended destiny.
Through a singular focus, what might we overlook in the periphery? What alternative encounters might have veered us onto a different path and altered our course.
Chance encounters shape our lives, yet unrealised paths might have led to an entirely different existence.
We again introduce a work created by emerging student artists who will explore a ladder of words from FEVER to DREAM. Exploring ways to portray simple concepts from unrelated words through film and performance, graduate and graduating students from the New Zealand School of Dance will bring their individuality to the programme through exploring innovative ways to approach the composition of new movement patterns.
D)rea(m)lity (Dream Reality)
D)rea(m)lity (Dream Reality) is a reflection on our inner worlds, drawing on the interrelationship between a subconscious and conscious state of being. 
The audience is collectively absorbed into a sort of dreamscape, a dance with elusive ideals in the presence of pervasive shadows. Traversing the textures of the imagined and the real, this piece explores tensions between the fears that create voids in our lives, and the illusions that fill them.
Working closely with Jefferson Chen (Goodspace), this is an exchange of bites of movement that Zoe creates with her dancers and Jefferson’s musical response. Pōneke Contemporary Dance Festival will see the premiere of this work, one that will stay in our repertoire for future Festival Programmes, with the aim to present this in Auckland – Zoe’s home base.

Presented as part of the Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance.

About the artist:
BalletCollective Aotearoa is a project based dance collective based in Pōneke, Wellington. We are dedicated to exploring new ways to tell the stories of Aotearoa, pushing the boundaries of ballet technique within the contemporary dance space. Collaboration across the arts sector is central to our vision, ensuring that kiwi-trained dancers have the opportunity to develop their artistry at home. Under the BalletCollective Aotearoa banner, we provide a platform for dancers and choreographers to experiment, collaborate, and create new works in contemporary ballet. We aim to bring ballet to new audiences and foster a supportive, innovative environment for artistic growth within the genre.
This year we continue to build a repertoire of homegrown, locally developed contemporary ballet. We aim to present newly created choreography that is both abstract and virtuosic, to highlight the individual strengths of each dancer and their authenticity as contemporary artist’s trained in a ballet technique. We celebrate kiwi ingenuity and collaborate with other kiwi artists to bring you works that reflect the environment we inhabit at this time.

events.id: The Improv Lounge-Thistle Hall, 293 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Improv Lounge
events.venue: Thistle Hall, 293 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
“Delightful energy, alertness and vulnerability . . . fresh and invigorating” – Theatreview

Improvised scenes are inspired by the anecdotes friends share while sitting on a couch. Stories are told from a place that is truthful; the improvisors take the ideas places that are sometimes serious, sometimes whimsical, always playful. WIT's Improv Lounge structure originates with "The Living Room" developed in Chicago nearly forty years ago by Charna Halpern at the iO Theatre.

The Wellington Improvisation Troupe, aka WIT, is a member-based community theatre group and registered charity, teaching and performing in Wellington since 2003. Our theatre is made up on the spot, usually inspired by suggestions from the audience and each other. WIT players share a love of storytelling and work together as a team to inspire the audience, we’re sometimes serious, often hilarious and always totally unpredictable.

Pay-what-you-can tickets from $5, and door sales. Entrance to the Upstairs Hall is in Arthur St, or through the exhibition space entrance in Cuba Street.

events.id: The Tīwhas Present: Māreikura-Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Tīwhas Present: Māreikura
events.venue: Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join the Tīwhas for a shared supper after the show, included in your ticket price.

“Me aro koe ki te hā o Hine-ahu-one.”

This Matariki, The Tīwhas present: ‘MĀREIKURA’ – a bold celebration of Wāhine Māori. From the celestial realms to the frontlines of history, we honour the trailblazing women who have left a beauty mark on the face of te ao!

Join Ngāti Tīwha, as the Queens – Dame Jthan, Slay West, Pānia and Tina Coco Couture – ignite the stage with the fire of Mahuika in this soul-stirring night of concert!

Channeling the essence of our mothers, sisters, aunties and nans, we pay tribute to the women who have shaped our lives.

This is our love letter to the unbreakable spirit of wāhine.

Witness the majesty of MĀREIKURA.

Fri 3 Jul – Sat 11 Jul

Price: $75 includes shared supper after the show
Timings: Tues – Sat 7.00pm, Sunday 4pm
Preview Night: Thursday 2 July
Audience Care: Whānau friendly, parental supervision recommended. Some coarse language.
Duration: 90 minutes, no interval.

Cast and Crew:
Dame Jthan – Jthan Morgan
Slay West – Levi Waitere
Pānia – Raureti Ormond
Tina Coco Couture – Te Hāmama Hohua
Kree Matthews
Ngāti Tiwha: Hayden Taylor, Tom Knowles, Kate Anderson, Marshall Rankin, Hami Hawkins, Emma Katene

events.id: Sappho: A Deliciously Sapphic Cabaret-The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Sappho: A Deliciously Sapphic Cabaret
events.venue: The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Let’s go Lesbians!

This July get ready to U-Haul down to The Fringe Bar for Sappho, A Deliciously Sapphic Cabaret!

There will be talking, laughing, loving, breathing, and absolutely no ultimatums.

Our cast is in a league of their own and will have you yearning and flustered with gay panic. From poetry to burlesque, drag to comedy, our show will leave you weak at the knees and wet in your seats…

Whether you are bisexual, pansexual, demi, ace, or none of our business this stunning show will be sure to please. Bring your friends, your big gay crush, and leave the rest to the history books.

Don’t be a useless sapphic, join the Pink Pony Club and get your tickets on Eventfinda from as little as $20.

Venue, seating & show info

Cash only door sales are available if not sold out prior ($35.00 per ticket)
GA seating is unallocated, make sure to arrive early to nab a good vantage point.


Doors open one hour before showtime.
If you are part of a large group, contact us to save seats together, or you may be split up.
Latecomers may not be admitted, you may be asked to wait before finding your seat if a performance is underway.


The event space is located up 6 upstairs, with narrow walkways between seating. The regular seating are black metal folding chairs, please contact us to discuss alternatives if this isn't an option for you. We will do what we can to accommodate as many bodies and abilities as possible.


Trigger warnings will be given before every show and we have a zero tolerance policy for abusive behaviour.


You can contact us at willysmackntush@gmail.com

events.id: The Veils | Fragile World Tour | Wellington-meow nui wellington,9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: The Veils | Fragile World Tour | Wellington
events.venue: meow nui wellington,9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
The Veils | Fragile World Tour | Wellington
Following the critically acclaimed release of ‘Asphodels’ in 2025, The Veils quickly return with a bold and invigorated new album titled ‘Fragile World’, out June 19 on V2 Records. Today The Veils alongside Banished Music announce a 10-date New Zealand tour including 6 band shows featuring the closing show of Strange Universe: Winter in Auckland, and 4 solo performances.

“It feels right to start our Fragile World tour back home in dear Aotearoa. These are some of our favorite towns to play in on earth and we can’t think of a better way to dust off this moth-bitten black suit and hat and get the show on the road. Come along, be merry, and as always come up and say hi” says Finn Andrews.

Arriving just over a year after their last release, ‘Fragile World’ marks a striking shift in tone and energy for the band. Recorded live to tape in New Zealand by Paddy Hill, with production by Tom Healy (Tiny Ruins, The Chills, Folk Bitch Trio), ‘Fragile World’, captures The Veils in an urgent and instinctive mode.

The album’s title is both a reflection of the present moment - a time in which many institutions appear to be crumbling before our eyes - and a metaphor for the act of creation itself. The process of making music, Andrews notes, is a delicate and fragile undertaking where thousands of small decisions gradually coalesce into a finished whole.

“We went into the studio with a lot of songs, but very little idea of the arrangements or instrumentation. It was truly exciting having no idea what this record would sound like and only a few weeks to figure it out. It’s mostly Tom and I playing everything, with Joseph McCallum coming in at times. It was all very instinctual, quite full-on, and scary at times - but a good kind of scary, not scary like the real world out there.”
‍
With its immediacy, intensity, and emotional clarity, ‘Fragile World’ stands as one of The Veils’ most compelling releases to date.

events.id: The WBS presents: Andrew London and the Swing Voters CD Launch concert-Abandoned Taproom Petone, 38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: The WBS presents: Andrew London and the Swing Voters CD Launch concert
events.venue: Abandoned Taproom Petone, 38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
‘Set Me Up By The Bar’, the long-awaited new album from Andrew London and an army of collaborators loosely dubbed the Swing Voters, will be officially launched at the Wellington Bluegrass Society on 3rd July. The band will play all twelve tracks from the new album which includes several of Andrew’s signature satirical offerings about supermarket etiquette, unintentionally falling out with neighbours and the pitfalls of playing in restaurants; some poignant country/folk tunes, and a handful of rollicking western swing-inspired songs that give opportunity for jazz-style improvisation to pianist Wayne Mason, saxophone/clarinet player Oscar Laven and Andrew of course on his trademark 1959 Hofner guitar.

The Swing Voters are a high energy group who allow themselves licence to extend a song as the mood takes them. This is more than obvious on the ‘Set Me Up By The Bar’ album which features no fewer than four saxophonists, namely Nils Olsen, James Tait Jamieson, Brian Omundsen and Oscar Laven. Oscar doubles on clarinet, James on Irish whistle. Kirsten and Ryan hold down the rhythm section, Mason plays keys on several tracks although Andrew contributes piano to the title track. Pedal steel guitarist Bill Bassett contributes some colour to a western swing song, and the ever-ready Neil Billington provides raunchy harmonica to the rocky ‘Shamrock Hotel’. The album will be available on CD, or to stream or download digitally from bandcamp.com.

With his ever-reliable partner Kirsten on bass, and the metronomic Ryan Watson on drums, the band will also play a selection of jazz, blues and swing standards from their 2019 album ’Standard Deviations’. There will be foot-tapping at the very least.

Andrew and Kirsten are delighted to be launching this album at the Wellington Bluegrass Society.

events.id: WELCOMER 'CALENDARS' RELEASE TOUR W/ BELLADONNA & KING HIT-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: WELCOMER 'CALENDARS' RELEASE TOUR W/ BELLADONNA & KING HIT
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
Welcomer are embarking on their first nationwide headline tour to celebrate the release of their debut album Calendars, out June 12 via trusted DIY stalwarts, Papaiti Records.
Calendars follows a string of European shows in 2025 and a self titled EP which was described by Under the Radar as ‘melancholic splendour.. in swirls of harmonious goodness, each packed with presence and perfectly placed’. Single Nothing of You Remaining was described by Rolling Stone AU as “blending folk influences with strings for a warm, layered pop sound”.
https://welcomer.bandcamp.com/album/calendars
Joined in Pōneke by Belladonna and King Hit
Belladonna is the musical project of Bella Cook, a songwriter with a well-honed knack for musical storytelling, capturing the quirk and romance of everyday life. The debut Belladonna album, Arcus Way, was released in 2025 - a stunning collection of sharp and affecting songs with paired back arrangements.
https://belladonnamusicnz.bandcamp.com/album/arcus-way
King Hit is a Pōneke-based four piece melding the abrasiveness of shoe-gaze and punch of hardcore with the melancholy of Midwest-emo. Originally conceived as a three-piece by principal songwriter, guitarist and frontman Stefan Jenkins, King Hit cut their teeth in the Wellington underground scene for the first half of 2025 before adding Caitlin Gabb on guitar and vocals, joining bassist Felix Bird and drummer Sebastian Bell as architects of the band’s formidable wall of noise.
https://kinghit.bandcamp.com/track/sap
Tickets via UTR

Belladonna is the musical project of Bella Cook, a songwriter with a well-honed knack for musical storytelling, capturing the quirk and romance of everyday life. The debut Belladonna album, Arcus Way, was released in 2025 - a stunning collection of sharp and affecting songs with paired back arrangements.

https://belladonnamusicnz.bandcamp.com/album/arcus-way

King Hit is a Pōneke-based four piece melding the abrasiveness of shoe-gaze and punch of hardcore with the melancholy of Midwest-emo. Originally conceived as a three-piece by principal songwriter, guitarist and frontman Stefan Jenkins, King Hit cut their teeth in the Wellington underground scene for the first half of 2025 before adding Caitlin Gabb on guitar and vocals, joining bassist Felix Bird and drummer Sebastian Bell as architects of the band’s formidable wall of noise.

https://kinghit.bandcamp.com/track/sap
events.description:
Welcomer are embarking on their first nationwide headline tour to celebrate the release of their debut album Calendars, out June 12 via trusted DIY stalwarts, Papaiti Records.
Calendars follows a string of European shows in 2025 and a self titled EP which was described by Under the Radar as ‘melancholic splendour.. in swirls of harmonious goodness, each packed with presence and perfectly placed’.
Single Nothing of You Remaining was described by Rolling Stone AU as “blending folk influences with strings for a warm, layered pop sound”.
The debut Belladonna album, Arcus Way, was released in 2025 - a stunning collection of sharp and affecting songs with paired back arrangements.
https://kinghit.bandcamp.com/track/sap

events.name: The Bubble Spectacular
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Get ready for a different sort of pop sensation. Enter the effervescent world of The Bubble Spectacular. Take warm water, liquid soap, glycerin, and the creative talents of Dr Hubble. With those simple ingredients, Dr Hubble conjures up a fun-filled, bubbly world. Prepare to be amazed as the ultimate master of bubbles creates an amazing bubble world. There’ll be little bubbles, big bubbles, volcanic bubbles, tornado bubbles, square bubbles, and with a bit of bubbly-magic, you might even end up inside a bubble. 
It’s the perfect beginning to the school holidays — a bubble party filled with laughter and audience participation. As bubbles fly around the auditorium, get ready to pop, bounce, and boogie in an unbubb-levably fun way. Catch it before it floats away.

events.id: The Veils tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: The Veils tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Banished Music
Following the critically acclaimed release of Asphodels in 2025, local heroes The Veils return with a bold and invigorated new album, Fragile World. Released June 19 on V2 Records, the album marks a striking shift in tone and energy for the band, with fearless leader Finn Andrews reflecting on his tendency to make each album as a rebellion against his last. Recorded live to tape at Roundhead by Paddy Hill, with production by Tom Healy (Tiny Ruins, The Chills), Fragile World captures The Veils in an urgent and instinctive mode. With its immediacy, intensity, and emotional clarity, Fragile World stands as one of The Veils’ most compelling releases to date.

events.id: Stacking Rings - Taster-Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Stacking Rings - Taster
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Create your own ring stack from copper, brass, and sterling silver in this fun and rewarding jewellery workshop.

Guided by experienced and supportive tutors, you’ll be introduced to the fundamental skills of jewellery making while creating a small collection of rings to take home.

During this 3-hour class, you’ll learn how to measure your finger, cut metal to size, and add textures and patterns using hammers and punches. You’ll also be shown how to solder your rings with a jeweller’s torch, before finishing them with filing, sanding, and polishing techniques.

By the end of the session, you’ll have crafted a thick sterling silver ring and a stack of copper or brass rings, including two plain round wires to texture as you like, and a choice of decorative brass pearl wire or square wire.

This class is ideal for beginners, groups of friends, or visitors wanting a memorable creative experience. Many students find jewellery making a mindful activity, offering a chance to slow down, work with their hands, and enjoy the rhythm of making.

The class fee includes all materials and consumables, such as solder, sandpaper, and polishing materials. Extra silver and other metals will be available for purchase in the studio for those who would like to expand their collection or upgrade one of their brass or copper wires to a second silver one.

Children aged 12 and over are welcome. Anyone under 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.

events.id: Ruapehu Mountaineer-Plimmerton Railway Station,Plimmerton, New Zealand, Porirua-All Events In
events.name: Ruapehu Mountaineer
events.venue: Plimmerton Railway Station,Plimmerton, New Zealand, Porirua
events.long_description:
Ruapehu Mountaineer
After a sold-out run in February, the Ruapehu Mountaineer is back. If you’ve been thinking about it, now’s the time to lock in a seat.

This is a full-day steam-hauled rail journey through the Central North Island to Mt Ruapehu. You’ll travel in comfortable heritage carriages with large viewing windows, an open-air viewing platform, and a café carriage onboard. It’s an easy, relaxed way to take in some of the best scenery on the network, from the Kapiti Coast through the Manawatū and across the Mangaweka viaducts.

At Ohakune, you’ll transfer by coach up to Whakapapa Village, where you’ve got a few hours to do your own thing. Grab lunch, take in the alpine views, or add on the Sky Waka gondola for a trip up the mountain.

It’s a long but rewarding day, and a pretty unique way to experience Mt Ruapehu. Ideal for families, groups, or anyone keen on a proper steam day out.

⸻

FARES

Plimmerton, Paraparaumu
Adult $219.00 | Child $179.00

Ōtaki, Levin
Adult $199.00 | Child $159.00

Palmerston North, Feilding
Adult $179.00 | Child $139.00

Taihape (limited availability)
Adult $159.00 | Child $129.00

Sky Waka Gondola add-on
$29.00

⸻

TIMINGS (Northbound)
Plimmerton 5:20am
Paraparaumu 6:00am

Levin 7:05am
Palmerston North 8:10am
Feilding 8:30am

Ohakune 12:15pm

RETURN (Southbound)
National Park 4:10pm

Levin 9:20pm
Ōtaki 9:45pm
Paraparaumu 10:10pm
Plimmerton 10:45pm

⸻

Seats are limited and expected to sell quickly.

Operated by Mainline Steam Heritage Trust
