events.id: The WBS presents: Andrew London and the Swing Voters CD Launch concert-Abandoned Petone,38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt 5012, New Zealand, Lower Hutt-All Events In
events.name: The WBS presents: Andrew London and the Swing Voters CD Launch concert
events.venue: Abandoned Petone,38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt 5012, New Zealand, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
The WBS presents: Andrew London and the Swing Voters CD Launch concert
‘Set Me Up By The Bar’, the long-awaited new album from Andrew London and an army of collaborators loosely dubbed the Swing Voters, will be officially launched at the Wellington Bluegrass Society on 3rd July. The band will play all twelve tracks from the new album which includes several of Andrew’s signature satirical offerings about supermarket etiquette, unintentionally falling out with neighbours and the pitfalls of playing in restaurants; some poignant country/folk tunes, and a handful of rollicking western swing-inspired songs that give opportunity for jazz-style improvisation to pianist Wayne Mason, saxophone/clarinet player Oscar Laven and Andrew of course on his trademark 1959 Hofner guitar.

The Swing Voters are a high energy group who allow themselves licence to extend a song as the mood takes them. This is more than obvious on the ‘Set Me Up By The Bar’ album which features no fewer than four saxophonists, namely Nils Olsen, James Tait Jamieson, Brian Omundsen and Oscar Laven. Oscar doubles on clarinet, James on Irish whistle. Kirsten and Ryan hold down the rhythm section, Mason plays keys on several tracks although Andrew contributes piano to the title track. Pedal steel guitarist Bill Bassett contributes some colour to a western swing song, and the ever-ready Neil Billington provides raunchy harmonica to the rocky ‘Shamrock Hotel’. The album will be available on CD, or to stream or download digitally from bandcamp.com.

With his ever-reliable partner Kirsten on bass, and the metronomic Ryan Watson on drums, the band will also play a selection of jazz, blues and swing standards from their 2019 album ’Standard Deviations’. There will be foot-tapping at the very least.

Andrew and Kirsten are delighted to be launching this album at the Wellington Bluegrass Society.

Sugar Moon Andrew London Trio



DOORS:
7.00pm

SHOW STARTS:
8.00pm

ENTRY:
$30 / $25 members

please bring cash to pay for tickets,
or book prior to the day via email to:


Plenty of tasty treats available at the venue, come for a drink (or two) and dinner before the show starts.

Here is the link for the Petone menu, and to book for dinner:
www.abandonedbrewery.co.nz/taprooms#Petone

events.id: Art Therapy for Well-Being: A Canvas for Self-Discovery-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Art Therapy for Well-Being: A Canvas for Self-Discovery
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Art Therapy for Well-Being: A Canvas for Self-Discovery
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Sometimes, our minds get stuck in habitual thought loops—endlessly cycling through the same worries, fears, and anxieties without ever finding a breakthrough. When words fall short, art therapy offers a different language. By shifting from thinking to creating, we bypass the noise of everyday stress and tap into an intuitive visual language that can surprise us with fresh insights, new perspectives, and profound meaning.
This workshop is a gentle, immersive introduction to a pathway of self-restoration, self-compassion, and self-worth—the vital foundations for true self-healing.
What you will experience:
A Shift in Perspective: Break free from repetitive thinking by engaging the creative, intuitive side of your brain.
Tools for Daily Coping: Learn how the simple act of making art can become a sanctuary for processing stress and finding grounding.
A Pathway to Wholeness: Explore how visual expression can reveal hidden strengths and foster a deeper sense of self-care.
Important Note: You do absolutely not need to be an artist or have any artistic experience to attend. This day is about the process of expression, not the final product. While this workshop is not a substitute for clinical individual or group therapy, it is a powerful, supportive introduction to the healing power of the arts.
What to Bring:
A journal and a pen or pencil. All other creative materials will be provided.  Tea and coffee provided, please bring your lunch and snacks and  photo ID.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.name: Make your own Maramataka Journal
events.venue: Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Prepare for the new year by creating your own Maramataka journal in these drop-in craft sessions.
Maramataka, the Māori lunar calendar, uses an understanding of the moon phases and their influence on the natural world to guide planting, fishing, harvesting, and daily activities.
Using templates provided by artist Tayla Hartemink, whānau can create their own personalised journals to take home and guide them throughout the year.
Best suits ages 3-8 and whānau.
The sessions will be held in our activity room on the Mezzanine level of Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui.
See our other dates each Saturday in July.

events.name: Mental Health First Aid Aotearoa
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Mental Health First Aid Aotearoa
NZ Citizens and residents pay $219 with ID.  f you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $499 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register..
Mental Health First Aid is an evidence-based workshop which gives people the skills and confidence to have supportive conversations with their whānau, co-workers and community and help guide them to professional help if needed.
You will learn to recognise common mental health challenges, how to provide initial help to someone experiencing a mental health challenge using a practical, evidence-based action plan, and how to respond in a crisis situation. During the workshop you will have the opportunity to practice new skills in a safe environment.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Mental health challenges covered in the course include:
Depression
Anxiety
Psychosis
Substance use problems
Mental Health Crisis Covered:
Suicidal thoughts and behaviours
Non- Suicidal self-injury
Panic attack
Traumatic events
Severe effects from Alcohol or other substance use
Aggressive behaviours
People who attend Mental Health First Aid training can complete a short online assessment to be accredited as a Mental Health First Aider. You must attend the full two days to be able to do the online assessment for accreditation. The accreditation is valid for three years.
If you choose not to be accredited, you will receive a certificate of participation if you attend the full course. You will also receive a workbook as part of your course fee.
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.long_description:
Wētā, Tuna and Kārearea are mates of Gary the Glowworm, and are travelling to attend his rave in the cave birthday party. The friends face a number of trials on the road, and learn that despite their differences, they can achieve their goals by working together! A tale for kiwi kids, featuring iconic NZ creatures, landscapes and fresh waiata by NZ singer songwriter Benny Tipene.

Land of the Long, Long Drive is the Winner of Playmarket’s 2022 Plays for the Young (3-8 Year-old category), and nominated for the PAYPA production of the Year (2024). events.id: Shalakazap!-Tararua Tramping Club Clubrooms, 4 Moncrieff St, Mt Victoria, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Shalakazap!
events.venue: Tararua Tramping Club Clubrooms, 4 Moncrieff St, Mt Victoria, Wellington
events.long_description:
How will an apprentice MatheMagician and a Fire Chief from Kindle save the village from the Flabberfamish? With some MatheMagics of course. A Riotous comedy, with a transforming set and audience participation.

Written by the phenomenal Jenny Wake ONZM - a veteran of New Zealand Television and Theatre. In her television career, she wrote, directed and produced ‘Play School’, wrote for ‘Bumble’ and directed ‘What Now’. Jenny has travelled the word championing children's theatre. Currently the National Coordinator of Performing Arts and Young People of Aotearoa (PAYPA) and the International Association of Theatre for Children and Young People (ASSITEJ) New Zealand.

This play has had sell-out tours, and international presentations at Brisbane's Out of the Box festival and Vancouver-based Axis Theatre.

Actors:
Haydn Carter is a Graduate of Whitireia's Stage and Screen. He is one of our favourites at KidzStuff and has been in Badjelly The Witch, Kiwi Courage and Goldilocks. This time he is returning to KidzStuff as our apprentice MatheMagician, Tyro. Amalia Calder is a Kāpiti local and Mum. She has been a performer for 29 years and is the Creative Director of KidzStuff. Amalia has been in numerous KidzStuff shows and first performed Shalakazap! in the year 2000. It was her 1st ever tour, fresh out of drama school, but this time she will be playing Fire Chief Fleet. Adriana Calabrese returns to KidzStuff after her debut performance in Badjelly the Witch in 2025. Adriana is an Italian and Brazilian, Pōneke-based actor and singer. She was most recently seen at Circa Theatre in Sense and Sensibility and Lizzie the Musical as well as The Court Theatre in The Importance of Being Earnest.

Recommended for ages 4 +
Special $7 preview show on Saturday July 4th 2026!

events.name: Alexia Urban Winery Presents: Pies & Pinot
events.venue: Alexia Urban Winery, 197 West Street, Greytown
events.long_description:
Nothing beats a classic wine and pie combo, which is why Alexia is turning on the ovens and firing up the pie warmer for the return of the world-famous (in Greytown) Pies & Pinot!

It’s a simple concept - hot gourmet pies eaten out of paper bags matched to the different expressions of Pinot Noir made by Alexia – Méthode Traditionelle Rosé (pink fizz), Rosé, White Pinot Noir and of course our classic Pinot Noir!

The pie warmer will be fully stocked with the iconic Clareville Bakery pies, the fresh cream doughnuts will be lined up, and the paper bags will be ready.

Don’t mind the crumbs, it’s wine bar turned pie bar and we can’t wait! Come hungry, thirsty, and wearing stretchy pants for the ultimate warming winter winner!

No bookings required, just turn up. Parking onsite, or a 15 minute walk from Greytown centre.

events.id: A Change Is Coming Walk Tour-Ilott Green, Cnr Jervois Quay & Harris St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: A Change Is Coming Walk Tour
events.venue: Ilott Green, Cnr Jervois Quay & Harris St, Wellington
events.long_description:
2026 marks the 40th anniversary of the passing in 1986, of the Homosexual Law Reform Act. The legislation set out to decriminalise homosexual activity between consenting adults and make it illegal to discriminate against someone based on their sexual orientation.

But this wasn’t the first attempt at homosexual law reform. Join us on this special 90-minute free walk tour to celebrate the people and movements that fought for a brighter future for rainbow communities.

Our journey begins at the location of the first public meeting in New Zealand, advocating for homosexual law reform in 1967. This was two years before the Stonewall uprising in the U.S. which went on to ignite Gay Liberation around the world.

The tour ends at Parliament, where we honour the Members of Parliament who have championed equality over the last five decades.

Along the way, we’ll reflect on some of the difficult realities rainbow communities have faced, from the injustices of the legal system, to the stances of the medical profession, churches and the military. As the late activist Ralph Knowles recalls, “As far as medicine was concerned, it was sick. As far as the law was concerned, it was a crime. As far as the church was concerned, it was a sin.”

Though at times the stories are challenging, this walk tour is ultimately uplifting, as we pay tribute to our amazing rainbow kaumatua and allies who led the way.

COST: Free!

DATE/TIME: 1pm, Saturday 4 July 2026

PLEASE REGISTER AT https://walktours.nz/book/
(if there are no registrations the tour won't go ahead)

STARTS: meet at the rugby sculpture, Jack Ilott Green, corner of Harris Street & Jervois Quay (just by Civic Square)

ENDS: Parliament grounds

DURATION: 1 hour 30mins

ACCESSIBILITY: the tour is on flat public footpaths, with a light incline as we enter Parliament grounds. Tours are limited to approximately 20 walkers.

The legislation set out to decriminalise homosexual activity between consenting adults and make it illegal to discriminate against someone based on their sexual orientation.
Join us on this special 90-minute free walk tour to celebrate the people and movements that fought for a brighter future for rainbow communities.

As far as the church was concerned, it was a sin.” Though at times the stories are challenging, this walk tour is ultimately uplifting, as we pay tribute to our amazing rainbow kaumatua and allies who led the way.

events.long_description:
Do you delight in the magic of putting pen to paper, but wish your handwriting could dance a little more gracefully across the page?

Join us for a gentle introduction to the art of italic writing. Over two hours, Gareth Rapson will work through 10 short lessons with the aim of changing your handwriting into something beautiful, elegant and expressive. This gathering welcomes all ages and skill levels.

Please bring your favourite pens and a lined notebook or lined paper.

Handouts will be provided and we'll have some Esterbrook fountain pens for you to use on the day if you want to try out your new skills with a traditional pen.

About Your Teacher: Gareth Rapson is an ex English teacher with an interest in all aspects of creativity. Often found with a book, pen and a notebook or at the Bridge table!

Book your ticket today via https://villagebookshop.co.nz/products/workshop-introduction-to-italic-writing

events.id: Refugees (Japan) + Erika Grant & Winterwood-Futuna Chapel, Futuna Close, Friend Street, Karori, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Refugees (Japan) + Erika Grant & Winterwood
events.venue: Futuna Chapel, Futuna Close, Friend Street, Karori, Wellington
events.long_description:
Refugees is the duo of Mary Meacha Goldfish with her partner, Kawol SamarQandi. The project became a platform for expansive, contemplative soundscapes that blend analog electronics, acoustic instrumentation, and poetic minimalism.⁣⁣⁣
⁣⁣⁣
⁣Mary Meacha Goldfish is a multidisciplinary musician and sound artist known for her atmospheric compositions and her philosophy of “tinnitus music,” an approach that designs organic acoustics while listening attentively to the sound of silence.⁣⁣⁣
⁣⁣⁣
⁣Central to Goldfish’s artistic philosophy is what she calls “tinnitus music.” Rather than treating silence as absence, she approaches it as a living field of subtle vibration. By designing organic acoustics and listening carefully to internal and environmental sound, she creates works that hover between presence and disappearance. ⁣⁣⁣
Her compositions invite listeners to attune themselves to micro-sounds, resonances, and the fragile boundary between noise and quiet.⁣⁣⁣
⁣⁣⁣
Through a life shaped by kitchens, harbors, choirs, ateliers, and analog synthesizers, Mary Meacha Goldfish continues to craft immersive sonic spaces— music that listens as much as it speaks.⁣⁣⁣

Kawol Samarqandi crafts non-national, “poor” music—stripped-down improvisations built only from his faltering guitar and trembling voice. In this sparse terrain of sound, silence becomes structural, and fragility becomes strength.⁣⁣⁣

For this special event local musicians Erika Grant, and Winterwood will join Refugees for a collaborative improvisational performance.

Saturday 4th July⁣⁣⁣
Futuna Chapel
67 Futuna Close, Karori
2:00pm⁣⁣.
$20.00 waged. $10.00 unwaged

events.id: Matariki Night Market-193 Rapaura Road, Rapaura,193 Rapaura Rd, Rapaura, Blenheim 7273, New Zealand, Blenheim-All Events In
events.name: Matariki Night Market
events.venue: 193 Rapaura Road, Rapaura,193 Rapaura Rd, Rapaura, Blenheim 7273, New Zealand, Blenheim
events.long_description:
Matariki Night Market
The Matariki Night Market returns to the Vines Village on Saturday, July 4th with new times from 3pm to 7pm. Join us for a special evening under the stars as we come together to acknowledge Matariki, the Māori New Year. Circumnavigate the Village's beautifully lit gardens full of live music, food offerings, stargazing, fire performances, local producers and craft artisans, face painting, storytelling, and restorative sauna sessions.

This third annual event has a twist – Matariki Around the World! Inspired by the book A Cluster of Stars, A Cluster of Stories Matariki Around the World written by New Zealand astronomer Rangi Matamua and New Zealand journalist Miriama Kamo, learn the star cluster (aka star cluster M45)’s significance from various countries around the world, its different names, and its different stories (some dating thousands of years old). Copies of the book will be available for purchase at the Vines Café during the event.

Become a celestial explorer with the Omaka Observatory team stargazing with their mobile telescopes and digital 3D celestial exploration kits. Learn all about the star cluster from Omaka as well as fun facts throughout the Vines Village with a special treasure hunt. Will you be able to find them all?

Hear stories from the A Cluster of Stars, A Cluster of Stories Matariki Around the World book, presented by actress Amanda Tollemache throughout the evening, including The Seven Brothers or the Lost Boys, Pleiades or the Seven Sisters, Subaru and Amaterasu, Ngau Poñu o Gul Poñu or the Pile of Potatoes, Freya’s Hens, and of course New Zealand's Matariki and more! Common themes in many of the stories are seasonal changes, love, tragedy, and the everlasting beauty of the night sky.

Dance under the stars to musical duo Double Shot, who will be performing all evening long with their upbeat, harmonious covers.

Gaze at the stars in the comfort of the Nordic Sauna, a mobile wood-fired sauna inspired by Nordic tradition. Nordic Sauna focuses on the three essentials of a good sauna: heat, steam and ventilation, creating a well-balanced experience. Their authentic wood-fired sauna delivers deep, steady heat, with a guaranteed temperature of 85–95°C every session. Traditional “leil”, created by pouring water over hot stones, brings the heat to life, adding softness, depth and intensity to each round. To avoid disappointment, book directly with Nordic Sauna for this event.

Explore Marlborough is working on transport options to make the event even more accessible and will post how to book this once details are confirmed.

All are welcome to this free event and proudly supporting our local Marlborough community. This is a time for remembrance, connection, and looking ahead with intention. Reflect on those who have passed, gather with friends and whānau, and celebrate the season alongside our Marlborough community.

It will be a stellar encounter around every Village corner!

*Please note that in case of bad weather, this event will be postponed to Saturday, July 11th with the same time.

Learn all about the star cluster from Omaka as well as fun facts throughout the Vines Village with a special treasure hunt.

events.id: Not a Figure of Speech (Side B) - a Screening By Grace Samboh-CIRCUIT, Level 2, 27 Dixon Street, Wellington, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Not a Figure of Speech (Side B) - a Screening By Grace Samboh
events.venue: CIRCUIT, Level 2, 27 Dixon Street, Wellington, Wellington
events.long_description:
Not a figure of speech is a screening of artist videos made in the Czech Republic, Japan, and Indonesia between 2015 and 2026. The programme brings together works encountered through collaborations, exhibitions, research projects, and long-term conversations.
Not a figure of speech is curated and presented by Grace Samboh, a writer, researcher, and exhibition-maker living and working in Yogyakarta, Jakarta, and wherever her friends are. Her work develops through long-term conversations with artists, researchers, educators, and cultural practitioners, often returning to questions of history, entanglements, care, and being together. Grace's visit to Aotearoa is presented by CIRCUIT and Gus Fisher with the support of the Asia New Zealand Foundation. Grace describes the programme:
"Not a figure of speech takes the form of a mixtape. Not because the works gathered here arrive at the same conclusions, nor because they can be reduced to a single theme, but because I have come to know them through time, repetition, and return. Some emerged through collaborations, commissions, research projects, and long conversations. Others I have encountered repeatedly through exhibitions, screenings, and ongoing exchanges with artists whose practices continue to accompany my own thinking. In some instances, I also appear within the works themselves. This is therefore not a survey assembled from a distance, but a temporary arrangement of works and relations that have remained useful to think with.
Across the two sides of this mixtape, questions recur in different forms. How does a concern become shared? How does discrimination become visible? What does it mean to remain in relation across differences of experience, authority, generation, or history? At what point does observation become participation? What remains to be done with what one now knows? The mixtape does not propose answers to these questions. Nor does it suggest that the works agree with one another. Instead, it follows a series of echoes and returns. One work leaves something unresolved for another to pick up. Certain questions disappear only to re-emerge later in a different form. Even the pauses between works are part of this choreography, allowing an encounter to linger before the next one begins. A mixtape assumes that sequence matters. One thing follows another, changing how both are experienced. What emerges from that sequence, however, remains open.
Each screening  is followed by a conversation between Grace Samboh and Mark Williams (CIRCUIT).
Side B - CIRCUIT, Wellington 3pm Saturday 3 July
●    Anchan/Anna Daučíková and Tamarra, Talking to you (2021)
●    Nadiah Bamadhaj, Terpesona dengan kegelisahan [Charmed by anxiety] (2022)
●    Griya Seni Hj. Kustiyah Edhi Sunarso, Hyphen—, Tom Nicholson with Ary "Jimged" Sendy, Aufa Ariaputra, Nasikin, Omar Aryarindra, & Munadi Alianto (Xonad), Sesudah banjir itu: No. 44 [When the flood is over: No. 44] (2026)
Image: Anchan/Anna Daučíková and Tamarra, Talking to you (2021)

Join us for a fun adults’ painting class at The Bond Store – a vibrant distillery in the heart of Paraparaumu, known for its award-winning New Zealand craft spirits. No painting experience is needed – your friendly artist will guide you step by step in a relaxed, judgement-free environment.

This event is all about creativity, conversation, and having a great time while unleashing your inner artist.

Your ticket includes a gin tasting tray, giving you the chance to sample some of The Bond Store’s specialty gins while you paint.

Whether you’re coming with friends, planning a date night, or flying solo, this is your chance to unwind, get creative, and experience the joy of painting with Paintvine.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website ⇒ paintvine.co.nz

Whether you’re coming with friends, planning a date night, or flying solo, this is your chance to unwind, get creative, and experience the joy of painting with Paintvine.
Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!
All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass).
At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

events.id: Tempo Schmempo at Cuba St Tavern: Vol II-Cuba St Tavern,172 Cuba Street Te Aro, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Tempo Schmempo at Cuba St Tavern: Vol II
events.venue: Cuba St Tavern,172 Cuba Street Te Aro, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Tempo Schmempo at Cuba St Tavern: Vol II
Following a sold-out debut that packed Cuba Street Tavern to the rafters, Tempo Schmempo returns this July with a bigger lineup, longer hours, and another celebration of Aotearoa's thriving underground dance music community.

Born on East London's dancefloors and forged across New Zealand's club and festival circuit, Tempo Schmempo has found another new home in Te Whanganui-a-Tara. This second instalment of the bi-monthly series takes things up a notch, stretching from 4pm through until 1am and featuring six acts across an extended journey of house, disco, Balearic sounds, leftfield grooves and dancefloor oddities.

This time, we're proud to present the delectable prix fixe extravaganza of Disco Drongos, B-Lo and Staciez Mum supported by Tempo Schmempo's founding fathers 22Tango, Everybody, and Vino Paradiso for your extended listening pleasure.

And what's more, we've once again teamed up with Duncan's Brewing Co. to pour the official Tempo Schmempo beer on tap for the night, creating the perfect accompaniment to nine hours of musical exploration and dancefloor mischief.

This one is going to be special. Tell your friends, book those tickets and we'll see you on the dancefloor.

Disco Drongos
B-Lo
Staciez Mum
Everybody
22Tango
Vino Paradiso

Saturday 4 July
4:00pm – 1:00am
Cuba Street Tavern

events.long_description:
Don't miss the Kāpiti Choral Festival Showcase 2026!

For the third time, the Kāpiti Choral Festival has returned, and what better way to showcase all of our amazing coastal talent than putting them all on the same bill! events.long_description:
The Warratahs return to The Thistle Inn this July, celebrating the release of their new single Quicksilver Days.

One of New Zealand's most beloved country-rock bands, The Warratahs have spent decades soundtracking road trips, late nights and dance floors across Aotearoa. Led by Barry Saunders, the band continues to deliver the songs and live performances that have made them a staple of the New Zealand music scene.

Joining them on the night is Richard Reugg.

Make a night of it with our Dinner & Show option, including a two-course dinner in one of The Thistle Inn's dining rooms before the performance. Dinner will be served from 6:30pm, before guests move through to the Main Bar for the show.

Saturday 4 July
Dinner: 6:30pm
Show Doors: 8:30pm

Ticket Options:
Early Bird Show Only - $40
Final Release Show Only - $45
Dinner & Show - $89

Door sales $50, subject to availability.

events.name: Dance On Screen Film Competition - Screening & Awards Night
events.venue: Hannah Playhouse,12 Cambridge Ter, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Dance On Screen Film Competition - Screening & Awards Night
New to this year’s Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance: introducing the Dance on Screen Film Competition!

Come explore the art of dance through the lens and on the big screen. Participants will receive the creative prompts on the first day of the Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance and will have the week of the festival to create their cinematic work of art. All films will be screened at the festival’s closing night and awards ceremony on Saturday 4 July at The Hannah Playhouse, Wellington. Purchase tickets to the screening and awards night here. Want to participate in the competition? Register a team before 28th June through the festival website.

Judges and prizes to be announced!

Presented as part of the Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance

events.name: Dance on Screen Film Competition - Screening & Awards
events.venue: Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
New to this year’s Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance: introducing the Dance on Screen Film Competition!

Come explore the art of dance through the lens and on the big screen. Participants will receive the creative prompts on the first day of the Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance and will have the week of the festival to create their cinematic work of art. All films will be screened at the festival’s closing night and awards ceremony on Saturday 4 July at The Hannah Playhouse, Wellington. Purchase tickets to the screening and awards night here. Want to participate in the competition? Register a team before 28th June through the festival website.

COMPETITION CATEGORIES
Lo-fi (filmed from a phone or similar low budget technology)
Hi-fi (filmed with professional film gear)


NON-COMPETITIVE CATEGORIES
Pre-existing dance films (any films created before the week of the festival do not qualify to be judged but will be screened)
International dance films (to qualify for judging you must live or have trained in NZ)

Judges and prizes to be announced!

Presented as part of the Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance - check out the rest of the programme at the website above.

Produced by Footnote New Zealand Dance, in collaboration with the Hannah Playhouse and support from Wellington City Council.

events.id: Guy Williams Live in Wellington-Tararua Tramping Club Clubrooms, 4 Moncrieff St, Mt Victoria, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Guy Williams Live in Wellington
events.venue: Tararua Tramping Club Clubrooms, 4 Moncrieff St, Mt Victoria, Wellington
events.long_description:
Guy Williams returns to Wellington with his new show:

"Rich People Are Stealing From You And Blaming Brown People And Trans People And Some People Believe Them Aaahhhhhh!"

Probably shouldn’t have given it all away in the title. Like most people I’ve been trying to figure out how the worst dickheads have taken over everything with their insane bullshit. So I’ve read three books (mostly) (one was a graphic novel) and listened to five audio books (AT NORMAL SPEED) to try and figure out WHAT THE F**K IS GOING ON?

Credits to impress my mum:
New Zealand Today
Taskmaster NZ
Guy Montgomery’s GuyMont Spelling Bee

★★★★ - The Age
★★★★ - Chortle
★★★★ - The Skinny
“Riotously entertaining!” ★★★★ The Student UK
“If you want to spend an hour laughing out your worries and frustrations, I can’t think of a better way to do it.” squirrelcomedy.com

events.venue: Copthorne Hotel & Resort Solway Park, 246 High St, Solway, Masterton
events.long_description:
The Queens are coming to Masterton for one night only! (Ticket price includes delicious three-course dinner and shows!)

Join award winning Drag Queen comedian Anita Wigl'it ('RuPaul's Drag Race') and her two Drag Queen friends; Betty and Nivalia for a night of dinner, drag and hilarity!

Over three fabulous hours you will enjoy a delicious three-course dinner (menu below) and be entertained by a full length, drag show spectacular in two Acts between courses. Expect comedy, sequins, stilettos and the most fabulous night that Masterton has ever seen!

Doors 6pm
Event / Dinner starts from 7pm

Seating : Please note that tables are in groups of 10 and that you may be seated at a combined table. You will be emailed closer to the event date to confirm the size of your party for seating.

Menu:
On the table on arrival:
Starter - Focaccia with Roasted Garlic Butter

Mains (Alternate serve):
Lamb Rump - Tender lamb rump, served with kumara mash, roasted vegetable medley, chargrilled seasonal greens, rosemary oil and rich red wine Ju GF

Crispy Skin Salmon - Flame-grilled salmon fillet served atop a vibrant quinoa salad with cherry tomatoes, cucumber, red onion, Kalamata olives, crumbled feta, finished with fresh parsley and zesty lemon-olives oil dressing GF DF

Creamy Cashew and Coconut Curry - Charred seasonal vegetables in a spiced curry, finished with toasted cashews, served with fragrant Jasmin rice. GF DF VG

Dessert:
Manuka Honey Creame Brulee - Silky Manuka honey custard with a caramelised sugar top, finished with house-made honeycomb, freeze dried raspberries and house made shortbread GF

Banoffee Cup - Layers of caramel , fresh banana, whipped cream and finished with brandy snap crumble GF DF VG

Chocolate Hazelnut Pudding - warm chocolate fudge hazelnut pudding that melts into own sauce served with vanilla beans ice cream N

events.name: NYO La Mer
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, 111 Wakefield Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Jack Bewley New Commission (World Premiere)
Britten Four Sea Interludes From Peter Grimes
Michael-Thomas Foumai Raise Hawaiki: Kealaikahiki (NZ Premiere)
Debussy La Mer

In 2026, the NZSO National Youth Orchestra brings together Aotearoa New Zealand’s best young musicians to perform Britten’s breathtaking Four Sea Interludes; Raise Hawaiki: Kealaikahiki, commemorating the trans-Pacific voyage of the Hōkūle’a waka from Hawai’i to Tahiti; a new commission by the 2026 NYO Composer-in-Residence Jack Bewley; and Debussy’s La Mer, one of the most influential masterpieces of the 20th century.

NZSO Members receive two complimentary tickets to the NYO concert of their choice.

events.name: Raw Collective: In The Clouds - Single Release Party
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
After a long-awaited return, Wellington's own Raw Collective are celebrating the release of their new single In The Clouds with a special hometown show at Rogue & Vagabond.

Known for blending hip-hop, funk, soul and jazz into a powerful live experience, Raw Collective bring together heavyweight musicians, big brass, smooth vocals and honest lyricism for a night of energy, groove and good vibes.

Join us as we launch the first chapter of a new era for the band, performing the new single alongside fan favourites and deep cuts from the catalogue.

✨ Special guests to be announced.

Come celebrate with us, raise a glass, and help us send Head In The Clouds out into the world.

events.id: The Hutt Valley Raw Comedy Quest-Abandoned Taproom Petone, 38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: The Hutt Valley Raw Comedy Quest
events.venue: Abandoned Taproom Petone, 38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
The capital's premier comedy competition. Witness the rising stars in stand-up comedy before they hit the big time.

The stepping stone for many New Zealand comedians, the Raw Comedy Quest has brought forth new comedy talent from across the country, with heats in Christchurch, Wellington, Palmerston North and Auckland.

From six heats, the top 24 go to the semifinals, and the top 10 to the Wellington final. 2024 Wellington finalist Chen Wang came runner up in the 2024 National Raw Comedy Quest, and Lydia Sainsbury runner up in 2025, can the capital win it this time?

Featuring guest MC 2025 Billy T Award nominee Jak Darling

Thanks to support from the Hutt City Council, Creative Communities scheme, Kiwi Gaming Foundation, and the Humorous Arts Trust

To enter email contact@humorous.co.nz, all you need is 6min of original material, and the guts to give it a go!

The stepping stone for many New Zealand comedians, the Raw Comedy Quest has brought forth new comedy talent from across the country, with heats in Christchurch, Wellington, Palmerston North and Auckland.
From six heats, the top 24 go to the semifinals, and the top 10 to the Wellington final.
2024 Wellington finalist Chen Wang came runner up in the 2024 National Raw Comedy Quest, and Lydia Sainsbury runner up in 2025, can the capital win it this time?

events.id: The Veils - Fragile World w/ Jazmine Mary-Meow Nui, 92 Vivian Street, Te Aro, Wellington, 6011, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Veils - Fragile World w/ Jazmine Mary
events.venue: Meow Nui, 92 Vivian Street, Te Aro, Wellington, 6011, Wellington
events.long_description:
Following the critically acclaimed release of ‘Asphodels’ in 2025, The Veils quickly return with a bold and invigorated new album titled ‘Fragile World’, out June 19 on V2 Records. Today, The Veils alongside Banished Music announce a 10-date New Zealand tour including 6 band shows featuring the closing show of Strange Universe: Winter in Auckland, and 4 solo performances.

“It feels right to start our Fragile World tour back home in dear Aotearoa. These are some of our favourite towns to play in on earth, and we can’t think of a better way to dust off this moth-bitten black suit and hat and get the show on the road. Come along, be merry, and as always, come up and say hi,” says Finn Andrews.

Arriving just over a year after their last release, ‘Fragile World’ marks a striking shift in tone and energy for the band. Recorded live to tape in New Zealand by Paddy Hill, with production by Tom Healy (Tiny Ruins, The Chills, Folk Bitch Trio), ‘Fragile World’ captures The Veils in an urgent and instinctive mode.

The album’s title is both a reflection of the present moment - a time in which many institutions appear to be crumbling before our eyes - and a metaphor for the act of creation itself. The process of making music, Andrews notes, is a delicate and fragile undertaking where thousands of small decisions gradually coalesce into a finished whole.

“We went into the studio with a lot of songs, but very little idea of the arrangements or instrumentation. It was truly exciting, having no idea what this record would sound like and only a few weeks to figure it out. It’s mostly Tom and me playing everything, with Joseph McCallum coming in at times. It was all very instinctual, quite full-on, and scary at times - but a good kind of scary, not scary like the real world out there.”
‍
With its immediacy, intensity, and emotional clarity, ‘Fragile World’ stands as one of The Veils’ most compelling releases to date.
events.description:
Following the critically acclaimed release of ‘Asphodels’ in 2025, The Veils quickly return with a bold and invigorated new album titled ‘Fragile World’, out June 19 on V2 Records.

These are some of our favourite towns to play in on earth, and we can’t think of a better way to dust off this moth-bitten black suit and hat and get the show on the road.
Come along, be merry, and as always, come up and say hi,” says Finn Andrews.

events.id: DJ Craze (US)-Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: DJ Craze (US)
events.venue: Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Five-time DMC World DJ Championships champion DJ Craze is a true heavyweight of global turntablism, renowned for pushing the boundaries of drum & bass, hip hop and bass music. From pioneering the presence of drum & bass on the DMC stage to delivering electrifying sets worldwide, Craze’s technical precision and fearless genre-blending have cemented his reputation as one of the most influential DJs of his generation.

Founder of Slow Roast Records alongside Kill the Noise, Craze has played a key role in shaping the US bass music landscape, while touring globally from underground clubs to major festivals. His sets are a masterclass in energy and control - seamlessly weaving rap, dubstep, Miami bass and drum & bass into a high-impact dancefloor experience.

This is more than a DJ set - it’s a chance to witness a battle champion and crowd-destroyer at the top of his game. Don’t miss one of the most technically gifted and influential DJs to ever touch the decks - secure your tickets now.

Support EThixx

Support Ethixx

For the past ten years, Ethixx has operated in the corners of New Zealand’s electronic underground, crafting a style that refuses to be easily categorised. An enigmatic presence behind the decks, he treats genres as fluid boundaries, blending everything from deep tech rollers to low-end club weight with patient, clinical precision. Preferring to let the music speak for itself, Ethixx builds an atmosphere rather than just playing a set -making him the ideal guide to take the crowd into the shadows before DJ Craze takes over.

Miso making Workshop Sat 4th July 2026
Miso making Workshop with Yuko and Anri

Japanese comfort food, Miso, is tasty, nutritious, and healthy...Why don't you make your own??

Sat 4th July 2026 12pm-1pm
Crofton Downs Primary School Hall, Wellington ( https://maps.app.goo.gl/bbQNHJHRGo4c6VGK7)
300g $69/p, 600g $99/p. $9 Off for repeaters or more than two people. BYO clean jar(s), mask and apron.

Making two jars you can enjoy different stages of fermentation.

*Please fill in the form to reserve your space. Applications close once the spaces filled up.
*Your reservation will be confirmed once your payment processed.
*Please understand the fee is basically non-refundable due to the arrangement of raw ingredients.

Looking forward to hearing from you...!

events.id: Winter Solstice Bike Rave-Te Ara Tupua Pathway,Lower Hutt, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Winter Solstice Bike Rave
events.venue: Te Ara Tupua Pathway,Lower Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Winter Solstice Bike Rave
Main Event

When: Saturday 20 June, meet from 5.30pm
Where: Te Ara Tupua, Petone end
Ride departs: 6pm, finishing at Glover Park for a beverage and a yarn.

It’s time to light up Te Ara Tupua.

Join us for a winter Bike Rave along Te Ara Tupua as we roll from Petone back into the city in a glorious movement of lights, good tunes, rave-adjacent outfits and weird wheeled creations.

Main meet-up will be at the Petone end of Te Ara Tupua from 5.30pm, then depart at 6pm for a cruise southbound along Te Ara Tupua back toward Wellington, finishing at Glover Park.
.
.
.
Warm-Up Group Ride

When: 4pm
Where: HNRY Stadium forecourt
Departs: 4.30pm for Petone

Got the fam, or simply want to get the legs moving early? Join the warm-up roll-in from the HNRY Stadium forecourt. We’ll ease into the evening, warm up the dance vibes with the little ones, and bring out the beats before heading towards Petone.

This part of the ride is family friendly. For anyone riding one-way with kids, trains run back toward town every 30 minutes from Petone.
.
.
.
What to Bring
• Lights!
• Optional - Speakers with an aux port. We’ll have FM receivers if you want to tune in and be part of the noise.
• Warm layers
• A bike or other wheeled creation
• Your finest, weirdest, most rave-adjacent outfit

The Warratahs | Thistle Inn, Wellington
The Warratahs return to The Thistle Inn this July, celebrating the release of their new single Quicksilver Days.

One of New Zealand's most beloved country-rock bands, The Warratahs have spent decades soundtracking road trips, late nights and dance floors across Aotearoa. Led by Barry Saunders, the band continues to deliver the songs and live performances that have made them a staple of the New Zealand music scene.

Joining them on the night is Richard Reugg.

Make a night of it with our Dinner & Show option, including a two-course dinner in one of The Thistle Inn's dining rooms before the performance. Dinner will be served from 6:30pm, before guests move through to the Main Bar for the show.

Dinner: 6:30pm
Show Doors: 8:30pm

events.long_description:
In July 2026, New Zealand marks 40 years since the passing of the Homosexual Law Reform Act 1986, a landmark change that decriminalised sex between consenting adult men and helped reshape the lives of rainbow communities across the country.

Join Walk Tours NZ and Wellington City Libraries for A Change Is Coming: Journey to Homosexual Law Reform, a powerful one-hour presentation tracing the long and difficult road to reform, presented at Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui Central Library.

Blending live storytelling, historic images, archival audio and personal memories, the presentation begins in 1967 with the first public meeting in New Zealand calling for homosexual law reform, held in what is now City Gallery Wellington. From there, it follows some of the social, political and personal struggles that led to the historic law change of 9 July 1986.
The presentation explores the realities many rainbow people faced before reform, including criminalisation, discrimination, police surveillance, medical stigma, religious condemnation and social exclusion.

At times confronting, but ultimately uplifting, A Change Is Coming is a celebration of resilience, protest, courage and community. It honours the activists, allies and rainbow communities whose determination helped transform New Zealand society, and whose legacy continues to shape our future.

The cooler, crisp days make it perfect to sink into our deep sofas and large open fireplaces as you relax with friends and family soaking in the atmosphere of a mid-winters Christmas feast at Wharekauhau Country Estate. Chef has recreated some of traditional Christmas dishes using the the seasonal produce for the estates garden.

events.id: Blue Sphere Services Tours-GreyFriars Motel, 138 Main Street, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Blue Sphere Services Tours
events.venue: GreyFriars Motel, 138 Main Street, Greytown
events.long_description:
Discover the Best of Wairarapa with Blue Sphere Tours

Blue Sphere Tours invites travellers to experience the charm, beauty, and hidden treasures of Greytown and the wider Wairarapa region through expertly crafted, fully hosted tours designed for comfort, enjoyment, and unforgettable memories.

From the moment guests are collected from their doorstep, every detail is taken care of. In partnership with Capital Personalised Tours, Blue Sphere Tours provides a seamless travel experience, transporting guests to some of the region’s most spectacular destinations before returning them safely home at the end of their journey.

Each tour has been thoughtfully designed to showcase the very best that South Wairarapa and the wider region have to offer. Travellers can immerse themselves in stunning landscapes, fascinating history, vibrant local culture, award-winning vineyards, boutique shopping, exceptional dining, and unique attractions that make Wairarapa one of New Zealand’s most captivating destinations.

A highlight of the tour is attendance at Greytown's spectacular Festival of Christmas Illumination, where the village is transformed into a magical wonderland of dazzling lights, festive displays, and enchanting winter atmosphere. Guests will also enjoy entry to the highly sought-after White Swan Snow Supper, an unforgettable evening of fine dining, festive hospitality, and seasonal celebration aboard one of Wairarapa's most iconic venues. Both Illumination tickets and White Swan Snow Supper tickets are included as part of the tour package.

Blue Sphere Tours specialises in creating memorable experiences for mature travellers, combining leisurely exploration with carefully selected activities and attractions. Every itinerary is meticulously planned, allowing guests to simply relax, enjoy the company of fellow travellers, and look forward to each new discovery.

Guests will stay at Greyfriars Motel, ideally located in the heart of charming Greytown village. With boutique shops, cafés, restaurants, and bars just a short stroll away, it provides the perfect base from which to explore the region. Accommodation options include single, twin-share, and triple-share rooms, with accommodation, transport, entry fees, Illumination tickets, White Swan Snow Supper tickets, and most meals included in the tour price.

Complimentary Door-to-Door Service

One of the many highlights of travelling with Blue Sphere Tours is the complimentary pick-up and drop-off service. Guests can enjoy the convenience of being collected directly from home and transported in comfort throughout their journey—making the entire experience truly stress-free from beginning to end.

Book Your Winter Wairarapa Escape

Whether travelling solo, with friends, or as part of a group, Blue Sphere Tours offers a welcoming and enriching way to discover one of New Zealand’s most beautiful regions while enjoying two of the season's most memorable events.

To reserve a place on an upcoming tour, contact Blue Sphere Tours on 0800 304 340 or email info@greyfriars.co.nz. A non-refundable deposit secures a booking, with full payment required two weeks before departure. Once payment is received, guests will be provided with a GST receipt, a detailed itinerary, and all the information needed for a memorable Wairarapa adventure.

Pack your bags, leave the planning behind, and experience the magic of Greytown's winter celebrations with Blue Sphere Tours.

events.id: Natural Textured Rings - Taster class-Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Natural Textured Rings - Taster class
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Make your own personalised ring - a gift for yourself or someone special! :-)

Learn the basic technique first in copper or brass, and then craft your final piece in silver!

Over this taster class, you’ll be guided through the process of heating and texturing metal, cutting and shaping with traditional jewellery tools. Use feathers, leaves, or fabrics to create a unique texture for a beautiful ring!

Learn to measure your finger, cut metal to length and texture it without rolling mill, hammers and punches. Then use a jewellers torch, sandpaper and polishing, and your beautiful new ring will be ready to wear.

This 3-hour class is ideal for beginners, groups of friends and visitors to the city.

Included in the class fee are copper and brass for practising on, a sterling silver ring strip and all consumables such as sandpaper, solder and chemicals. Extra metals are available for purchase in the studio.

Kids aged 12 (or so) are welcome to attend the class, but any person under the age of 16 must be accompanied by adults.

events.id: Titanium Earrings-Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Titanium Earrings
events.venue: Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington
events.long_description:
In this super-fast 3-hour class, you will make a pair of earrings fit for a superhero!

Titanium is an amazingly light metal that can transform from a subtle grey to a whole range of super vibrant, or sleek and pale colours, from gold, pink, purple or deep blue, to light blue, palest green and subtle grey.

You’ll begin by practising fundamental metalworking techniques on copper, then experiment with flame-colouring titanium to understand how heat transforms its surface. With these skills, you’ll craft your own earrings, exploring texture and colour.

We’ll use a rolling mill to imprint natural or abstract patterns into the titanium sheet, shaping pieces into clean geometric forms—rectangles, diamonds, triangles, or squares. You can also experiment with layering elements for added depth and variation.

Finish your design with a choice of comfortable hooks or hoops, and leave with a unique, wearable piece.

Titanium is hypoallergenic, and your findings options include surgical stainless steel and gold-plated stainless steel.

You should take this class:
- If you like large earrings that are bold and badass
- If you like earrings to be feather light
- If you like colourful earrings that will really make a statement
- If you like pastel blues and simple shapes
- If you love earrings and want to try something new
- If you’re curious to learn more about using Titanium in your jewellery making - this is an ideal way to dip your toes.

Kids aged 12 (or so) are welcome to attend the class, but an enrolled parent or caregiver must accompany anyone under the age of 16

events.id: Sunday Jazz - Andy Sugg-The Rogue & Vagabond-Rogue & Vagabond
events.name: Sunday Jazz - Andy Sugg
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
The Rogue's regular Sunday afternoon jazz. Funky, jazzy, soulful jazz. Beer, hangs, and jazz, on a Sunday, perfect!

This week featuring Australian saxophonist Andy Sugg with his quartet

Leading Australian sax player, Andy Sugg, is touring NZ in July and he’s assembled a stellar line-up of NZ jazzers—Roger Mannins (tenor sax), Umar Zakaria (bass) and Mark Lockett (drums)—to play with. This will be the first time this formation has played together.



Also premiering are a collection of Andy’s original tunes, which together with some choice standards, make great vehicles for improvisation in the best tradition of contemporary jazz.



Andy and Roger’s collaboration goes back a long way, starting with the powerhouse three-sax group TTTenor (which also included John Mackey) and continuing more recently with the band led by American bass heavyweight, Gene Perla. Andy and Roger share great respect and admiration for each other’s playing. On stage they challenge and spur each other on. It makes for fiery music-making.”

Music from 5pm, FREE ENTRY

events.id: Wairarapa Cinémathèque presents THE GODFATHER (1972)-Regent 3 Cinemas, 230 Queen Street, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: Wairarapa Cinémathèque presents THE GODFATHER (1972)
events.venue: Regent 3 Cinemas, 230 Queen Street, Masterton
events.long_description:
The Godfather (1972)
Dir. Francis Ford Coppola | USA | 2h 57m | Crime, Drama

Francis Ford Coppola’s masterwork traces the rise and transformation of the Corleone family, balancing intimate family drama with the brutal realities of organised crime. Featuring iconic performances by Marlon Brando and Al Pacino, The Godfather remains one of the most influential films in cinema history. This film screening is also a homage to the amazing American actress, Diane Keaton.

This screening is presented as part of the programme for the Wairarapa Cinémathèque 2026.

Wairarapa Cinémathèque is dedicated to celebrating film as part of our artistic and cultural heritage. We value the communal experience of cinema, bringing people together through curated screenings, discussions, and the sharing of films of significance. Your membership ensures access to this shared experience, while providing benefits such as exclusive screenings and partner discounts.

events.long_description:
Dusty Sunday Social Club
Dusty Sunday Social Club is your new favourite way to close out the weekend - a soothingly sonic safe space for those spirited souls who've pushed the boat out slightly further than expected, only to find there's still some gas left in the tank.

Designed to be the flip side of our Cuba St Tavern series, this is the perfect opportunity to make more memories with the new friends you forgot about making memories with on Friday's dance floor.

No big statements or lofty concepts - barely an advertised line up. Just a finely tuned sound system and a handful of likeminded selectors, embarking on a musical journey that will push their tastes to the outer limits, as they thread the needle between genres, eras, styles and sounds.

We'll be back on Sunday 5th July with a line up of Tempo Schmempo's finest to be revealed on the day.

Free entry.

events.name: Bach Trio in Concert
events.venue: 293 Cuba Street, New Zealand 6011,293 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Bach Trio in Concert
WCGS Presents Bach Trio in Concert 17:45 to 18:30.
Featuring:
Bach Trio – Mike Hogan, Christopher Hill, and Jake Church

This trio has come together to perform and ultimately record some new transcriptions of music composed by J. S. Bach. These pieces are well known keyboard works, but this is the first time transcriptions of these pieces have been made for guitar.

Programme:
Partita for keyboard No.2 in C minor (BWV826): Christopher Hill and Mike Hogan - Guitar Duet

Andante from Sonata No.4 for Organ in E minor (BWV 528): Christopher Hill, Mike Hogan, Jake Church

Other solo Bach pieces by Christopher Hill and Jake Church.

Mike Hogan:
Mike Hogan is a Classical guitarist, composer and producer who has worked in music for most of his life. Mike has an Honours Degree (first class) in composition as well as a BMus/performance from the NZ School of Music. Across his career Mike has worked in many styles including Western Classical, Folk, Indie, Punk, Rock, Reggae and Indian Classical as well as with Theatre and Dance production. Mike has performed around the world in venues and festivals such as: Sarakasi Dome, Kenya, Festnapuan, Vanuatu, Jaipur Literature Festival, India, NZ Arts Festival, Darwin International Guitar Festival, Sofar Sounds New Delhi.

Jake Church:
Jake Church is a classically trained guitarist that aims to evoke character in everything he plays. Along with numerous performance accolades, he is a scholar in collaborative practices with a Master of Musical Arts from Victoria University of Wellington. Jake enjoys exploring collaborative processes to create new-music and mix-discipline compositions.

Chris Hill:
Christopher Hill is a versatile classical guitarist whose playing is shaped by a diverse background in classical, jazz, and flamenco traditions. A graduate of Victoria University of Wellington, he further honed his craft through studies in Spain, developing a unique musical expression that is perfectly suited to the intimate acoustics of a church setting. Christopher is well-known to New Zealand audiences through his extensive touring with the New Zealand Guitar Quartet and performances with the New Zealand Symphony Orchestra.

events.id: 'Tag yourself' Printmaking - Creative Arts Holiday Programme-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: 'Tag yourself' Printmaking - Creative Arts Holiday Programme
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join us for a fun two day print making session with award winning print maker - Jude Gordon. The focus will be yourself while learning the art of collography. A collagraph is a versatile printmaking technique whereby the printing plate is created by gluing various textured materials on to a rigid base. The plate is then covered in ink and rolled through a press again and again to create multiple prints of the one image.

Due to use of heavy equipment, minimum age for this workshop is 12 and above.

Monday & Tuesday, 6 & 7 July, 10-1pm

events.id: Fundamentals of Drawing - Creative Arts Holiday Programme-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Fundamentals of Drawing - Creative Arts Holiday Programme
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
In this two day course you will learn the basics of structure and perspective so you can draw anything you like. Returning students are assigned additional challenges in order to promote continuous development of their drawing journey.

We'll be drawing on paper with pencils and pens. There's a strong emphasis on learning structure and perspective drawing, with the aim to feel more confident in drawing anything - real or imagined. We will be drawing things from real life, from photos and from our imagination.

What you will learn in the class.

- Perspective - learning the basics to help draw forms and spaces convincingly.
- Using perspective to draw organic forms.
- The structure of human faces - Constructing the face to understand it.
- The structure of human figures - Drawing the body from the skeleton out.
- The structure of animal and creature bodies - Seeing the similarities to humans.
- Shading and shadows - Understanding light as it hits objects.
- Composition - Arranging elements in your picture.
- Using different B&W mediums like pencils, pens, inks.

This course is catered to ages 10 years or older. Basic materials provided.

Mon & Tues, 6 & 7 July, 10-1pm

events.long_description:
Matariki Art Classes
Celebrate the magic of Matariki with a creative school holiday treat for your tamariki.

Join us at Kai Tahi for our Matariki Art Classes, running throughout the first week of the school holidays in collaboration with Sams Art House. It’s a wholesome, hands-on activity that’s perfect for keeping little creatives busy and inspired!

Each session is just $5 per child, making it a budget-friendly option for whānau. Kids will create a unique art project, with all materials provided.

Ages: 4-12 years
Limited spots: 30 kids per class - bookings required.
Parents/caregivers must stay: this is a shared creative experience, not a drop-off session.

Please let Sams Art House know if you can no longer attend, as there is a waitlist of keen little artists ready to join in.

Book online here: https://book.samsarthouse.co.nz/search/roadshow/34

All enquiries: (be sure to save the address so you don’t miss any updates).

Monday 6 July, 10am – 12pm
Matariki Nature Weaving Board

Wednesday 8 July, 10am – 12pm
Matariki Mandala Stars

Friday 10 July, 10am – 12pm
Matariki Celestial Suncatchers

Saturday 11 July, 2 –4pm
Matariki Memory Treasure Boxes

Sunday 12 July, 2 – 4pm
Matariki Wish Weaving Wall Hanging

events.name: Sewing - Creative Arts Holiday Programme for Tweens & Teens
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Create your own bucket hat, tote bag or cushion. This two-day workshop is designed to introduce sewing to 10- 16-year-olds. Those with sewing experience will be extended.

Students will learn essential techniques to create their very own tote bag and or bucket hat! Monday & Tuesday, 6 & 7 July, 10-2pm
Tuesday & Wednesday, 14 & 15 July, 10-2pm

events.id: Wellington Marine Life VR Experience-ZEALANDIA Eco-Sanctuary, Waiapu Road, Karori, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Wellington Marine Life VR Experience
events.venue: ZEALANDIA Eco-Sanctuary, Waiapu Road, Karori, Wellington
events.long_description:
Dive beneath the surface and explore Wellington’s Blue Belt - no snorkel required!

Using virtual reality headsets, take a few minutes these school holidays to see the underwater world through the eyes of a scuba diver: swim through the deep reefs of Kapiti Island, be surrounded by schools of fish at Whitireia Park, glide through the lush marine forests of Taputeranga Marine Reserve, and explore a shipwreck! Expect close encounters with octopus, vibrant sea life and even a cute shark with the team from Explore Your Coast!

This Matariki, Zealandia is exploring Waitā, the star associated with oceans. Find more Matariki events and materials on our website.

This free, drop-in event is suitable for the whole family and will be held in the Zealandia Visitor Centre Foyer. Regular admission fees to the sanctuary apply, and overflow carparking is available at Birdwood Street during the busy school holiday period.

To learn more about our local underwater forests, visit Explore Your Coast at https://exploreyourcoast.co.nz/

events.id: Mrs Llama the Very Kiwi Farmer-Gryphon Theatre, 22 Ghuznee St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Mrs Llama the Very Kiwi Farmer
events.venue: Gryphon Theatre, 22 Ghuznee St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
On her lively New Zealand farm, Mrs Llama is trying to keep everything under control… but between some very pesky rabbits and a cast of unforgettable animal characters, things quickly spiral into hilarious chaos. With the help of the audience, she brings a collection of beloved stories to life, each with a playful Kiwi twist.

Perfect for young audiences and the young at heart, Mrs Llama the Very Kiwi Farmer is a feel-good theatrical adventure full of surprises, silliness, and just a little bit of farmyard mayhem.

Brought to you by Toybox Theatre, supported by Wellington Repertory.

events.id: Paint and Wine Night - Van Gogh Sunflowers-The Borough, 162/168 Main Road, Tawa, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Paint and Wine Night - Van Gogh Sunflowers
events.venue: The Borough, 162/168 Main Road, Tawa, Wellington
events.long_description:
Looking for a creative night out in Tawa, Wellington? Join us for a fun beginner-friendly adults painting class in a bar!

Our artist guides you step-by-step, so absolutely no art experience is needed. It’s the perfect excuse to switch off, get creative, and try something a little different.

Whether you’re coming along solo, planning a date night, or organising a catch-up with friends, Paintvine is all about good vibes, creativity, and having fun while you paint – you might even surprise yourself with what you can create!

Drinks: No drinks are included in the ticket price, but drinks are available for purchase.

Food: A delicious menu will be available to purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit! Our events are held in licensed venues, so you can purchase drinks to sip on while you paint. At the end of the night, you’ll leave with a painting you created yourself – you might even surprise yourself with what you can achieve!

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website ⇒ paintvine.co.nz

events.name: Massey University Animals and Agriculture Day 2026
events.venue: Riddet BuildingPalmerston North
events.long_description:
Interested in a career with animals or agriculture? Come and get a taste of what Massey can offer.
This event is for high school students who are interested in science and want to find out more about careers in Animal Science, Agriculture, Horticulture and environmental Science. You will get a taste for the different specialisations we offer by taking part in activities and seeing students projects.
This is a free event during the school holidays for Year 11-13 high school students. Attendees MUST be 15 years of age. There will be lots of opportunities to get all your questions answered by students and staff.

The event starts at 10am and ends at 3pm, lunch will be provided.

events.id: Wellington Community Ballet Theatre Movie Fundraiser-Light House Cuba Cinema, 29 Wigan Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Wellington Community Ballet Theatre Movie Fundraiser
events.venue: Light House Cuba Cinema, 29 Wigan Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Wellington Community Ballet Theatre is hosting a movie night to raise funds for our next production. Join us for a movie about art to support the arts! Our next show, "Winter's Saga", is contemporary dance based on Norse mythology. The show is on 18-23 August at the Hannah Playhouse and tickets are available through Eventfinda.

The latest film from one of cinema’s most prolific directors, Steven Soderbergh (Black Bag, Ocean’s Eleven), The Christophers is a richly comedic exploration of art and legacy led by a magnetic paring of Ian McKellen and Michaela Coel (Mother Mary).

Close to completely giving up on her artistic career, Lori (Coel) works out of a food truck whenever she’s not doing freelance restoration. But when she gets approached by the estranged children of renowned painter Julian Sklar (McKellen) with an offer to forge his abandoned paintings so they can collect the inheritance once he passes, she accepts. Ingratiating herself into Julian’s life under the guise of being an assistant, Lori’s ruse makes way for a constant battle of wits, twists, and shifting allegiances as they delve into deviousness to reveal thorny truths about artistic integrity.

"The double act of McKellen and Coel has the onscreen chemistry of the year"
★★★★★ - THE GUARDIAN

Content warning: rated M for offensive language

The latest film from one of cinema’s most prolific directors, Steven Soderbergh (Black Bag, Ocean’s Eleven), The Christophers is a richly comedic exploration of art and legacy led by a magnetic paring of Ian McKellen and Michaela Coel (Mother Mary).

events.id: Book Group: The Odyssey by Homer-The Library, 1/53 Courtenay Place, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Book Group: The Odyssey by Homer
events.venue: The Library, 1/53 Courtenay Place, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
The Odyssey is one of the foundational works of literature, an enduring narrative that has been told and retold. The work is one of two major epics of ancient Greek literature attributed to Homer. (The other, of course, is The Illiad.)

After ten years fighting in the Trojan War, Odysseus, the cunning king of Ithaca, can finally set sail for home, where his wife, Penelope, and son, Telemachus - only a baby when Odysseus left for Troy - are waiting for him. However, further perils await as our hero battles gods, monsters and himself during his ten-year struggle to return home.

Beyond literature, The Odyssey has been adapted for the stage, for symphonies, ballets and operas, as well as many film and TV adaptations. In fact, in mid-July Christopher Nolan's major feature film of The Odyssey will hit the screens - we'll have to arrange an outing.

Do you keep meaning to read more of the great classics? At Wellington Classic Literature Club, we read works that have stood the test of time, whether they were written in 1780, 1880 or even 1980. We meet on the first Tuesday of the month.

RSVP
Bookings essential. Get your free ticket by clicking 'Register' above. Limited places.

If you've signed up, but things change and you're not going to be able to make it along, please email classiclitwgtn@gmail.com as we may be able to make more spaces available for others.

VENUE
We meet in the ‘Games Room’, The Library bar's private area overlooking Courtenay Place. After you enter the bar, head to your right (up the step) and through a smaller space with booths to the large carpeted space beyond (behind the big wooden sliding door).

ABOUT US
Wellington Classic Literature Club has been running for more than a decade - since the start of 2013 - and we have a great bunch of regulars as well as readers who join us for particular books or simply when it suits.

events.id: InBetween Nowhere and Everything-BATS Theatre, 1 Kent Tce, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: InBetween Nowhere and Everything
events.venue: BATS Theatre, 1 Kent Tce, Wellington
events.long_description:
Inbetween Nowhere and Everything is a play about cowboys... in space. It plays upon the traditional tropes of the old west; identity, survival, and camaraderie - but through the lens of an expansive science fiction world.

Set in a distant future where stardust is fuel and war is a commonplace, we follow three strangers, a Lover (Alex Quinn), a Killer (Zoe Harris) and a Nobody (Simon Ulm) as they find shelter within an escape pod and are flung into the deepest recesses of empty space, far from any soul or star.

As these strangers float through a haunting and isolating cosmos, they start to find solace in each other, sharing their cowboy songs and stories around the proverbial campfire. Throughout these stories, their pasts and true selves become unveiled with the help of a fourth character, the enigmatic and shadowy THEM (Míren Welsh Ahipene Duffin).

Throughout these stories however, these three strangers find that their lives may not be as distant as they first thought, and that soon, they may have to decide who they'll choose to be when the chips are down...

“AN OTHERWORDLY MEDITATION ON THE LUCK OF THE DRAW...”
GARY HENDERSON

This ambitious, thought-provoking, charming, and poignant play is the debut independent work of emerging Wellington theatre maker Nathaniel Smith. Featuring elements of live music and dance alongside the traditional structure of a one-room play, it is packed full of moments that will stay with the audience long after they've left the theatre.

In the Mo' Theatre Co. is a brand new company of emerging, local practictioners. Many of the cast and crew are graduates of theatre programmes at Te Herenga Waka and Te Auaha. This project showcases dynamic, exciting new talent in a bold, orginal new work.

Follow @inthemo_theatreco

CREW

Town and Trademen:

The Saddlemaker - Oli Sims
The Barber - Tam Maxwell
The Saloon Keeper - Zoe Harris

The Craftsmen and Smithies

The Harvester of Stars - Ethan Cranefield
The Radar - Tim Johnston
The Rocket ship Blacksmith - Jamie Sayers

The Outlaws

The Stagecoach Highwaywoman - Lucy Aitken
The Bounty Hunter - Rachel Tuki

The Village Head

Town Marshall - Rachel
The Wrangler - Sam
The Sheriff - Nathaniel

CAST
The Cowboys

A Lover: Alex Quinn
A Killer: Zoe Harris
A Nobody: Simon Ulm
THEM: Miren Welsh Ahipene Duffin

events.id: Waenga-Aotea College, Okowai Road, Porirua - Mana-Event Finder
events.name: Waenga
events.venue: Aotea College, Okowai Road, Porirua - Mana
events.long_description:
Created by Hariata & Tamati Moriarty and directed by Jim Moriarty, 'Waenga' centres on a young wahine Māori who is arrested and finds herself in a police holding cell. Alongside her journey is Peyton, who is coming to understand what a Tangata Tiriti partnership is and Grayson, a young Māori lawyer navigating his role in the system.

Performed by an ensemble cast, including well known rangatahi Hariata and Tamati Moriarty, the play is presented in Te Rākau’s unique Theatre Marae framework, creating space for collective reflection and shared experience.

Te Rākau is the longest running Māori theatre company in Aotearoa founded in 1989.

Recommended age 12+ contains swearing and depicts some violence. Children under 12 can accompany for free with a paying adult.

events.id: A Revisitation of The Kaiwharawhara Restoration-Tāne Whakapiripiri - Ōtari Visitors Centre, 150 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: A Revisitation of The Kaiwharawhara Restoration
events.venue: Tāne Whakapiripiri - Ōtari Visitors Centre, 150 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington, Wellington
events.long_description:
A quarter of a century ago Ōtari’s valley was full of rubbish, grass and weeds. Jonathan Kennett and Bronwen Wall led teams of volunteers – some of them still working in the valley – to restore the forest. This year, in a Trust-sponsored project, they are measuring the results of all that planting and weeding and their findings are quite outstanding. Come and be inspired.

Located in Tāne Whakapiripiri, Ōtari Visitor Centre. Gold coin donation. No need to book, just turn up!

This seminar is part of the Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush Trust’s annual seminar series. This year, they are on the first Tuesday of the month, March – November, to celebrate 100 years of Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush.


Ōtari 100: Celebrating 100 years of native plants, conservation, and community. 1926-2026.

events.long_description:
Presented by: TEG MJR & TEG Live
Get ready as South African sensation Jeremy Loops returns to bring the vibes to Wellington’s Meow Nui this July!
After a massive 2025, including a standout set on the Acoustic Stage at Glastonbury, the world’s favourite high-energy-folk powerhouse is back for a strictly limited run of headline shows.
Known for his legendary live performances that blend acoustic folk, alt pop and urban grit with loop-pedal wizardry, Jeremy Loops delivers a show that is part intimate storytelling, and part stadium-sized party.
From massive breakout anthems like Down South and Waves to the soaring new melodies of his latest record Feathers And Stone, expect an infectious atmosphere and world-class musicianship. Every room becomes a massive communal celebration. Every night is a pure explosion of energy.
Catch his exclusive Wellington performance. One night only, at Meow Nui, supported by Tom River.

events.id: He Puna Ora, He Wai Ora | Screenprinting Workshops (ages 8-10)-Nōku te Ao Capital E, 65 Victoria Street, Wellington Central, Wellington., Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: He Puna Ora, He Wai Ora | Screenprinting Workshops (ages 8-10)
events.venue: Nōku te Ao Capital E, 65 Victoria Street, Wellington Central, Wellington., Wellington
events.long_description:
Join local artist Tayla Hartemink for a hands-on screenprinting workshop exploring the importance of protecting our moana and waterways.

Te moana is an important source of life; it sustains ecosystems, provides habitat for native species, and supports our communities through recreation, kai gathering, and drinking water. However, many of our waterways are at risk from human activity.

In this hands-on workshop, tamariki aged 8-10 learn to screenprint as they express their ideas around the importance of our waterways and our collective responsibility in protecting them.

The workshop begins at Ngā Paiaka Bleachers with Wellington City Libraries to spark inspiration. Participants then head to the Kia Mākūkū! Makerspace Wet to bring their ideas to life using ink and fabric, screenprinting their own designs onto recycled tees or tote bags using stencils created by Tayla.

Tote bags and t-shirts are provided.
events.description:

However, many of our waterways are at risk from human activity.
In this hands-on workshop, tamariki aged 8-10 learn to screenprint as they express their ideas around the importance of our waterways and our collective responsibility in protecting them.
The workshop begins at Ngā Paiaka Bleachers with Wellington City Libraries to spark inspiration.

events.id: Star Stories - A Matariki Art Activity-Greytown Gallery, 115 Main Street, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Star Stories - A Matariki Art Activity
events.venue: Greytown Gallery, 115 Main Street, Greytown
events.long_description:
Celebrate the magic of Matariki with a hands-on creative experience at the Greytown Art Gallery.

You are invited to paint your own wooden star, choosing colours and patterns that reflect one of the Matariki stars or your own hopes and wishes for the year ahead. This activity is offered for a koha, welcoming everyone to take part in the spirit of generosity and community. Once complete, each star becomes part of a growing outdoor art installation, carefully placed in a special Matariki display in front of the gallery.

Together, these stars form a vibrant, community-created artwork that honours connection, remembrance, and new beginnings. Fun and full of wonder for all ages!
