events.id: The Boy With Wings-Wairarapa Events Centre, Holloway St, Carterton-Event Finder
events.name: The Boy With Wings
events.venue: Wairarapa Events Centre, Holloway St, Carterton
events.long_description:
A comic, touching, and environmental story using the ageless wonder of puppetry, live music and exquisite hand-crafted sets that magically unfold out of boxes, telling an epic fable of one young man’s life-altering journey. All this is set against the backdrop of a contemporary ornithological lecture on the miracle of the great migration of Kuaka (Bar-tailed Godwits). As these fragile birds set out on their extraordinary journey from New Zealand to Alaska, watching them take to the sky in awe is Jack-the-Everyman, who is contemplating a journey of his own to seek the ‘treasure’ that will save his dying orchard.

Award-winning theatre company ‘Birdlife Productions’ promise laughter, tears and thrilling adventure, enticing us to ask the question; “how do we trust our inner map – how do we find our own wings?” Suitable for ages 5 to 95!

Seating layout: Flat floor seating with cushions and mat for children to sit closer if they wish.

events.id: Balfolk Class - Newtown-Newtown Community Centre - Te Whare Hapori o Ngā Puna Waiora,7 Colombo St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Balfolk Class - Newtown
events.venue: Newtown Community Centre - Te Whare Hapori o Ngā Puna Waiora,7 Colombo St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Balfolk Class - Newtown
Balfolk is a folk dance and music movement from Europe that gives new life to traditional dances. A ‘Bal Folk’ is a social dance event that brings people of all ages and walks of life together through group and partner dances to have fun and connect. It's a friendly, social and energising space.

The class will run over 6 weeks. We will learn and practice different group and partner dances you would commonly find in a Bal Folk.
Dances are simple and easy to learn but also have depth so once you have learnt the basics, we can explore and play around with variations.
Balfolk is accessible to people with all skill levels. You don’t need any previous dance experience, curiosity is all you need 😊

When: Wednesdays from 6:00 to 7:30pm

Location: Newtown Community Centre

Price: $60 for the block of 6 classes
$15/class for casual attendance
Students/concession/unwaged: koha
Pay at the door

events.id: Matariki Ahi Kā-Odlin’s Plaza, Wellington Waterfront-Wellington NZ
events.name: Matariki Ahi Kā
events.venue: Odlin’s Plaza, Wellington Waterfront
events.long_description:
Gather with friends and whānau to celebrate the Māori New Year on the waterfront. Matariki Ahi Kā, presented by Wellington City Council, honours culture, history, and family. Experience a free, immersive journey along the waterfront. It features fire, stunning projections, live performances, and a ceremony for loved ones who have passed away. There are also kai courts where you can stop off for a bite and listen to live music. Starting at 6pm and running for three hours. You can soak up the atmosphere from 9 to 12 July. 
The rising of Puanga and the Matariki star cluster (Pleiades) mark the Māori New Year. It is a special time to get together and restore faith and hope for the future. Māori refer to celebrating whanaungatanga or kinship. Be with others, share stories and kai, and plan to work towards a sustainable future.

events.id: Future Builder: AI and Productivity 2026 | Wellington-The Angus HotelLower Hutt, Wellington Region-Event Brite
events.name: Future Builder: AI and Productivity 2026 | Wellington
events.venue: The Angus HotelLower Hutt, Wellington Region
events.long_description:
A practical NZ workshop by Wunderbuild, Vision 2 Estimating, VEO Building Co & Future Builder on AI, productivity, and workflows.
AI & Productivity for Builders 2026 — New Zealand

You don't need to know everything about AI. You just need to know what's worth your time.
AI & Productivity for Builders 2026 is a full-day, in-person workshop designed specifically for New Zealand's residential building industry — and it's coming to three cities this July.
This isn't a conference. There's no stage filled with tech people telling builders how to run their businesses. It's a practical working day, built around the real challenges builders face, and run by people who understand the industry from the inside.
WHAT THIS DAY IS ABOUT
Running a building business in 2026 means managing more complexity with less margin for error. Costs shift. Timelines compress. Clients expect more. And somewhere in the middle of all of it, you're supposed to be across quoting, procurement, site delivery, and administration — all at once.
Better tools exist. Smarter workflows are possible. AI is no longer something on the horizon — it's already being used by builders across Australia and New Zealand to save time, reduce rework, and make better decisions faster.
This workshop brings together four industry partners — Wunderbuild, Vision 2 Estimating, VEO Building Co, and Future Builder — with one shared goal: to help you understand what's actually useful, show you how it works in a real building business, and help you leave the day with a clearer picture of what to do next.
WHAT TO EXPECT
The day is structured to be practical, interactive, and genuinely useful — not a back-to-back slide deck marathon.
You will spend time at your table working through ideas with other builders, reflecting on what applies to your business, and building a one-page Productivity Roadmap that captures your key takeaways, identifies workflow gaps, and gives you clear next steps to take back to your business.
Across the day you will explore:
🔹 How AI tools are being used in building businesses right now — not in theory, but in practice, demonstrated live
🔹 Smarter estimating and pre-construction workflows — how better processes at the quoting stage reduce stress, variations, and cost blowouts downstream
🔹 3D visualisation and client communication — how builders are winning more work and managing client expectations before a slab is poured
🔹 Project delivery and operations — how AI-powered platforms can give you real-time visibility across cost, schedule, procurement, and labour
🔹 The tools worth knowing about in 2026 — and how to evaluate what fits your business without wasting time on things that don't
🔹 Your one-page Productivity Roadmap — built throughout the day, so you leave with a personalised plan rather than a notebook full of notes you'll never open again
WHO THIS IS FOR
This workshop is for residential builders at any stage of the AI and productivity conversation — whether you are already experimenting with tools in your business, curious about where to start, or simply want to understand what's changing and what it means for you.
If you run or manage a residential building business and want to come away from a day with practical clarity — not just inspiration — this is for you.
THE PARTNERS BEHIND THE DAY
Wunderbuild is a cloud-based construction management platform built for residential builders, combining project delivery tools with an AI layer that provides real-time insight, forecasting, and decision support across every project.
Vision 2 Estimating brings AI-powered 3D modelling and visualisation to the pre-construction phase — helping builders and their clients see a project before it's built, win more work at the quote stage, and reduce costly variations.
VEO Building Co provides builder-to-builder credibility on the day. Having used the tools and workflows covered in the workshop inside a live residential building business, VEO brings honest, practical perspective on what works and what doesn't.
Future Builder is a community and education platform dedicated to helping builders build better businesses — covering business systems, AI adoption, and the mindset shifts that separate good builders from great ones.
THE FORMAT
Doors open at 8:30am for registration and coffee. The program runs from 9:00am through to 3:00pm.
The day includes live demonstrations, facilitated table discussions, structured reflection time, and a dedicated session to complete and peer-review your Productivity Roadmap. Presenters are available throughout the day — not just on stage.
Bring a laptop or tablet. It may come in handy.
THREE CITIES. ONE PROGRAM.
📍 Auckland — Monday 6 July 2026
📍 Hamilton — Tuesday 7 July 2026
📍 Wellington — Thursday 9 July 2026
All three events run the same program. Register for the city that works best for you.
Seats are limited at each location. Register now to secure your place.

Across the day you will explore: 🔹 How AI tools are being used in building businesses right now — not in theory, but in practice, demonstrated live 🔹 Smarter estimating and pre-construction workflows — how better processes at the quoting stage reduce stress, variations, and cost blowouts downstream 🔹 3D visualisation and client communication — how builders are winning more work and managing client expectations before a slab is poured 🔹 Project delivery and operations — how AI-powered platforms can give you real-time visibility across cost, schedule, procurement, and labour 🔹 The tools worth knowing about in 2026 — and how to evaluate what fits your business without wasting time on things that don't 🔹 Your one-page Productivity Roadmap — built throughout the day, so you leave with a personalised plan rather than a notebook full of notes you'll never open again

events.id: Holiday Programme - The Mega Multi Function Alarm-Maungaraki School, Dowse Drive, Maungaraki, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: Holiday Programme - The Mega Multi Function Alarm
events.venue: Maungaraki School, Dowse Drive, Maungaraki, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Keep your belongings safe with this ultimate personal security accessory. The unit has a grant total of 5 different alarm triggers. Depending on the application you can choose from a Sound Sensor, Light Sensor, Vibration Sensor, Radar Sensor or Trip Wire. Each sensor can be turned on and off from inside the unit and the sensitivity of each can be tweaked with a small screwdriver. The unit activates with a key switch so only the key holder can disable it. Whether it's your school bag, lolly stash, bedroom or forest fort - its safety and security is assured.

During the class you will get to solder all the electronics onto the custom Printed Circuit Board, assemble and screw together all the various components, and customise your alarm tones as you upload the code to the built in microprocessor. The unit is powered by 4 AA Batteries and of course you get to take it home with you at the end of the day. See the video below to learn more!

MakeRoom holiday programmes run every term break as well as over the summer. They are made up of one day workshops focusing on a big build with lots of fun learning along the way. Everyone gets to take the project they have constructed home with them.

- One day sessions
- Only 8 Kids per instructor
- 9am to 3.30pm
- $140 (including GST) for the day
- Open to kids aged 10-15
- Parents and Guardians welcome

On the day:

- Students should bring their own food and water bottle (morning tea and lunch)
- Students should wear closed toed shoes for safety in the workshop
- Students with long hair should have it tied back for safety

events.id: Holiday Programme - The Voice Changer-Crofton Downs Primary School, Chartwell Drive, Crofton Downs, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Holiday Programme - The Voice Changer
events.venue: Crofton Downs Primary School, Chartwell Drive, Crofton Downs, Wellington
events.long_description:
Ever wanted to sound like a robot, a monster, a space explorer, or something completely out of this world? Introducing The Voice Changer — a hands-on electronics project that lets you record your voice, save your sound files, and then play them back with weird and wonderful effects.

The unit has its own handheld microphone, built-in speaker, headphone output, screen, buttons and menu controls. You can save up to 6 recordings of around 20 seconds each, then experiment with effects like echo, pitch shifting, distortion, tremolo and reverb to completely transform the sound. You can control everything from the unit itself, or connect to it from a phone or computer to adjust settings using a simple web interface.

On the day you will get to solder the electronics, assemble the laser-cut case, fit the speaker, screen, buttons and microphone, and load the code onto the onboard microcontroller. The unit is powered by a USB cable so no batteries required. At the end of the day you’ll walk away with a fully working voice-changing gadget that you built yourself, ready to prank your family, invent strange characters, or create your own sci-fi sound effects.

No previous experience is required and you’re welcome to bring a grown up if you want to.

MakeRoom holiday programmes run every term break as well as over the summer. They are made up of one day workshops focusing on a big build with lots of fun learning along the way. Everyone gets to take the project they have constructed home with them.

- One day sessions
- Only 8 Kids per instructor
- 9am to 3.30pm
- $140 (including GST) for the day
- Open to kids aged 10-15
- Parents and Guardians welcome

On the day:

- Students should bring their own food and water bottle (morning tea and lunch)
- Students should wear closed toed shoes for safety in the workshop
- Students with long hair should have it tied back for safety

events.name: Book Binding - Creative Arts Holiday Programme for Teens
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
In this workshop you will be guided in assembling your chosen materials for your book, create decorative covers and learn Coptic binding - an elegant and simple binding that is unlimited in versatility.

No special tools or materials are needed and yet a wide spectrum of creative results can be created, from a notebook, sketch book and album to a highly crafted artwork. The books themselves can be made from found items, recycled material or quality art papers and card. You will produce one bound book and one bound album. Once you have Coptic binding in your skill set you can produce your choice of books in any shape or form, for any purpose.

All basic materials supplied.

Bring a drink bottle and morning tea.

Minimum recommended age for this course is 12-16 years, adults also welcome.

events.id: He Puna Ora, He Wai Ora | Screenprinting Workshops (ages 11-14)-Nōku te Ao Capital E, 65 Victoria Street, Wellington Central, Wellington., Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: He Puna Ora, He Wai Ora | Screenprinting Workshops (ages 11-14)
events.venue: Nōku te Ao Capital E, 65 Victoria Street, Wellington Central, Wellington., Wellington
events.long_description:
Join local artist Tayla Hartemink for a hands-on screenprinting workshop exploring the importance of protecting our moana and waterways.
Te moana is an important source of life; it sustains ecosystems, provides habitat for native species, and supports our communities through recreation, kai gathering, and drinking water. However, many of our waterways are at risk from human activity.

In this hands-on workshop, rangatahi aged 11–14 learn to screenprint as they express their ideas around the importance of our waterways and our collective responsibility in protecting them.

The workshop begins at Ngā Paiaka Bleachers with Wellington City Libraries to spark inspiration. Participants then head to the Kia Mākūkū! Makerspace Wet to bring their ideas to life using ink and fabric, screenprinting their own designs onto recycled tees or tote bags using stencils created by Tayla.

Tote bags and t-shirts are provided.

events.id: National Celebration - 40 Years of Homosexual Law Reform-New Zealand Parliament,The Legislative Council Chamber, Pipitea, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: National Celebration - 40 Years of Homosexual Law Reform
events.venue: New Zealand Parliament,The Legislative Council Chamber, Pipitea, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
National Celebration - 40 Years of Homosexual Law Reform
40 Years of Homosexual Law Reform – Community Celebration at Parliament

Join us in the historic Banquet Hall at Parliament as we come together to celebrate 40 years since the passing of the Homosexual Law Reform Act — a landmark moment that helped transform Aotearoa New Zealand into a more inclusive and just society.

This special evening will honour the courage, determination, and advocacy of those who fought for equality, while celebrating the vibrant and diverse rainbow communities who continue that legacy today.

The programme will feature speeches from past and present activists, sharing reflections on the movement, the challenges faced, and the progress achieved over four decades. Guests will also enjoy performances from talented members of the community, bringing music, storytelling, and creative expression to the celebration.

More than a commemoration, this event is also an opportunity to connect across generations of the rainbow community and allies. Join activists, community leaders, supporters, and friends for an evening of reflection, celebration, and whakawhanaungatanga in one of the country’s most significant civic spaces.

Together, we honour the past, celebrate the present, and look toward a future where equality and inclusion continue to grow.

events.id: Pub Sing-Boundary Tap & Kitchen, 3 Raumati Rd, Raumati-Event Finder
events.name: Pub Sing
events.venue: Boundary Tap & Kitchen, 3 Raumati Rd, Raumati
events.long_description:
Pub Sing - where the audience turns into the choir at the pub, with a live rock band,

No sheet music, no experience needed, and absolutely no solos.

Katy reveals three songs on the night (usually a crowd-pleaser, a ballad, and a banger), and even if you don’t know them, you’ll pick them up fast with colour-coded lyrics on screen.

You learn different harmony options, sing along with the band, and blend into a room of up to 80 voices, so no one’s listening to you; they’re singing with you.

There’s banter, laughs, a bit of silliness, and a whole lot of feel-good energy. It’s not karaoke (shudder), it’s everyone singing together just for the fun of it, and it’s the perfect end-of-week lift you needed.

*Please check the ticket booking link for updates or changes to dates*
*All ticket sales goes directly to the band*

events.long_description:
Step into a world of glamour, music, and high stakes with Casino Royale — a unique brass band show with singers, dancers, and actors. Journey through the story of a legendary casino, from its glittering beginnings to its final night, brought to life through iconic music inspired by Las Vegas, film, and popular culture.

Dress to impress, enjoy a drink, try your luck with a game of bingo, and settle in for a lively night of entertainment. Whether you're a seasoned concertgoer or just looking for a great night out, Casino Royale promises an unforgettable evening of style, storytelling, and spectacular live music.

events.id: Krafty Kuts 'Turbulence' Album Tour-Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Krafty Kuts 'Turbulence' Album Tour
events.venue: Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
KRAFTY KUTS 'Turbulence' ALBUM TOUR | Matariki Weekend. Get a sneak peek of Krafty's new album 'Turbulence' releasing 10 July on Against The Grain. Drawing from UKG, breaks, bassline and jazz, it's his most significant record in over a decade.

A pioneer in the most literal sense. Krafty's Slam The Breaks series and his Mixmag Instant Party covermount didn't just define an era, they set the blueprint for the bass movement that soundtracked the 21st century. Two Breakspoll award-winning albums, a FabricLive 34 mix, and a back catalogue spanning Ministry of Sound, OWSLA, Fabric, Skint and Finger Lickin' tell you everything about where he sits in the canon.

On the road his CV reads like a who's who: Big Day Out alongside Rage Against The Machine and Bjork, Summadayze straight after Groove Armada, Glastonbury, Bestival, sell-out nights at Fabric, Brixton Electric and Matter. He's shared stages with Grand Master Flash, Z Trip, Thievery Corporation and Kurtis Blow and played everything from the Chinese Grand Prix to Spotify's launch party.

Local support from BRYCE | WADE | MASON | STU

events.id: Rivering-The Rogue & Vagabond-Rogue & Vagabond
events.name: Rivering
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
Come join us for an original music debut of the project Rivering. Lily Rose Atu recently finished studying Jazz at Te Kōkī, NZ School of Music, and is excited to share her original works at the Rogue & Vagabond on Thursday the 9th of July. These tunes are a collection of reflections from the past few years, inspired by her love for jazz and neo-soul. All proceeds will go into recording this album later on this year.

The music will be brought to life by the incredible: Jono Ben Zur on bass, Paulina Hernandez on piano, Ranu Wijesuriya on drums, Frank Paton on trumpet, Elisa Bird on baritone, Victoria Ferry on vocals, and Lily Rose Atu on saxophone and vocals.

Doors: 8.30pm
Music from: 9pm

* Wheelchair accessible venue | Accessible bathroom available

Lily Rose Atu recently finished studying Jazz at Te Kōkī, NZ School of Music, and is excited to share her original works at the Rogue & Vagabond on Thursday the 9th of July.

events.id: Peter Hook and The Light-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Peter Hook and The Light
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Bassist Peter Hook has intimate knowledge of the music of bands Joy Division and New Order. He’s one of the founding members of both British new wave bands. In July 2026, he is bringing his band The Light to The Opera House in Wellington. Together they will perform New Order’s album ‘Get Ready’ in full to celebrate its 25th anniversary. To make the concert even better, they will also perform a selection of greatest hits from Joy Division’s and New Order’s back catalogue. Get ready to enjoy songs like Disorder, Shadowplay, Transmission, Love Will Tear Us Apart, Blue Monday, and True Faith. 
The dark and ethereal sound of Joy Division and New Order ushered in the post-punk era and continue to be influential in the rock music scene. Peter Hook and The Light are careful to meticulously recreate their music in a live setting. Audiences are in for a show where treasured songs are played with the passion and energy they deserve.
events.description:

To make the concert even better, they will also perform a selection of greatest hits from Joy Division’s and New Order’s back catalogue.
Peter Hook and The Light are careful to meticulously recreate their music in a live setting.

events.id: The Jungle Giants tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: The Jungle Giants tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Spotify, Triple J, Untitled, Amplifire, & Select Music
The Jungle Giants are back, baby!
After touring the world for the past 5 years the energetic 4 piece return to stages down under with with their 5th studio album, ‘Experiencing Feelings of Joy’. Supported by up and comers The Tullamarines and Tear Drive these shows are for tastemakers and dancefloor demons alike.
The album’s lead single ‘Tell Me How it Feels’ is a clear indication of this new era. Fresh vulnerability and sound but distinctly Jungles. With close to half a billion streams under their belt and a reputation for lighting up festival stages this tour unveils a bold new evolution of The Jungle Giants’ signature sound.
Ticket options include: general admission or general admission with a digital download of the brand new album, 'Experiencing Feelings of Joy', which will be emailed on release day, May 8.

events.id: Āio: a Puoro Māori Sound Journey-175 Vivian Street, New Zealand 6011,175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Āio: a Puoro Māori Sound Journey
events.venue: 175 Vivian Street, New Zealand 6011,175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Āio: a Puoro Māori Sound Journey
Āio: a Puoro Māori Sound Journey

Experience a peaceful Māori sound journey blending taonga pūoro, story, and crystal bowls for deep relaxation and inner calm

“Āio” speaks to peace and inner calm. In this immersive evening, pūrākau, taonga pūoro and waiata are woven together with the healing vibrations of crystal singing bowls, creating a space for deep listening, rest and restoration.

The organic tones of traditional Māori instruments including kōauau, pūtōrino, porotiti and pūtātara blend with the resonance of crystal bowls, chimes and ukulele, inviting you to slow down, soften and reconnect with your wairua and body. Through story, sound and vibration, this is a gentle journey inward.

After the sound journey, share in a nourishing plant-based dinner and dessert, included in your ticket, a warm way to close the evening together.

What to bring:

Yoga mats, blankets and cushions will be provided, but you are welcome to bring extra for comfort!

Meet your hosts & musicians: Smita, Peter, and Alexandra

Smita (Ngāi Te Rangi) was born and raised in Aotearoa. From a young age she was drawn to music as a means for reflection and self-expression. During her music studies at university, she was introduced to taonga pūoro, and this sparked a personal journey of reconnection to te ao Māori and her own whakapapa. Following university, she lived in an ashram for several years where she was introduced to the wonderful practice of bhakti yoga. She now lives with her husband in the Wellington region, where they regularly participate in kirtan (mantra music meditation) at Bhakti Lounge.

Peter is Pākehā, with grandparents from England and Ireland, and has been on a four-year journey learning the art of taonga pūoro — the traditional Māori musical instruments of Aotearoa. Guided by the wisdom and generosity of expert makers and players, he has learned both to craft and to play these unique instruments. For Peter, taonga pūoro offer a way to connect deeply with sound, story, and the natural world, and he is excited to share their voices as part of this sound bath experience.

Alexandra is a sound healing practitioner devoted to nurturing healing in both mind and body. She believes that sound vibration holds the power to expand consciousness and restore harmony and balance in our lives. Her journey into sound healing began with the realization of how deeply sound vibration was affecting her psyche, making her more mindful of the music she listens to and the intention it carries. Along the way, she has drawn inspiration from artists such as Deva Premal, The Flying Mystics, Stevin McNamara, and the Bhakti Lounge Kirtan. Their music has profoundly influenced her own sound healing practice, inspiring her to explore sound as a tool for transformation, connection, and inner peace.

events.id: Matariki Festival Paetūmōkai-5710,Kahutara, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Matariki Festival Paetūmōkai
events.venue: 5710,Kahutara, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Matariki Festival Paetūmōkai
Matariki herenga waka – for all of us.

Kia ora Paetūmōkai whānau, Wairarapa Moana communities. and manuhiri, it's time for us to start planning our Matariki celebrations again.

Soon we will put the call out; for those of you who are storytellers, pumpkin choppers, kawakawa gatherers, kaumatua and kuia chauffeurs, chair putter-outers, table decorators, steam pudding makers, sweepers, child wranglers, photographers, leftover hangi dispersers, fairy light arrangers; our small town makes wonderful things happen when we gather together. If you have helped before, thank you, and if you can help again- thank you- but if you need to rest, we understand. If you haven't helped before- nau mai, haere mai, welcome- and in advance, thank you!

This year’s theme of "Matariki herenga waka" is all about inclusion, and encouraging ALL people to celebrate Matariki together. The theme has strong connections to multi-culturalism and the sharing of cultural practices; we can ALL connect to the core values of Matariki and embrace the celebration of the holiday.

The Māori New Year is a time of reflection, celebration, and preparation. It is a time to remember loved ones, come together with whānau to give thanks to what we have and look forward to the promise of a new year.

In the meantime; here's a new Matariki waiata to get your heart singing. Don't worry, there'll still be the Matariki Macarena!

Silly Billies is Wellington’s premier alternative comedy playground. Every month, we assemble a line-up of the city’s top comedians and let them loose to test drive their weirdest, most experimental ideas.

Matariki is a time to remember and honour those who have passed, including ancestors and loved ones. We have created a special place in our heritage garden to commemorate with our Mai i te Whenua ki te Rangi Tree of Remembrance, translating to ‘From earth to sky Tree of Remembrance.’

Our Tree of Remembrance is for everyone to come reflect and share a message during this meaningful time. Write your message on colourful star-shaped paper and adorn our tree with your treasured words and memories.

Self-guided activity | Donations appreciated

events.id: Matariki Whānau Day-Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa, 55 Cable St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Matariki Whānau Day
events.venue: Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa, 55 Cable St, Wellington
events.long_description:
A family-friendly Matariki celebration filled with free cultural activities, workshops and interactive experiences inspired by waka voyaging, Pacific traditions and celestial navigation.

This special Matariki Whānau Day invites tamariki and whānau to explore the stories, mātauranga and cultural connections carried across Te Moana-nui-a-Kiwa through hands-on activities, creative workshops and live demonstrations.

Families can take part in a design workshop with artist and carver Selwyn Palmer exploring the remote Polynesian islands of Rennell and Bellona, discover the Fijian environmental calendar and seasonal food knowledge with Emele Ugavule, and learn about Pacific navigation through star compass demonstrations from the navigators of Ngahiraka Mai Tawhiti.

Visitors can also explore Kiribati culture with St Joseph's Kiribati Community, complete a Te Papa Taonga Trail inspired by the tohu of Matariki, and enjoy Matariki-themed face painting throughout the day.

Suitable for all ages.

This event includes NZSL interpretation and wheelchair access.

events.venue: Te Papa Museum,Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Matariki Whānau Day
Huia mai ki te whakanui i a Matariki mā ngā ngohe pai mā te whānau, ngā awheawhe me ngā mahi utukore.
***
Come together to celebrate Matariki with free whānau-friendly activities, workshops, and activities.

Waka hourua (double-hulled voyaging canoes) are central to Māori and Pacific celestial navigation stories, and our ancestors’ journeys across Te Moana-nui-a-Kiwa (The Pacific).

Te hōtaka matua | Programme:
▪️Face paint with Matariki-inspired designs
▪️Artist and carver-led design workshop
▪️Learn about lunar phases in the Fijian environmental calendar
▪️Discover how to use a star compass to voyage across the pacific
▪️Learn about the importance of the frigate bird in Kiribati culture
▪️Complete a Te Papa taonga trail

This event will have NZSL interpretation. A full schedule, including information for Deaf whānau, will be shared soon.

✨ Kimi tonutia | Find out more: tepapa.nz/Matariki2026

🎟️ Entry to Te Papa is free for all New Zealanders and people living in Aotearoa New Zealand.

📅 Don’t miss out on our other whānau-friendly events! Stay in the loop at tepapa.nz/newsletter

events.id: Star Glaze 2026 Mid Winter Ceramics Festival-Otaki Racing Club, 47 Te Roto Road, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Star Glaze 2026 Mid Winter Ceramics Festival
events.venue: Otaki Racing Club, 47 Te Roto Road, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Join Ōtaki Pottery Club for the 2026 Star Glaze Mid Winter Ceramics Festival, an exciting celebration of art and the traditions of Matariki, set against the picturesque backdrop of the Ōtaki Māori Racing Club.

The festival kicks off with a captivating opening ceremony, marking the beginning of a vibrant weekend filled with diverse hands on activities, inspiring exhibits, delicious food, lively music, and much more. Immerse yourself in the rich heritage of Matariki and discover the magic of pottery in a festival like no other.

Friday also features race day at the venue, adding to the atmosphere and making for a lively start to the weekend. On Saturday, don’t miss the public raku firing, a hands on and visually striking experience that is always a highlight for visitors.

Haere mai ki te Hui Whakanui Toi Ahurea o te Takurua 2026, ko te Star Glaze Mid Winter Ceramics Festival, he hui whakanui ngā toi me ngā tikanga o Matariki, e tū ana i te whāriki whakahirahira o te Ōtaki Māori Racing Club.

Ka tīmata te hui ki tētahi whakatuwheratanga whakahirahira, e tohu ana i te tīmatanga o tētahi wiki whakangahau, kī tonu i ngā mahi ā-ringa, ngā whakaaturanga whakaaweawe, ngā kai reka, ngā puoro ora, me te nui atu. Ruku atu ki te hōhonutanga o ngā tikanga o Matariki, ā, kitea te mākutu o te mahi uku i roto i tētahi hui kāore he rite.

Gate entry is $10 on Friday, no entry fee on Saturday.

events.id: Matariki-Greytown Town Hall, 89 Main St, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Matariki
events.venue: Greytown Town Hall, 89 Main St, Greytown
events.long_description:
Matariki comes alive in Greytown with an afternoon and evening of culture, creativity, storytelling and community celebration in and around Stella Bull Park. Gather beneath the winter sky to celebrate the Māori New Year with kapa haka performances from local tamariki and rangatahi, including Greytown School and Kuranui College performers.

Families can take part in hands-on activities throughout the day, including glow-in-the-dark star painting at Greytown Art Gallery, located in Stella Bull Park. Inspired by the Matariki cluster, you can paint and hang stars. Wander through the park at your leisure to see artworks created by children from Greytown School.

Warm up with delicious kai, including a traditional hāngī prepared by the Kuranui College Netball Team, alongside fry bread, sweet treats and festive winter fare. Find them at Stella Bull Park from 3pm. Limited Hangi available.

As darkness falls, enjoy a special projected retelling of the Matariki story, created by Stun and illuminated across the Blackwell & Son’s building just up the road at 110 Main St, creating a striking visual centrepiece for the celebrations.

With music, light, art and shared kai, Greytown’s Matariki celebration is a beautiful opportunity to come together, reflect, remember and look forward to the year ahead

events.id: Shooting the Stars- Wainuiomata Regional Park-Wainuiomata Recreation Area,Lower Hutt, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Shooting the Stars- Wainuiomata Regional Park
events.venue: Wainuiomata Recreation Area,Lower Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Shooting the Stars- Wainuiomata Regional Park
Calling all aspiring astro-photographers!

Join us to get close-up and personal with our galaxy and learn the basics of dark sky shooting from a professional.

Are you a photographer interested in learning the tips and tricks of dark sky photography? We are thrilled to be offering this exclusive astrophotography workshop and stargazing session guided by Glen Butler (Night & Light Photography) and Wellington Astronomical Society as part of Greater Wellington’s Dark Sky Series.

This event is strictly limited to 30 people, register now via this link:


Please only register if you have the equipment requirements outlined below. What to bring (minimum requirements):
• Camera – with the ability to change manual settings of aperture, shutter speed and ISO
• A wide-angle lens or zoom (e.g. 14mm, 24mm, 35mm)
• A stable tripod
• A remote shutter release or the ability to put your camera onto (2-second) self-timer mode
• Empty memory card(s)
• Spare/fully charged camera batteries
• Head torch (ideally with red light setting)
• Warm clothing and sturdy footwear

During this introductory session to astrophotography, you will learn the fundamentals and some creative ideas to begin taking captivating images in a unique dark sky environment. Wellington Astronomical Society will also have their telescopes on hand for you to have a closer look at our sparkling Southern Skies.

Why Wainuiomata? Wainuiomata Regional Park is close to achieving dark sky place accreditation and offers some of the region's best stargazing just 30 minutes from Wellington's CBD. The location of the park provides a sanctuary from city light pollution, allowing budding astro-photographers a stunning night sky to shoot.

This event is not suitable for children under 18.
events.description:
Shooting the Stars- Wainuiomata Regional Park Calling all aspiring astro-photographers!
Join us to get close-up and personal with our galaxy and learn the basics of dark sky shooting from a professional.
We are thrilled to be offering this exclusive astrophotography workshop and stargazing session guided by Glen Butler (Night & Light Photography) and Wellington Astronomical Society as part of Greater Wellington’s Dark Sky Series.
This event is strictly limited to 30 people, register now via this link: Please only register if you have the equipment requirements outlined below.
Wainuiomata Regional Park is close to achieving dark sky place accreditation and offers some of the region's best stargazing just 30 minutes from Wellington's CBD.

events.id: Date Night with Caitlin & Reuben-The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Date Night with Caitlin & Reuben
events.venue: The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
We’ve spent the last 5 years living together, how hard could it be writing an event description together?

This show is about anything and everything, but mostly about us. How we met, where we’re going, which coalition member we hate more than the other two dickheads, et cetera.

We have lots in common... we both studied at Otago, we both do stand-up comedy, and we both love each other ♥️ It did take ages to choose a name for this show. Our first idea, "Flight of the Conchords", was sadly already taken. But, we figured Date Night was perfect, as we always talk about finding more time to spend together; forgetting time has already arrived, we only need to decide who else to share it with.

Regardless, it'll be an unforgettable night, and we’d love to share the joy with you.

Reuben Crisp

Winner, Best MC – Dunedin Comedy Awards 2025, 2024, 2021, 2020
Winner, Spirit of the Slam – Otago Poetry Slam 2021
"regaled the crowd with tales of the Dunedin of yore" – Critic Te Arohi, August 2021
"split-second timing, confidence, and brilliantly off-colour gags" – Otago Daily Times, March 2018
Caitlin Owen

Runner up – Otago RAW Comedy Finals 2025
Winner, Breakout Talent – Dunedin Comedy Awards 2025
Finalist – Otago RAW Comedy Finals 2024
Winner, Best Newcomer – Dunedin Comedy Awards 2024

events.id: Cantina Comedy-Sunday Cantina,1 Renown Rd, Raumati South, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu-All Events In
events.name: Cantina Comedy
events.venue: Sunday Cantina,1 Renown Rd, Raumati South, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Cantina Comedy
Laugh away the winter cold on 10th July.

Join us at Sunday Cantina in Raumati from 7pm for a stand-up showcase bringing some heat to a cold winter night.

Twelve comedians. Five minutes each. Rapid-fire punchlines, bold new material, and just enough chaos to keep things interesting. If one joke doesn’t land, don’t worry — another comic is stepping up straight after.

Expect sharp one-liners, gloriously awkward confessions, and stories that probably should’ve stayed in the group chat. It’s fast, it’s fun, and it’s the perfect Thursday night excuse to get out of the house.

Grab your tickets, your friends, a cold drink, a warm meal and settle in for a night of laughs you'll be talking about for a month.

Tickets are just $10 — cheaper than a round of drinks and guaranteed to last longer.

events.id: Rag Poets Matariki Concert-Hilltop, 17 Upland Road, Kelburn, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Rag Poets Matariki Concert
events.venue: Hilltop, 17 Upland Road, Kelburn, Wellington
events.long_description:
Gate open 6.30 for 7pm Music Start. Friday 10 July 2026.
The Rag Poets are back to celebrate Matariki. The band features Carl Evensen – vocals and percussion (Fourmyula), Rob Clarkson – drums, Alan Norman – accordion and 12 string guitar, who along with Rob played for many years with NZ’s seminal Country band The Warratahs, and Gary Creary on guitar. Gary hails from Toronto and has extensive experience including a stint in the fabled Hawks Nest Band. George Barris, on bass, was a member of The Underdogs, Jigsaw, Fresh Air, Highway, Blerta, and now also plays with Laura Collins and the Back Porch Blues Band. The Rag Poets play many of those ‘heady’ Wellington hits from the 60’s & 70’s era, as well as new material they’ve written and a bunch of covers from the likes of John Prine, Boz Scaggs and Neil Young.Teas, coffee & snacks provided. BYO anything else you'd like.

Come along and enjoy this great gig in our special space, up close and personal.
These are private house concerts, not for profit, supporting our dedicated musicians. Visit www.hilltophouseconcerts.co.nz to reserve a seat. Suggested donation $30. Email confirmation will include address and details.
Teas, Coffee and Snacks provided. You're welcome to bring anything you'd like to eat or drink.
Parking available in the Coupon zones of Upland Rd & Glasgow St free after 6pm weekdays, and free all through the weekends. The Wilson Carpark at the top of the Cable Car charges 24/7.

events.id: Delaney Davidson Baby Heavyweight Tour-St Peters Village Hall, Cnr Beach Rd and Ames St, Paekākāriki-Event Finder
events.name: Delaney Davidson Baby Heavyweight Tour
events.venue: St Peters Village Hall, Cnr Beach Rd and Ames St, Paekākāriki
events.long_description:
Delaney Davidson is a New Zealand Singer Songwriter, Musician, and Visual Artist.

A wry sense of humour, some theatre of unease, and a voice classed as not conventionally beautiful all combine to breathe life and space into the niche he has carved out for himself, winning him critical acclaim across the world for his original take on things.

His seminal influence and work with Marlon Williams on their Sad But True series as well as his visionary production on Tami Neilson’s acclaimed album’s Dynamite, Don’t Be Afraid and Chickaboom!, have also earned him great praise from the media and huge respect amongst his peers. Sync placements in The Expanse, Nashville TV Series, Bad Sisters, and Brokenwood Mysteries have taken Delaney’s music globally.

Baby Heavyweight, born in a kitchen in Switzerland, is Delaney Davidson’s 11th solo album. Somewhat more groovy than his previous releases, this is the second album he has made with Merk (Mark Perkins). Building on the smooth depth of the previous album Out Of My Head, and taking it into a new realm of story telling and emotive music.

Hitting the road with a stacked band featuring Heather Webb, Cass Basil, Alex Freer and Ryan Fisherman, Delaney sets his sights on a show that will deliver the album in all its complexity and nuance. Truly something to be witnessed. Do Not Miss Out.

Incontestably one of Aotearoas most influential and lauded contemporary songwriters - Under The Radar

…..beautifully haunting Rolling Stone

events.name: Star Glaze 2026 - Mid Winter Ceramics Festival
events.venue: Ōtaki-Māori Racing Club,Otaki, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Star Glaze 2026 - Mid Winter Ceramics Festival
A vibrant two days filled with diverse events, including Have-A-Go wheel, handbuilding and weaving on Saturday and Raku on Saturday alongside the extended pottery exhibition in the OMRC grandstand.

Join Ōtaki Pottery Club for the 2026 Star Glaze Mid Winter Ceramics Festival, an exciting celebration of art and the traditions of Matariki, set against the picturesque backdrop of Ōtaki Māori Racing Club.

The festival kicks off with a captivating opening ceremony, heralding the beginning of the festival. We wowed by the captivating exhibits, delicious food, lively music, and much more. Immerse yourself in the rich heritage of Matariki and discover the magic of pottery in a festival like no other.

Entry $10 on Friday during OMRC Raceday.

www.otakipotteryclub.org/star-glaze

events.id: All Blacks vs Italy-Hnry Stadium, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: All Blacks vs Italy
events.venue: Hnry Stadium, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Make it your mission to support the All Blacks in 2026 when they face Italy at Hnry Stadium. This is an exciting match as it’s Italy’s first test in Wellington. It’s also only the fourth time they’ve met in New Zealand since the 1987 Rugby World Cup. With a 5:10pm kick-off, the match is designed to be family-friendly. Bring the whole whanau for an action-packed afternoon at the stadium. 
The All Blacks’ fixture against Italy is part of the inaugural Nations Championship. This is a new international rugby tournament featuring 12 of the world’s top nations across July and November. Each match has competition points on the line leading to the final, so the Wellington game is important. Wellington will be awash with rugby fever, so head out, support the All Blacks and say ‘ciao’ to all the Italian fans in the city.

events.id: Kiri Te Kanawa Song Quest 2026-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Kiri Te Kanawa Song Quest 2026
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Widely regarded as the launchpad for New Zealand’s leading operatic talent, this competition has championed emerging singers since 1956. In the Kiri Te Kanawa Song Quest 2026, elite singers vie for a share of $95,500 in prizes. Watch as six finalists perform with the New Zealand Symphony Orchestra conducted by Brad Cohen, at the Grand Final Gala. International judge Frederica von Stade will announce the winner live at the end of the night. 
For the first time in its 70-year history, the competition bears the name of its most famous winner, Dame Kiri Te Kanawa. The Gisborne-born singer won the competition in 1965 and went on to an illustrious operatic career across the globe. 
The 2026 judge, Frederica von Stade, is one of the most beloved mezzo-sopranos of her generation, celebrated for her expressive artistry and enduring contribution to classical music. Affectionately known as “Flicka,” she made her Metropolitan Opera debut in 1970 and went on to perform more than 300 times with the company. In concert, she has collaborated with eminent conductors and orchestras across the globe.

events.id: Luude-Shed 6, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Luude
events.venue: Shed 6, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Christian Benson is known professionally as Luude. The Australian drum and bass producer is best known for his 2021 cover of the 1980s iconic anthem Down Under. The D&B rework gave it a whole new dynamic and sold millions of copies. Five years and many successful mixes later, Luude is riding a wave of success. He’s toured relentlessly across some of the biggest stages in dance music and now comes to Wellington’s Shed 6. Along with Down Under, you’ll hear big hits like Big City Life, TMO, and Oh My. 
Luude’s recording achievements also include an ARIA Award for Best Dance Release and a nomination for the Michael Gudinski Breakthrough Artist Award. He also won Best International Artist at the 2022 Drum and Bass Awards, three BBC Radio 1 Hottest Records, and platinum, gold, and silver Certifications. He is also half of Australian electronic duo Choomba.

events.id: Postmodern Jukebox — The Future is Vintage-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Postmodern Jukebox — The Future is Vintage
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The world’s foremost purveyors of time-twisting hits, Postmodern Jukebox, return to Wellington for one highly anticipated show at The Opera House. 
Founded in New York, the trailblazing troupe made up of singers, dancers, and instrumentalists brings their brand new ‘The Future is Vintage’ tour to New Zealand shores. Concertgoers can expect to enter a parallel musical universe where modern-day earworms and iconic pop tracks are reimagined. Expect everything from 70s rock classics and 80s Britpop hits to current chart-toppers, movie and video game soundtracks. Dress in your vintage best for the full time-travel experience. 
“We’re humbly presenting our own unique vision of a spectacular future; one that is built upon the timeless musical genres of the past and the authentically human spirit of creativity that inspired them,” says PMJ founder and arranger, Scott Bradlee. 
Come and be swept away by the romance and swagger of the past while enjoying your favourite tunes from now.

events.id: EBONY LAMB + GRAM ANTLER Tour | Vogelmorn WELLINGTON Te Whanganui-a-Tara-Vogelmorn Bowling Club,93 Mornington Road, Brooklyn, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: EBONY LAMB + GRAM ANTLER Tour | Vogelmorn WELLINGTON Te Whanganui-a-Tara
events.venue: Vogelmorn Bowling Club,93 Mornington Road, Brooklyn, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
EBONY LAMB + GRAM ANTLER Tour | Vogelmorn WELLINGTON Te Whanganui-a-Tara
ELGA Presents

⚫️EBONY LAMB + GRAM ANTLER at Vogelmorn Bowling Club
⚫️ SATURDAY 11th July | 7PM DOORS - 7.30PM MUSIC
⚫️ Limited Tickets ŵ UTR https://www.undertheradar.co.nz/gig/102939/Ebony-Lamb-And-Gram-Antler-New-Zealand-Tour.utr

EBONY LAMB and GRAM ANTLER return home from Tokyo this winter for a sweet 5-date tour of New Zealand at some of their favourite venues. Performing as a duo and releasing Gram Antler's long awaited debut album 'Novel Lover' alongside Ebony's beloved catalogue of original work and road-testing new creations inspired since relocating to Japan in 2025.

“words spilling out of a voice imbued with the honey-soaked hue of an artist like Sharon Van Etten” Rolling Stone Aus/NZ

⚫️ LOCATION: VOGELMORN BOWLING CLUB, UPSTAIRS, 93 Mornington Road, Brooklyn Wellington 6021

Don't miss this double headline performance as they traverse their own personal musical landscapes together, sliding the screen open for the audience to share in a night of intimate songwriting, atmospheric harmonies and the strong audience rapport they are renowned for.

“If heaven is not within sight, is certainly is within hearing” RNZ

⚫️ EBONY LAMB - Watch https://www.youtube.com/@ebonylambmusic
Ebony Lamb, is one of New Zealand’s finest singer-songwriters, with a poetic voice and a vivid, surrealistic intimacy, turning the minutiae of everyday life and the defining memories that stay with us into a rich melange of indie-folk-jazz-alt-pop. Her debut self-titled album was produced with two of New Zealand most prestigious artists Bic Runga and Kody Nielson and released on Nadia Reid's boutique label Slowtime Records to much critical acclaim.

Drawing comparisons to Gillian Welch, Cat Power and Neko Case, Ebony released three critically-acclaimed albums with her former band Eb & Sparrow in Wellington New Zealand before embarking on a solo career under her own name and a progressive musical direction. Ebony transforms everyday observations into a rich tapestry of sound that resonates deeply with a wide listening audience.

⚫️ GRAM ANTLER - Watch https://www.youtube.com/@GramAntler/videos
For years, Gram Antler has been a foundational, quiet force in the Aotearoa New Zealand indie landscape—anchoring the sweeping and sharp melodies of EBONY LAMB, PASHTAG and EB & SPARROW with his dynamic vocal, bass and guitar work; while also creating music for podcasts, such as RNZ’s award-winning Mr Lyttle Meets Mr Big, and the odd stand-in as a session player for FRENCH FOR RABBITS and MARLON WILLIAMS.

A reformed choirboy, a post-alt-country romantic now operating from the neon-hued, late-night sprawl of Tokyo, Japan, Gram Antler makes music that feels like a beautiful, hazy contradiction. Gram Antlers style draws on a fluid, sophisticated line from the timeless glam of Roxy Music to the velvet-gloom of Richard Hawley; Slacker Yacht-Rock—a world of hooky melodies, lush analog textures, and cinematic, late-night introspection that mirrors the atmospheric shift of his new surroundings.

‘Novel Lover‘ is Gram Antlers long awaited debut album, out July 10th 2026 and was recorded in close collaboration with Tui Award-winning, Melbourne-based producer BRETT STANTON (THE PHOENIX FOUNDATION, BEASTWARS)

LINKS TO ALL THINGS EBONY LAMB GRAM ANTLER
https://ebonylamb.com/
https://www.instagram.com/ebonylamb___/
https://linktr.ee/gramantler

events.id: Creepy Cute Market-Thistle Hall, 293 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Creepy Cute Market
events.venue: Thistle Hall, 293 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
The Creepy Cute Market returns to Wellington this winter… darker, stranger, and more irresistibly adorable than ever.

When: Saturday, 11 July 2026 | 9:30am – 2:30pm
Where: Thistle Hall, Level 1, Upper Cuba Street, Wellington

Step into a delightfully eerie world where spooky meets sweet and weirdness is celebrated. After a wildly successful run, the Creepy Cute Market is back with a fresh lineup of talented local makers, artists, and creators bringing their most hauntingly charming treasures.

Explore stalls packed with:

• Dark art, prints & illustrations
• Gothic fashion & quirky accessories
• Spooky-cute homewares & décor
• Handmade oddities, curiosities & collectibles
• Weird little treasures you absolutely don’t need… but will definitely want

Whether you live for the macabre, love all things alternative, or just enjoy discovering something a little different, this market is your invitation to embrace the strange.

Free entry | All ages welcome | Dress up if you dare

Come wander the weird side of Wellington… if you’re brave enough

11th July — Stitched Landscapes (large) - 10 - 2pm

- Build a landscape from fabric scraps
- Intro to mindful stitching techniques
- Learn on a large embroidery hoop (to take home)
- Intro to hand embroidery stitches
- Tea/coffee & snacks provided

About:
Escape to Martinborough this winter for a cosy, creative workshop, held at a gorgeous working studio in the heart of a boutique wine village.
These 'nature inspired' workshops are filled with mindful stitching, eco‑printing and indigo dyeing techniques, and will be tailored towards small groups, beautiful materials, and a slow, relaxed pace.

Workshops run Saturdays from June–Aug at Thunderpants , Naples St, Martinborough. Bookings are essential and spaces are limited, so get in quick to snaffle your spot!

Please email: steffwillmore@gmail.com to book or for more info call: 021 268 6748

- Saturdays at Thunderpants HQ - June, July & Aug
- All materials included -100% natural textiles & plant pigments
- Small groups, cosy atmosphere, hands‑on guidance by Steff Willmore, a local textile artist and sustainable arts practitioner.
- Tea, coffee and snacks provided at all workshops
- Lunch provided for 4–5 hr workshops (warming winter soup + crusty bread)
- Bookings essential - steffwillmore@gmail.com or call 021 268 6748

Free museum tours created especially for tamariki

Matariki storytime sessions with Moira Wairama

events.id: Matariki Movie Matinee: War Stories Our Mother's Never Told Us (1995)-Regent 3 Cinemas, 230 Queen Street, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: Matariki Movie Matinee: War Stories Our Mother's Never Told Us (1995)
events.venue: Regent 3 Cinemas, 230 Queen Street, Masterton
events.long_description:
Matariki Movie Matinee: War Stories Our Mothers Never Told Us (1995)

Join us for a special Matariki screening of the acclaimed Aotearoa documentary War Stories Our Mothers Never Told Us by Dame Gaylene Preston.

Seven New Zealand women share their personal stories of love, loss, marriage, and service during the Second World War. Told in their own voices and woven with archival footage, the film offers an intimate and powerful portrait of a generation whose experiences were often left unspoken.

Rather than narration, the film allows the women to speak directly, creating a simple and deeply moving record of memory, resilience, and everyday life during wartime Aotearoa.

As part of Matariki, this screening reflects the star Pōhutukawa, associated with remembrance and those who have passed. The event creates a space for reflection, storytelling, and intergenerational connection through shared experience.

Event schedule
11.30am | Optional shared kai (seasonal soup and bread) at Café Strada, Masterton
12.15pm | Film screening at Regent 3 Cinemas, Queen Street, Masterton

Following the film | Post-screening kōrero and audience Q&A with Dame Gaylene Preston

Tickets
$15 | Film only
$25 | Film + shared Matariki lunch
Cash sales available on the day, subject to availability.

Ticket proceeds
In accordance with the wishes of Dame Gaylene Preston, all $15 cinema ticket sales will be donated to Te Waiwhaakata Wairarapa Film Festival, supporting its kaupapa of celebrating, preserving, sharing, and creating films that connect people with place.

Acknowledgements
This screening is proudly supported by the Masterton District Council Matariki Fund, which contributed $500 toward cinema hire and film screening rights.

Film Duration: 95 minutes
Presented by The Story Studio as part of the 'Follow the Stars' Matariki Grant administered by Masterton District Council.

events.id: Zine-Making with Bryce Galloway-Blackwell & Sons, 110 Main Street, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Zine-Making with Bryce Galloway
events.venue: Blackwell & Sons, 110 Main Street, Greytown
events.long_description:
Join zinester, artist, and academic Bryce Galloway for a hands-on zine-making workshop. Whether you're new to zines or already immersed in DIY publishing, this workshop is a space to explore how personal writing can shift from private reflection into a creative, shareable form.

Many zines begin the same way journals do, as fragments of daily life, observations, sketches, and unfiltered thoughts. In this hands-on workshop, you will receive prompts from Bryce, and each participant will contribute a page to a collective zine that you can take home at the end. Use the skills you learn to continue working on your own individual zine in the future.

All materials are provided, and your ticket includes a copy of Bryce’s brand new book, 'Zines NZ: Punk to Present'. This richly illustrated exploration of Aotearoa’s zine culture traces the scene from its 1980s punk origins through to today’s vibrant communities, preserving voices that are often fleeting, personal, and hard to archive.

What is a zine?
A zine (pronounced “zeen”) is a noncommercial, often homemade or digital publication typically focused on niche or unconventional topics. Lo-fi and personal, zines are usually produced in small batches by passionate creators. They exist in a unique space between private diary and public storytelling - a way to give shape to perspectives that might otherwise remain unseen.

About the tutor:
Bryce Galloway is a long-time zinester whose publication Incredibly Hot Sex with Hideous People is Aotearoa’s longest-running zine. Known for its diaristic writing and playful, hand-drawn style, his work exemplifies how journal-like material can become compelling, shareable art. Bryce is also a founding member of the Kirikiriroa Hamilton Zinefest, part of the Wellington Zinefest team, and a Fine Arts lecturer at Massey University’s School of Art.

Details:
Saturday July 11th
1:30 - 4pm

Price: $65 - includes the workshop and a copy of Bryce's book 'Zines NZ: Punk to Present'
All materials provided

Please note: our venue is up one flight of stairs and there is not an elevator.
events.description:
Whether you're new to zines or already immersed in DIY publishing, this workshop is a space to explore how personal writing can shift from private reflection into a creative, shareable form.
Many zines begin the same way journals do, as fragments of daily life, observations, sketches, and unfiltered thoughts.
In this hands-on workshop, you will receive prompts from Bryce, and each participant will contribute a page to a collective zine that you can take home at the end.
All materials are provided, and your ticket includes a copy of Bryce’s brand new book, 'Zines NZ: Punk to Present'.
About the tutor: Bryce Galloway is a long-time zinester whose publication Incredibly Hot Sex with Hideous People is Aotearoa’s longest-running zine.

events.id: Champagne Country: An Insider’s Méthode Traditionnelle Experience-Alexia Urban Winery, 197 West Street, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Champagne Country: An Insider’s Méthode Traditionnelle Experience
events.venue: Alexia Urban Winery, 197 West Street, Greytown
events.long_description:
Go behind the bubbles at Alexia Urban Winery as winemaker Jane Cooper reveals the craft behind New Zealand’s “Champagne” — known as Méthode Traditionnelle.

As one of the country’s few 100% in-house producers, Alexia completes every step of the process on site. Enjoy a rare insider’s preview of what’s still ageing and developing, from base wine to finished sparkling, and discover how time, patience, and precision transform them in the bottle. Hear behind-the-scenes stories from the winery and have the language of fizz demystified so you’ll leave knowing exactly what’s in your glass and how it got there.

This is a fun, informative event - regardless of whether you’re a devoted wine enthusiast or just curious about the magic behind the mousse, you’ll enjoy an afternoon that offers a true insider’s perspective on crafting exceptional NZ fizz.

The event includes a glass of Méthode Traditionnelle on arrival, all tasting pours and light snacks. Vegetarian and GF options available. Due to the nature of this event, other dietary requests cannot be offered.

$45 per person, please purchase your tickets through https://www.alexia.co.nz/buy-alexia-wine

events.id: Matariki Double-Feature: Ngāti (1987) & The Camera on the Shore (2009)-St Peters Village Hall, Cnr Beach Rd and Ames St, Paekākāriki-Event Finder
events.name: Matariki Double-Feature: Ngāti (1987) & The Camera on the Shore (2009)
events.venue: St Peters Village Hall, Cnr Beach Rd and Ames St, Paekākāriki
events.long_description:
Barry Barclay's Ngati is of historical and cultural significance in Aotearoa New Zealand as it is the first feature film written and directed by Māori.

The film is set in 1948 in a small town on the east coast of New Zealand during the impending closure of a freezing works and the threat of unemployment for the local community.

The 4pm screening of "Ngati" will be followed by a short kai break before a 6.15pm screening of "The Camera on the Shore" (2009), a documentary about the late Barry Barclay that will be introduced by director Graeme Tuckett.

events.id: Kotuku Choir-Greytown Town Hall, 89 Main St, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Kotuku Choir
events.venue: Greytown Town Hall, 89 Main St, Greytown
events.long_description:
Kotuku Choir is a fully registered Charitable Trust ( Kotuku Music Academy Support Trust), founded to grow children from diverse backgrounds into young ambassadors who become the voice for their own communities, for Aotearoa and for those who have lost their voice through displacement or trauma.

Kotuku singers come from Blenheim, Lower Hutt, Wellington, Alfredton and Foxton!

On Saturday 11 July, Kotuku will sing their very first performance of a new arrangement of Jingle Bells NZ farmers' style.

The song will be released as a music video for Christmas 2026, but Kotuku is gifting Greytown a preview just before the magic of snowfall at 6pm in Greytown Square. Kotuku will also sing a collection of well-loved carols.

events.id: Sat Afternoon Jewellery Making - Beginners-Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Sat Afternoon Jewellery Making - Beginners
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Spend your Saturday afternoons immersed in creativity, learning the timeless craft of jewellery-making in our warm, welcoming studio.

This short course is the perfect introduction for complete beginners, guiding you step-by-step through the essential techniques used by jewellers. You’ll start by working in copper or brass, building confidence as you learn to cut, heat, shape and texture metal. From there, you can choose to move into silver and bring your ideas to life in more refined pieces.

A beautiful textured ring band is an ideal first project, but once you’ve mastered the basics, the possibilities really open up—create earrings, pendants, or small bespoke pieces that reflect your personal style. You’ll learn how to solder using a jeweller’s torch, refine your finishing techniques, and polish your work to a professional shine. It’s a hands-on, deeply satisfying process, and there’s nothing quite like wearing something you’ve made yourself.

Materials are purchased as you go, so you can tailor your projects to your budget and ideas. Beginners typically spend around $50–$70 on metals over the course.

This block is ideal for teens and young creatives aged 12 and up.
Please note: anyone under 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.

events.id: Matariki Laughs with Ben Hurley and friends-The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Matariki Laughs with Ben Hurley and friends
events.venue: The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
The Wellington Comedy Club in conjunction with Wellington Community Revitalisation Society present Matariki Laughs

With Ben Hurley
As seen on The Project, Seven Sharp, and more
With a massive but hugely likeable stage presence, Hurley can win over even the toughest room. A sharp improvisational mind and well honed original material make Hurley a popular act with audiences and critics alike.

Opeti Vaka - 2026 Billy T Award nominee
If you ask him how why he started performing comedy, he'll tell you "Well I actually started running out of options.."
Born in Wellington, with a dollop of South Auckland style, add in a sprinkle of life in the fast lane with his very large whanau and their antics, take a pinch of "what the f**k?", and wrap it all up in a person of multiple colours, those colours being 2 shades of brown (Tongan & Maori), well you get a very comedic perspective of his world.

Mamaeroa Munn
A multi-award-winning stand-up comedian, improviser and writer based in Wellington, New Zealand. A regular on the professional stand-up circuit in Pōneke, Mo has taken shows to the NZ International Comedy Festival, Melbourne Comedy Festival, TAHI solo festival of the arts, and Chicago iO Festival. In 2025, Mo debuted their first solo hour in the NZ International Comedy Fest, taking home the award for Best Newcomer.

Jak Darling
2025 Billy T Nominee and Pōneke Powerhouse Jak Darling has been charming audiences all over Aotearoa and across the ditch. Their Matariki resolution is to make you laugh so get ready for some tongue, cheek, and a whole... lot more!

events.id: Waenga-Avalon Intermediate - Te Kura Waenga o Motutawa, 1041 High St, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: Waenga
events.venue: Avalon Intermediate - Te Kura Waenga o Motutawa, 1041 High St, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
A special Matariki event of Māori theatre in Lower Hutt.

Created by Hariata & Tamati Moriarty and directed by Jim Moriarty, 'Waenga' centres on a young wahine Māori who is arrested and finds herself in a police holding cell. Alongside her journey is Peyton, who is coming to understand what a Tangata Tiriti partnership is and Grayson, a young Māori lawyer navigating his role in the system.

Performed by an ensemble cast, including well known rangatahi Hariata and Tamati Moriarty, the play is presented in Te Rākau’s unique Theatre Marae framework, creating space for collective reflection and shared experience.

Te Rākau is the longest running Māori theatre company in Aotearoa founded in 1989.

Recommended age 12+ contains swearing and depicts some violence.

events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
Lockie Bennett is a jazz guitarist based in Wellington… but not for much longer! Catch him before he leaves for the UK.

The rhythm section features Auckland-based drummer George Brajkovich and Wellington stalwart bassist Elijah Mulheron. This new and exciting permutation of the trio toured the North Island earlier this year as part of a 12-stop tour across Australia and New Zealand.

The trio will be playing the music of Charlie Parker, Bud Powell, Tadd Dameron and other notable composers of the early 20th century.

$25 General Admission

Doors at 6:30pm
Music at 7pm

Poster by Dawn Wang

events.id: Postmodern Jukebox [WELLINGTON]-Opera House-All Events In
events.name: Postmodern Jukebox [WELLINGTON]
events.venue: Opera House
events.long_description:
Postmodern Jukebox [WELLINGTON]
The world’s foremost purveyors of time-twisting hits, Postmodern Jukebox, have announced their highly-anticipated return to Australia and New Zealand in June and July 2026!

Having previously performed multiple sold-out tours across Oceania, the trailblazing troupe of singers, dancers and instrumentalists will hit our shores with their brand new The Future is Vintage tour.

“In the midst of the AI hype all around us, some folks have gone so far as to proclaim that in the not-so-distant future, all of the music that we listen to will be AI generated – we’ll gladly take the other side of that wager,” says PMJ founder and arranger, Scott Bradlee. “We’re humbly presenting our own unique vision of a spectacular future; one that is built upon the timeless musical genres of the past and the authentically human spirit of creativity that inspired them.”

Concertgoers can expect to enter a parallel musical universe where modern-day earworms and iconic pop tracks alike are reimagined. Expect everything from ’70s rock classics and ’80s Britpop hits, to current chart-toppers, movie and video game soundtracks. Dress in your vintage best, for the full time-travel experience!

A TEG Dainty presale begins on Thursday November 20 at 11am (local). Sign up for access:

https://www.tegdainty.com/tour/postmodernjukebox2026/

Tickets to general public go on sale on Friday November 21 at 11am (local).

Having previously performed multiple sold-out tours across Oceania, the trailblazing troupe of singers, dancers and instrumentalists will hit our shores with their brand new The Future is Vintage tour.
“In the midst of the AI hype all around us, some folks have gone so far as to proclaim that in the not-so-distant future, all of the music that we listen to will be AI generated – we’ll gladly take the other side of that wager,” says PMJ founder and arranger, Scott Bradlee.
“We’re humbly presenting our own unique vision of a spectacular future; one that is built upon the timeless musical genres of the past and the authentically human spirit of creativity that inspired them.” Concertgoers can expect to enter a parallel musical universe where modern-day earworms and iconic pop tracks alike are reimagined.
Expect everything from ’70s rock classics and ’80s Britpop hits, to current chart-toppers, movie and video game soundtracks.

Australian producer LUUDE is in the middle of an exciting global breakout moment. Arriving on the scene with back to back global hits "Down Under" and "Big City Life" (which he made for a bit of fun) which have sold millions of copies and earned multi-platinum certifications around the world - he followed this with the Bru-C collaboration "TMO" (UK Top 50) and the Moby sampling "Oh MY" (UK Top 100) - This run of singles makes him one of the highest selling artists in Drum and Bass across the last decade. 
In the short time since he's toured relentlessly across some of the biggest stages in dance music launched his new imprint "No Instruction" as a much needed outlet for more underground pursuits and built that brand over the last 6 months as a platform for new talent.
Luude's recording achievements also include an ARIA award for Best Dance Release, nomination for the Michael Gudinski Breakthrough Artist Award, "Best International Artist" at the 2022 Drum and Bass Awards, 3x BBC Radio 1 Hottest 100 Records and 9x Platinum, 2x Gold, 1x Silver Certifications. 


Live Nation VIP Package Terms and Conditions:
- Please note that the contact details provided at the point of purchase, email & mobile, are the details used to communicate all VIP information
- An email will be sent to the original purchaser of the tickets 2-4 business days before the event with timings and additional information.
Please contact Live Nation here if this is not received.
- A text will also be sent with the check in time & location the day before the show. Keep an eye out for a text on the day of the show in case of any changes or additional information.
Note if attending a show in Australia we are only able to send a text to Australian numbers. If attending a New Zealand show, we are only able to send a text to New Zealand numbers. At this time, we are unable to send to international numbers, outside of Australia & New Zealand.
Please contact us here if you have not received VIP Information.
- It is your responsibility as the purchaser to follow up information if it is not received
- VIP purchasers may need to arrive 1-3 hours before doors open to ensure delivery of all benefits
- Original purchasers name must be known in order to check-in and receive your benefits
- If a VIP Gift (e.g. laminate, drink voucher or merchandise item) is included in the VIP package you purchased, unless otherwise notified, VIP gifts will only be available for collection pre-show from the VIP check-in at the venue. VIP Gifts will not be dispatched after the show
- Given the dynamic nature of live events, we may need to make changes to parts of your VIP Package including seating and entry arrangements, start times, merchandise, food and beverage service arrangements and other things. When changes need to be made, where possible, we will endeavour to provide alternatives that overall have an equivalent value as your package
- If applicable, Early Entry refers to first access onto the floor, there is no sectioned off area (unless otherwise stated)
- If applicable, collectible laminate is commemorative only and does not gain or authorise access into the venue or backstage areas
- Choose carefully as we will not provide refunds for a change of mind or because you can’t make the event. Refunds and exchanges will only be made subject to ticketing terms and conditions, and applicable law, including the Australian or New Zealand Consumer Law
- For any questions regarding your concert ticket, please reach out to the point of purchase
- For any enquiries about your VIP Experience check out our FAQ’s or submit a request here

events.id: Mark de Clive-Lowe │Remixes & Reimaginations │Remix Set-Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Mark de Clive-Lowe │Remixes & Reimaginations │Remix Set
events.venue: Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
New Zealand-born, Tokyo-based pianist, composer and producer Mark de Clive-Lowe returns with Remixes & Reimaginations, a new collection celebrating decades of boundary-pushing work across jazz, broken beat, house and electronic music. Known internationally for his ability to seamlessly fuse live instrumentation, improvisation and technology, MdCL has collaborated with artists including Moodymann, Kamasi Washington, DJ Spinna, Sheila E, Kenny Dope and Kyoto Jazz Massive.

More than a traditional remix album, Remixes & Reimaginations explores the art of transformation - taking existing works and uncovering entirely new dimensions within them. Spanning soulful broken beat, deep house, jazz and experimental club sounds, the collection features reworks of artists including Close Counters, We Are KING, Omar and Anthony Joseph, each crafted with deep respect for the original while pushing the music somewhere unexpected.

Renowned for his dynamic live remix performances, Mark de Clive-Lowe creates sets that blur the line between concert, club and spontaneous composition. Every performance is built in real time, reshaping familiar sounds into something immediate, immersive and completely unique.

Support from Pōneke's own Benny Salvador, DJ, multi-instrumentalist, producer and radio show host. Benny has won over audiences throughout New Zealand with his eclectic, dancefloor-friendly selections that find the common threads between house, techno, electro, disco-funk, dub, boogie and beyond.
