events.id: Postmodern Jukebox - The Future Is Vintage-The Opera House, 111 - 113 Manners Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Postmodern Jukebox - The Future Is Vintage
events.venue: The Opera House, 111 - 113 Manners Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Back due to popular demand! Scott Bradlee’s Postmodern Jukebox to return to New Zealand with ‘The Future Is Vintage’ 2026 Tour!

NEW ZEALAND

AUCKLAND - Kiri Te Kanawa – Friday July 10
WELLINGTON - Wellington Opera House – Saturday July 11
CHRISTCHURCH - Isaac Theatre Royal – Sunday July 12

Tickets on sale Friday November 21 at 11am local time. For ticketing and tour details go to www.tegdainty.com

POSTMODERN JUKEBOX has announced their brand-new show, which will take them to cities across UK, Europe, Australia and New Zealand, to kick off their 2026 THE FUTURE IS VINTAGE World Tour.

The highly acclaimed troupe of singers, dancers and instrumentalists will be performing their new show in the group’s signature time-twisting style – putting a retro spin on everything from 70’s rock classics and 80’s Britpop hits, to current chart toppers, movie and video game soundtracks. With a heavy dose of danceable numbers alongside jaw-dropping showstoppers THE FUTURE IS VINTAGE works equally well as the perfect date night or family outing.

“In the midst of the AI hype all around us, some folks have gone so far as to proclaim that in the no-to-distant future, all of the music that we listen to will be AI generated…we’ll gladly take the other side of that wager,” say PMK founder and arranger, Scott Bradlee. “On the 2026 Postmodern Jukebox UK/Europe/Australia/New Zealand tour, we’re humbly presenting our own unique vision of a spectacular future; one that is built upon the timeless musical genres of the past and the authentically human spirit of creativity that inspired them”.

When Bradlee started making YouTube videos that remade the hits of today in the classic styles of yesterday, a “viral” success story was born —one that quickly led to sold out shows across North America and Europe in the Summer of 2014.

The touring act received rave reviews from industry publications and world-renowned artists alike. In the years that followed, “PMJ” built a reputation as the “Saturday Night Live of singers” by introducing audiences to dozens of exceptional musical artists and turning them into Bonafide stars.

Twelve years and two billion views on their YouTube channel later, a Postmodern Jukebox show has become something of an annual musical tradition for hundreds of thousands of dedicated fans, all over the world. The rare concert act that appeals across generations, older audience members will fall in love with the timeless sounds and nostalgic sensibilities, while younger fans will be thrilled by the fresh take on current pop culture and the sheer virtuosity of the performers on stage.

Dress in your vintage best for the full time-travel experience and remember: The Future Is Vintage!

As seen on The Project, Seven Sharp, and more

events.long_description:
Move and Groove workshops in the Hutt Valley
Come Dance with WIDance!

Experience the excitement and creativity of the Move and Groove Workshop, where dancing is about enjoyment and self-expression. This event welcomes everyone to join WIDance and move to our favourite dance tracks, celebrating the joy of movement together.

Workshop Activities

Participate in dance-based games designed to get everyone moving and laughing, while learning the basics of dance.
Discover different ways to move in a friendly and creative environment.
Collaborate with others to choreograph short dances set to your favourite songs.

Whether you are passionate about dancing or simply curious to give it a try, the Move and Groove Workshop offers a fantastic chance to connect with others, have fun, and express yourself through movement.

The workshop is open to anyone aged 5 to 35 and beyond. Bring plenty of energy, invite your friends, and get ready to dance and have a great time!

WHEN: Saturdays 11th and 25th July, 11am - 1pm

WHERE: Te Mako Community Centre, Naenae

COST: $15 for one person, $25 for two people, $40 for three people. A 10% discount if booking both workshops.

TO BOOK: Email or phone 021 050 1565


This project is proudly supported by Hutt City Council.

Who are we?
WIDance is one of Wellington's leading dance providers for disabled and non disabled dancers, striving to provide equal opportunities for all to dance. Established in 2010, we run open classes, workshops, and performance events throughout the Wellington region.

Our mission is to provide equal opportunities to dance experiences for diverse communities, advocating for access and empowerment.

events.long_description:
Fantastic Voyage | Tea Time Talkies
Directed by Richard Fleischer

Starring Donald Pleasence, Raquel Welch, Stephen Boyd

60th Anniversary


--- The brilliant scientist Jan Benes develops a way to shrink humans, and other objects, for brief periods of time. Benes, who is working in communist Russia, is transported by the CIA to America, but is attacked en route. In order to save the scientist, who
has developed a blood clot in his brain, a team of Americans in a nuclear submarine is shrunk and injected into Benes' body. They have a finite period of time to fix the clot and get out before the miniaturization wears off. ---

This is a TEA TIME TALKIE screening where a delicious Devonshire Tea* awaits for upgraded tickets holders (a cup of tea and traditional homemade scones with cream and jam). This is served upstairs in the Grand Lobby at the intermission.

Admit only tickets are available for $15 or you can upgrade to include a Devonshire Tea for $24.

*No dietaries catered for.

Schedule:
2:35pm – Cinema doors open
2:45pm - Feature starts
3:30pm - Intermission / Devonshire Tea available for collection upstairs in the Grand Lobby
3:45pm – Feature resumes

The Undercurrent presents The Bitter Brothers' Honky-Tonk Circus!

events.name: School Holiday Steam
events.venue: Paekakariki Station Museum, Tilley Rd, Paekākāriki
events.long_description:
School Holiday Steam
Steam Trains between Paekakariki & Porirua | 12th July 2026

Break the School Holiday Boredom!
Join Steam Incorporated this School Holidays, as we invite you onboard our heritage train for a brilliant steam train ride for the whole family!

Trains are operating all Sunday between Paekakariki and Porirua, a beautiful scenic run along the Paekakariki Escarpment on the Kapiti Coast. In our run between Pukerua Bay and Paekakariki, we pass through 6 short tunnels! A section of railway line with origins dating back to 1886. A scenic train journey for the whole family, steam along the coast onboard our heritage train with our friendly team!

These trains are topped & tailed by one of our coal-fired steam locomotives, and our Da class diesel electric.

Return Fares

Family - $99 - 2 Adults & 2 Children

General Admission - $29 - Aged 3+

Infant - Free - No allocated seat

Available Times Departing Paekakariki:
Sunday 12th July

Departing Paekakariki Station at 8:45am
Departing Paekakariki Station at 10:15am
Departing Paekakariki Station at 11:45am
Departing Paekakariki Station at 1:45pm
Departing Paekakariki Station at 3:15pm


Available Times Departing Porirua:
Sunday 12th July

Departing Porirua Station at 9:35am
Departing Porirua Station at 11:05am
Departing Porirua Station at 12:35pm - With Short Layover!
Departing Porirua Station at 2:35pm

The 12:35 pm departure will be stopped in Paekakariki for approximately 30mins.

Beat the School Holiday blues— our steam train awaits.
Please note that if booking online, our booking system does add on a 5% booking fee to the above fares. Steam Incorporated does not see this revenue.

Group bookings of 10+ please contact us via email admin@steaminc.org.nz or call us on 0800 783 264 for a personalized package.

events.id: Curly Hair Cutting Look & Learn - Tahni Daniels & Donna The Curl Next Door-Arthur AndrewWellington, Wellington Region-Event Brite
events.name: Curly Hair Cutting Look & Learn - Tahni Daniels & Donna The Curl Next Door
events.venue: Arthur AndrewWellington, Wellington Region
events.long_description:
Curl-cutting class teaching simple techniques, styling and content tips to grow a diverse clientele with guidance from Tahni and Donna
What to expect
Join Tahni and Donna for a live Look & Learn focused on simple, effective, salon‑friendly curl cutting. This class centres on Tahni’s award‑winning textured cut — the method that earned her the 2025 Global Digital Hair Award for Best Texture Cut.

Tahni will break down her curl‑cutting approach in a way that is clear, practical, and immediately usable behind the chair. She’ll share:

• How she uses precision sectioning and clean, controlled cutting
• How to create consistent shape across a wide range of curl patterns — helping you confidently work with more diverse clients
• The tools and products she relies on every day in the salon
• Her step‑by‑step styling process for achieving volume, definition, and longevity
• How she has grown her brand and built a loyal following
• Simple, approachable content‑creation strategies to help you grow your business and attract a more diverse clientele

Tahni and Donna have a strong, balanced working dynamic that elevates the entire class experience. They work seamlessly together, offering different perspectives that complement one another. Donna is an incredible asset to Tahni, adding clarity, real‑world application, and supportive insight throughout every stage of the demonstration.

This class is designed for stylists at every stage whether you’re refining your curl skills or building confidence, consistency, and a more inclusive clientele. You’ll leave with techniques and strategies that are grounded, practical, and ready to use immediately behind the chair and online.

events.venue: Space Place at Carter Observatory, 40 Salamanca Rd, Kelburn, Wellington
events.long_description:
Nau mai haere mai! Matariki is upon us once more, and we invite you to celebrate the New Year with us at Space Place.

This is a time for us to remember and honour our loved ones who have passed, reflect and express gratitude for what we have, and look forward to the year ahead.

Bring your whānau, chosen or extended, for a day of live Matariki star talks in the planetarium, kapa haka from Tīwhanawhana, a whānau craft activity, and storytelling with Moira Wairama.

There are limited tickets available, book now to secure your place.

events.id: Otaki Market - July-Otaki Market, 58 Main Highway, Ōtaki, Kapiti Coast, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Otaki Market - July
events.venue: Otaki Market, 58 Main Highway, Ōtaki, Kapiti Coast, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Nau Mai Haere Mai, meet you at the Market.
Discover a vibrant outdoor market nestled on State Highway One in Ōtaki, just across from New World. Join us to greet our regular stallholders and special guests from the community.
---
PARKING
There is plenty of free parking available at the Otaki train station.
Main Highway is a perfect spot for customers to the market
60 Minute Parking zone behind Riverstone Cafe.

CASH / ATM
Some vendors have eftpos, and all take cash. There is an ATM located on the main highway.

FOOD VENDORS
And when hunger strikes, our lineup of food trucks and stalls is ready to tantalize your taste buds. From waffles to hangi, seafood chowder to fry bread, sausage sizzles, and a delectable assortment of rice, noodles, and home baking – a feast awaits.

BUSKER
We often have an awesome busker, who'll be playing chilled out middle of the road tunes.

- Art & Craft
- Native trees, flowers and plants
- Candles, soaps and wax melts
- Antiques and collectables
- Produce, including yummy honey!
- Pre-love clothing, shoes and accessories
- Tools for the home and garden
- Toys and books for tamariki

---
STALL HOLDERS
Would you like to be a stall holder?
Join our market family, fill out the enquiry form: www.otakiwomensclub.org/stall-holder

events.id: Vibratory Attunement Matariki Celebration-Builders of the Adytum New Zealand, 1 Treadwell Street, Naenae, Lower Hutt 5011, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: Vibratory Attunement Matariki Celebration
events.venue: Builders of the Adytum New Zealand, 1 Treadwell Street, Naenae, Lower Hutt 5011, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Welcome everyone.

Join with us in celebrating the spiritual relationship between ourselves, Astrology, the Holy Qabalah and Matariki.
Join with us in celebrating the oneness of life.
Join with us in meditation.

The Builders of the Adytum (B.O.T.A.) vibratory attunement ritual is a spiritual practice that aims to align the aspirant with higher vibrational frequencies and foster a deeper connection to universal forces.

Guided imagery or personal visualisations are used to focus your mind and connect with symbolic archetypes and divine forces.

events.id: Alice in Wonderland-Southward Theatre, Otaihanga Rd, Otaihanga, Paraparaumu-Event Finder
events.name: Alice in Wonderland
events.venue: Southward Theatre, Otaihanga Rd, Otaihanga, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
When curious Alice falls down the rabbit hole, she lands in the topsy-turvy world of Wonderland, where nothing makes sense and everything is wonderfully silly!
She meets the White Rabbit, the cheeky Khandallah Cat, and a host of oddball characters… all whispering about the terrifying Jabberwocky, who everyone is scared of, but no one has ever actually seen.
Meanwhile, the fiery Queen of Hearts is demanding jam tarts, ready to shout “Off with their heads!” at any moment!
With laughs, songs, and plenty of audience fun, Alice must navigate the madness, scare the Jabberwocky out of Wonderland and find her way home.

events.id: Between the Wines & the Lines: Where Great Books Meet Beautiful Wines-Alexia Urban Winery, 197 West Street, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Between the Wines & the Lines: Where Great Books Meet Beautiful Wines
events.venue: Alexia Urban Winery, 197 West Street, Greytown
events.long_description:
Join winemaker Jane Cooper in conversation with Mrs Blackwells Village Bookshop owner Millie Blackwell as they explore the art of matching books to wine.

Jane will present four Alexia wines, each showcasing a different style and story in the glass. To complement each pour, Millie will introduce both a fiction and a non-fiction title chosen to echo the wine’s character and mood - creating an experience that weaves together taste, texture, aroma, and narrative.

Traversing wine styles and literary genres in a fun atmosphere, it’s an afternoon designed to open minds to new reading and wine discoveries. Leave with a fresh appreciation for how books and wine can complement one another — and perhaps with a new bottle and book to enjoy at home.

Purchase your tickets from https://www.alexia.co.nz/buy-alexia-wine. Limited numbers, $25 per person.

Event ticket includes all tastings. Books and wines tasted will also be available to purchase on the day.

events.id: Clay & Create workshop-Wellington Candle Co., Queen Charlotte Drive, Aotea, Porirua - Mana-Event Finder
events.name: Clay & Create workshop
events.venue: Wellington Candle Co., Queen Charlotte Drive, Aotea, Porirua - Mana
events.long_description:
Unplug, slow down, and enjoy an afternoon of creativity.

Join us for a relaxed small-group clay workshop where you'll create your own beautiful embossed jewellery dish using air-dry clay (feel free to let your imagination run - we'll be using air-dry clay to create decorative pieces such as trinket dishes, bowls, ornaments, plant markers, incense holders, or whatever inspires you). No experience is needed – just bring yourself and enjoy the process.

This workshop is designed for adults looking for a mindful and creative experience. Whether you're coming with friends, family, or simply treating yourself, you'll enjoy a welcoming atmosphere, good conversation, and the satisfaction of creating something unique with your own hands.

Using quality air-dry clay, you'll learn simple techniques to shape, emboss, and personalise your jewellery dish using a selection of stamps, textures, and decorative elements. Your finished piece can be used to hold jewellery, keys, crystals, keepsakes, or simply displayed as a decorative piece in your home.

What's Included
• All clay and materials provided
• Use of stamps, textures and decorative tools
• Step-by-step guidance throughout the session
• Tea, coffee and light refreshments
• A small group experience (maximum 7 guests)

events.id: Elouan Quartet with Bridget Douglas (Flute)-St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Elouan Quartet with Bridget Douglas (Flute)
events.venue: St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
A diverse, intimate and engaging presentation of mixed ensemble chamber music for flute and string quartet. The performers are Jessica Oddie – violin, Anna van der Zee – violin, Alexander McFarlane – viola, Ken Ichinose – cello, and Bridget Douglas - flute. All are NZSO players.

This programme moves between the rich Romantic lyricism of Amy Beach and Arthur Foote, the contemporary fluidity of Salina Fisher’s Mata-Au (inspired by the Clutha River), and the rhythmic energy of Alberto Ginastera’s folk-infused Impresiones de la Puna. Ludwig van Beethoven’s string quartet anchors the programme, offering a timeless contrast to the surrounding works. Audiences will be immersed in a journey through soundscapes that evoke flowing rivers, twilight atmospheres, and dynamic folk rhythms. With a strong connection to water and place, this programme resonates deeply in Aotearoa, where waterways are both culturally and environmentally significant.

Amy Beach, Theme and Variations for Flute and String Quartet, Op 80
Salina Fisher, Mata-Au (for string trio)
Arthur Foote, A Night Piece for Flute and String Quartet
Ludwig van Beethoven, String Quartet No 6 in B-flat major Op 18
Alberto Ginastera, Impresiones de la Puna (flute and string quartet)

This concert is presented in association with Chamber Music NZ

events.id: SOUNZ at Toi Aro - Rangatuone/Conduction Workshop-Toi Pōneke Arts Centre, 3-6 Market Lane, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: SOUNZ at Toi Aro - Rangatuone/Conduction Workshop
events.venue: Toi Pōneke Arts Centre, 3-6 Market Lane, Wellington
events.long_description:
Riki Pirihi leads a new conducted work, inviting the music community of Pōneke to create music together in celebration of SOUNZ Centre for New Zealand Music's relocation into Toi Aro. This work is supported by Toi Aro, and Wellington City Council, with funding from the Nicholas Tarling Charitable Trust.

To attend, register on the SOUNZ website: https://sounz.org.nz/news/rangatuone

Rangatuone (the weaving of gestures) represents an innovative exploration at the intersection of Indigenous musical traditions and contemporary compositional practices. Riki will lead a workshop with some written material and some guided improvisation for musicians, with an informal public performance to follow.

Sunday 12 July, Toi Aro, 3 Market Lane

- 3pm - workshop. The workshop is free to attend, but places are limited. Please register on the SOUNZ website: https://sounz.org.nz/news/rangatuone
- 5pm - performance, with kai and kōrero to follow

Matariki Together Nau mai haere mai!
Matariki is upon us once more, and we invite you to celebrate the New Year with us at Space Place.
This is a time for us to remember and honour our loved ones who have passed, reflect and express gratitude for what we have, and look forward to the year ahead.
Bring your whānau, chosen or extended, for a day of live Matariki star talks in the planetarium, kapa haka from Tīwhanawhana, a whānau craft activity, and storytelling with Moira Wairama.
There are limited tickets available, book now to secure your place.

events.long_description:
Otaki Market - July
Nau Mai Haere Mai, meet you at the Market. 🌞

Discover a vibrant outdoor market nestled on State Highway One in Ōtaki, just across from New World. Join us to greet our regular stallholders and special guests from the community.
---
PARKING
There is plenty of free parking available at the Otaki train station.
Main Highway is a perfect spot for customers to the market
60 Minute Parking zone behind Riverstone Cafe.

CASH / ATM
Some vendors have eftpos, and all take cash. There is an ATM located on the main highway.

FOOD VENDORS
And when hunger strikes, our lineup of food trucks and stalls is ready to tantalize your taste buds. From waffles to hangi, seafood chowder to fry bread, sausage sizzles, and a delectable assortment of rice, noodles, and home baking – a feast awaits.

BUSKER
We often have an awesome busker, who'll be playing chilled out middle of the road tunes.

🎨 Art & Craft
🌳 Native trees, flowers and plants
🍶 Candles, soaps and wax melts
🕰️ Antiques and collectables
🍯 Produce, including yummy honey!
👚 Pre-love clothing, shoes and accessories
⚒️ Tools for the home and garden
🧸 Toys and books for tamariki
Come for the finds, stay for the flavor.
---
STALL HOLDERS
Would you like to be a stall holder?
Join our market family, fill out the enquiry form: www.otakiwomensclub.org/stall-holder

events.id: Holiday Programme - The Voice Changer-Miramar Central School, 38 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Holiday Programme - The Voice Changer
events.venue: Miramar Central School, 38 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Ever wanted to sound like a robot, a monster, a space explorer, or something completely out of this world? Introducing The Voice Changer — a hands-on electronics project that lets you record your voice, save your sound files, and then play them back with weird and wonderful effects.

The unit has its own handheld microphone, built-in speaker, headphone output, screen, buttons and menu controls. You can save up to 6 recordings of around 20 seconds each, then experiment with effects like echo, pitch shifting, distortion, tremolo and reverb to completely transform the sound. You can control everything from the unit itself, or connect to it from a phone or computer to adjust settings using a simple web interface.

On the day you will get to solder the electronics, assemble the laser-cut case, fit the speaker, screen, buttons and microphone, and load the code onto the onboard microcontroller. The unit is powered by a USB cable so no batteries required. At the end of the day you’ll walk away with a fully working voice-changing gadget that you built yourself, ready to prank your family, invent strange characters, or create your own sci-fi sound effects.

No previous experience is required and you’re welcome to bring a grown up if you want to.

MakeRoom holiday programmes run every term break as well as over the summer. They are made up of one day workshops focusing on a big build with lots of fun learning along the way. Everyone gets to take the project they have constructed home with them.

- One day sessions
- Only 8 Kids per instructor
- 9am to 3.30pm
- $140 (including GST) for the day
- Open to kids aged 10-15
- Parents and Guardians welcome

On the day:

- Students should bring their own food and water bottle (morning tea and lunch)
- Students should wear closed toed shoes for safety in the workshop
- Students with long hair should have it tied back for safety

events.id: Intro to 2D Traditional Animation - Creative Arts Holiday Programme-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Intro to 2D Traditional Animation - Creative Arts Holiday Programme
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
This is a beginners course in 2D animation where tweens & teens will learn the time-honoured principles of hand-drawn animation and how to apply them to your own animation project. Over this two day workshop, students may choose to work on their own project and incorporate their own ideas with the help of our tutor, or if you'd rather, undertake an assigned project.

You can use any device, ipad or laptop you already own (except a mobile – it's really not big enough to do good animation on). The school does not provide any digital equipment. It's important that the app/programme is installed on the student's device before the course begins, and that they have had a play on it to get used to how it works. This is so that the student is at least a little familiar with their chosen software so that it's enough to begin learning animation techniques on day one. More information, including a list of free to use programmes can be found at our website.

This workshop is for ages 10 and above.

Two day workshop: Monday & Tuesday, 13 & 14 July, 10-1pm

events.id: MON Wellington - Empower Her Networking - Visibility & Owning Your Presence-The Arborist Rooftop BarWellington, Wellington Region-Event Brite
events.name: MON Wellington - Empower Her Networking - Visibility & Owning Your Presence
events.venue: The Arborist Rooftop BarWellington, Wellington Region
events.long_description:
Empower Her, female networking group that offers a welcoming and safe environment for women of all business backgrounds and experiences
WELLINGTON - The Arborist Rooftop Bar
Monthly Topic: Visibility & Owning Your Presence Online and In Person
Hosted by: Tabitha Arthur
Visibility is not about being everywhere or having something new to say every single day. It's about showing up consistently, in the right places, with a message that the right people can hear and recognise. This month we're exploring what it really means to own your presence, online and in person, and how to get clear on a core message that you can repeat with confidence until it sticks.
Want to get your ticket for FREE?
Are you a woman in business looking for a supportive community to help you connect, grow, and learn?
As an Inner Circle member, you can attend as many of our networking hubs as you like for FREE! This includes our hubs across Auckland, NZ, Australia and the UK. Enjoy unlimited access to our netwokring events and take full advantage of the community and networking opportunities.
Join the Inner Circle
Join the Inner Circle to unlock even more benefits:
Monthly Masterclasses: Learn from industry experts on topics that will elevate your business and mindset.
Accountability Calls: Stay on track with our supportive community and achieve your goals.
Personal Branding Opportunities: Create your own masterclasses and shine as an expert.
Exclusive Discounts: Enjoy special rates on Empower Her events and live workshops.
Vibrant Community: Share your journey, get support, and celebrate your successes with empowering women.
Join the Inner Circle now and become part of a wonderful community of women in business who are ready to build their dream life and business.
Empower Her is a global female networking group, that offers a welcoming and safe environment for women of all business backgrounds and experiences.
Whether you're just starting out with new business ideas or have years of experience under your belt, all are welcome! Empower Her provides a safe space where you can share your business with others, connect with like-minded women in business, and prioritise collaboration over competition.
Our groups value the power of connection, and we believe that together, we can achieve more than we ever could alone. That's why we're committed to fostering a supportive community that empowers each member to reach their full potential. Our groups are focused on connection, inspiring stories, celebrating each other's successes and sharing our vulnerable moments.
Join our free community
The Inner Circle
Follow us on Instagram
Empower Her Co-Founders Victoria and Charlotte are highly passionate and dedicated to empowering female leaders in the business world. By joining Empower Her, you will not only expand your professional network but also gain access to a supportive community of like-minded individuals who are committed to helping each other succeed.Find out more about us and check up our upcoming events on our website.

events.id: Rewa Rewa Station Craft Day-Rewa Rewa Station, 166 Rewa Rewa Road, Tinui, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: Rewa Rewa Station Craft Day
events.venue: Rewa Rewa Station, 166 Rewa Rewa Road, Tinui, Masterton
events.long_description:
Our on-farm Craft Open Days are aimed primarily at knitters, spinners, crocheters and other fibre crafters.

Nestled in the small rural community of Tinui is Rewa Rewa Station. Operating as a hill country Sheep & Beef breeding farm with a strong focus on NZ Wool & Fibre. Romney, Corriedale cross Romney, Polwarth & Gotland sheep provide the fleeces to create fibre products for crafters, which are sold via the Rewa Rewa Station website, and at Fibre Markets. Our Studio Shop is also open during Craft Days or by appointment outside of these days.

Our Craft Days allow you to work on your own project, seek advice, learn a new fibre skill or maybe just spend time with fellow fibre crafters while potentially ticking off a project or two in the process.

Please bring your knitting, crocheting, spinning, hand sewing, or any project you are working on and enjoy a drive in the country!

Coffee, tea and snacks provided, but bring your lunch and stay awhile.

Everyone is welcome, the more the merrier!

Note: Craft Days are primarily held on the 3rd Saturday of every mont,h but occasionally we do have to juggle the dates.

events.id: Pottery Wheel Throwing & Hand Building for Teens-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Pottery Wheel Throwing & Hand Building for Teens
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Explore the techniques of pottery making including creating a mug using handbuilding processes and making a small bowl using throwing techniques on a pottery wheel. Over three fun and focussed sessions, students will be shown how to centre clay and make a small bowl on the wheel and trim it. Cups and bowls will be glazed by the tutor and ready for pickup approximately 3-4 weeks later depending on drying conditions.

This workshop is limited to three students only to ensure each person has individualised attention.

Bring an apron or wear clothes that you are comfortable getting clay on.

Suitable for ages 12+.

Monday - Wed, 1-3pm.

events.id: Paint and Wine Night - Matariki Stars-The Arborist Rooftop Bar, 166 Willis St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Paint and Wine Night - Matariki Stars
events.venue: The Arborist Rooftop Bar, 166 Willis St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Looking for a creative night out in Wellington? Join us for a fun beginner-friendly adults painting class in a bar!

Our artist guides you step-by-step, so absolutely no art experience is needed. It’s the perfect excuse to switch off, get creative, and try something a little different.

Whether you’re coming along solo, planning a date night, or organising a catch-up with friends, Paintvine is all about good vibes, creativity, and having fun while you paint – you might even surprise yourself with what you can create!

Drinks: No drinks are included in the ticket price, but drinks are available for purchase.

Food: A delicious menu will be available to purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit! Our events are held in licensed venues, so you can purchase drinks to sip on while you paint. At the end of the night, you’ll leave with a painting you created yourself – you might even surprise yourself with what you can achieve!

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website ⇒ paintvine.co.nz

events.id: THE ALIVENESS OF THINGS: HARO LEE, HELEN RICKERBY, AND AIRINI BEAUTRAIS-Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa,Red Gates te Papa, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: THE ALIVENESS OF THINGS: HARO LEE, HELEN RICKERBY, AND AIRINI BEAUTRAIS
events.venue: Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa,Red Gates te Papa, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
THE ALIVENESS OF THINGS: HARO LEE, HELEN RICKERBY, AND AIRINI BEAUTRAIS
Debut poet Haro Lee’s Watching Television in a Love Motel takes on self, family, and past though the cultural lens of TV. Helen Rickerby’s genre-bending poetic memoir, My Bourgeois Apocalypse, is made with fragments from journals of the ‘weird years’, including the pandemic, while Airini Beautrais’s return to poetry finds her crafting a portrait of an uneasy time in The Salt Quilt, both sharp-witted and self-aware.

These three innovative poets are joined by Chris Tse for a conversation about bending poetic form to make past and future come alive.

Writers on Mondays is presented by the International Institute of Modern Letters and The Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa. The 2026 series will run from 12.15—1.15 pm each Monday from 6 July to 28 September at Te Papa and Circa Theatre, with two special evening sessions at Meow. Admission is free and all are welcome. The series is supported by the Letteri family.

The full programme can be viewed here: https://www.wgtn.ac.nz/modernletters/about/events/writers-mondays

Come celebrate Aotearoa writers with us at Writers on Mondays – we look forward to seeing you there.

events.id: Time Management Workshop-Toitu Poneke Community & Sports Centre - The , 49 Kilbirnie Crescent, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Time Management Workshop
events.venue: Toitu Poneke Community & Sports Centre - The , 49 Kilbirnie Crescent, Wellington
events.long_description:
Do you need more hours in your day? Would you like to become less busy and more productive? Learn how to prioritise, how to handle interruptions, plan ahead, beat common time wasters, delegate and much more. You've all heard the expression 'Time is money' Well, actually, time is more valuable than money because time is a non-renewable resource. Do you ever have those times when you've been busy all day but feel that you have not achieved what you need to? Effective Time Management is crucial in today's busy and competitive world. Successes and failures in work and daily activities largely depend on how time is managed. Therefore, Time Management is the number one skill to master. Do you ever feel overwhelmed by the sheer volume of work to be done? Do you ever feel more negative than positive? Then our Time Management workshop is for you. Let us show you and your organisation how to get the most out of a day with better time management skills.

To book email - info@biztrainers.co.nz or call 04 974 8790

events.id: Holiday Programme - Ancient Runes Puzzle Box-Maungaraki School, Dowse Drive, Maungaraki, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: Holiday Programme - Ancient Runes Puzzle Box
events.venue: Maungaraki School, Dowse Drive, Maungaraki, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Introducing our new updated “Ancient Runes Puzzle Box”. Keep your treasures safe in the coolest way possible. Only you will know the special code to release the catch hidden under the mechanical Iris! This new, improved version has 2 code wheels that you can use to set your own unique code, as well as 2 code pins that are stored on the box but have to be inserted correctly to activate the mechanism. If everything is set a hidden push button will then activate a solenoid that releases the catch.

Powered by 4 AAA batteries (provided), you’ll get to solder the components, screw and glue all the intricate pieces together and unwrap the puzzle to learn how it works. And, of course, you'll get to take it home! No previous experience is required, and you’re welcome to bring a grown-up if you want to.

MakeRoom holiday programmes run every term break as well as over the summer. They are made up of one-day workshops focusing on a big build with lots of fun learning along the way. Everyone gets to take the project they have constructed home with them.

- One day sessions
- Only 8 Kids per instructor
- 9am to 3.30pm
- $140 (including GST) for the day
- Open to kids aged 10-15
- Parents and Guardians welcome

On the day:

- Students should bring their own food and water bottle (morning tea and lunch)
- Students should wear closed toed shoes for safety in the workshop
- Students with long hair should have it tied back for safety

events.name: The Pantoloons Present Alice in Wonderland
events.venue: Wairarapa Events Centre, Holloway St, Carterton
events.long_description:
When curious Alice falls down the rabbit hole, she lands in the topsy-turvy world of Wonderland, where nothing makes sense and everything is wonderfully silly!
She meets the White Rabbit, the cheeky Khandallah Cat, and a host of oddball characters… all whispering about the terrifying Jabberwocky, who everyone is scared of, but no one has ever actually seen.

Meanwhile, the fiery Queen of Hearts is demanding jam tarts, ready to shout “Off with their heads!” at any moment!
With laughs, songs, and plenty of audience fun, Alice must navigate the madness, solve the mystery, and find her way home.

Duration: 1 hour

Seating Layout: Small Seating block with some flat floor seating.

events.name: Night Sky Gazing Walk
events.venue: Wellington Region,Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Night Sky Gazing Walk
This walk is for anyone keen on walking to a spot that has very little light pollution. The walk is a gentle walk along a track. Suitable for kids. No dogs on this walk, just people.
On this date we will be experiencing a new moon so there will be 0% illumination.
This makes it the most ideal time to star gaze.

Whats needed for this walk will be the following….
A good head torch…
Gumboots or good shoes. The grass will be wet and the ground will be soft.
Warm clothing.
We will cap this walk to 40 people (going) so if it’s something you are serious about please make sure mark going on the event.
Bring your camera along or your phone to snap some photos. Chloe and I will be happy to tell you how to get a great photo at night 📸

events.id: Waenga-Te Tūhunga Rau (Strathmore Community Centre), 108 Strathmore Avenue, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Waenga
events.venue: Te Tūhunga Rau (Strathmore Community Centre), 108 Strathmore Avenue, Wellington
events.long_description:
Created by Hariata & Tamati Moriarty and directed by Jim Moriarty, 'Waenga' centres on a young wahine Māori who is arrested and finds herself in a police holding cell. Alongside her journey is Peyton, who is coming to understand what a Tangata Tiriti partnership is and Grayson, a young Māori lawyer navigating his role in the system.

Performed by an ensemble cast, including well known rangatahi Hariata and Tamati Moriarty, the play is presented in Te Rākau’s unique Theatre Marae framework, creating space for collective reflection and shared experience.

Te Rākau is the longest running Māori theatre company in Aotearoa founded in 1989.

Recommended age 12+ contains swearing and depicts some violence. Children under 12 can accompany for free with a paying adult.

events.id: Ōtari Glow Worm Tour-Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Ōtari Glow Worm Tour
events.venue: Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington
events.long_description:
Take a guided night tour through Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush and discover some of the fascinating night-time residents on your way to see the titiwai/glowworms. Enjoy the tranquil beauty of the bush after dark and witness the magical glow of these unique creatures.

This outdoor night-time tour takes just over an hour.
The tour requires a moderate level of fitness; the route includes numerous steps and a steep incline to and from the glowworms.
No dogs, and not suitable for babies and young children.

Meet at the Ōtari Visitors Centre, 150 Wilton Road. Wrap up in warm clothes, wear sturdy walking shoes, and bring a torch or head lamp.

Glow Worm Tour Ticket Refund Policy:
• No Refunds: We do not offer refunds for Glow Worm Tour tickets.
• Ticket Transfers: If you can’t attend your booked tour, with 2 weeks notice we can put you on a future tour, subject to availability.
• Weather Conditions: Tours operate rain or shine. In the event of extreme weather or unsafe conditions, we will reschedule your tour or provide a refund.

For any changes or enquiries, please get in touch with us in advance at Treehouse@wcc.govt.nz or call 04 499 1400.

This tour is operated by the Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush Trust. Join the Trust from only $20 a year and the glow worm tour is free, plus you’ll be supporting the amazing work the Trust does. Join today at owbt.nz/membership-donations/

events.id: A free workshop for the general public "The Better Endings Project"-Nelson Tasman HospiceNelson, Nelson-Event Brite
events.name: A free workshop for the general public "The Better Endings Project"
events.venue: Nelson Tasman HospiceNelson, Nelson
events.long_description:
How to support care of the dying and create compassionate communities
What is the Better Endings Project
The Better Endings Project is about educating the community on the basics of caring for people who are dying. This workshop is for the general public and is not designed for health professionals.
Who is the course for?
For the general public who find themselves supporting and caring for people approaching end of life or those anticipating this situation.
What does Nelson Tasman Hospice offer?
The facilitators will give practical tips on how you can provide comfort to someone who is dying, and what you can do to plan for your own and your family's future. This programme is an interactive course and limited to 12 participants. Please register early to avoid disappointment.

Participants will learn:
What to expect when someone is dying
How compassionate communities can improve care of dying
Why end of life planning is important and what resources are available
Simple measures to provide comfort at end of life
About the sanctity of the last hours/days of life and how to cope and support others after death

Details:
Who can attend? General Public - This workshop is not designed for Health Professionals
Date:Wednesday 15 July 2026
Time: 1.00pm - 3.30pm
Cost: Free to attend
Venue: Nelson Tasman Hospice, 331 Suffolk Road, Stoke, Nelson
For more information contact the Nelson Tasman Hospice Education Team
E: education@nelsonhospice.org.nz
T: (03) 546 3950

events.id: Revolution's Daughter-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Revolution's Daughter
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026 - Opening Night

His revolution. Her truth.
Alina Fernández grew up in Havana not knowing she was Fidel Castro’s daughter, an open secret kept from her alone. Years before escaping Cuba and rebuilding her life in Miami, she made a choice that would define her: to speak out, and to keep speaking out. She calls it the only thing she ever really did, the one act she refused to shrink from. The film weaves Alina’s defiance into the broader fabric of Miami’s Cuban exile community, where artists and storytellers across generations carry the inheritance of revolution in their own ways, exploring identity, belonging, and what it costs to use your voice when silence would be easier.

events.id: The Racket Lounge-Bedlam and Squalor, Upstairs, 18 Garrett Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Racket Lounge
events.venue: Bedlam and Squalor, Upstairs, 18 Garrett Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Grab a spot on our couch and enjoy the noise at The Racket Lounge - a chill classical music series showcasing up-and-coming Pōneke musicians.
If you’ve never been to a classical concert before, or if you have and now you want to see talented emerging musicians experimenting and playing what they want to play, then The Racket Lounge is for you. Over the past two seasons we’ve seen classical covers, jazz ensembles, video game soundtracks, original compositions, loop pedals, poetry and much more. Our 2026 season is shaping up to be just as epic, plus with your tickets starting at just $5 you can afford to take a chance on something new.
Each concert will feature a different line up of musicians and genres, to be announced a week before each session. If you’re a musician looking to perform at The Racket Lounge, then head to the New Zealand String Quartet’s website to see how to apply.
So, we’ll see you at Bedlam and Squalor to make a racket!
The Racket Lounge is created and presented by the New Zealand String Quartet Trust - Te Rōpū Tūrū o Aotearoa, New Zealand’s longest-running professional string quartet. With support from Wellington City Council.
Our ticket prices range from $5 to $100, so just pick the ticket price you feel like paying. At The Racket Lounge, we believe everyone should have access to high-quality but affordable art. If you're a bit strapped for cash right now, but still want a good night out, no worries! Just book yourself a $5 ticket! If you can afford to pay a little more and want to do some good for your community, then pick one of our higher ticket prices and rest assured that your dollars are going towards funding great, accessible art in your city.
All proceeds from ticket sales will go directly into presenting the next Racket Lounge, including paying musicians and artists, hiring instruments and marketing costs. Plus, by choosing a higher price point, you're ensuring that we're able to continue to offer tickets at a lower price point for those who need it.
PLEASE NOTE: Bedlam & Squalor is a licensed venue, so under 18s must be accompanied by a parent or legal guardian. Everyone must bring ID.

events.id: Public Lecture - Prof Heidi Hornik-St John's in the City Presbyterian Church, 170 Willis Street., Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Public Lecture - Prof Heidi Hornik
events.venue: St John's in the City Presbyterian Church, 170 Willis Street., Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Discover how art brings meaning to life.

Looking at Select Western Paintings: Art Historical and Theological Insights.

An analysis of visual examples will show how Western painters have interpreted biblical stories in ways that shape and reflect faith, ethics, and everyday
action.

Throughout the centuries, painters trained in the Western tradition have used their formal skills and talents to interpret biblical stories using their mind’s eyes. In Christian communities, the biblical narrative served as a thematic source of reflection on a contemporary issue of that society (e.g., Chrisitan habits and virtues, moral issues facing Christians, formative and liturgical practices). Several of these themes will be discussed through an analysis of the visual examples using theological insights and art historical methods.

events.id: TRAGEDIES 'TAUPIRI' SINGLE RELEASE SHOW-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: TRAGEDIES 'TAUPIRI' SINGLE RELEASE SHOW
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join Wellington trip-hop trio Tragedies in celebrating the release of their upcoming single 'Taupiri'. Coming from the reo Maori word for 'a loving embrace', Taupiri is a queer love song for the end of the world that celebrates the small moments of bliss carved out of dark times. Tragedies' blend of trip-hop, alt-rock and post-punk explores the overlap of beauty and horror with ethereal harmonies, haunting melodies and moments of heavy industrial grit that invite the listener to be vulnerable and revel in the tragedies and triumphs of life.
Joined by screaming grunge-folk icon Diplopoda and alt-rock knock outs Hit Me playing tracks from their new EP 'The Trial', the evening promises to be a tragic night to remember. Be ready for a night of emotional carnage and smudged eyeliner.
Tickets $10 via UTR

events.id: The Lives of My Father-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Lives of My Father
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

Spies, lies and a box of video tapes.
Didrik thought his Norwegian father was a journalist. In reality, he was a CIA spy who fired AK-47s in the Kuwaiti desert and infiltrated cocaine laboratories in Colombia. When Didrik finds a box of old tapes and documents in his father’s attic, he uncovers a story almost too strange to be true. For the first time his father, Bjørn, begins to speak openly about his past. As Didrik digs deeper, the life and lies of a man who blurred the lines between journalism, propaganda, and international espionage begins to emerge. But can any of it be trusted?

events.id: Harlem Globetrotters — 100 Year Tour-TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Harlem Globetrotters — 100 Year Tour
events.venue: TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The year 2026 will see the American exhibition basketball team, the Harlem Globetrotters, celebrate their 100-year anniversary. Wellington gets to celebrate with them when they visit in July. The team will take over TSB Arena and again revel in 100 years of thrills, laughs, and sheer basketball joy. If you aren’t already a fan, then sit back and watch their combined athleticism, sense of theatre, and comedy play out on the court. The gravity-defying dunks and dexterous tricks will have you roaring for more. 
The team has travelled the world visiting more than 120 countries and territories. Their 26,000 exhibition games continue to win new fans wherever they go. From the epic pre-game magic to unforgettable post-game interactions — come and experience their most legendary tour yet.

Blindfold Yin Yoga: a Sensory Reset
Blindfold Yin Yoga: a Sensory Reset

Take a moment to pause and take a breath, through experiencing blindfolded Yin Yoga. If you need recharging, this is a perfect way to ground your senses through a slow, gentle reset. Release known and unknown tension by moving your body and using breathwork to give back to the body that carries you daily. Let's reset.


Bhakti Lounge provides:

Yoga mats, blindfolds and yoga blocks

If you like to, you are welcome to bring:

An extra yoga mat, blanket, waterbottle


Meet your host Nicola!

Nicola of Namaste Nicola is a dedicated yoga teacher with over 20 years of experience, teaching across Australia and New Zealand. With over 300 hours of training internationally, her gentle approach is soulful, grounded in yoga philosophy.

She creates a space that is calm and nurturing, helping students calm down and rest deeply to reconnect themselves through practices such as yoga nidra, hatha and yin yoga.

The programme assists Registered Nurses (RNs) and Enrolled Nurses (ENs) working in Aged Residential Care (ARC) and District Nursing (DNs) to obtain the skills and knowledge required to manage a syringe driver in their work setting. * A pre programme workbook will be emailed to participants prior to the workshop.

events.id: Wellington THURS -Empower Her Networking -Visibility & Owning Your Presence-The Arborist Rooftop BarWellington, Wellington Region-Event Brite
events.name: Wellington THURS -Empower Her Networking -Visibility & Owning Your Presence
events.venue: The Arborist Rooftop BarWellington, Wellington Region
events.long_description:
Empower Her, female networking group that offers a welcoming and safe environment for women of all business backgrounds and experiences
WELLINGTON - The Arborist Rooftop Bar
Monthly Topic:  Visibility & Owning Your Presence Online and In Person
Hosted by: Tabitha Arthur
Visibility is not about being everywhere or having something new to say every single day. It's about showing up consistently, in the right places, with a message that the right people can hear and recognise. This month we're exploring what it really means to own your presence, online and in person, and how to get clear on a core message that you can repeat with confidence until it sticks.
Want to get your ticket for FREE?
Are you a woman in business looking for a supportive community to help you connect, grow, and learn?
As an Inner Circle member, you can attend as many of our networking hubs as you like for FREE! This includes our hubs across Auckland, NZ, Australia and the UK. Enjoy unlimited access to our netwokring events and take full advantage of the community and networking opportunities.
Join the Inner Circle
Join the Inner Circle to unlock even more benefits:
Monthly Masterclasses: Learn from industry experts on topics that will elevate your business and mindset.
Accountability Calls: Stay on track with our supportive community and achieve your goals.
Personal Branding Opportunities: Create your own masterclasses and shine as an expert.
Exclusive Discounts: Enjoy special rates on Empower Her events and live workshops.
Vibrant Community: Share your journey, get support, and celebrate your successes with empowering women.
Join the Inner Circle now and become part of a wonderful community of women in business who are ready to build their dream life and business.
Empower Her is a global female networking group, that offers a welcoming and safe environment for women of all business backgrounds and experiences.
Whether you're just starting out with new business ideas or have years of experience under your belt, all are welcome! Empower Her provides a safe space where you can share your business with others, connect with like-minded women in business, and prioritise collaboration over competition.
Our groups value the power of connection, and we believe that together, we can achieve more than we ever could alone. That's why we're committed to fostering a supportive community that empowers each member to reach their full potential. Our groups are focused on connection, inspiring stories, celebrating each other's successes and sharing our vulnerable moments.
Join our free community
The Inner Circle
Follow us on Instagram
Empower Her Co-Founders Victoria and Charlotte are highly passionate and dedicated to empowering female leaders in the business world. By joining Empower Her, you will not only expand your professional network but also gain access to a supportive community of like-minded individuals who are committed to helping each other succeed.Find out more about us and check up our upcoming events on our website.

events.id: Shalakazap-Te Raukura ki Kapiti, Coastlands Theatre, 34a Raumati Road, Raumati, New Zealand 5032,34 Raumati Rd, Raumati Beach, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu-All Events In
events.name: Shalakazap
events.venue: Te Raukura ki Kapiti, Coastlands Theatre, 34a Raumati Road, Raumati, New Zealand 5032,34 Raumati Rd, Raumati Beach, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Shalakazap
Tyro, apprentice MatheMagician, can't get his spells right. So, when Fire Chief Fleet arrives with a monster mathemagical problem, they need to ask the audience for help. A Riotous comedy, a transforming set and audience participation.

KidzStuff Theatre for Children brings the magic of live performance to your tamariki these July school holidays, a Wellington's family-friendly favourite since 1997. These 45-minute shows are packed with local Kiwi heart, inviting kids to enjoy a high-energy, theatrical escape. Make some memories and join the fun!

Written by the phenomenal Jenny Wake ONZM - a veteran of New Zealand Television and Theatre. In her television career, she wrote, directed and produced ‘Play School’, wrote for ‘Bumble’ and directed ‘What Now’. Jenny has travelled the word championing children's theatre. Currently the National Coordinator of Performing Arts and Young People of Aotearoa (PAYPA) and the International Association of Theatre for Children and Young People (ASSITEJ) New Zealand.

This play has had sell-out tours, and international presentations at Brisbane's Out of the Box festival and Vancouver-based Axis Theatre.

Ideal for children aged 2–10.

Under twos free on parent's lap.

--

For accessibility seating information and wheelchair space bookings please email or call 04)9740535 during office hours.

Door sales are only available if the event doesn't sell out prior.

events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

What does it mean to make something that lasts? From a pink church in Greymouth to Dick Frizzell’s studio, from a Kiwi art collection under the hammer to Joshua Oppenheimer’s meditation on immortality, these four shorts ask whether art, like life, is ever truly finished.

This is a collection of four short films:
Gloria
Good Kunst
The Weight of the World
The Revolution Against Death

events.id: Tough Old Broads (Dures à cuire)-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Tough Old Broads (Dures à cuire)
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

Breaking barriers doesn’t have an expiration date.
Three trailblazing women broke barriers in their time: Kathrine Switzer, the first woman to officially run the Boston Marathon; Siila Watt-Cloutier, the first woman to connect climate change to human rights; and Sharon Farmer, the first woman to act as Chief White House Photographer. Today, they continue to pursue their passions. With determination and grit, these ladies shed light on what it takes to make it in a man’s world, and what old age looks like when you’re a self-described “tough old broad”.

events.name: Whiria te Tangata: Weaving People Together
events.venue: Bhakti Lounge Wellington,175 Vivian Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Whiria te Tangata: Weaving People Together
Pause, breathe and weave: a soulful putiputi workshop blending Māori tradition, creativity and connection in a welcoming community space.

About this Event


Whiria te Tangata: Weaving People Together

Join us for a putiputi weaving workshop with local weaver Haley Te Rire. Share a moment to pause, breathe and create. Rooted in the traditions of raranga (Māori weaving) and shaped by her whakapapa as uri of Tūwharetoa ki Kawerau, this wānanga invites you to explore the beauty of weaving as both craft and connection.
Whether this is your first time weaving or a return to familiar rhythms, you’ll be welcomed into a calm, communal space. Leave with your own putiputi that carries intention, aroha, and the echo of those who came before us.




Please stay for a delicious plant-based dinner and dessert afterwards, included in your ticket!



Meet your host Haley: 

Haley is a local weaver and uri of Tūwharetoa ki Kawerau who shares the art of raranga as a way of fostering connection, creativity, and wellbeing. Grounded in her whakapapa and guided by mātauranga Māori, Haley sees weaving as more than a craft, it is a way to slow down, reconnect, and honour the stories and knowledge carried through generations. 



Agenda


🕑: 05:45 PM - 06:00 PM
Arrive to Bhakti Lounge

🕑: 06:00 PM - 07:00 PM
Whiria te Tangata: Weaving People Together
Host: Hosted by Haley

🕑: 07:00 PM - 08:15 PM
Plant-based dinner and dessert!

events.id: In Between The Verses-BATS Theatre, 1 Kent Tce, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: In Between The Verses
events.venue: BATS Theatre, 1 Kent Tce, Wellington
events.long_description:
After years of chasing a music dream, Emma Rutherford faces the question everyone with a creative degree eventually hears: “have you thought about going teaching?”

Originally from a rural North Canterbury town where everyone knows everyone, Emma Rutherford packed up her life, her microphone and a truckload of unsolicited advice to chase the impossible dream of making it in the music industry.

Bringing original music, storytelling, comedy and brutal small town wisdom to the stage, Emma unpacks what it really means to pursue a creative career when everyone around you has a “real job”.

This is Emma’s final shot at making it in the industry before she gives in, heads to teachers college, and becomes “sensible.”

Emma’s music has reached #3 on the Hot 20 Aotearoa Music Charts and been featured in Rolling Stone AU/NZ “Best NZ Music of the Week.

Parental discretion advised (strong language)

Accessibility: The Dome is located on the first floor of the BATS venue with two sets of stairs required to access the space. There is no elevator access to the Dome Theatre, please note only access is via stairs.

events.long_description:
The winter brings a programme of three works celebrating the heritage of the Royal New Zealand Ballet and the contributions of outstanding artists from our past, present and future.

At its heart is the New Zealand premiere of Sir Frederick Ashton’s Birthday Offering, performed as a centenary tribute to Southland-born ballerina Rowena Jackson and as part of Ashton Worldwide 2024–8. Completing the programme are two works created for the RNZB: Sarah Foster-Sproull’s exuberant Ultra Folly, inspired by the rhythmic energy of La Folia, and Andrea Schermoly’s Stand to Reason, a powerful tribute to the women of Aotearoa and the enduring fight for equality. Together, these three works honour legacy, connection and the timeless spirit of dance.

17/07/2026 Pre-Preformance Talk 6:30pm
18/07/2026 Warm Up, Curtain Up 6:20pm

events.id: Come Together - Radiohead, OK Computer-The Opera House, 111 - 113 Manners Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Come Together - Radiohead, OK Computer
events.venue: The Opera House, 111 - 113 Manners Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
New Zealand’s most successful rock series ever, COME TOGETHER, returns in 2026 with an electrifying trio of landmark albums: Bruce Springsteen’s Born to Run, Radiohead’s OK Computer and Talking Heads’ Stop Making Sense.

Each album will be performed live, in full, by a rotating supergroup of Aotearoa’s most acclaimed rock artists and musicians — followed by a second set of classics and deep cuts that span each artist’s extraordinary catalogue.

From the wall of sound production of Born to Run, to the haunting dystopian brilliance of OK Computer, to the joyful and immersive perfection of Stop Making Sense, the 2026 series celebrates three albums that have cemented their place in music history. Performed in some of the country’s most beautiful venues, Come Together once again promises epic musicianship, emotional punch and the kind of once-in-a-lifetime collaborations that have made the series a cultural phenomenon.

Released in 1997, OK Computer shattered rock conventions and captured the anxious spirit of a changing world. Hailed as a masterpiece on release — and consistently ranked among the greatest albums of all time — it remains as haunting, expansive and relevant today as ever.

Performed front to back, the album’s intricate soundscapes and emotional weight will be brought to life before a second set celebrating Radiohead’s rich and restless back catalogue.


Performed by: Ed Knowles, Milan Borich, Seamus Johnson and the Come Together band — with more artists to be announced.

*Booking fees apply

events.id: Emma Rutherford Presents: In Between The Verses-BATS Theatre,1 Kent Terrace, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Emma Rutherford Presents: In Between The Verses
events.venue: BATS Theatre,1 Kent Terrace, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Emma Rutherford Presents: In Between The Verses
Originally from a rural North Canterbury town where everyone knows everyone, Emma Rutherford packed up her life, her microphone and a truckload of unsolicited advice to chase the impossible dream of making it in the music industry.

Bringing original music, storytelling, comedy and brutal small town wisdom to the stage, Emma unpacks what it really means to pursue a creative career when everyone around you has a “real job”.

This is Emma’s final shot at making it in the industry before she gives in, heads to teachers college, and becomes “sensible.”
Emma’s music has reached #3 on the Hot 20 Aotearoa Music Charts and been featured in Rolling Stone AU/NZ “Best NZ Music of the Week.

16 - 18 July, 7pm, Bats Theatre, Wellington.
I can’t wait to see you there!

events.id: Live Music - Ryan Edwards-Get Fixed Bicycle Cafe & Panhead Bar,16 Parumoana St, Porirua City Centre, Porirua 5022, New Zealand, Porirua-All Events In
events.name: Live Music - Ryan Edwards
events.venue: Get Fixed Bicycle Cafe & Panhead Bar,16 Parumoana St, Porirua City Centre, Porirua 5022, New Zealand, Porirua
events.long_description:
Live Music - Ryan Edwards
Ryan Edwards is a singer, guitarist and performer of the highest quality. Kiwi born and raised, he took his music overseas where he played on stages and in cafes and bars in England, France, Ireland and The United States.

Whether he’s playing his own original songs or giving his own feel to some of the best songs ever written - his warm vocals, his knack for knowing the right song to sing at any moment and his tremendous repertoire makes him a favourite with audiences of all ages.

"I was lucky to grow up in a time when music was something special. The radio stations played the latest music, but they also dipped back into the 60's and 70's, and I just soaked it all up. Many of the songs I sing are unlearned - they're just 'in there' somehow - from just listening."

events.id: Winter Deco Rail Experiences-Steam Incorporated, 217 State Highway 1, Paekākāriki-Event Finder
events.name: Winter Deco Rail Experiences
events.venue: Steam Incorporated, 217 State Highway 1, Paekākāriki
events.long_description:
Winter Deco Rail Experiences - July 2026

Step back in time this winter and experience the sights, sounds, and romance of steam rail travel as Steam Incorporated brings a fleet of heritage trains to Hawke's Bay for four unforgettable days. From elegant dining experiences and family adventures to steam-hauled journeys through the countryside and classic cinema outings, our Winter Deco Rail Experiences offer something for everyone. Explore the full programme below!

TRAVEL IN STYLE
To Napier - Friday 17 July | Homeward Bound - Monday 20 July 2026
Travel between the Kāpiti Coast or Manawatu, and Napier aboard our heritage train, enjoying a relaxed journey through some of the lower North Island's most spectacular scenery. Available as one-way or return travel to connect with your Winter Deco weekend.

THE VINTAGE VINES EXPRESS
Dannevirke / Waipukurau - Napier | Friday 17 July 2026
An elegant evening combining heritage rail travel with Hawke's Bay hospitality. Travel by train from Waipukurau to Napier before enjoying either a premium dining experience at Mission Estate Winery or a free evening in Napier, before returning home via Premium Coach.


THE HAWKE'S BAY FLYER - SELLING QUICKLY!
Napier / Hastings / Waipukurau - Woodville & Return | Saturday 18 July 2026
Experience a full-day steam-hauled adventure through the heart of Hawke's Bay and Central Hawke's Bay. A fantastic opportunity to enjoy the sights, sounds, and atmosphere of Steam Incorporated in action.

A NIGHT AT THE MOVIES - NEW!
Napier / Hastings - Waipukurau & Return | Saturday 18 July 2026
Travel by heritage train to Waipukurau for a special screening of the classic film Casablanca. Your journey includes return rail travel, entry to the theatre, and a delicious dinner box.

HAWKE'S BAY HUFF 'N' PUFF
Napier - Hastings & Return | Sunday 19 July 2026
Enjoy a fun-filled steam train ride between Napier and Hastings, perfect for families and first-time steam travellers. A classic school holiday experience for all ages.

THE TAKAPAU STEAM EXPRESS
Waipukurau - Takapau & Return | Sunday 19 July 2026
Journey onboard our heritage train through the rolling landscapes of Central Hawke's Bay. A relaxed and scenic excursion showcasing rail travel at its finest.

THE MOVIE MAGIC EXPRESS - NEW!
Napier / Hastings - Waipukurau & Return | Sunday 19 July 2026
Travel by heritage train to Waipukurau for a special screening of Disney's NEW RELEASE live-action Moana. Your afternoon includes return rail travel, movie admission, popcorn, and a drink.

events.id: Alice in Wonderland-Little Theatre, 2 Queens Dr, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: Alice in Wonderland
events.venue: Little Theatre, 2 Queens Dr, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
When curious Alice falls down the rabbit hole, she lands in the topsy-turvy world of Wonderland, where nothing makes sense and everything is wonderfully silly!
She meets the White Rabbit, the cheeky Khandallah Cat, and a host of oddball characters… all whispering about the terrifying Jabberwocky, who everyone is scared of, but no one has ever actually seen.
Meanwhile, the fiery Queen of Hearts is demanding jam tarts, ready to shout “Off with their heads!” at any moment!
With laughs, songs, and plenty of audience fun, Alice must navigate the madness, solve the mystery, and find her way home.

events.id: Legacy (Héritières)-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Legacy (Héritières)
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

A women’s musical gathering where memory becomes celebration.
At the crossroads of musical documentary, art film and poetic essay, this film delves into the musical project of the same name, bringing together more than twenty female musicians of different generations and cultural backgrounds. Through intimate testimonies and music, the film explores maternal legacy and the heritage carried between women across generations. Kiwi and Canadian co-directors Parry-Canet and Pion offer a full immersion into intergenerational memory.

events.id: Astrophotography Masterclass: Capture the Southern Sky-Star Safari Observatory, 1169 Ponatahi Road, Carterton-Event Finder
events.name: Astrophotography Masterclass: Capture the Southern Sky
events.venue: Star Safari Observatory, 1169 Ponatahi Road, Carterton
events.long_description:
Spend an evening under one of the world’s finest dark skies and learn how to photograph the Milky Way, constellations and deep-sky objects. This immersive masterclass is designed for curious explorers, hobby photographers and anyone who has ever looked up and wished they could capture what they saw.

Your instructor is an experienced astro-educator, and you’ll be taken step by step through the art and science of night-sky photography with DSLR or mirrorless cameras. You’ll learn how to focus at infinity, choose the best camera settings, build compositions under the Milky Way, and work with RAW files, noise control and exposure strategy. You’ll photograph live star fields and leave with images you can genuinely be proud of.

No astronomy expertise or prior astrophotography experience is required — just your curiosity. Bring a camera and tripod if you have them, but if not, you can still attend, learn from telescope-linked imaging feeds, and gain the skills and confidence to shoot the cosmos on your own.

Held at Star Safari, right in the Wairarapa dark-sky heartlands, this experience takes place under the same southern skies recognised by Lonely Planet as one of the world’s top stargazing destinations. If you’ve ever wanted to try astrophotography, this is the moment, and this is the place.

What you’ll gain:
- Confidence to shoot the night sky
- Proven camera settings and workflow
- Real photos of the Milky Way and southern star fields
- Knowledge you can use anywhere the southern sky is visible
- A rare, premium learning experience under a world-class sky

Perfect for couples, travellers, creatives, astronomy lovers, and anyone who wants a deeper connection to the night.

Spaces are limited to keep the experience personal.

Talent gets you seen. Hunger gets you hired.

events.id: Bastille Day x Tour de France 2026-Alliance Française Welington - French Cultural Centre,78 Victoria Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Bastille Day x Tour de France 2026
events.venue: Alliance Française Welington - French Cultural Centre,78 Victoria Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Bastille Day x Tour de France 2026
Celebrate two of France's greatest icons in one unforgettable evening!

About this Event

Celebrate two of France's greatest icons in one unforgettable evening. Join us for a Bastille Day celebration with a Tour de France twist, an interactive night of French wine, friendly competition, and great company.

6:00–6:30pm — Kick-off & French Buffet
Start the evening with a warm welcome, a spread of French bites and an introduction to what's ahead. A perfect moment to catch up, mingle, and get into the Bastille Day spirit.
6:30–7:50pm — Activities
The group splits into two teams : the Yellow Jersey and the Dot Jersey and rotates through two exclusive sessions:
🍷 Wine Tasting — Discover 4 carefully selected French wines, guided through each one at your own pace. No expertise required just curiosity and a glass.
🚴 Tour de France Activity — A fun, fast-paced challenge inspired by the world's greatest cycling race. May the best team win.
Each team completes both sessions over two 40-minute rounds.
7:50–8:00pm — Wrap-up & Closing
Share your highlights and raise a final glass to France.

🚫 18+ event only
🗓️ Friday, 17 July 2026, 6pm-8pm
📍 Alliance Française Wellington (78 Victoria Street, Wellington)

events.description:
Bastille Day x Tour de France 2026 Celebrate two of France's greatest icons in one unforgettable evening!
Join us for a Bastille Day celebration with a Tour de France twist, an interactive night of French wine, friendly competition, and great company.
A perfect moment to catch up, mingle, and get into the Bastille Day spirit.
No expertise required just curiosity and a glass.
Each team completes both sessions over two 40-minute rounds.

events.id: PopRox Presents: An Improvised Nativity at The New Zealand Festival of Christmas-The Offering Greytown,65 Main St, Parkvale, New Zealand, Clareville-All Events In
events.name: PopRox Presents: An Improvised Nativity at The New Zealand Festival of Christmas
events.venue: The Offering Greytown,65 Main St, Parkvale, New Zealand, Clareville
events.long_description:
PopRox Presents: An Improvised Nativity at The New Zealand Festival of Christmas
PopRox is bringing their beloved improvised Christmas comedy show to the New Zealand festival of Christmas! PopRox is a professional improv theatre company from Wellington, specialising in slick, hilarious (and a little bit naughty) comedy shows, and for one night only their fully improvised retelling of the greatest story of them all is lighting up the stage at The Offering in Greytown.

A heavily pregnant Mary and a doting Joseph embark on a road-trip where they'll escape an evil king, come across three wise guys and just try to deliver this holy baby. Think "Life of Brian" with a heavy dose of kiwi humour! It's a night of laughs, heartfelt moments and great food with a dinner and a show at the gorgeous The Offering cafe. Enjoy an adults-only night out filled with laughter where these expert performers craft a story with a little help from you! Will Mary give birth in the back seat of Mazda Demio? Will the evil King Herod outlaw casual Friday? Will the three wise guys give the audience any helpful advice at all? There's only one way to find out. Book your tickets now and be sure to arrive early to enjoy drinks and a delicious dinner at The Offering.

“PopRox delivers on so many levels in its improvisation and comedic skill, with an ensemble of gifted and affecting actors... a performance that dazzles in its brilliance." Mitchell Manuel. Theatreview.

“All of the mahi these performers put in results in a show that is hilarious, engaging and leaves you wanting so much more.”
“What PopRox is doing here is nailing the tone and absurdity that the Nativity surely must have been filled with” Elliott Lam. Wellyott.

“it’s very clever...a rollicking good laugh to ease the pre-Christmas stress.” Sarah Catterall. The Post.

Show Running Information:

6:00pm- The Offering opens for dinner service
7:00pm- Main meal last orders taken
7:30pm- Show Begins
8:30pm (Approx.)- Show Finishes

PopRox is an ensemble of Wellington's top improv comedians, fresh off a two year residency at Circa Theatre and hit original shows at Wellington's "The Hannah" Theatre. Entering their 5th year as a company, PopRox's signature blend of compelling storytelling, hilarious one-liners, dazzling improvised music and slick production is sure to bring the warmth to Greytown this winter. Featuring decades of performing experience with award-winning comedians PopRox is an up and coming collective you don't want to miss.

Entering their 5th year as a company, PopRox's signature blend of compelling storytelling, hilarious one-liners, dazzling improvised music and slick production is sure to bring the warmth to Greytown this winter.

events.id: The Life We Leave-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Life We Leave
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

A greener way to say goodbye.
When Washington State legalises human composting, entrepreneur Micah Truman bets everything on a new vision of deathcare. But when the conventional deathcare industry resists, he is forced to pivot and learn to care for the grieving while transforming his business. With funeral directors Brie Smith and Katey Houston, he builds the first large-scale terramation facility, reshaping how we grieve and return to the Earth. What begins as an ambitious venture becomes personal as they rediscover human connection, ritual, and legacy.

events.id: Waenga-Ōrongomai Marae, 1 Railway Ave, Maidstone, Upper Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: Waenga
events.venue: Ōrongomai Marae, 1 Railway Ave, Maidstone, Upper Hutt
events.long_description:
Created by Hariata & Tamati Moriarty and directed by Jim Moriarty, 'Waenga' centres on a young wahine Māori who is arrested and finds herself in a police holding cell. Alongside her journey is Peyton, who is coming to understand what a Tangata Tiriti partnership is and Grayson, a young Māori lawyer navigating his role in the system.

Performed by an ensemble cast, including well known rangatahi Hariata and Tamati Moriarty, the play is presented in Te Rākau’s unique Theatre Marae framework, creating space for collective reflection and shared experience.

Te Rākau is the longest running Māori theatre company in Aotearoa founded in 1989.

Recommended age 12+ contains swearing and depicts some violence. Children under 12 can accompany for free with a paying adult.
