events.id: Glow In the Dark Titiwai Tours - New Zealand Glow Worms-Wellington Botanic Garden, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Glow In the Dark Titiwai Tours - New Zealand Glow Worms
events.venue: Wellington Botanic Garden, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington
events.long_description:
A fascinating tour into the world of titiwai, New Zealand's glow worms, in Wellington Botanic Garden ki Paekākā. This outdoor night-time tour takes about an hour, and all are welcome to join. If bringing children, we find it most suitable for those aged 7 years and older.

Meet at the Founders’ entrance to Wellington Botanic Garden ki Paekākā on Glenmore Street. Please bring your ticket, a torch, and an umbrella if it’s raining. We'll see you there!
This tour is operated by the Friends of Wellington Botanic Garden.

Glow Worm Tour Ticket Refund Policy

• No Refunds: We do not offer refunds for Glow Worm Tour tickets.

• Ticket Transfers: If you can’t attend your booked tour, with 2 weeks notice we can put you on a future tour, subject to availability.

• Weather Conditions: Tours operate rain or shine. In the event of extreme weather or unsafe conditions, we will reschedule your tour or provide a refund.

For any changes or enquiries, please get in touch with us in advance at Treehouse@wcc.govt.nz or call 04 499 1400.

events.id: Guy Williams: Rich people are stealing from you and blaming...-Wairarapa Events Centre, Holloway St, Carterton-Event Finder
events.name: Guy Williams: Rich people are stealing from you and blaming...
events.venue: Wairarapa Events Centre, Holloway St, Carterton
events.long_description:
Guy Williams – Rich people are stealing from you and blaming brown people and trans people and some people believe them aaahhhhhh!

Probably shouldn’t have given it all away in the title. Like most people I’ve been trying to figure out how the worst dickheads have taken over everything with their insane bullshit. So I’ve read three books (mostly) (one was a graphic novel) and listened to five audio books (AT NORMAL SPEED) to try and figure out WHAT THE F**K IS GOING ON?

Credits to impress my mum:
New Zealand Today (Netflix)
Taskmaster NZ (Binge)
Guy Montgomery’s GuyMont Spelling Bee (ABC)
The Weekly (ABC)
The Hundred (Nine)

★★★★ – The Age
★★★★ – Chortle
★★★★ – The Skinny

“Riotously entertaining!” ★★★★ The Student UK
“If you want to spend an hour laughing out your worries and frustrations, I can’t think of a better way to do it.” squirrelcomedy.com

SEATING LAYOUT: Flat floor seating with staging. For accessibility seating information and wheelchair space bookings please email events@cdc.govt.nz or call 06 379 4081 during office hours.

Rating & warnings: 15+ Strong language & adult themes

events.id: Live Music - Shaun Preston-Plimmerton Boating Club,66 Moana Road, Plimmerton, New Zealand, Porirua-All Events In
events.name: Live Music - Shaun Preston
events.venue: Plimmerton Boating Club,66 Moana Road, Plimmerton, New Zealand, Porirua
events.long_description:
Live Music - Shaun Preston
Get ready for an unforgettable night of world-class music and entertainment as New Zealand’s favorite piano man, Shaun Preston, takes the stage live at The Jetty! Known for his incredible showmanship and infectious energy, Shaun effortlessly blends stunning vocal talent with masterclass piano skills. His dynamic repertoire ranges from soulful, acoustic dinner sets to high-octane, crowd-pleasing dance floor hits—and don’t be surprised if he finishes the night performing right on top of his signature white grand piano! Having performed globally from Los Angeles to London, and for elite audiences including royalty, Shaun brings a world-traveled performance directly to our stunning waterfront. Pair his electrifying, sing-along style with the beautiful coastal views and unmatched atmosphere at The Jetty for the ultimate night out. Spaces are strictly limited for this exclusive evening. This will be a fabulous night of music. We will not be offering bookings or table service for this event and a reduced food menu will be on offer. This is a ticketed event and tickets can be purchased via Eventfinder

events.id: Monthly Jazz Night: Andrew London Duo-The Thistle Inn, 3 Mulgrave St, Thorndon, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Monthly Jazz Night: Andrew London Duo
events.venue: The Thistle Inn, 3 Mulgrave St, Thorndon, Wellington
events.long_description:
This July, we’re delighted to welcome the Andrew London Duo to Thistle Inn for our monthly jazz night.

Known for their witty storytelling, rich harmonies, and effortless blend of jazz, blues and swing, Andrew and Kirsten London bring a warm, lively energy that has charmed audiences across New Zealand and beyond. Expect an evening filled with clever originals, much-loved classics, and the kind of musicianship that feels perfectly at home in the Main Bar.

From Western swing to jazz-inspired favourites, this is music made for good conversation, a glass of red, and settling in for the night.

Friday 17 July - from 7pm

Dinner bookings are available in our dining rooms beforehand, before enjoying the show in the Main Bar.

Bookings recommended via reservations@thistleinn.co.nz

events.id: Mehana at Vogelmorn Upstairs-Vogelmorn Hall, 11 Vennell Street, Brooklyn, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Mehana at Vogelmorn Upstairs
events.venue: Vogelmorn Hall, 11 Vennell Street, Brooklyn, Wellington
events.long_description:
Mehana is celebrating 10 years of bringing Balkan music to Wellington and is thrilled to be playing this iconic venue for the very first time.

Expect a vibrant mix of Balkan sounds, from soulful and haunting melodies to high-energy dance tunes that will get you moving. Inspired by Roma musical traditions and enriched by our own original compositions, Mehana creates a unique and unforgettable live experience.

Join us for an evening of great music, dancing, and celebration. Tickets are koha—simply follow the link on Under the Radar to reserve your spot or get it at the door.

We’d love to see you there!

“The Upstairs Bar and Smoked and Pickled Restaurant will be open from 3pm, to book a
table for pre-show kai email Alice at smokednpickled@gmail.com”

"Vogelmorn Upstairs is not a wheelchair accessible building. There is an accessible
bathroom in the space.

This is an all ages venue but those under the age of 16 must be accompanied by a designated adult after 8pm.

The Vogelmorn Precinct is a community space located in a residential area. With that in mind, the team ask that you be kind to others' and that when you leave the gig, you do so quietly to avoid disturbing our neighbours.”

events.id: KING PARROT (AUS) W/ SLAVE CADAVER, POSTNATAL ABORTION & MACED-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: KING PARROT (AUS) W/ SLAVE CADAVER, POSTNATAL ABORTION & MACED
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
KING PARROT : Wellington NZ @ Valhalla w/- Slave Cadaver + Postnatal Abortion + Maced
FRIDAY JULY 17!
Tickets on sale now! via UTR
King Parrot are pleased to announce their return to New Zealand to launch the blistering new album A Young Persons Guide to King Parrot. Described as a hostile and abrasive slab of sensory destroying anthems, the record features ten thoroughly crafted blasts through the psyche of this crazed Aussie quintet. Recorded at Melbourne’s Dangertone studios with engineer Luke Walton, it’s the first full-length album since 2017’s critically adored Ugly Produce offering and their first new music since 2020’s Holed Up In The Lair EP. Pulling the new songs together was a meticulously creative endeavour and the final product is the most complete, aggressive, and urgent the band has ever sounded.
The band have been in full throttle touring mode since the release in early 2025 and are currently out on a massive 39 date jaunt through the USA alongside Gwar and Soulfly. Much of the last year was spent touring the globe in support of Pantera all over Europe, the UK and the USA where King Parrot amassed performing exactly 100 shows for the year. New Zealand fans can expect a band that is firing on all cylinders and awaiting their first shows in NZ in many years, comments vocalist Matt Young "It's been a while between drinks but we were most certain that we must return to NZ this year and give you guys a dose of King Parrot! We cant wait to get back and see our kiwi mates and bring our brand of off the kilter grind, thrash and punk rock chaos, get your tickets now and well see you real soon!"

Much of the last year was spent touring the globe in support of Pantera all over Europe, the UK and the USA where King Parrot amassed performing exactly 100 shows for the year. New Zealand fans can expect a band that is firing on all cylinders and awaiting their first shows in NZ in many years, comments vocalist Matt Young "It's been a while between drinks but we were most certain that we must return to NZ this year and give you guys a dose of King Parrot! We cant wait to get back and see our kiwi mates and bring our brand of off the kilter grind, thrash and punk rock chaos, get your tickets now and well see you real soon!"
events.description:
KING PARROT : Wellington NZ @ Valhalla w/- Slave Cadaver + Postnatal Abortion + Maced KING PARROT : Wellington NZ @ Valhalla w/- Slave Cadaver + Postnatal Abortion + Maced

Described as a hostile and abrasive slab of sensory destroying anthems, the record features ten thoroughly crafted blasts through the psyche of this crazed Aussie quintet.
Recorded at Melbourne’s Dangertone studios with engineer Luke Walton, it’s the first full-length album since 2017’s critically adored Ugly Produce offering and their first new music since 2020’s Holed Up In The Lair EP.
New Zealand fans can expect a band that is firing on all cylinders and awaiting their first shows in NZ in many years, comments vocalist Matt Young "It's been a while between drinks but we were most certain that we must return to NZ this year and give you guys a dose of King Parrot!

events.id: LEGO® Art Display and Whānau Fun Day at Te Papa Tongarewa-Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa,Red Gates te Papa, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: LEGO® Art Display and Whānau Fun Day at Te Papa Tongarewa
events.venue: Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa,Red Gates te Papa, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
LEGO® Art Display and Whānau Fun Day at Te Papa Tongarewa
We’re excited to once again bring you the LEGO® Art Display & Whānau Fun Day at Te Papa Tongarewa these school holidays!

Come and explore incredible artworks created entirely from LEGO® by neurodivergent tamariki from across the Wellington region. This special event celebrates creativity, imagination, and the unique talents of our young artists.

Over the two-day event you can also enjoy:
-LEGO® free-build activities
-Speed build competitions with LEGO® spot prizes
-A dedicated quiet hour for those who would benefit from a lower-sensory environment

Whether you’re a LEGO® enthusiast, looking for a fun whānau outing, or wanting to support our amazing young artists, we’d love to see you there!

📅 Event Schedule

DAY 1 — 17 July

10.00am: LEGO® Art Display and free-build open to the public
Throughout the day: LEGO® free-build available

DAY 2 — 18 July

9.00am: Quiet Hour
A low-sensory session for visitors to enjoy the LEGO® artworks and free-build activities in a calmer environment (wider Te Papa exhibitions remain closed)

10.00am: Art Display and free-build reopen to the public

11.00am–1.00pm: LEGO® Speed Builds with spot prizes
Hosted by Chris Lam Sam

2.00pm: Event closes

We can’t wait to welcome you for two days of creativity, connection, and LEGO® fun!

events.id: billy woods (Armand Hammer)-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: billy woods (Armand Hammer)
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
For billy woods, literate rhymes and inventive flows are littered through all his works. So much so, it’s hard to pinpoint his exact genre. But for the New York-based rapper, his music career began in the early 2000s, however, he was largely unknown until 2012 after the release of his solo album History Will Absolve Me. Since then, woods has gone onto release music under Armand Hammer – a collaborative project with ELUCID – and an array of solo albums like Today, I Wrote Nothing (2015), Maps (2023) and more.

His latest solo release – the 2025 album GOLLIWOG – has been deemed his most experimental yet. So much so, Pitchforkdescribed it as “a twisty horrorcore masterpiece where history and nightmares are indistinguishable” and a “perfect showcase for his craft”. It mixes horror, humour, surrealism and Afropessimism into the one work: think African zombies, time traveling trap cars, malevolent ragdolls and a dying Frantz Fanon

events.id: Kabul, Between Prayers-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Kabul, Between Prayers
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

As one brother lives by doctrine, the other grows up inside it.
A devoted soldier of the Taliban’s ideology that has shaped his destiny since birth, Samim struggles between the alluring promises of martyrdom and the mundanity of his daily existence as a husband and farmer. Samim’s younger brother, Rafi, idolises him as he navigates the confusion of adolescence, leaving behind playfulness to enter a world shaped by decades of military intervention and resulting radicalisation.

events.name: Menzies Album Release - w/ Sig Wilder & Felix Bird, Meow, Wellington, Holding My Cold Hand..
events.venue: Meow,9 Edward Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Menzies Album Release - w/ Sig Wilder & Felix Bird, Meow, Wellington, Holding My Cold Hand..
After the release of their acclaimed debut album ‘Holding My Cold Hand, Even Though Yours Is Warm’, hitting #1 with both singles on the 95bFM Top 10, and #1 on the NZ Alt Charts, Te Whanganui-a-Tara’s Menzies play their home-town album release show at Meow.

Supported by two of Wellington's most engaging live acts Sig Wilder, and Felix Bird.

The album and their heralded live performances capture everything that makes Menzies one of the country’s most exciting emerging acts.

“Wow, you guys gave me so much energy” - MJ Lenderman on Menzies at Camp a Low Hum
“There’s no one else like them” - Right Chord Music UK

‘Holding My Cold Hand, Even Though Yours Is Warm’ blends twisted guitars, sing-them-later melodies, and deeply Aotearoa storytelling - the Interislander, trays of sausage rolls, Suzy Cato, the All Blacks.

Frontman Chris Brown's former life as a magician bleeds into the band's memorable live shows with surprise and delight. The result is a live experience that is joyous, heartfelt, devastating, communal, and completely unforgettable.

A show you feel as much as hear.

Don't miss Menzies live.

Tickets: https://meow.flicket.co.nz/events/4b379978-5a72-4a4a-8c3d-98f83ed8b540/reservation

Sig Wilder:
https://sigwilder.bandcamp.com/

Felix Bird:
https://felixbird.bandcamp.com/

Menzies:
https://linktr.ee/___menzies___

events.id: Burnout to Balance - Proven Techniques to Pause, Recover & Return!-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Burnout to Balance - Proven Techniques to Pause, Recover & Return!
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
In today’s high-pressure world, burnout is often worn as a badge of honour—until the physical and mental toll becomes impossible to ignore. True recovery requires more than just "taking a break"; it requires resetting the nervous system from the inside out.
Led by Simon, a former high-stakes litigation lawyer who successfully navigated his own severe burnout, this workshop bridges the gap between understanding stress and actively healing from it. This practical, evidence-based workshop is designed to take you on a journey from chronic depletion to sustainable balance. You will explore what burnout actually is, identify your personal triggers, and master powerful physiological techniques to pause the stress response, recover your vitality, and return to life and work with renewed resilience.
What We Will Cover
Part 1: Understanding the Burnout Cycle (The Setup)
Before we can fix the problem, we must understand it. We begin by unpacking the foundations of chronic stress to help you map your own journey.
What is Burnout? Defining the psychological and physical parameters of exhaustion.
The Root Causes: Identifying the systemic, professional, and personal drivers of chronic stress.
The Nervous System Hijack: How prolonged pressure traps the body in a constant "fight-or-flight" state, impacting sleep, digestion, and cognitive function.
Part 2: The Science of Physiological Recovery (The Solution)
While mindset matters, burnout lives in the body. We will introduce the fastest, most effective tool for resetting a hijacked nervous system: Functional Breathing Training.
The Three Dimensions of Health: How biochemical, biomechanical, and psychophysiological breathing patterns dictate your stress levels.
Measuring Your Stress Tolerance: Learn how to assess your current breathing efficiency and physiological stress baseline.
Immediate Relief vs. Long-Term Resilience: Practices that offer instant calm during acute anxiety, paired with lifelong training to rebuild your nervous system.
What You Will Walk Away With
By the end of this one-day workshop, you will:
Recognise the Warning Signs: Deeply understand your own burnout triggers and early warning indicators.
Interrupt the Stress Response: Master proven, discreet techniques to pause the "fight-or-flight" response in real-time.
Improve Everyday Well-being: Know how to optimise your breathing patterns to combat poor sleep, fatigue, and brain fog.
A Personalised Recovery Plan: Walk away with a practical toolkit to integrate self-regulation and physical recovery into a busy professional life.
What to Bring: Please bring a notepad and pen to track your personal pacing metrics, record your breathing patterns, and map out your recovery strategy.
Participant Self-Management & Safety
This workshop is designed for adult learners. It is a supportive, respectful space where you are encouraged to engage at your own comfort level and self-manage your energy throughout the day (including taking breaks to decompress as needed).
Please note: The advanced physiological recovery techniques practiced in this workshop are highly effective for nervous system regulation. No prior experience with breathwork or yoga is required.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Manawatū Distance Orientation Day | Massey University | 18 July 2026-Massey University Students' Association (MUSA)Turitea, Manawatū-Whanganui Region-Event Brite
events.name: Manawatū Distance Orientation Day | Massey University | 18 July 2026
events.venue: Massey University Students' Association (MUSA)Turitea, Manawatū-Whanganui Region
events.long_description:
Start your distance study with confidence at Massey University Manawatū. Meet support teams, tour the campus & library, lunch and connect.
About this event
New to distance study with Massey University and want a confident start? Join us on our Manawatū campus (Palmerston North) to meet staff, connect with key support teams, and meet other distance students from your region.
You’ll also have the chance to get your bearings on campus (including a campus/library look-around). We’ll provide kai (lunch) and plenty of time for questions and conversations.
Explore the full schedule of the day on our Distance Open Day webpage.
You’ll leave with:
practical tips for managing study alongside work, whānau and life
clarity on the key systems you’ll use and where to get help
connections with other students you can keep in touch with
a clearer plan for your first few weeks of semester
Who this event is for
New Massey University students beginning distance (online) study in 2026
Students of all ages (school-leavers through to those returning to study)
Support people/whānau are welcome to attend with you (optional)
What to bring
Yourself — no need to prepare anything
A notebook or device if you like taking notes
Any questions you’d like to talk through
Comfortable clothing and footwear
Tickets
Distance student registration — Free
For new students starting distance study with Massey University in 2026.
Support person/whānau — Free (optional)
For a friend or whānau member attending with you.
Accessibility and support
If you have any access needs (mobility, hearing, vision, sensory, caring responsibilities, etc.), please let us know when you register or contact us at nationalevents@massey.ac.nz so we can support you.
Location
Student Lounge, Student Services Building, Massey University, Tennent Drive, Palmerston North.

events.id: Cobblestones Victorian Christmas Winter Fair-Cobblestones Heritage Village, 169 Main Street, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Cobblestones Victorian Christmas Winter Fair
events.venue: Cobblestones Heritage Village, 169 Main Street, Greytown
events.long_description:
Step into festive cheer at Cobblestones, a celebration of tradition, craftsmanship, and community spirit.
Set in the charming Cobblestones Victorian village, visitors can explore artisan treasures and enjoy the sights, sounds, and delights of a classic Victorian Christmas fair.
From handcrafted gifts and unique stalls to festive food and mulled treats, there’s something for everyone. Live entertainment brings the village to life, while children’s games and face painting add fun for all ages.

More than a market, it’s a magical experience where the spirit of Christmas shines, and lasting memories are made.

Highlights:
• Artisan gift stalls
• Live entertainment
• Kids’ activities
• Festive food and beverages

events.id: Regional Stamp, Coin and Postcard Fair-Knox St Columba Presbyterian Church, 574 High St, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: Regional Stamp, Coin and Postcard Fair
events.venue: Knox St Columba Presbyterian Church, 574 High St, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
The Regional Fair offers stamps, coins, postcards and similar collectables from dealers and local stamp societies.

The societies will be exhibiting over 1100 pages of competitive exhibits and displays showing the wide range of subjects and ways of using stamps and other collectable material. They will also be holding some seminars on collecting topics.

The Fair provides opportunities for collectors to find items for their collections, meet like-minded people, or seek answers to questions about their hobby.

People who have been given collections from family members will find that the dealers and societies can make offers to purchase, advise alternate ways of selling, or perhaps encourage you to take up the hobby yourself.

If you have an item you would like identified or a collection where you would like to gain some understanding of value or how to sell it, why not bring it along and seek answers?

The venue has free parking available. A cafe offering a wide range of food and refreshments will be available.

events.long_description:
Alexia Urban Winery is transforming into a Hot Cheese Shack to celebrate the greatest wine and food combination of all….wine and hot cheese!

Their ‘Hot Cheese Tin’ is the star of the show…a delicious fondue-style hot cheese dip…with plenty of bread to dunk of course!

Parking onsite or a gentle 15-minute stroll from Greytown centre.

events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

How can you forget when the wounds of the past are still fresh?
The 2009 Maguindanao Massacre was the deadliest attack on journalists in history. Through the eyes of Reynafe Castillo, daughter of the missing 58th victim, the film blends animation, archival footage and harrowing memories to chronicle a family’s relentless fight for justice amidst a landscape of political violence.

events.long_description:
Join Monty the Monstar Colour Run – Miharo Ora’s Fundraising Event for a fun, colorful day full of laughs and good vibes!
🌈 Monty the Monstar Colour Run – Fundraising for Mīharo Ora

📅 Friday 18 July 2026
📍 Motueka Quay Waterfront – Salt Water Baths to Staples Street Carpark

Come down, get colourful and support your local charity! This is a fun, family-friendly event open to ALL ages — walk, run, roll or stroll, it doesn't matter. Just come and have a good time and be part of something really special for our community. 🎉

Motueka Fire Service will be there with there Awesome Escape Maze

All registrations include your glasses — which can collect to 222 high st Motueka or collect on the day

Show your support ! If you would like to make a Donation to Help Open the doors on Motueka's first Neurodiverse centre then CLICK HERE We are a Registered NZ Charity (CC64581) A little goes a long way 1 in 5 people are Neurodiverse so the chances that someone close to you are on the spectrum or have children that are, Your donation goes towards helping families in your community and give our Tamariki a safe place to thrive. 🌈🩷

——————————————————
🏆 KIDS — THIS ONE IS FOR YOU!
——————————————————

Want to score some sweet prizes?! Here's how:

👉 Ask Mum, Dad or your whānau and jump online to:
🌐 myprofilepage.co.nz
Choose Miharo Ora in the Drop Down

Sign up, create your fundraising page and get your family and friends to sponsor you for the run. Earn $25 in sponsorship and you've already unlocked your choice of some awesome prizes!

🪙 Earn coins online as you fundraise and save them up for the prize YOU want — it's that easy!

Don't want prizes? No worries — you can choose to give your prize money to KidsCan or NZ Forest & Bird instead. How cool is that! 💚

——————————————————
🙌 VOLUNTEERS — WE NEED YOU!
——————————————————

This event doesn't happen without amazing people behind it! We're looking for volunteers to help with:

✅ Course Marshalls
✅ Colour Station Crews
✅ Registration & Ticketing
✅ Prizes Team
✅ Set Up & Pack Down

Think this sounds like you? Email us:
📧 miharoora.nz@gmail.com

——————————————————
🏪 LOCAL BUSINESSES — LET'S PARTNER UP!
——————————————————

Want to get your brand in front of the whole community? We'd love to have you involved!

You can:
• Sponsor a prize
• Donate goods or items for the event
• Get your logo on our banners, posters, shirts and advertising

There are loads of branding opportunities available — this is a fantastic chance to show Motueka you back your local community.

📧 miharoora.nz@gmail.com

——————————————————

Hit GOING or INTERESTED so we can get an idea of numbers, and share this with your whānau, friends and workplaces!

Let's make this a day to remember. 🌈✨

Together, we let your rainbows shine. 🌟

events.id: First Cuts are the Deepest (บาดเล็กเจ็บลึก)-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: First Cuts are the Deepest (บาดเล็กเจ็บลึก)
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

Three Thai children, six years of becoming.
Filmed over six years, the film follows three children growing up within Thailand’s education system. Potter adjusts to a new home and faith in the deep South; Nut comes of age in a spiritual boarding school; while Tee moves through an overcrowded classroom in Bangkok. Without commentary, the film observes how everyday moments of friendship, confusion, and solitude quietly shape who they become over time.

Q&A: Sopawan Boonnimitra (Director) and Peerachai Kerdsint (Director) will be present at the Takapuna Beachside Cinemas, Auckland screening.

events.id: Learn to Add Art to Your Travel Journal with Elena Brambilla-Greytown Distilling Co - Judgement Room, Level 2, 110 Main Street, Greytown, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Learn to Add Art to Your Travel Journal with Elena Brambilla
events.venue: Greytown Distilling Co - Judgement Room, Level 2, 110 Main Street, Greytown, Greytown
events.long_description:
Travel has a way of awakening our senses: the rhythms of an unfamiliar town, the colours and the detail of a new place. Travel journaling is a beautiful way to capture those moments. Instead of simply taking photos, you can slow down, observe, and translate what you see and feel into expressive pages filled with colour and personal notes.

In this relaxed and welcoming workshop, Elena will guide you through some techniques that make it easy to add watercolour art to your journal.

Together we’ll create a festival-inspired journal page using colours, shapes, and small sketches to capture the atmosphere of the NZ Festival of Christmas. You’ll then be able to take your page home and add your own reflections and words about your experience in Greytown. This can be the beginning of a travel journal that grows with every trip you take.

Included in the workshop:

- Traveler’s Company Watercolour Paper Notebook (value $11.90)
- MD Drawing Pen – Black 0.3 (value $9.90)
- Blackwing Natural Pencil (value $7.90)
- Watercolour paint for use on the day, and a paintbrush you can take home.

All you need to bring is your curiosity and a willingness to explore the world through your own creative lens.

Book your ticket now at https://villagebookshop.co.nz/products/workshop-travel-journal-with-elena-brambilla

events.id: For Life-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: For Life
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

Separated by bars and borders, motherhood becomes an act of resistance.
Hind, a Palestinian woman, has been living apart from her husband, Ramadan, who has been held in an Israeli prison, serving a life sentence for over twenty years. Their rare visits are limited to brief moments in a space divided by a glass partition, where physical contact is impossible. Despite everything, Hind remains determined to fulfill her desire to become a mother. To do so, she must secretly smuggle her husband’s sperm out of prison and undergo IVF treatment. Hind’s arduous journey intersects with the stories of other prisoners’ wives who share the same desire, forming a space of collective solidarity. For Palestinian women, this ordinary desire gradually transforms into an extraordinary act of resistance under the conditions of occupation.

events.long_description:
Festive Night Glow @ The New Zealand Festival of Christmas
For one night only, Molewood Orchard transforms into a glowing wonderland during The New Zealand Festival of Christmas.

Step through the hidden door and follow illuminated pathways into the heart of the orchard, where hot air balloons are inflated and will glow, tethered to the ground and set to music.

Grab a bite to eat from one of the food trucks, toast marshmallows over the open fire, and gather around the bonfire as you soak up the magic of this unforgettable evening. Proudly supporting the Greytown Fire Brigade.

Postponement date is Friday 24 July.
If this event needs to be postponed, your tickets will automatically be transferred to the postponement date.

You will be notified on the day of any changes due to weather.

The Festive Night Glow is designed to go ahead in typical winter weather conditions. Please dress warmly and come prepared to enjoy the experience, rain or shine.

Sorry, no dogs allowed.

Tickets:
Adult : $20.00 each
Child: $12.50 each
Family (2 adults, up to 2 kids): $50.00 each
Family (2 adults, up to 3 kids): $55.00 each
Family (2 adults, up to 4 kids): $60.00 each

events.id: Music and the Mystical Experience-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Music and the Mystical Experience
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

Where music meets the mind’s inner journey.
Musician and sound engineer Michael Sutherland takes us on a metaphorical tour of his latest musical project involving over twenty talented New Zealanders, aimed at aiding the controversial practice of psychedelic therapy. Weaving artist interviews, numerous performances, and appearances from veterans in the psychedelic field, including Chris Bache, the film gives an insight into how music enhances a psychedelic therapy session, and how much thought and effort goes into getting that music just right.

Screening Note: Companion immersive project in the World Press & Doc Edge Immersive Exhibition.

Q&A: Isabel Corfiatis (Director), Michael Sutherland (Producer), Keegan Connor (Producer) will be present at The Capitol Cinema, Auckland screening. Isabel Corfiatis (Director) will be present at the Roxy, Wellington screening.

events.long_description:
The Terrace & The Table: A 5-Course Degustation of Food and Wine

Join us for an exclusive culinary journey through the heart of Martinborough!

We are thrilled to announce a special collaboration between the award-winning Union Square Bistro & Bar and the iconic Nga Waka Vineyard.

For one night only, experience a 5-course degustation designed to showcase the very best of our local terroir.
Head Chef Ali alongside Owner/Chef Adam Newell have crafted a bespoke menu that marries his signature modern-NZ style with French flair, using the finest seasonal Wairarapa produce.

Hosted by Winemaker Paul Mason of Nga Waka, each course is expertly paired with a selection of premium Nga Waka wines—from their elegant, site-driven Chardonnays to the powerful and concentrated Pinot Noirs that have defined their legacy for over 30 years.

The Details:
- Date: Saturday, 18 July 2026
- Time: 6:00pm onwards
- Location: Union Square Bistro & Bar, The Martinborough Hotel
- Tickets: $150 per person (Includes all 5 courses and Wine Pairings)

Space is highly limited for this exclusive evening. Secure your table early so you don't miss out!
- Phone: (06) 306 8350
- Email: info@unionsquare.co.nz

Please advise of any dietary requirements at the time of booking. R18 for Alcohol. Low and No Alcohol options may be substituted. Please enjoy responsibly.

events.id: Collapse (Effondrement)-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Collapse (Effondrement)
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

A return home becomes a vantage point on violence, distance, and denial.
Shortly after October 7, 2023, Director Even returns to what was once her home, traversing for over two years the remnants of the kibbutz of Nir Oz and its environs, now repurposed for tourist itineraries and military training exercises. From distant vantage points along the fence demarcating the Gaza Strip, she observes the gradual ruination across the border and the calculated maneuvers of IDF troops. A cinematic essay, the film chronicles systematic violence while exposing the muted indifference of spectators.

events.id: Waenga-Wainuiomata Marae, Parkway Extension & Wellington Road, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: Waenga
events.venue: Wainuiomata Marae, Parkway Extension & Wellington Road, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Created by Hariata & Tamati Moriarty and directed by Jim Moriarty, 'Waenga' centres on a young wahine Māori who is arrested and finds herself in a police holding cell. Alongside her journey is Peyton, who is coming to understand what a Tangata Tiriti partnership is and Grayson, a young Māori lawyer navigating his role in the system.

Performed by an ensemble cast, including well known rangatahi Hariata and Tamati Moriarty, the play is presented in Te Rākau’s unique Theatre Marae framework, creating space for collective reflection and shared experience.

Te Rākau is the longest running Māori theatre company in Aotearoa founded in 1989.

Recommended age 12+ contains swearing and depicts some violence. Children under 12 can accompany for free with a paying adult.

events.id: Delaney Davidson Baby Heavyweight Tour-Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Delaney Davidson Baby Heavyweight Tour
events.venue: Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Delaney Davidson is a New Zealand Singer Songwriter, Musician, and Visual Artist.

A wry sense of humour, some theatre of unease, and a voice classed as not conventionally beautiful all combine to breathe life and space into the niche he has carved out for himself, winning him critical acclaim across the world for his original take on things.

His seminal influence and work with Marlon Williams on their Sad But True series as well as his visionary production on Tami Neilson’s acclaimed album’s Dynamite, Don’t Be Afraid and Chickaboom!, have also earned him great praise from the media and huge respect amongst his peers. Sync placements in The Expanse, Nashville TV Series, Bad Sisters, and Brokenwood Mysteries have taken Delaney’s music globally.

Baby Heavyweight, born in a kitchen in Switzerland, is Delaney Davidson’s 11th solo album. Somewhat more groovy than his previous releases, this is the second album he has made with Merk (Mark Perkins). Building on the smooth depth of the previous album Out Of My Head, and taking it into a new realm of story telling and emotive music.

Hitting the road with a stacked band featuring Heather Webb, Cass Basil, Alex Freer and Ryan Fisherman, Delaney sets his sights on a show that will deliver the album in all its complexity and nuance. Truly something to be witnessed. Do Not Miss Out.

Incontestably one of Aotearoas most influential and lauded contemporary songwriters - Under The Radar

…..beautifully haunting Rolling Stone

events.id: Elton John vs Billy Joel *NZ Tribute*-Copthorne Hotel & Resort Solway Park, 246 High St, Solway, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: Elton John vs Billy Joel *NZ Tribute*
events.venue: Copthorne Hotel & Resort Solway Park, 246 High St, Solway, Masterton
events.long_description:
NZ’s Award-winning hit tribute show “Elton John vs. Billy Joel” returns to Solway Masterton as part of their NZ-WIDE ENCORE TOUR!

Doors 7pm , show starts 7.30pm sharp

After wildly successful sell-out shows across New Zealand, join Cam and Sam for an intimate night out as they wow audiences both young and old performing the greatest hits of Elton John and Billy Joel LIVE! Piano Man, Rocket Man, Uptown Girl, Goodbye Yellow Brick Road, We didn’t start the fire, Bennie and the Jets, She’s always a woman, Crocodile Rock and many many more.

Your favourite Elton and Billy songs are in safe hands with Cam and Sam, these professional touring NZ musicians have performed on live national television, commercial radio and featured in the NZ Music Charts in careers spanning two decades. This amazing Piano Man extravaganza features astonishing costumes, two grand pianos in black and white, a lighting show plus plenty of banter and all the songs you love.

Let's face it, Elton John and Billy Joel require little introduction, their music has provided a soundtrack for generations of loving fans. Englishman Sir Elton John has sold over 300 million records, making him one of the best-selling music artists of all time. He has more than fifty Top 40 hits on the UK Singles Chart and US Billboard Hot 100, including seven number-ones in the UK and nine in the US, as well as seven consecutive number-one albums in the US.

American Billy Joel has sold more than 150 million records and ranks as one of the most popular recording artists and respected entertainers in the world. Joel has had 33 Top 40 hits and 23 Grammy nominations. In 1999 he was inducted into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame and has received the Recording Industry Association of America Diamond Award, presented for albums that have sold over 10 million copies.

events.name: Lads, Lads, Lads - Coronation: A Drag King Show
events.venue: The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Huzzah! It’s time for the Coronation!

A fresh batch of royal recruits are set to POP their cherries and don their crowns for the very first time this April at the Fringe Bar.

These virginal Princes are ready to become KINGS right in front of your very eyes!
There will be primping!
There will be preening!
There will most definitely be pelvic thrusting!

Hosted by Drag Daddy Willy SmacknTush.

Don’t miss their crowning glory, grab your tickets on Eventfinda now from just $20 and ensure the Patriarchy prospers.

Support your local Drag Kings!


Venue, seating & show info

Cash only door sales are available if not sold out prior ($35.00 per ticket)
GA seating is unallocated, make sure to arrive early to nab a good vantage point.


Doors open one hour before showtime.
If you are part of a large group, contact us to save seats together, or you may be split up.
Latecomers may not be admitted, you may be asked to wait before finding your seat if a performance is underway.


The event space is located up 6 upstairs, with narrow walkways between seating. The regular seating are black metal folding chairs, please contact us to discuss alternatives if this isn't an option for you. We will do what we can to accommodate as many bodies and abilities as possible.


Trigger warnings will be given before every show and we have a zero tolerance policy for abusive behaviour.


You can contact us at willysmackntush@gmail.com

Welcome to "Rising Star," the hottest drag competition in Wellington - where the spotlight is on, the outfits are sparkling and the competition is FIERCE!

Join us as we embark on a journey where charisma, uniqueness, nerve, and talent collide as we search for Wellington's next "Rising Star".

We don't just have contestants; we have visionaries, trailblazers, and the future icons of the drag world performing their absolute hearts out for you all.

So come one, come all and watch as the best drag queen, king, or anything in between emerges as the one and only, the undeniable, the legendary... Rising Star 2026!

Any questions please contact Olivia on oliviadreamsnz@gmail.com.

✨❄️ Brewtown Winter Craft Market ❄️✨

Step into a world of winter magic at the Brewtown Winter Craft Market! 🌨️🛍️ Join us on Sunday, 19 July 2026 from 9am – 2pm for a cozy, joy filled day celebrating creativity, community, and all things handmade.

events.venue: Wellington Candle Co., Queen Charlotte Drive, Aotea, Porirua - Mana
events.long_description:
Curious about AI but not sure where to start?

Join this hands-on, beginner-friendly workshop and learn how tools like ChatGPT and Claude can help with everyday tasks, life admin, travel planning, writing, meal planning, learning new skills, and much more.

In this interactive 2-hour session, you'll discover practical ways to use AI, learn simple prompting techniques, and work on a real project that's relevant to you. No technical knowledge required.
Bring your laptop or smartphone and leave with the confidence to start using AI in your daily life.

Perfect for professionals, parents, retirees, side hustlers, and anyone curious about what AI can actually do.

Due to limited spots, your tickets are non-refundable.

events.id: BECOMING HER-The Herb FarmAshhurst, Manawatū-Whanganui Region-Event Brite
events.name: BECOMING HER
events.venue: The Herb FarmAshhurst, Manawatū-Whanganui Region
events.long_description:
Get ready to embrace your power and shine bright at BECOMING HER – your journey starts here!
Join Us for BECOMING HER!

This is day one of three part journey, for the next three months once a month we will be gathering in circle with the intention to bring awareness & awaken what's waiting to come to light.
Together over the space of three weekends, we hold hands and walk the path home to bring forth the woman waiting to be beckoned. We begin the first two circles in winter where we go within.
Naturally throughout the colder months we stay inside to hibernate, and this opens the pertect opportunity to go within to the heart of ourselves and plant the seeds for flowers to grow which will lead us into our final circle in Spring.

BECOMING HER is an event to connect you back to your heart & get out of your head. Its in the silence you hear, you breathe again & you let yourself receive.

This is the therapy girls day you didn't know you needed or maybe you did that's okay to, come as you are is our motto.

We can't wait to see you there

Bri & T x

events.id: Winter Foraging for Wild Edible Weeds-Innermost Gardens Site, Lawson Place, Mt Victoria, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Winter Foraging for Wild Edible Weeds
events.venue: Innermost Gardens Site, Lawson Place, Mt Victoria, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join expert forager Felicity Joy of Wild Botanica for a comprehensive Wild Edible Weeds Foraging Workshop in the beautiful community gardens of Mount Victoria, Wellington.

This inspiring experience will open your eyes to the abundance of edible wild plants growing right under your nose.

We’ll unlock the secrets of foraging, connecting with the land, and turning humble "weeds" into delicious, nourishing food. You’ll gain confidence in identifying common edible plants, learn their botanical names, and understand their medicinal and nutritional benefits. We’ll also explore ethical foraging practices, how to spot toxic lookalikes, and share simple, practical ways to incorporate wild foods into your daily meals.

This isn’t just about food: it’s about sustainability, resilience, and reconnecting with the environment in a meaningful way. You'll leave with a deeper appreciation for the land, new skills to support local food security, and the knowledge to make your own simple meals from wild ingredients.
Class sizes are limited, so book early to secure your spot.

For more details and bookings, visit
www.wildbotanica.co.nz

follow @wildbotanica.nz on social media.

For questions, email felicity@wildbotanica.co.nz.
Join my email newsletter to hear about upcoming foraging workshops: https://www.wildbotanica.co.nz/pages/newsletter

events.id: Khandallah Park Restorers Working Bee-Khandallah Park, Woodmancote Rd, Khandallah, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Khandallah Park Restorers Working Bee
events.venue: Khandallah Park, Woodmancote Rd, Khandallah, Wellington
events.long_description:
If you enjoy the beautiful environment of Khandallah Park, join us for a working bee.

We are a very informal group who try to help the plants, birds and other native species in the park thrive by removing invasive plants and replanting with native plants.

Weather permitting, we meet on the third Sunday of each month for two hours of working at your own pace, after which we enjoy a picnic afternoon tea.

If you’d like to help on 21 June, contact us for where to meet up, what’s planned for the afternoon, and also so we can be sure there’s enough cake and cups for afternoon tea. Email Angus Napier: napiers.ace@gmail.com

events.id: Midnight Blue, Emerald Green Or White Jug Clay Workshop-Alfred Memelink Artspace Gallery, 223 The Esplanade Petone, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: Midnight Blue, Emerald Green Or White Jug Clay Workshop
events.venue: Alfred Memelink Artspace Gallery, 223 The Esplanade Petone, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Create a stunning clay jug using hand building techniques, textures and pressed shapes. Around 5 weeks after the class these will be fired and glazed using the colour of your choice- midnight blue, emerald green or crisp white- creating a gorgeous art piece to display in your home or gift to another. The jug size is approximately 15cm x 12cm and will be water tight once glazed so you can display your favourite flowers with style.

The venue is upstairs in the beautiful Alfred Memelink Artspace gallery where we will create overlooking the sea and surrounded by stunning art works.

Working with clay is good for the soul! Immersing in a gentle creative activity helps to switch off a busy mind, and making decorative vessels is fun and achievable even if this is your first time using clay.

Price includes all materials, glaze and double firings. All materials are provided so all you need to do is turn up ready to have some fun and creativity.

Booking is essential.

events.id: Natural Textured Rings - Taster class-Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Natural Textured Rings - Taster class
events.venue: Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington
events.long_description:
Make your own personalised ring - a gift for yourself or someone special! :-)

Learn the basic technique first in copper or brass, and then craft your final piece in silver!

Over this taster class, you’ll be guided through the process of heating and texturing metal, cutting and shaping with traditional jewellery tools. Use feathers, leaves, or fabrics to create a unique texture for a beautiful ring!

Learn to measure your finger, cut metal to length and texture it without a rolling mill, hammers and punches. Then use a jeweller's torch, sandpaper and polishing, and your beautiful new ring will be ready to wear.

This 3-hour class is ideal for beginners, groups of friends and visitors to the city.

Included in the class fee are copper and brass for practising on, a sterling silver ring strip and all consumables such as sandpaper, solder and chemicals. Extra metals are available for purchase in the studio.

Kids aged 12 (or so) are welcome to attend the class, but any person under the age of 16 must be accompanied by an adult.

events.id: featherStrength (Feather Strength)-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: featherStrength (Feather Strength)
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

Raising a child, reshaping a life.
Filmed over a decade, one father gradually relinquishes his professional life to meet the relentless demands of raising a child with severe developmental challenges. With a camera and no crew, he films intimate fragments of daily life – routine, rupture, exhaustion, and unexpected resilience – as the weight of the new normal presses against the limits of what he can sustain. A meditation on care, acceptance, and the strength required to navigate life’s wilderness. It is a story about learning to bend without breaking, and about discovering meaning not in resolution, but in the act of continuing – living the life you didn’t plan for.

Screening Note: This film is preceded by the short film Chilapa Girl (Niña Chilapa) (17 mins).

events.id: Mel die Storieverteller - LITTLE THEATRE, WELLINGTON - Internet Sensasie-Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Mel die Storieverteller - LITTLE THEATRE, WELLINGTON - Internet Sensasie
events.venue: Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Mel die Storieverteller - LITTLE THEATRE, WELLINGTON - Internet Sensasie
Wellington! Mel is opgewonde om julle in te sluit in haar toer - dit gaan n onvergeetlike middag wees, moet dit nie misloop nie!

In 2023 het nuuskierige gehore opgedaag om te sien hoe internetsensasie, Mel die Storieverteller in lewende lywe op ‘n verhoog lyk. Nou stroom almal na haar vertonings want Mel is toe skreeusnaaks in die regte lewe ook (vir mans en vroue). In haar derde komiese een-vrou vertoning, Internet Sensasie, gaan gehore uitvind dat Mel nou kan sing ook.

Mel deel uit eerstehandse ervaring stories vanuit die elektroniese oerwoud vol aandagsoekers en liggelowiges: Oom Riekert se groot fout, ‘n beplande vuisgeveg in ‘n taai plek met die naam KuduKraal, hoe ‘Mel die Storieverteller’ ontstaan het en die siekte op haar voorkop.

Die stories is vars, onverwags en bleddie belaglik, maar blykbaar alles waar, wel, so sê Facebook, en ons almal weet wat op die internet staan is ‘n feit, maar kan betwyfel word, tensy jy dit wil glo!

Vertoning duur: 80 minute (Geen pouse)
Styl: Komiese Storievertelstyl
Ouderdomsbeperking: Geen onder 14’s
Venue: Little Theatre, Lower Hut (at the Dowse Art Museum)
Adres: 2 Queens Drive, Hutt Central, Lower Hutt 5010, New Zealand
Kaartjieprys: NZ$40
Vertoning begin: 16h00
Kaartjies: www.meldiestorieverteller.co.za

events.id: A War on Women-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: A War on Women
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

A feminist resistance forty years in the making.
Triggered by Mahsa Amini’s death in morality police custody, the film looks beyond headlines to uncover Iranian women’s forty-year struggle against the theocratic regime. Through interviews, archival material, and rare footage from inside Iran, the film traces an unbroken resistance shaped by prison, exile, and courage across generations and borders. Historical and urgent. Personal and political. A testament to the enduring spirit of Iranian women and a call to stand with them.

events.id: Afrikaans Comedy - Mel die Storieverteller-Little Theatre, 2 Queens Dr, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: Afrikaans Comedy - Mel die Storieverteller
events.venue: Little Theatre, 2 Queens Dr, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Mel die Storieverteller is 'n Suid-Afrikaanse komediant met meer as 480,000 aanlyn volgelinge. Sy is bekend vir haar skerp humor en unieke blik op die lewe. Op 19 Julie 2026, bring Mel die Storieverteller haar 3de komiese vertoning 'Internet Sensasie' al die pad oor die waters na Wellington, Lower Hutt- sodat Suid-Afrikaners kan lag in hul taal.

Afrikaans: Mel die Storieverteller bring Afrikaanse komedie vir almal wat die huis mis. Sy deel stories vanuit haar ervaring met die internet: Oom Riekert se groot fout, ‘n beplande vuisgeveg in ‘n taaiplek met die naam KuduKraal, en die siekte op haar voorkop. Die stories is vars, onverwags en bleddie belaglik - Perfek vir die manne en die vroue wat wil kom lag in hul taal.

English: Mel the Storyteller brings Afrikaans comedy to everyone who misses home. She shares stories from her experience with the internet: Oom Riekert’s big mistake, a planned fistfight in a dodgy joint called KuduKraal, and the secret sickness on her forehead. The stories are fresh, unexpected, and bloody ridiculous — perfect for men and women who want to laugh in their own language.

Show starts: 16:00 - 17:30
Doors Open: 15:00

No under 14s - Language and themes

events.id: Elon Musk Unveiled – The Tesla Experiment-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Elon Musk Unveiled – The Tesla Experiment
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

When profit becomes more important than human lives.
It is 2014 and tech billionaire Elon Musk unveils his plans for a self-driving car in front of an enthusiastic audience. What consumers don’t know is that Tesla’s “autopilot” function is far from road-ready, and new owners are being used to improve the software by providing their data. A fatal accident in Florida triggers a protracted lawsuit to hold Tesla accountable, and it is not the only incident. Shocking footage from dashcams and security cameras shows the ruthlessness of Tesla cars suddenly braking or accelerating, and the crashes that follow.

Supported by Geothe Institut NZ

events.id: Half Moon-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Half Moon
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

What is the role of art in times of great suffering and tragedy?
New York based clarinettist virtuoso and composer Kinan Azmeh, a Damascus-born musician living in exile, attempts to find meaning and purpose after the outbreak of war in Syria. Director Scheffer follows Kinan over several years as he attempts to navigate this crisis of identity and purpose, travelling the world for concerts with other world-class musicians (including Yo-Yo Ma), revisiting his former mentors, and developing his own unique style of improvisational composition. A profound exploration of the role art can play in forging identity and community.

events.id: Unlikely Kin-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Unlikely Kin
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

Whales, trees, and kinship beyond species.
Filmed over five years, the film follows elder Hori Parata (Ngāti Wai) and his son Te Kaurinui as they lead the sacred practice of harvesting stranded whales—not for consumption, but to honour whakapapa and revive traditional medicine used to treat kauri dieback, a disease killing New Zealand’s ancient trees. Alongside this, we follow their conservation work protecting the kiore, the Polynesian rat that arrived in Aotearoa with the great Māori migration. Interweaving themes of kinship between species, cultural survival, and Māori-led conservation, the film offers rare access to a sacred world where whales, forests, and even rats are revered as living ancestors.

Made with the support of NZ On Air, RNZ, New Zealand Film Commission, Pacific Islander in Communication.

Screening Note: In-cinema only. This film screens exclusively in cinemas in Auckland, Wellington and Christchurch. It will not be available on the Doc Edge Virtual Cinema. We encourage you to catch it on the big screen.

Q&A: Kim Webby (Director) and Michael Jonathan (Director) will be present at the screening.

Alongside this, we follow their conservation work protecting the kiore, the Polynesian rat that arrived in Aotearoa with the great Māori migration.

Love in all its forms — between partners, species, and the places we call home. A granddaughter captures sixty years of love meeting ten years of Alzheimer’s, while a 1970s discovery rewrites what we thought we knew about seagulls and partnership. Tender, surprising, and quietly profound.

This is a collection of four short films:

Love Birds (28 mins)
Stoats’ Last Stand (15 mins)
The Place Where Memories Hide (22 mins)

events.id: Phileo Quartet-Michael Fowler Centre, 111 Wakefield Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Phileo Quartet
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, 111 Wakefield Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Comprising members of the Vienna Philharmonic, and founded by New Zealand violinist Benjamin Morrison, the Phileo Quartet will be bringing the unique warm timbres and waltz-like momentum of one of the world's greatest orchestras to Wellington this July, performing works by Brahms, Korngold, Hugo Wolf, and Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart.

PROGRAMME

Hugo Wolf, Italian Serenade
Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart, String Quartet No. 19 in C major, K. 465 “Dissonance”
Johannes Brahms, String Quartet No. 2 in A minor, Op. 51 No. 2
Erich Wolfgang Korngold, String Quartet No. 2 in E-flat major, Op. 26

ARTISTS

Benjamin Morrison, Violin
Holger Groh, Violin
Robert Bauerstatter, Viola
Wolfgang Härtel, Cello

While each of the world’s top-tier orchestras has a unique sound, perhaps none is more strongly identifiable than the fabled refinement, blend, balance, warmth, and unified aesthetic of the Vienna Philharmonic, from which the Phileo Quartet has been drawn.
In their first ever tour of New Zealand, and under the leadership of Ōtautahi Christchurch-born violinist Benjamin Morrison, the Phileo Quartet will present a programme that speaks directly to the unique sound, style, and traditions of the Vienna Philharmonic.

"The upcoming concert tour is a personal highlight for me as it will be the embrace of two lands I call home!" says Benjamin. "The programme we are bringing is a real treat, and I not only look forward to proudly showing my colleagues where I come from, but also to share this wonderful music with Aotearoa."

events.id: Finding Honk-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Finding Honk
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

A search for reconciliation, shaped by truth and imagination.
When singing legend Hemi Pere is diagnosed with a terminal illness, he tasks his son, Hayden, with finding an estranged family member, “Honk”, so they can make peace before time runs out. As Hayden navigates Honk’s connections within the criminal underbelly, he must decipher clues and red herrings to solve the mystery of Honk’s whereabouts. The closer Hayden comes to finding Honk, the further he travels into danger. To fulfil his father’s dying wish, Hayden is pulled into the shadows of his own family history, where vengeful enemies and hidden truths threaten everything he holds dear.

Q&A: Eldon Booth (Director), Heydon Hohaia (Subject, Producer) will be present at The Capitol Cinema, Auckland and The Roxy, Wellington screenings.

events.id: Sukundimi Walks Before Me-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Sukundimi Walks Before Me
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

Where a river’s rights become a people’s fight.
The Sepik River is the mother line for Papua New Guinea communities. Winding through mountains and rainforests, she is the crucial vertebrae connecting and supporting the region’s rare biodiversity and spiritual consciousness. But her livelihood and her communities are threatened by the proposal of a copper-gold mine being built near at her headwaters, which could extract, erode and pollute an environment that she has sustained for millennia.   The children of this river, led by Manu Peni, create a grassroots campaign to stop the mine from being built, resisting the forces of colonial bureaucracy and Western narratives of ‘development’ by invoking the Spirit of the river and indigenous knowledge.  Sukundimi Walks Before Me, explores this existential fight through lyrical expressions of existence, resistance and life along the mother river.

This film is supported by RNZ, Screen Inc, Pacific Islanders in Communications (PIC), the Shark Island Foundation, the Three Springs Foundation, Screen Australia and VicScreen.

Q&A: Matasila Freshwater (Director), Kerry Warkia (Producer), Maria Tanner (Producer) will be present at all Auckland screenings. Matasila Freshwater (Director) will also be present at The Roxy, Wellington screening.

The Sepik River is the mother line for Papua New Guinea communities.

events.id: Quotemonster Debate - Palmerston North-Manawatu Golf Club IncPalmerston North, Manawatu-Wanganui-Event Brite
events.name: Quotemonster Debate - Palmerston North
events.venue: Manawatu Golf Club IncPalmerston North, Manawatu-Wanganui
events.long_description:
A bold new event format for advisers. Join us for a high-energy event designed to challenge thinking and spark smarter conversations.
The financial advice industry is evolving rapidly — regulation, technology, remuneration, KiwiSaver, medical insurance, AI, compliance… and plenty of strong opinions.
This event series puts the spotlight on those opinions.
While you’ll still receive valuable partner insights, practical updates, and short presentation segments, the centrepiece of the event is a structured, live debate between advisers on one carefully selected controversial industry question.
This isn’t a traditional roadshow — and it isn’t just a debate either. It’s a dynamic mix of structured debate, practical updates, and interactive engagement designed to make industry events more meaningful and memorable.
Spaces are limited to ensure a high-quality experience and strong engagement.
Come ready to think.
Come ready to challenge.
Come ready to vote.
Arrive from 9:15am for tea, coffee, and networking. Session begins promptly at 9:30am.

events.id: B+LNZ Marlborough Farming for Profit Mid-Winter Dinner 2026-Lansdowne HubBlenheim, Marlborough-Event Brite
events.name: B+LNZ Marlborough Farming for Profit Mid-Winter Dinner 2026
events.venue: Lansdowne HubBlenheim, Marlborough
events.long_description:
Marlborough Mid-Winter Dinner: a night of farming stories, top speakers, great food, and celebration for the local farming community.
After another big year on farm, it’s time to put down the tools and enjoy a well-earned night out at the Marlborough Mid-Winter Dinner. This event has become a firm favourite on the calendar for our local farming community, and this year promises to be another great one.
Hear real stories from real farmers, sharing the journeys, challenges, and opportunities that have shaped their businesses.
We’ve lined up a couple of outstanding speakers who know what it takes to grow and adapt in farming:
Jojo Wallis
Large-scale deer and cattle farmer with a strong development background. Jojo will share insights from his latest challenge, developing a newly acquired 5,000ha property in the Haka Valley, as well as some great stories from a family deeply connected to both farming and aviation.
Hugh and John Jackson - Father & Son duo.
Farming in Te Akau (just north of Raglan), this father-son duo is currently running approximately 2,000ha, 2,000 cattle and 2,500 ewes. 4 generations ago the initial 240ha was purchased in 1947. With Hugh as the 2025 Young Farmer of the Year and John bringing extensive governance experience in the agricultural education sphere most notably in the development of the Agribusiness in schools curriculum and the development of Growing future farmers.
Keeping the evening running smoothly is our MC, Kurt Lindsay from Bayleys.
Dinner will once again be catered by the team at Kaiaka, and we’re grateful for the support of our sponsors: Marlborough Helicopters, Bayleys, Rabobank, MPI On Farm Support, Blenheim Toyota FMG and Segard Masurel.
A big thank you to the Marlborough Farming for Profit Committee for continuing to drive this event for the benefit of our local farming community.
Your Beef + Lamb Northern South Island Director and Board Chair Kate Acland will also be in attendance on the night. Kate will be available the hour before the event commences to answer any specific questions you might have.
Come along, bring your mates, and join us for a night of good food, good yarns, and stories worth taking home.

A big thank you to the Marlborough Farming for Profit Committee for continuing to drive this event for the benefit of our local farming community.

events.id: The Trial of Alec Baldwin-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Trial of Alec Baldwin
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

Convicted by the headlines before the courtroom.
On October 21, 2021, cinematographer Halyna Hutchins is killed on a movie set by a prop gun held by the actor Alec Baldwin. In the immediate aftermath of the tragedy, virtually no one doubts that it was an accident and a deeply traumatizing experience for everyone involved. After the shock subsides, questions of responsibility, guilt, blame, punishment and justice begin circulating in the media. Baldwin becomes a lightning rod for online haters and a target for pushy paparazzi. With prosecutors weighing criminal charges, what unfolds is driven less by the facts surrounding the case and more by who Alec Baldwin is, both as a man and a public figure.

events.id: Ranginui: Call of the Ice-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Ranginui: Call of the Ice
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

What is revealed depends on how closely you are willing to look.
Antarctica in winter is one of the most extreme places on Earth: isolated, locked in darkness, and -50°C. Māori astronomer Rangi Matamua and language expert Mataia Keepa enter this frozen world, where science and Indigenous knowledge meet in real time. Rangi carries a personal promise to honour his grandfather’s dying wish and share Māori starlore beyond Aotearoa. When unexpected snowstorms roll in, they are forced to stay longer than planned, cut off inside a continent that continues to shift and close in around them. Through auroras, storms, first sunrise and shifting ice, they learn to read a living landscape most will never witness.

Made with the support of Te Māngai Pāho, TVNZ and RNZ.

Q&A: Julia Sartorio (Director & Producer), Miriama Kamo (Producer) will be present at all Auckland screenings. Julia Sartorio (Director & Producer) will also be present at the Roxy, Wellington screening.

events.long_description:
BOHM PRESENTS
EDDIE IZZARD
THE TRAGEDY OF HAMLET

This production comes directly from her critically acclaimed solo performances of Shakespeare’s HAMLET, which were triple-extended in New York, Chicago, and London.

Nominated for New York’s: Drama League’s Distinguished Performance Award and Outer Critic’s Circle Outstanding Solo Performance Award.

“An impressive sweeping performance." ★★★★ – The Telegraph

“When it gets to the famous soliloquies, Izzard…smashes all three of the great ones.” ★★★★ – The Chicago Tribune

“Eddie Izzard’s Hamlet is spectacular.” – Dame Judi Dench

Akin to the original performances of HAMLET, Eddie will perform on the blank canvas of a bare stage, letting Shakespeare’s words and pure storytelling take the spotlight.

events.venue: Studio 73, 73 Main Street, Greytown
events.long_description:
Murder and Mystery returns to Studio 73 as part of Greytown's Festival of Christmas
There will be murder and mystery at Greytown Little Theatre in July when Radio STU73 returns with A Sherlock Holmes Radio Mystery , adapted from the story, 'A Scandal in Bohemia', and Agatha Christie’s 'Butter in a Lordly Dish'. ‘The Times’ of London called it, "one of Agatha Christie’s most gruesome murder plots”.
Join the studio live audience for the 1945 Christmas Season as these two radio plays are performed to ‘listeners’ and audience alike. This show is in the style of the Hercule Poirot inspired radio mysteries that have been as part of the Greytown’s Festival of Christmas in 2024 and 2025. This year we are back in Studio 73 for a cabaret style show.

events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

The best tribute isn’t a speech—it’s a bucket list.
When Royd Tolkien, great-grandson of J R R Tolkien, loses his brother, Mike, to Motor Neurone Disease (ALS), Mike leaves behind one final act of love and mischief: a bucket list of 50 challenges designed to drag his reluctant, comfort-zone-hugging brother into the land of the living. What follows is an unexpected journey across New Zealand that pushes a grieving man further than he ever imagined possible. Narrated by Benedict Cumberbatch, with Peter Jackson, Billy Boyd, John Rhys-Davies and Weta Workshop along for the ride, the film is a raucous, tender argument that the best eulogy isn’t a speech, it’s a bucket list.

events.id: The War Below: Restoring Hope in the Solomon Islands-Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The War Below: Restoring Hope in the Solomon Islands
events.venue: Roxy Cinema, 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
Doc Edge Festival 2026

Beneath the beauty lies a dormant terror.
In the Solomon Islands, thousands of unexploded WWII bombs remain under homes and schools. 80 years after the war, bombs continue to kill and injure families. Lorettalyn lives with severe injuries from a blast that killed her husband and young son, now raising her remaining children while navigating long-term physical and economic hardship. Maeverlyn, who survived a separate explosion that severely injured her and killed members of her community, establishes Bomb Free Solomon Islands – a survivor-led organisation advocating for awareness, support, and long-term solutions to the ongoing threat.

Screening Note: This film is preceded by the short film, Digital Traditions: Kastom Keeper (19 mins).

Q&A:
Tuki Laumea (Director & Producer), Cleo Fraser (Producer, Executive Producer) and Florie Dausabea (NZ Solomon Islands community leader) will be present at all Auckland screenings.
Tuki Laumea(Director & Producer), Cleo Fraser(Producer, Executive Producer) will also be at the Wellington screening.

events.long_description:
The Business Roadshow – Wellington Day | Networking & Leadership Workshop
World-class leadership workshop + genuine connection networking for women in business. Limited spaces, high value.

About this Event

The Business Roadshow Wellington
A collaboration between &
Sponsored by Two/FiftySeven

📍 Two/FiftySeven, Wellington CBD
🗓️ 23rd July 2026
🕙 10am - 12pm

Empower Her Community and Stelar Business Event have joined forces to create The Business Roadshow. A nationwide series of intimate, high-value events for women in business, rolling out across Aotearoa ahead of the Stelar Business Event in October.

What to expect
Each event is two focused hours, packed with real value, real connection, and the kind of conversations that actually move the needle in your business.

A leadership session with Kate Ruby Bradshaw

Kate is the co-founder of Stelar Business Event and an internationally recognised coach and facilitator who has spent 18 years delivering transformational leadership experiences to leaders across the globe. Her session will stretch how you think and sharpen how you lead; this is the kind of training people pay hundreds of dollars for, and it's included in your $20 ticket.

Facilitated networking with Empower Her Community

Not the awkward business-card shuffle. Real, facilitated connection where you'll genuinely meet and get to know other brilliant women in business in your area. Because the connections you make in business aren't just nice to have, they're often the thing that changes everything. You'll leave with new relationships already forming, and if you're heading to Stelar Business Event in October, you'll already know some of the women who'll be in that room too.

Light refreshments included - because great conversations are even better with something in hand.

This is for you if you're ready to think bigger, connect deeper, and leave operating at a higher level in your business.
You're done with surface-level events. You want substance, genuine connection, and a room full of women who share your drive. That's exactly what this is.

Your ticket
Tickets are just $20 - essentially a commitment fee to say you'll show up. The value in the room will far exceed it. These events are intentionally intimate, so spaces are strictly limited. Grab your spot before it's gone.

What should I wear?
Business casual. Polished, comfortable, and ready to have great conversations. Think of it as dressing like the business leader you are, because you are one.

About the organisers
is a vibrant global community for women in business, built around genuine connection, support, and growth. Founded by Victoria Bahadoor and Charlotte Clark, Empower Her Community creates spaces where every woman feels welcome, valued, and part of something bigger.
is Aotearoa's premier values-led event for established women in business, where ambition is anchored in integrity and intelligent conversations spark meaningful action. The Business Roadshow is the national series leading into the Stelar Business Event 2026 on 17th October in Auckland.

Haven't got your Stelar Business Event ticket yet?
As part of the Empower Her community, you get 15% off. Use code EMPOWERHER at stelarbusinessevent.com
We'll see you there.

events.id: Songs from the Shaky Isles: A Short History of Popular Music in New Zealand-Corner Molesworth and Aitken Streets, Thorndon, New Zealand 6011,Cellar-Cafe, Molesworth St, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Songs from the Shaky Isles: A Short History of Popular Music in New Zealand
events.venue: Corner Molesworth and Aitken Streets, Thorndon, New Zealand 6011,Cellar-Cafe, Molesworth St, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Songs from the Shaky Isles: A Short History of Popular Music in New Zealand
Gareth Shute dives into his book Songs From the Shaky Isles to deliver a whirlwind overview of the past 100 years of popular music in Aotearoa.

Join us at the Library or online:

New Zealand Music: From pubs to playlists
From the era of ‘six-o'clock swill’ venue restrictions through to the grassroots 80s battle for radio airtime, then on to the seismic shift of the streaming age, Gareth Shute reveals how our music has always been intertwined with wider culture.

Gareth’s talk will be packed with fun, behind-the-scenes anecdotes about the wild characters who helped define the New Zealand music scene and will also be accompanied by some fascinating historic images from the Alexander Turnbull Library collection.

About the speaker
Gareth Shute writes regularly for the New Zealand music history website Audioculture | Iwi Waiata and is also the author of six books, including Hip Hop Music in Aotearoa, which won a national book award. He is also a musician who helps run Lil’ Chief Records and has toured overseas with two of their bands — The Brunettes, and The Ruby Suns. His current band is Thee Golden Geese.

Image: Gareth Shute. Book cover of Songs From The Shaky Isles: A Short History of Popular Music in New Zealand (Bateman Books, 2025).
