events.long_description:
Focus on Grammar [ESOL Levels 3 and 4]
NZ Citizens and residents pay $95 with ID, all others pay $175.
Can you communicate well but feel you need to review some basic grammar like tenses, articles and prepositions? This topic-based course will help you improve your grammar skills. 
By the end of the course, you will: 
• Be able to structure sentences more correctly
• Have more control over verb forms and tenses and how they are used with negatives and questions
• Use articles more correctly and with the meaning that you want to convey
• Be more confident using prepositions and adverbs
• Speak and write with more control over your grammar
• Have some answers to your grammar questions 
If you're enrolling with us for the first time, please contact the CEC office (04 385 8919) before enrolling so that we can check the level of your English to make sure that you are in the correct class.

events.id: The Joy of Improvisation-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: The Joy of Improvisation
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
The Joy of Improvisation
Improv is theatre without a script and a fun way to build your skills in many areas, including team work, public speaking, storytelling and on-your-feet creative thinking.
This is the perfect place for absolute beginners to get acquainted with the joy and art of improvisation, inspired by the generous creative philosophy of Keith Johnstone. It's also a great place to get back into improv if you've been away from it for a while.
WIT has been teaching this course in association with the CEC for almost 20 years now. It uses fun games and structured exercises to teach the foundation skills of improv. These are a prerequisite for further courses and membership of WIT (www.wit.org.nz), as well as useful in themselves.
If you have questions about the course, please contact WIT directly on info@wit.org.nz.
By the end of the course, you'll have: 
• Explored basic principles of improvisation
• Enjoyed interacting in a friendly, positive and supportive environment
• Developed more confidence, presentation and communication skills
• Experienced joyful moments of improvisation
The course will end with a directed demonstration of class exercises for invited friends and family.
[This is a physical activity that involves an element of risk.  While all possible measures are taken to prevent accidents or injury, please note that participation is at your own risk]. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Never Mind the Rowlocks - Here's the Wellington Sea Shanty Society EP release tour (Pōneke/Wgtn) #1-Breaker Bay & Moa Point Community,150 Breaker Bay Rd, Breaker Bay, Wellington 6022, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Never Mind the Rowlocks - Here's the Wellington Sea Shanty Society EP release tour (Pōneke/Wgtn) #1
events.venue: Breaker Bay & Moa Point Community,150 Breaker Bay Rd, Breaker Bay, Wellington 6022, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Never Mind the Rowlocks - Here's the Wellington Sea Shanty Society EP release tour (Pōneke/Wgtn) #1
The wave doesn’t conform to the shape of the rock. It smashes it. The sea doesn’t pretend to be hospitable. It summons courage. And a shantyband never licks the captain's boot. They release another EP!

The best shanties invite us to open our hearts along with the bottle. To draw strength from camaraderie and drown our pettinesses. To seek the rough edge of the wave and savour the salt within. And so, the WSSS have issued a call to arms with their new collection: Never Mind the Rowlocks, Here’s the Wellington Sea Shanty Society.

Never fear if you find yourself adrift on a sea of AI slop, lapping at your hull, lulling you to sleep — Lake Davineer, Vorn dont Le Père Était Marin, Young Nic, Tomas el Largo, and Louis Quatorze are back again to fill your sails and slap the codfish slime clean off your cheeks.

Just when everyone is lamenting the empty galleys of the good ship Wellington, and those ol’ scurvy-mongers are promising renewed bounty if you would only tack five degrees to port or starboard come election time, these songs remind us the sea at least will sustain us, and there’s rum and (metaphorical) guns for them that wants to fight.

Lin Seal | Wellington Nautical History Monthly

⚓⚓⚓

The scuttlebutt is true...⛵

The most seaworthy sea shanty band in the South Pacific is releasing their long-awaited 4th collection of new and old maritime bangers:
'Never Mind the Rowlocks - Here's the Wellington Sea Shanty Society'.

All aboard as we set sail for the salty horizon. Prepare to be battered once again by the 4-sailor squall of pure shanty.

ALL SHOWS

WED 3 JUNE - 5Tapped Limited, Waiharakeke/Blenheim
THU 4 JUNE - Scott’s Brewing, Ōamaru
FRI 5 JUNE - Pioneer Hall, Ōtepoti/Dunedin
SAT 6 JUNE - Naval Point Club, Ōhinehou/Lyttleton
SUN 7 JUNE - The Dog’s Bone, Whakatū/Nelson
FRI 3 JULY - Point Chev Sailing Club, Tāmaki Makaurau/Auckland
SAT 4 JULY - Leigh Sawmill, Leigh (Arvo show)
SUN 5 JULY - Calliope Sea Scout Hall, Devonport
FRI - 31 JULY - Breaker Bay Hall, Pōneke/Wellington #1
SAT - 1 AUG - Breaker Bay Hall, Pōneke/Wellington (KIDS show)
SAT - 1 AUG - Breaker Bay Hall, Pōneke/Wellington #2

Tickets to all shows: https://wellingtonseashantysociety.com/shows


⚓⚓⚓

WELLINGTON SEA SHANTY SOCIETY

Aotearoa New Zealand's Most 'Sea Worthy' Shanty Group™ have been belting out traditional and original sea songs from Aotearoa, France, Spain, the UK and more, since time immemorial (2012).
The W.S.S.S have toured Aotearoa and France multiple times and are known for their rowdy and participatory live shows. They've performed at Paimpol (one of the world's largest shanty festivals), Splore, Albany Folk Festival (Aus), Quebec Sea Shanty Festival (Canada) and a shipload of other festivals in NZ, France & Canada.

They will be playing new classics from their forthcoming EP 'Never Mind the Rowlocks' as well as your foamy favourites from the 'Sea Shanties of the 70s, 80s, & Today!' EP and their 'Now That's What I Call Sea Shanties' series.

It smashes it.
The sea doesn’t pretend to be hospitable.
And so, the WSSS have issued a call to arms with their new collection: Never Mind the Rowlocks, Here’s the Wellington Sea Shanty Society.
They will be playing new classics from their forthcoming EP 'Never Mind the Rowlocks' as well as your foamy favourites from the 'Sea Shanties of the 70s, 80s, & Today!'

events.id: The Pearl Jam Experience-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: The Pearl Jam Experience
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
The Pearl Jam Experience Announces Ultimate Greatest Hits Tour for New Zealand

Kiwi rock fans are in for the ultimate celebration of grunge history as The Pearl Jam Experience crosses the Tasman to deliver a definitive, career-spanning greatest hits set. This special tour will honour the massive catalog of the Seattle icons, delivering a full-throttle, high-energy performance packed to the brim with Pearl Jam’s biggest anthems and fan favourites.
Widely regarded as the premier tribute to Pearl Jam, the band is renowned for recreating the atmosphere, the madness, and most importantly, the sonically accurate music that defined a generation.
Their commitment to the music’s authenticity goes beyond the stage. In a remarkable nod to Pearl Jam’s golden era, the band recently recorded a collaborative cover of the hit song Go with former Pearl Jam drummer Dave Abbruzzese. Abbruzzese, who drove the rhythm section during the band's peak explosive years, lent his signature powerful drumming to the track and mixed it for the group.
The Pearl Jam Experience also maintains a deep connection to the band's philanthropic legacy. They are proud supporters of the EB Research Partnership (EBRP), the largest nonprofit funding research for a cure for Epidermolysis Bullosa (EB), co-founded by Jill and Eddie Vedder.
For New Zealand fans, this is the ultimate chance to experience a high-octane concert that embodies the true sound and spirit of Pearl Jam, delivered with the energy, precision, and respect this legendary rock catalog deserves.

San Fran holds a Supervised License. Minors are welcome to attend events at San Fran so long as they are accompanied by a legal guardian at all times whilst at the venue.

The Pearl Jam Experience | San Fran, Wellington 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗣𝗲𝗮𝗿𝗹 𝗝𝗮𝗺 𝗘𝘅𝗽𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗲𝗻𝗰𝗲 𝘕𝘦𝘸 𝘡𝘦𝘢𝘭𝘢𝘯𝘥 𝘛𝘰𝘶𝘳 2026 𝗧𝗶𝗰𝗸𝗲𝘁𝘀 𝗼𝗻 𝘀𝗮𝗹𝗲 𝗻𝗼𝘄 ➥ bit.ly/pjx26-well 📆 Friday, July 31 📍 San Fran, Wellington 𝘜𝘯𝘥𝘦𝘳 18 𝘢𝘭𝘭𝘰𝘸𝘦𝘥 𝘸𝘪𝘵𝘩 𝘨𝘶𝘢𝘳𝘥𝘪𝘢𝘯 Doors: 8:00pm 𝐓𝐡𝐞 𝐏𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐥 𝐉𝐚𝐦 𝐄𝐱𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐀𝐧𝐧𝐨𝐮𝐧𝐜𝐞𝐬 𝐔𝐥𝐭𝐢𝐦𝐚𝐭𝐞 𝐆𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐬𝐭 𝐇𝐢𝐭𝐬 𝐓𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐍𝐞𝐰 𝐙𝐞𝐚𝐥𝐚𝐧𝐝 Kiwi rock fans are in for the ultimate celebration of grunge history as The Pearl Jam Experience crosses the Tasman to deliver a definitive, career-spanning greatest hits set.
This special tour will honour the massive catalog of the Seattle icons, delivering a full-throttle, high-energy performance packed to the brim with Pearl Jam’s biggest anthems and fan favourites.
Widely regarded as the premier tribute to Pearl Jam, the band is renowned for recreating the atmosphere, the madness, and most importantly, the sonically accurate music that defined a generation.
Their commitment to the music’s authenticity goes beyond the stage.
In a remarkable nod to Pearl Jam’s golden era, the band recently recorded a collaborative cover of the hit song Go with former Pearl Jam drummer Dave Abbruzzese.

events.id: Capital of Kai-Te Waka Huia Wellington Museum, 3 Jervois Quay, Queens Wharf-WOAP
events.name: Capital of Kai
events.venue: Te Waka Huia Wellington Museum, 3 Jervois Quay, Queens Wharf
events.long_description: How did Wellington become a culinary paradise? Capital of Kai tells the story through 100 years of Wellington's food creatives, restaurant workers, and café culture. It's a love letter to the hospo whānau who have shaped the city. Walk through galleries of heritage treasures: the Green Parrot's working griddle, Hiakai's light-up signage, Il Casino's couch, Shanghai restaurant's 1980s menu, the Matterhorn café's cowbell. You'll see the arc from 1930s British hotel dining (roast mutton, boiled vegetables, milky tea) through European influences brought home by migrants and travellers, and the diverse cuisines - Chinese, Malaysian, Indian, Syrian, Greek, Māori - that define Wellington now. Hungry for more? Feed your curiosity at Capital of Kai.

events.id: Konbini Moments-Orient, 29b College Street-WOAP
events.name: Konbini Moments
events.venue: Orient, 29b College Street
events.long_description:
Konbini Moments returns - bigger, bolder, and more delicious than ever. Inspired by the joy of Japan’s iconic convenience stores, this year’s event builds on the favourites you loved last time, with an exciting line-up of new flavours and thoughtful additions.
Expect the return of the most popular offerings; mochi donuts, melon pan, onigiri, and Japanese sandos, each reworked with fresh ideas, seasonal twists, and flavour combinations designed to surprise and delight. These favourites made last year’s Konbini Moments week such a hit.
Konbini Moments is about celebrating everyday Japanese food culture: the small pleasures, the clever flavours, and the beauty of well-made convenience.

events.id: Miramar Taco'ver-Double Vision Brewing, 128a Unit E Park Road-WOAP
events.name: Miramar Taco'ver
events.venue: Double Vision Brewing, 128a Unit E Park Road
events.long_description:
The laneway beside Double Vision Brewing transforms into a full Mexican street fiesta. Expect a stacked lineup of taco vendors serving bold, authentic flavours, from slow-cooked meats and grilled proteins to handmade tortillas and cow tongue tacos (the crowd favourite that asks questions and gives bold answers). Mexican dancers and live entertainment bring the atmosphere to life from start to finish. Free entry, family-friendly, pay as you go. 
This year, there's an extra draw: three brand-new festival beers brewed for Visa Wellington On a Plate make their exclusive first appearance here. It's a sneak peek at what's coming, a celebration of Mexican culture supported by the Mexican Embassy since day one, and proof that the best food spreads through word of mouth, locals telling locals, friends bringing friends, the kind of buzz that fills a laneway.

events.id: The Siren Song Tavern-Little Beer Quarter, 6 Edward Street-WOAP
events.name: The Siren Song Tavern
events.venue: Little Beer Quarter, 6 Edward Street
events.long_description:
For August, LBQ becomes The Siren Song Tavern, a place where sea folk, land folk, and the windblown commuter gather to drink, converse, and trade tales of the ocean and whatever waits beyond Oriental Bay. This is where sailors would gather if they'd discovered craft beer. 
Expect proper tavern fare built for winter nights and long conversations: slow-braised beef and dark ale pie with mash and winter greens, lamb shoulder with rosemary jus, roasted kūmara and caramelised onion tart. Behind the bar: ales worth docking for, rum with backbone, rotating New Zealand craft options, and two house cocktails brewed exclusively for the Tavern. Dishes pair with beer and rum, the kind of food that keeps you steady while the stories grow taller. Come in out of the southerly, find your crew, add your version to the legend.

events.id: Wellington Opera — Manon-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Wellington Opera — Manon
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
For its 2026 season, Wellington Opera brings one of the most popular and enduring French operas to life. Manon by Jules Massenet will feature Kiwi-Samoan tenor Pene Pati and Kiwi-Egyptian soprano Amina Edris. Joining this powerhouse duo is another New Zealander, baritone Phillip Rhodes will sing the role of Lescaut. Manon tells the tragic love story of Manon Lescaut and the Chevalier des Grieux. The story unfolds in five acts, exploring themes of love, desire, and the consequences of choices made in pursuit of pleasure. 
Pene Pati is widely known for his Sol3 Mio fame. He is a recent New Zealand Arts Laureate and is the first Samoan tenor to perform on the world’s top stages, including the Opéra national de Paris, Covent Garden, London and the Metropolitan Opera, New York.

events.id: Never Mind the Rowlocks - Here's the WSSS Kids show (Pōneke/Wgtn)-Breaker Bay & Moa Point Community,150 Breaker Bay Rd, Breaker Bay, Wellington 6022, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Never Mind the Rowlocks - Here's the WSSS Kids show (Pōneke/Wgtn)
events.venue: Breaker Bay & Moa Point Community,150 Breaker Bay Rd, Breaker Bay, Wellington 6022, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Never Mind the Rowlocks - Here's the WSSS Kids show (Pōneke/Wgtn)
Kids shanty show!
Come singalong to your favourite whānau-friendly sea shanties!

Featuring:
- a treasure hunt!
- nautical games!!
- best dressed prizes!!!

⚓⚓⚓

The scuttlebutt is true...⛵

The most seaworthy sea shanty band in the South Pacific is releasing their long-awaited 4th collection of new and old maritime bangers:
'Never Mind the Rowlocks - Here's the Wellington Sea Shanty Society'.

All aboard as we set sail for the salty horizon. Prepare to be battered once again by the 4-sailor squall of pure shanty.

ALL SHOWS

WED 3 JUNE - 5Tapped Limited, Waiharakeke/Blenheim
THU 4 JUNE - Scott’s Brewing, Ōamaru
FRI 5 JUNE - Pioneer Hall, Ōtepoti/Dunedin
SAT 6 JUNE - Naval Point Club, Ōhinehou/Lyttleton
SUN 7 JUNE - The Dog’s Bone, Whakatū/Nelson
FRI 3 JULY - Point Chev Sailing Club, Tāmaki Makaurau/Auckland
SAT 4 JULY - Leigh Sawmill, Leigh (Arvo show)
SUN 5 JULY - Calliope Sea Scout Hall, Devonport
FRI - 31 JULY - Breaker Bay Hall, Pōneke/Wellington #1
SAT - 1 AUG - Breaker Bay Hall, Pōneke/Wellington (KIDS show)
SAT - 1 AUG - Breaker Bay Hall, Pōneke/Wellington #2

Tickets to all shows: https://wellingtonseashantysociety.com/shows

⚓⚓⚓

WELLINGTON SEA SHANTY SOCIETY

Aotearoa New Zealand's Most 'Sea Worthy' Shanty Group™ have been belting out traditional and original sea songs from Aotearoa, France, Spain, the UK and more, since time immemorial (2012).
The W.S.S.S have toured Aotearoa and France multiple times and are known for their rowdy and participatory live shows. They've performed at Paimpol (one of the world's largest shanty festivals), Splore, Albany Folk Festival (Aus), Quebec Sea Shanty Festival (Canada) and a shipload of other festivals in NZ, France & Canada.

They will be playing new classics from their forthcoming EP 'Never Mind the Rowlocks' as well as your foamy favourites from the 'Sea Shanties of the 70s, 80s, & Today!' EP and their 'Now That's What I Call Sea Shanties' series.

events.id: Good Craic-Regent, 55 Ghuznee Street-WOAP
events.name: Good Craic
events.venue: Regent, 55 Ghuznee Street
events.long_description:
In partnership with the Embassy of Ireland, Wellington On a Plate 2026 kicks off at Regent with two evenings celebrating a culture that knows how to throw a great party.

On the bar the Regent team will be slinging a bunch of Irish whiskeys, Guinness of course, and a lineup of Irish-themed cocktails including the Tipperary. Aoife Gallagher-Forbes, head chef at Swimsuit and 2024 VWOAP Next Gen winner, takes over the Regent kitchen with a short menu of her favourite Irish meal - Beef and Guinness pie, colcannon, buttered soda bread and Irish sherry trifle to finish. Get the full meal or a combo of the four. Naturally they'll collaborate on an Irish Coffee cocktail for the occasion.
Irish musicians also will settle in for a seisiún each night.

Cocktails from 2pm, food from 5pm. Free to attend.

events.id: Pāua to the People-Cambridge Road Vineyard, 32 Cambridge Road-WOAP
events.name: Pāua to the People
events.venue: Cambridge Road Vineyard, 32 Cambridge Road
events.long_description:
For many of us, pāua is widely known, yet rarely tried beyond fritters, or the classic creamed version. Get it right, though, and it deserves a place at any kiwi table. Get it wrong and you may as well sole your boots with it. 

Join a collection of chefs, fishermen, and locals for a deep dive into the life, nutrition, and uses of our most iconic seafood. The masterclass starts with local wine, then into demonstrations of technique in the despatch, preparation, and cooking of pāua, and open forum discussion as you explore ultimate expressions of this taonga. Roll your sleeves up to learn techniques first-hand at a grazing pace, with refreshments to hand. A communal meal follows, celebrating our local oceans and prepared by masterful chefs. Pāua appears eight ways, kelp in all its glory, and smoke, fire, and fish take centre stage. 
Your ticket includes welcome beverages, eight courses featuring pāua in multiple expressions, matched wines or non-alcoholic options throughout.

Join a collection of chefs, fishermen, and locals for a deep dive into the life, nutrition, and uses of our most iconic seafood.

events.id: Gustioso-Capitol Restaurant, 10 Kent Terrace-WOAP
events.name: Gustioso
events.venue: Capitol Restaurant, 10 Kent Terrace
events.long_description:
Capitol brings a set menu cooked with clarity and intention: love of local produce and respect for the seasons.

Start with focaccia alla griglia, grilled bread with Wairarapa extra virgin olive oil. Move into a twice-cooked soufflé of house-aged and smoked cheddar. The centrepiece: a pastry-topped pie of Syrah-braised venison shank with puréed parsnip, deep, rich, built to linger. Finish with semifreddo alle prune, black doris plum ice with candied orange.

This is a four course set menu for $87.50. Alcohol can be purchased separately.

events.id: Pōneke Pony-Beach Babylon, 232 Oriental Parade-WOAP
events.name: Pōneke Pony
events.venue: Beach Babylon, 232 Oriental Parade
events.long_description:
Middle-earth has found its way to Oriental Bay. For August, Beach Babylon hosts a tavern pop-up that feels like somewhere you stumble into wandering hobbits and stay longer than planned, candlelight, conversation, a space removed from the everyday.

The menu nods to tavern traditions: Shire comfort, Rohan indulgence, Gondor richness. Food that leans generous and satisfying, designed for settling in rather than moving on too quickly. Hobbits eat early and often. Dwarves favour bold, indulgent flavours. Rangers keeping things simple and well chosen. And somewhere in the mix, the quiet suggestion that a certain ring might not be entirely accounted for. The drinks keep things grounded. Local craft beers, classic pours, and a few special concoctions for those with a sense of adventure. Choose your path through three courses, entrée, main, dessert, with options that work whether you're a hobbit or a ranger. Costumes welcome. Just arrive hungry.

events.id: A Secret Worth Keeping-QT Wellington, 90 Cable Street-WOAP
events.name: A Secret Worth Keeping
events.venue: QT Wellington, 90 Cable Street
events.long_description:
You are invited into a world of secret passwords, hidden rooms and late-night indulgence, where the evening unfolds far beyond the dining table. With a nod to 1920s speakeasies and French jazz supper clubs, this one-night-only event takes over QT Wellington, revealing its hidden corners. You're are encouraged to dress for the occasion and settle into the atmosphere of the roaring twenties.
A whispered password is your invitation to an evening of mystery, indulgence and unexpected encounters. The night begins in the Blind Tiger with cocktails, canapés, freshly shucked oysters and high stakes before unfolding
 into an immersive dining experience accompanied by live music upstairs in Maison Hippo. Gather around the table for a three-course shared feast that celebrates the best of New Zealand produce with a French twist. Designed to spark conversation, connection and a little intrigue, this is a night where every detail is part of the story.
The ticket includes canapés, an oyster station, a three-course dinner, one cocktail, one glass of champagne, and all entertainment. Additional beverages are available on consumption.

events.id: Glory of the Cape-Union Square, 10 Memorial Square-WOAP
events.name: Glory of the Cape
events.venue: Union Square, 10 Memorial Square
events.long_description:
Chef and owner Adam Newell shares a menu celebrating the wild coast and world class cuisine of Cape Palliser. This three course feast features the very best of the South Wairarapa: start with Cape Palliser poached crayfish ravioli with pāua and watercress beurre blanc. Move to Pirinoa lamb, tender, and spiced, with winter greens and dukkah. Finish with Meyer lemon posset and vanilla shortbread fingers. The Margrain Vineyard team will be there too, sharing the story of some of the oldest vines in Martinborough and the wines selected for each course. 
Your ticket includes three courses with wine pairings and sparkling water.

events.id: A Culinary Tale of India-Sushil's Musclechef Café, 27 Aotea Drive-WOAP
events.name: A Culinary Tale of India
events.venue: Sushil's Musclechef Café, 27 Aotea Drive
events.long_description:
Experience the diverse flavours of India through Masterchef Contestant and two-time Burger Wellington finalist Chef Sushil’s personal culinary journey. This six-course degustation alternates between the rich, wheat-based dishes of North India and the light, spicy, rice-based specialties of South India, reflecting centuries of regional tradition and coastal influence.
Each course is introduced by Chef Sushil, who shares the stories and family traditions behind the recipes that have been passed down over generations. Guests will enjoy a seated dinner where each dish highlights the iconic techniques, spices, and flavours that define its region.
The ticket includes the full six-course degustation, chef-led introductions, and a take-home gift bag with treats.

events.id: Crying Tiger-Tuk Tuk Thai Kitchen, 15 Hartham Place North-WOAP
events.name: Crying Tiger
events.venue: Tuk Tuk Thai Kitchen, 15 Hartham Place North
events.long_description:
Charcoal smoke, sizzling meat, and you're in Nong Khai, a small northeast Thai town where families gather around the grill for Crying Tiger steak. Beef steak marinated 48 hours in coriander root, garlic, black pepper, oyster sauce, palm sugar, whiskey, and mandarin juice, char-grilled until the meat weeps with its own juices.
The chef grew up cooking this dish with family and friends. It arrives with sticky rice and a Northern Thai Isarn dipping sauce of tamarind, lime juice, ground chillies, and fresh coriander. Alongside: a sharp granny smith apple salad dressed with chilli and fish sauce, topped with prawns; and tom zapp, slow-cooked pork soft bones that melt into a soup fragrant with kaffir lime, lemongrass, and ground rice.
This is a three course set menu for $75.  Alcohol can be purchased separately.  Minimum 2 orders per table.

events.id: The Lock INN-Thistle Inn, 3 Mulgrave Street-WOAP
events.name: The Lock INN
events.venue: Thistle Inn, 3 Mulgrave Street
events.long_description:
Before late licences and 3am closes there was the lock-in.
When the doors officially shut, the real night began. If you were in the know, you stayed. Curtains were drawn, doors were locked and glasses filled. For one night only, The Thistle Inn is bringing that spirit back through an after-hours evening of drinks, canapés, long-table dining and live music. 
The Lock INN is a modern take on the after-hours tradition - an evening that begins with drinks and canapés before guests gather for lavish long-table dining once the doors close to the public.
Think generous shared plates, flowing drinks, and the kind of feast that’s built for passing, pouring and lingering at the table. As the evening unfolds, a live band shifts the atmosphere as the night carries on, as things move into a more social close.
It will be a little indulgent, a little rebellious and entirely unforgettable.
Your ticket includes canapés, a five-course shared feast, four drinks served throughout the evening, and live music.

events.id: Beekeeping - Beginners-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Beekeeping - Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Beekeeping - Beginners
By the end of the workshop, you'll be able to:
• Describe how a bee colony functions
• List the components of a hive
• State the key steps involved in setting up a beehive
• Describe the key aspects of managing a bee colony
• Explain where to obtain bees and equipment
• List sources for finding more in-depth information about beekeeping 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. events.long_description:
Cooking: Homemade Chinese Egg Noodles and Pan-fried Buns
Homemade egg noodles topped with your favourite sauce creates a tasty meal. Learn how to create Chinese style egg noodles that can serve as the basis for many traditional noodle dishes.  Create soft meat buns in the traditional Chinese style. Filled with succulent and juicy meat filling, these buns are perfect for a simple breakfast, lunch or snack.
Some communal ingredients will be supplied but a list of additional ingredients needed will be sent out when course numbers are confirmed. (approximate cost $35)
Please bring an apron, dish cloth, small rolling pin, sharp knife, tea towels and containers to take food home.
Note: course is not suitable for those who are vegetarian or gluten-free.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
make a selection of Chinese egg noodles and buns to take home
understand more about the Chinese egg noodle-making process
understand more about the Chinese bun-making process
feel more confident to make Chinese egg noodles and buns by hand at home
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. events.name: Creative Problem-Solving Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Creative Problem-Solving Workshop
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Through engaging puzzles, group activities, and proven creativity techniques, you'll discover how to tackle challenges from new angles and find solutions you might never have considered. Learn to break through mental blocks, generate innovative ideas, and lead groups to make better decisions together. This workshop draws on Neil's background in teaching, test-preparation, and creative thinking to help you unlock your natural problem-solving abilities in a supportive environment.
What You'll Get From This Workshop:
·       Simple techniques to generate ideas individually or in a group: brainstorming, mind-mapping, and more
·       Practice solving puzzles that sharpen your lateral thinking skills
·       Learn how to overcome blocks and restrictive thinking patterns
·       Experience effective group leading methods you can use with your own teams
·       Discover how to identify root causes of problems instead of just treating symptoms
·       Take home specific tools for approaching problems differently
·       Build confidence in leading problem-solving sessions with others
Who This Workshop Is For: This workshop is for anyone who faces challenges at work, leads meetings or teams, manages projects, or simply wants to become a better problem solver. Perfect for team leaders, community organizers, and anyone who's ever been stuck in an unproductive meeting wondering "there must be a better way!" No special background needed—just bring your curiosity and willingness to try new approaches.
What to Expect: A day filled with interactive activities, problem-solving challenges, and practical techniques you can use right away. You'll learn proven steps and while practicing facilitation skills to help groups make decisions more effectively. Neil creates a relaxed atmosphere where everyone feels comfortable contributing ideas and experimenting with new approaches. You'll leave with specific tools you can apply to your own challenges, plus handouts to help you remember what you've learned.
Neil's unique skill as an educator is his rare ability to blend evidence-based teaching techniques with the perfect dash of humor and storytelling, creating a learning environment where complex concepts become accessible and new skills feel immediately applicable to your real-world challenges.
  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.name: Crime Writing - An Introduction
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Crime Writing - An Introduction
Step into the world of crime writing and discover how to build stories that grip readers from page one. Whether you’re brand new to writing or already have a few drafts under your belt, this course will sharpen your skills and push your storytelling further.
 Bring a notebook and pens—you’ll need them.
 Across the sessions, you’ll explore:
Writing fundamentals that keep prose tight and compelling.
Crime genres and what makes each tick.
Heroes, villains, and everyone caught between.
Research techniques that make your fiction feel authentic.
Plotting, story arcs, and whether outlines are your best friend—or sworn enemy.
How to craft loglines, taglines, and blurbs that hook readers instantly.
 Designed and taught by Cat Connor, internationally published crime writer with a shelf full of thrillers, this course is equal parts practical and fun. Expect a fast pace, plenty of exercises, and tools you can carry into any writing project.
 By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Map out a crime novel or short story.
Create believable, layered characters.
Spot (and fix) passive writing.
Build solid research habits.
Use plot diagrams to tighten structure.
Write sharp loglines, taglines, and blurbs.
 Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Knitting for Beginners
Come and reap the benefits of a mindful and meditative craft-form.  Meet other people interested in making things with their hands, chat over crafting and find out what the craft craze is all about.
Join crafter Stella Carruthers as she shares her ten years plus knitting experience in this beginner’s knitting class that supports each student as an individual learner.
Master basic knitting techniques, learn how to cast stitches on and off the needles, explore the knit and purl stitches and practice how to hold the needles in a way that is comfortable for you - then learn how to knit in the English style.
Knitting needles and yarn are included in the course fee, but you're welcome to bring along your own 4-ply yarn if you wish. 
By the end of the course you will have created two squares of knitting, one in basic garter stitch and the other utilising both knit and purl stitches to create the knitted fabric called stocking stitch.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. events.id: Thriving Through Change: Growing Resilience & Cultivating Courage-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Thriving Through Change: Growing Resilience & Cultivating Courage
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Thriving Through Change: Growing Resilience & Cultivating Courage
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Cultivating true resilience is an intentional practice of the mind, body, and soul. It is rooted in deep self-knowledge and personal empowerment. In our rapidly changing world, the ultimate question we must ask ourselves is: How can I unlock my unique strengths and capacities to not just survive these demanding times, but truly thrive?
By implementing a "living" practice of mindful strategies, creative expression, and physical awareness, we can empower our thinking, elevate our emotional well-being, and bring a renewed vitality to our life's work.
What to Expect
This workshop offers you a rich tapestry of practical tools, storytelling, and collaborative discussion. Together, we will explore diverse pathways to foster courage, helping you shift from a state of survival to one of thriving—even while navigating the daily pressures of stress, grief, anxiety, or the fear of an uncertain future.
What You Will Take Away:
Mindful Strategies: Daily, actionable practices to anchor your thoughts and reduce overwhelm.
Creative & Physical Outlets: Expressive tools to release tension and process complex emotions.
A Personal Roadmap: A clearer understanding of your innate strengths to help you navigate future transitions with confidence.
Details & What to Bring:
Duration: Full-day workshop
What to bring: A personal journal and your favourite pen or pencil. (However, paper and pen will be provided if you need).
Step out of the survival loop. Join us to reclaim your inner strength and cultivate the courage to thrive.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your session.
 Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Writing Short Stories
In this one-day course, we’ll explore the elements that make short stories shine, from creating compelling character dynamics, to choosing the point of view that best serves your story.
Through a mix of discussions and practical writing exercises, participants will leave the course with all the tools they need to write engaging short stories.
Please bring pen and paper, and the name of a short story you like.  Tea and coffee available for your breaks, please bring your lunch and snacks.

- Write using different points of view

- Use detail to develop setting and character traits
- Identify places to send your completed short stories

events.id: South Summit - 'RUN IT BACK' New Zealand Tour | Wellington-San Fran,171 Cuba Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: South Summit - 'RUN IT BACK' New Zealand Tour | Wellington
events.venue: San Fran,171 Cuba Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
South Summit - 'RUN IT BACK' New Zealand Tour | Wellington
Australia’s South Summit are coming back to New Zealand in support of their new album ‘RUN IT BACK’ (released 12th June). The ‘RUN IT BACK New Zealand Tour’ see’s South Summit return to Queenstown, Lyttleton, Auckland and Wellington for an epic run of their iconic high energy shows.

‘RUN IT BACK’ marks the bands second album release, and has featured singles ‘TOP OF THE HILL’ and ‘ON THE DASH’. The twelve-track collection of music represents a period of experimentation for the band; one where they found themselves pulled towards threads of funk, hip hop and R&B, while also driven by a desire to galvanise their signature blend of reggae and rock.

The New Zealand tour promises South Summit’s energetic live performance, with tracks from the new album mixed in with some classic fan favourites.

events.id: Waenga-Te Raukura ki Kapiti, Coastlands Theatre, 34a Raumati Road, Raumati, New Zealand 5032,34 Raumati Rd, Raumati Beach, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu-All Events In
events.name: Waenga
events.venue: Te Raukura ki Kapiti, Coastlands Theatre, 34a Raumati Road, Raumati, New Zealand 5032,34 Raumati Rd, Raumati Beach, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Waenga
Created by Hariata & Tamati Moriarty and directed by Jim Moriarty, Waenga centres on a young wahine Māori who is arrested and finds herself in a police holding cell. Alongside her journey is Peyton, who is coming to understand what a Tangata Tiriti partnership is and Grayson, a young Māori lawyer navigating his role in the system.

Performed by an ensemble cast, including well known rangatahi Hariata and Tamati Moriarty, the play is presented in Te Rākau’s unique Theatre Marae framework, creating space for collective reflection and shared experience.

Te Rākau is the longest running Māori theatre company in Aotearoa founded in 1989.

Recommended age 12+ contains swearing and depicts some violence. Children under 16 to be accompanied by a caregiver.

--

For accessibility seating information and wheelchair space bookings please email or call 04)9740535 during office hours.

Door sales are only available if the event doesn't sell out prior.

events.id: Never Mind the Rowlocks - Here's the Wellington Sea Shanty Society EP release tour (Pōneke/Wgtn) #2-Breaker Bay & Moa Point Community,150 Breaker Bay Rd, Breaker Bay, Wellington 6022, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Never Mind the Rowlocks - Here's the Wellington Sea Shanty Society EP release tour (Pōneke/Wgtn) #2
events.venue: Breaker Bay & Moa Point Community,150 Breaker Bay Rd, Breaker Bay, Wellington 6022, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Never Mind the Rowlocks - Here's the Wellington Sea Shanty Society EP release tour (Pōneke/Wgtn) #2
The wave doesn’t conform to the shape of the rock. It smashes it. The sea doesn’t pretend to be hospitable. It summons courage. And a shantyband never licks the captain's boot. They release another EP!

The best shanties invite us to open our hearts along with the bottle. To draw strength from camaraderie and drown our pettinesses. To seek the rough edge of the wave and savour the salt within. And so, the WSSS have issued a call to arms with their new collection: Never Mind the Rowlocks, Here’s the Wellington Sea Shanty Society.

Never fear if you find yourself adrift on a sea of AI slop, lapping at your hull, lulling you to sleep — Lake Davineer, Vorn dont Le Père Était Marin, Young Nic, Tomas el Largo, and Louis Quatorze are back again to fill your sails and slap the codfish slime clean off your cheeks.

Just when everyone is lamenting the empty galleys of the good ship Wellington, and those ol’ scurvy-mongers are promising renewed bounty if you would only tack five degrees to port or starboard come election time, these songs remind us the sea at least will sustain us, and there’s rum and (metaphorical) guns for them that wants to fight.

Lin Seal | Wellington Nautical History Monthly

⚓⚓⚓

The scuttlebutt is true...⛵

The most seaworthy sea shanty band in the South Pacific is releasing their long-awaited 4th collection of new and old maritime bangers:
'Never Mind the Rowlocks - Here's the Wellington Sea Shanty Society'.

All aboard as we set sail for the salty horizon. Prepare to be battered once again by the 4-sailor squall of pure shanty.


⚓⚓⚓

WELLINGTON SEA SHANTY SOCIETY

Aotearoa New Zealand's Most 'Sea Worthy' Shanty Group™ have been belting out traditional and original sea songs from Aotearoa, France, Spain, the UK and more, since time immemorial (2012).
The W.S.S.S have toured Aotearoa and France multiple times and are known for their rowdy and participatory live shows. They've performed at Paimpol (one of the world's largest shanty festivals), Splore, Albany Folk Festival (Aus), Quebec Sea Shanty Festival (Canada) and a shipload of other festivals in NZ, France & Canada.

They will be playing new classics from their forthcoming EP 'Never Mind the Rowlocks' as well as your foamy favourites from the 'Sea Shanties of the 70s, 80s, & Today!' EP and their 'Now That's What I Call Sea Shanties' series.
events.description:

events.id: South Summit ‘RUN IT BACK’ New Zealand Tour | Wellington-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: South Summit ‘RUN IT BACK’ New Zealand Tour | Wellington
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
South Summit are soon to release their sophomore album, RUN IT BACK on June 12 and to celebrate, they have announced RUN IT BACK NZ TOUR.
Coming to Aotearoa this July and August the band will take on Queenstown hotspot Yonder on Thursday 30 July, The Loons, Lyttleton on Friday 31 July, San Fran, Wellington on Saturday 1 August and Auckland’s Double Whammy on Sunday 2 August. 
Run It Back marks the band's second album, featuring stand out singles ‘TOP OF THE HILL’ and ‘ON THE DASH’ which have sent strong signals toward the confident evolution of their sound. The twelve-track record  represents a period of experimentation for the band; one where they found themselves pulled towards threads of funk, hip hop and R&B, while also driven by a desire to galvanise their signature blend of reggae and rock.
In 2024, they released their highly anticipated debut album The Bliss, a reggae-infused indie rock record that showcased both tight songwriting and a willingness to experiment across styles. It debuted at #2 on the ARIA Album Charts and propelled the band into a new tier, with heavy Triple J support, major festival slots and international touring, including appearances at Falls Festival, Groovin The Moo, SXSW Sydney, Vivid LIVE Debut at the Sydney Opera House plus a huge Australasian headline tour.
After a recent visit to Aotearoa to play at Six 60’s new Country & Roots Festival Sweet Home Aotearoa, South Summit now return to New Zealand for their third headline tour in the country. These four shows promise the bands’ renowned live charisma and classic fan favourites, whilst welcoming fans into a brand new era. 

San Fran holds a Supervised License, meaning minors are welcome to attend events at San Fran so long as they are accompanied by a legal guardian at all times whilst at the venue. Minors are of course not allowed to purchase alcohol under any circumstance.

events.id: Beginners Mahjong Lunch-Mr Go's, 3 Eva Street-WOAP
events.name: Beginners Mahjong Lunch
events.venue: Mr Go's, 3 Eva Street
events.long_description:
Pull up a chair and learn a game that’s meant to be shared. This relaxed Sunday session at Mr Go’s pairs beginner mahjong tuition with a generous three-course Chinese lunch - perfect for friends, couples, or anyone happy to join in tables of four.

Guests will be welcomed with tea and a brief introduction to the game. In the first session (10.30am–1pm), you’ll learn and play first, then settle in for lunch. In the afternoon (2–4.30pm), lunch comes first, followed by the game. 

Expect a warm, social room and plenty of help as you go. The three-course menu features dishes such as dumplings and spring rolls to start, General Tso’s chicken to share, Shanghai braised pork belly with rice and pickles for main, and a coconut & egg tart to finish. 
Ticket price includes all food, mahjong tuition and guided play, tea on arrival, and a take-home mahjong guide. Alcohol available to purchase.

events.id: Maille à Maille-Le Bouillon Bel Air, 88 Tory Street-WOAP
events.name: Maille à Maille
events.venue: Le Bouillon Bel Air, 88 Tory Street
events.long_description:
Since 1747, Maille has been synonymous with French gastronomy. The supplier to the court of King Louis XV, it remains one of the most recognised French condiment brands in the world. This August, Maille teams up with Le Bouillon Bel Air for Menu du Jour: the special collaborative bistro series serving up every Sunday of August.

Le Bouillon Bel Air brings a corner of 1890s Paris to Wellington, offering classic French cuisine in a fast, affordable setting that feels anything but ordinary. Each Sunday brings a new menu of bistro dishes with Maille mustard woven through the cooking. A meeting of two French institutions, every Sunday in August.

events.id: Pasta Giapponese-Kazaguruma, 272 Cuba Street-WOAP
events.name: Pasta Giapponese
events.venue: Kazaguruma, 272 Cuba Street
events.long_description:
Taste the dishes driving Japan's newest food obsession: pasta made with Japanese ingredients, techniques and instincts. Think Wagyu bolognaise, Yakitori Aglio e Olio, shiso pesto, and mentaiko cream — dishes that feel completely familiar and entirely new at the same time.
It's a combination that's been quietly building for decades, ever since Japanese chefs trained in Italy brought authentic technique home and discovered how well their own ingredients responded to it. What's emerging now is something neither culture could have made alone. 

Your ticket includes two courses and a glass of organic Marlborough wine.

events.id: Kumutoto Kai me te Kōrero-Graze Wine Bar, 95 Upland Road-WOAP
events.name: Kumutoto Kai me te Kōrero
events.venue: Graze Wine Bar, 95 Upland Road
events.long_description:
Kumutoto Awa was one of the living streams of Pōneke long before the city buried it underground in 1866. This four-hour guided hīkoi traces its path from the Kelburn hills to the waterfront, connecting it through kai, kōrero, and the people who are working to restore the place of the awa in the city's future – The Kumutoto Restoration Project. You'll start with a light lunch and cocktail at Graze in Kelburn which will be a collaboration between Chef Max Gordy and the Kumutoto Community Orchard founder and avid forager Milan Magan. You will also meet your hosts Martin and Micheline who will regale you with stories about Wellington's lost streams. From there, the group walks to the only remaining stretch of the Kumutoto stream still visible above ground, where soup waits alongside more kōrero about the whakapapa of the awa. The route then follows the buried path of Kumutoto down the Terrace to Bert's Bar for a final cocktail created with a foraged ingredients from Kumutoto before finishing at the mouth of the awa on the waterfront. All food and drinks across the event are included in your ticket, with ingredients foraged from the Kumutoto Community Orchard and ngahere. Wear comfortable shoes and dress for the weather. The route covers approximately two kilometres and includes steps.
The ticket price includes an optional foraging workshop with Milan Magan. You'll spend an hour in the orchard and ngahere learning about what you'll be eating and drinking the following day. Saturday 1 & 29 August 9-10am.

You'll start with a light lunch and cocktail at Graze in Kelburn which will be a collaboration between Chef Max Gordy and the Kumutoto Community Orchard founder and avid forager Milan Magan.
The route then follows the buried path of Kumutoto down the Terrace to Bert's Bar for a final cocktail created with a foraged ingredients from Kumutoto before finishing at the mouth of the awa on the waterfront.

events.id: Licence to Sip-Southern Cross, 39 Abel Smith Street-WOAP
events.name: Licence to Sip
events.venue: Southern Cross, 39 Abel Smith Street
events.long_description:
In this game of secrets, your best weapon is word of mouth. Head to Southern Cross for a wine tasting where what you say is just as important as what you sip. 
Licence to Sip pairs premium Spy Valley wines with hands-on gameplay. Start with a glass of bubbles, then work in teams to solve clues, crack codes, and “unlock” each of your five wine pours. 
Every glass is matched with a roaming canapé and a new layer of the mystery. As the rounds unfold, you’ll share intel, compare notes, and stay one step ahead of the competition. Your ticket includes five wine pours, canapés, guided commentary, and a chance to win prizes. 
 Gather your team of 2 to 6 friends and get ready for a tasting that’s equal parts clever, social, and delicious.

events.id: Speak, Easy...-Lustre Kitchen and Dining, 7-13 Seaview Road-WOAP
events.name: Speak, Easy...
events.venue: Lustre Kitchen and Dining, 7-13 Seaview Road
events.long_description:
Simon Sweetman, Wellington's poet and writer, will host an evening of off-beat poetry, live jazz, and prohibition-era food served as roaming tapas. Think 1920s glamour meets modern vulnerability. The poetry explores what this year's theme actually means: gossip, confession, taboo, inhibition, and Simon reads from his own collection alongside tributes to the beat poets. Then the mic opens. The Lustre team will read their own and if you want to read something you've written (or just stashed away), you can! Spot prizes appear for categories Simon dreams up on the night.

Five to six substantial canapés and small plates flow throughout, built from what Lustre's hyper-seasonal local suppliers have available that week, it's comfort food meets glamour. The drinks menu reflects the era: cocktails, old fashioneds, sparkling wine, moonshine.

Signature High Tea's | Steam Incorporated
☕🚂 Introducing Steam Incorporated’s NEW Signature High Tea Experiences! 🚂☕

Step aboard for a refined winter escape unlike anything else on the Kāpiti Coast. Selected Sundays this August, travel in heritage style behind the magnificent 111-year-old steam locomotive Ab 608 ‘Passchendaele’ aboard a leisurely rail journey from Paraparaumu or Waikanae through the scenic Horowhenua countryside.

events.id: Next Gen Cook Off-LTD., Level 1, 60 Dixon Street-WOAP
events.name: Next Gen Cook Off
events.venue: LTD., Level 1, 60 Dixon Street
events.long_description:
The first four Mondays in August, an up-and-coming chef takes over the Everybody Eats kitchen at LTD and delivers a three-course dinner built from rescued food, cooking it their way and style. Walk in, take a seat, and let the Everybody Eats volunteers whānau take care of the rest. No bookings. Pay what you can. 
Sixth year running, and the model stays simple: good food, good company, and a room full of people making a difference one plate at a time. Every course uses rescued ingredients, because eating is fundamental and everyone deserves access to something . 

3 August — Oscar Little, Hummingbird
Oscar Little started his culinary career five years ago as a commis chef at Shed 5 and The Crabshack, where he learned what it means to commit to a team. He’s since worked his way up to chef de partie at Hummingbird. Along the way he’s volunteered with Everybody Eats, and it’s that spirit of giving back to the community that drives him to compete this year.

10 August — Brayden Puketapu, Koji
At 22, Brayden Puketapu is already developing a cooking voice that’s distinctly his own. Working at Koji, he’s drawn to the creative challenge of transforming overlooked ingredients and putting his own stamp on both classic and contemporary dishes. His heritage is central to that identity, grounding his food in something personal while keeping it fresh and inventive.

17 August — Eugenous Evan, Rosella
Indonesian-born Eugenous Evan came to Wellington five years ago and hasn’t looked back. After stints at Shepherd, Hillside & Daisy’s, and Supra, he’s now chef de partie at Rosella, where he’s been for just over a year. For Eugenous, cooking is an act of care: a way to share something of himself with every person who sits down to eat.

24 August — Aryan Dhankhhar, Mr Go’s
Aryan Dhankhhar’s love of food started young, holding weekend cooking competitions with his sister and himself in their family kitchen in Haryana, India, with their mother as judge. Since then he’s built a career spanning hotels, restaurants, and catering, working across techniques from sous vide to traditional barbecue. A highlight: leading the team at Wilson Barbecue during Wellington On a Plate 2024, where they set a world record selling over 6,000 burgers in a single day. Now at Mr Go’s, he brings that same drive and precision to every plate.

Next Gen Cook Off is proudly supported by The Gas Hub.

He’s since worked his way up to chef de partie at Hummingbird.
Along the way he’s volunteered with Everybody Eats, and it’s that spirit of giving back to the community that drives him to compete this year.
After stints at Shepherd, Hillside & Daisy’s, and Supra, he’s now chef de partie at Rosella, where he’s been for just over a year.

events.id: Bon Voyage-WOLF by Foxglove, 33 Queens Wharf-WOAP
events.name: Bon Voyage
events.venue: WOLF by Foxglove, 33 Queens Wharf
events.long_description:
The kitchen meets the office with the South African head chef WOLF and French business leader have come together through six courses that fuse their two cuisines and take you on a trip that starts in Paris and lands in Cape Town. Menus are styled as boarding passes, drinks menus as passports, and three exclusive cocktails reflect French and South African classics. 

Start with shared biltong, then pumpkin and cinnamon macarons. A ratatouille crumble with goat cheese follows. The mains are the heart: slow-cooked Boeuf Bourguignon with a South African twist. French cheeses paired with South African relish and roosterkoek bridge the continents. A cardamom-infused crème brûlée topped with a traditional koeksister closes the night. It's a generous, memorable, and intimate. 

Your ticket includes six courses, shared starters, and a cheese course. Alcohol available to purchase.

events.id: Once Upon a Time in Wellington-Supra, Level 1, 31 Dixon Street-WOAP
events.name: Once Upon a Time in Wellington
events.venue: Supra, Level 1, 31 Dixon Street
events.long_description:
We are hosting some very special guest chefs over a 2 week period, with each chef joining us for a 2 night collaboration. Their identities won't be reveiled until the day before via Instagram. Speculate, believe the rumours, start the rumours for all we care! Three local legends, one from outside Wellington, and a roster that reads like the highlight reel of modern cooking in this country. It will be a small a la carte format during our usual trading hours.  Walk ins are encouraged but bookings for groups of 4 or more it is highly recommended. The menus will be small and à la carte throughout. 
Guest #1 (4-5 Aug) The Taipan
Our first guest is a previous collaborator, you know him, you love him, we love him. If you've eaten his food before, you know subtlety isn't on the menu. Expect bold and aggressive flavours, and probably some offal. 
Guest #2 (7-8 Aug) The Kodiak Bear
Lover of restaurants and a wizard with words on paper. We are very excited to introduce this guy and give him a creative platform to show Wellington something special from a unique perspective. 
Guest #3 (12-13) The Suzumebachi
Joining us from the Hawkes Bay, This man brings a tempered approach to cooking from his time cooking in Japan. Now an established award winning chef in New Zealand, and someone we consider at the pinnacle of modern cookery in this country. Expect Japanese flavours and techniques utilizing the some of the best produce NZ has to offer.
Guest #4 (14-15) The Bighorn Ram
With many curls in his horns, this guy has been pivotal and arguably one of the most important chefs Wellington has ever known, setting the stage for many of us who followed. An award winning chef who pioneered modern cuisine in this City, you wont want to miss this. Expect a measured and wise approach to fun flavour combinations with precision.

events.long_description:
This is an intimate guided tasting exploring the conversation between chocolate and wine. Across five curated pairings, taste each element individually before bringing them together, uncovering how sweetness, tannin, texture and origin transform one another.

From Pacific-origin chocolate to expressive fortified wines, each match tells a story of how it was found; through experimentation, happy accidents, and recommendation. Led by Gabe Davidson (co-founder and bean-to-bar maker) and Josh Pointon (one of the country's top sommeliers), this is a structured, indulgent tasting designed to be flavour-focused and genuinely educational. 
Your ticket includes five chocolate pairings and five wines.

Your ticket includes five chocolate pairings and five wines.

events.id: Battle of the Wine Regions-Rosella, 18 Majoribanks Street-WOAP
events.name: Battle of the Wine Regions
events.venue: Rosella, 18 Majoribanks Street
events.long_description:
Three winemakers, three regions, one night.
Join us at Rosella for our annual charity dinner where three of New Zealand’s best winemakers go head-to-head, each representing their own region in a battle for bragging rights. Each winemaker will present their wines across a four course lineup, pairing their selections in their own way. Your job is to taste, compare, and decide who gets it right.
Expect thoughtful pairings and a bit of friendly rivalry, with each round poured side by side. At the end of the night, while you settle into dessert, we’ll tally the votes and crown the winning region.
Good wine, good people, and a cause worth backing. Proceeds going to Life Flight.

events.id: A Royal Affair-Noble Rot Wine Bar, 6 Swan Lane-WOAP
events.name: A Royal Affair
events.venue: Noble Rot Wine Bar, 6 Swan Lane
events.long_description:
You're invited to an evening shaped by ceremony, tradition, and a touch of intrigue. Inspired by the wines that grace state banquets and formal royal occasions of the British Royal Family, this intimate gathering draws from producers associated with the Royal Household. Bottles will be chosen for their pedigree, poise, and quiet authority. For one exclusive evening, Noble Rot’s cellar reflects the mysterious spirit of Buckingham Palace’s own, offering a rare opportunity to taste wines in the style of those served to the Monarchy and its most esteemed guests.
You’ll be welcomed with a glass of Champagne from one of the Family’s favoured Royal Warrant houses, before indulging in a luxurious 5-course degustation with paired wines fit for a King and Queen. As each special bottle is revealed, the expert sommelier team will guide each pour, sharing the provenance of the producers and the historic royal occasions where these styles have been served.

events.id: Ferry Tale Pop Up-Wellington Railway Station, Bunny Street-WOAP
events.name: Ferry Tale Pop Up
events.venue: Wellington Railway Station, Bunny Street
events.long_description:
Introducing the Ferry Tale, a Wairarapa lamb burger with smoked scamorza, Zany Zeus roasted garlic yoghurt, pickled red onion, rocket, and spiced tomato relish, in a Pōneke Bakery pretzel bun.

Inspired by the shared stories and flavours of Te Whanganui-a-Tara and the journeys across Cook Strait / Moana o Raukawakawa, the offering is built around comfort, familiarity, and the spirit of travel between islands, served in the iconic Interislander ship box. The pop-up travels across the city through August, appearing outside Wellington Railway Station for most of it's time but also at a few mystery locations throughout the festival. Burger and fries served takeaway, with chutney included for dipping.

events.id: French Cheese Escape-Le Marché Français, 262 Thorndon Quay-WOAP
events.name: French Cheese Escape
events.venue: Le Marché Français, 262 Thorndon Quay
events.long_description:
Step into a little corner of France without leaving Wellington.
French Cheese Escape invites you on a curated tasting journey through France’s most iconic French cheese and their regions. During this relaxed experience, you’ll enjoy a guided “fromage flight” featuring four carefully selected French cheeses - from creamy and delicate to bold and beautifully aged.
Each guest will receive a tasting passport and region guide, allowing you to travel from North to South, East to West and beyond, discovering the stories, traditions and terroir behind each cheese. Alongside your selection, you’ll enjoy artisan crackers or baguette and a perfectly matched paste to complement the flavours.
Come with friends, a date, or solo, and immerse yourself in the tastes of France at your own pace.

events.id: Dessert First-Le Ciel Bakery, 6/100 Tory Street-WOAP
events.name: Dessert First
events.venue: Le Ciel Bakery, 6/100 Tory Street
events.long_description:
For Chef Shuchi dessert has always been the most emotional part of a meal. It's where people slow down, and where conversation softens. For this event you're encouraged to skip right to dessert for this five course dessert tasting menu each tied to a memory. Some from Shuchi's life, some shared in our collective memories, all rooted in the small, often overlooked moments that shape how we experience sweetness.

Raspberry panna cotta tastes like school lunches and summer berries. Rich brownie with hazelnut mousse is the sweet thing you crave after a long day. The lemon cake that became Le Ciel's bestseller without anyone realising. Apple crumble soufflé carries memories of Shuchi's grandmother. Passionfruit posset brings the taste of home from Tanzania. Each course is plated with technique and restraint, refined, but never losing its warmth or intent. You'll spend an evening in the warm, slow space where dessert deserves to be.

Your ticket includes five plated courses plus one palate cleanser.

events.id: Scent + Savour-Iris Store + Studio, 78a Victoria Street-WOAP
events.name: Scent + Savour
events.venue: Iris Store + Studio, 78a Victoria Street
events.long_description:
Fragrance and chocolate share a quiet language of layers, memory, and mood. This guided tasting brings the two together in a way that feels both thoughtful and unexpected.
Hosted at Iris Store + Studio, guests are led through five pairings of fine chocolate and fragrance over 90 minutes. Each pairing is explored through guided scenting and tasting, with commentary that draws out the parallels and contrasts between the two.
Your ticket includes a guided tasting of five chocolates paired with fragrance. Guests will also receive a 10% discount to use on a future purchase in-store.

events.id: This is How We Eat-Chow, Level 1, 45 Tory Street-WOAP
events.name: This is How We Eat
events.venue: Chow, Level 1, 45 Tory Street
events.long_description:
Chow celebrates the most powerful recommendation of all: the one passed across a family table. Jimmy Mahakit (Isaan, Thailand) and Kunpheng "Ricki" Zhu (Jiangsu, near Shanghai) have each spent ten years as Sous Chefs at Chow. For one night, they cook together, each presenting dishes learned at home through watching, tasting, and being guided at the stove. These are home-learned recipes, passed down through families and remembered rather than written. That's word of mouth in its purest form.

Jimmy's cooking leans bold, herb-heavy, and grilled, with chilli heat and fermentation. Ricki's focuses on balance, braise, clarity, and precision. The evening follows Chow's signature format across eight shared courses: herb-led, grilled, fried, and braised, finishing with a composed dessert from each chef. Portions are substantial and designed for sharing. Then what begins as dinner becomes a proper Chow party, with matched cocktails flowing and a DJ building the energy.

Your ticket includes an eight-course sharing dinner and four half cocktails matched across the courses. Additional drinks and optional matched cocktail pairings are available for purchase.

These are home-learned recipes, passed down through families and remembered rather than written.

events.id: Wine World Cup-Noble Rot Wine Bar, 6 Swan Lane-WOAP
events.name: Wine World Cup
events.venue: Noble Rot Wine Bar, 6 Swan Lane
events.long_description:
This year, as the world turns its eyes to the football world cup, Noble Rot brings the competition to the table. For one night, the drama of football is reimagined in the glass, with each course framed as a head-to-head clash between two wine nations.
The evening begins with a welcome pour before a five-course degustation unfolds in match format. Each course is served with two wines presented side by side. It could be Argentina vs Chile or Italy vs France. You're invited to compare style, structure, character and flair across the world’s leading wine countries. The final course delivers one last decisive pairing to close the evening. The Noble Rot sommelier team will guide each “match,” sharing insight into regions, rivalries, and defining characteristics that give every wine its competitive edge.
Your ticket includes an amuse bouche on arrival, five courses, an arrival wine, and nine matched wines served head-to-head throughout the evening.

events.id: Raising Great Kids in an Overindulgent World-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Raising Great Kids in an Overindulgent World
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Raising Great Kids in an Overindulgent World
This workshop will be a confronting one that will expose you to the risks that children face in this modern overindulgent world. Many have become used to being the constant centre of attention and are unable to delay gratification and aren’t competent in everyday skills. They have few self-care skills and skills relating to others, can’t take personal responsibility and don’t know what is enough or what is “normal” for others – these children end up having a difficult time of it in life and you will too.
 You’ll take away new insight and knowledge plus strategies that you can apply that very night to help your child to be a responsible, likeable and respectful person.
By the end of the session, you’ll be able to:
• Reflect on the current context parents are faced with today
• Find the relevant research
• Practise the parenting tools and discuss the model
• Know where to go to get support
Aimed at parents, grandparents and caregivers of children of all ages.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Talk Truffle To Me-Intercontinental Hotel Wellington, 2 Grey Street-WOAP
events.name: Talk Truffle To Me
events.venue: Intercontinental Hotel Wellington, 2 Grey Street
events.long_description:
Executive Chef Carl Maunder has spent two decades with truffles: exploring, refining dishes that let them truly shine, building an entire culinary philosophy around one ingredient. 

Indulge in five courses built around Périgord and Bianchetto truffles at the height of their season. Hand-prepared scallops with truffle, beef fillet Rossini reimagined, house-made truffle brie. Every course carries the same special ingredient, yet every course reveals something different about this heady ingredient. Carl shares insights throughout the long lunch: why this preparation, why this wine, what makes a truffle matter enough to shape a chef's craft. You'll taste the earthy weight of it, catch the aroma before the plate lands, understand the precision behind each dish. Each course is paired with a beverage chosen to meet the truffle's character.

events.id: The Cheese Bar-Garage Project Wild Workshop Taproom & Cellar Door, 7 Marion Street (access via Furness Lane)-WOAP
events.name: The Cheese Bar
events.venue: Garage Project Wild Workshop Taproom & Cellar Door, 7 Marion Street (access via Furness Lane)
events.long_description:
After a barnstormer last year, Garage Project and Le Marché Français return with Cheese Bar, a carefully selected cheese and beer experience popping up for three nights at the Wild Workshop. This is à la bonne franquette: no bookings, no set menus, just exceptional French and locally sourced cheeses matched beautifully with beer. 

Build your own board from a counter display of soft, washed rind, bloomy rind, alpine-style, goat's milk, and blue cheeses. Comté in multiple ages, Brie de Meaux, Camembert-style options, chèvres, and French blues. Or order a guided tasting cheese and beer flight with Garage Project and Wild Workshop beers. Cheese-forward small plates, baked brie with seasonal accompaniments, raclette-style melted cheese over potatoes and cornichons, croque-monsieur-inspired toasties, cheese, and charcuterie boards, round out the offering.

events.id: The Flipper-LTD., Level 1, 60 Dixon Street-WOAP
events.name: The Flipper
events.venue: LTD., Level 1, 60 Dixon Street
events.long_description:
Bright lights, laminated menus, and paper-lined trays. Nate Auld, head chef at Mr Go's, rewinds the clock at LTD. with a 1990s New Zealand–style fish and chip shop, seen through a knowing lens. It's nostalgic and a little tongue-in-cheek: a highbrow, lowbrow take built for people who grew up on battered everything. 
Book a table or walk in for a one-hour sitting between 5–10pm and order from a succinct menu that treats the classics with care, without taking them too seriously. Mussel fritters that actually crunch, battered or crumbed fish done properly, hot dogs in their finest fried form, pineapple fritters dusted in cinnamon sugar, not shit chips, plus proper seasonings with lemon and housemade sauce. Drinks are there to smash back: Spritzes, crisp whites, and Waikato from the chilli bin. Dishes and drinks range from $6–$16.

events.id: Beast Feast-Volco - Egmont Street, 11 Egmont Street-WOAP
events.name: Beast Feast
events.venue: Volco - Egmont Street, 11 Egmont Street
events.long_description:
Offal has a reputation it doesn't deserve. At Volco, the kitchen eats it every day, rotated through the menu, balanced carefully, treated right. For this five course feast each course will feature secondary cuts from chicken, pig, and lamb. Expect fried chicken gizzard, beef fat salted caramel, and the kind of food that will have you questioning if these truly are "off cuts". 
This five-course tasting menu celebrates the cuts that are often talked about between chefs, the ones that butchers used to give away and are now being sought out by adventurous eaters. Alcoholic and non alcoholic drinks available for purchase on the night.

events.id: Midwinter Lunar New Year-Dragons Chinese Restaurant, 25 Tory Street-WOAP
events.name: Midwinter Lunar New Year
events.venue: Dragons Chinese Restaurant, 25 Tory Street
events.long_description:
Warm yourself in the heart of winter with a vibrant Lunar New Year feast. Let the Dragons team bring colour, flavour, and good fortune to a midwinter banquet designed to brighten the season. 

The table seats ten, your neighbours, your friends, strangers who are about to become familiar. Everyone stands to toss the Yu Sheng prosperity salad high into the air, and the chaos and laughter start. Then ten courses unfold: eight savoury dishes, each one imbued with symbolic meaning. Golden chicken for family unity. Whole fortune duck for fidelity. Crispy fish for abundance. Long-life noodles for longevity. It's food, luck, and togetherness served at once and you'll leave feeling blessed not only for the meal, but for this convivial and communal celebration. 

Your ticket includes the full ten-course shared banquet, a lion dance performance, and beverages throughout: welcoming tea, and three beers per guest.

events.id: Secret Sauce Lab-Pickle & Pie, 6 Lombard Street-WOAP
events.name: Secret Sauce Lab
events.venue: Pickle & Pie, 6 Lombard Street
events.long_description:
If your fridge door rattles every time you open it, stacked full of half-eaten jars, forgotten condiments, and experimental bottles, this class is for you. Learn from the Pickle & Pie pros how to fill those shelves with things you actually made yourself and will have you finishing them in no time. 

Move through mayos, spicy seasonings, pickles, honeys, understanding the technique behind fermentation and the balance that turns ingredients into something worth keeping. 

Your ticket includes one glass of wine, hearty canapés and two jars or bottles of condiments you create during the class, plus recipes to keep experimenting. Additional wine and beverages are available for purchase.

events.id: Beyond Breakfast-KAIOTA, 55 Molesworth Street-WOAP
events.name: Beyond Breakfast
events.venue: KAIOTA, 55 Molesworth Street
events.long_description:
Coffee doesn't just have to be your morning flat white. KAIOTA's team is taking the ritual you know, that daily catch-up, that business meeting moment, and threading it through six courses where coffee flavours weave its way through the whole menu (but don't worry the caffeine stays low). You'll move through appetiser, entree, main, palate cleanser, main and finally dessert, each one infused with the depth and subtlety of coffee in a different way. The point isn't the buzz, it's how coffee adds bitterness, depth, and warmth where you don't expect it.

Your ticket includes all six courses, each paired with a crafted alcoholic taster chosen to complement the coffee's role. Additional beverages are available to purchase.

events.id: International Coworking Day | Bubbles & Brunch at Q Space Lower Hutt-Q Space | Coworking Lower Hutt,134 Queens Drive, Lower Hutt, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: International Coworking Day | Bubbles & Brunch at Q Space Lower Hutt
events.venue: Q Space | Coworking Lower Hutt,134 Queens Drive, Lower Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
International Coworking Day | Bubbles & Brunch at Q Space Lower Hutt
Celebrate International Coworking Day at Q Space with bubbles, brunch, and a complimentary coworking experience.

About this Event

Join us as we celebrate International Coworking Day at Q Space Lower Hutt.
This special event is open to both members and the wider business community. Whether you already cowork or are curious about trying it for the first time, this is your opportunity to experience the energy, connection, and flexibility that coworking offers.
We’re marking the occasion with a relaxed midday celebration featuring brunch-style bites and bubbles — both alcoholic and alcohol-free options available.
Come for the celebration - Stay for the coworking.

Why Attend:
International Coworking Day recognises the power of shared workspaces to foster collaboration, productivity, and community.
If you’ve ever been curious about coworking, this is the perfect day to try it. If you’re already a member, this is our way of celebrating you.
This will be one of our larger community moments for the season — we encourage early registration.

TICKETS:
Q Space Members – Complimentary
Free entry for all current Q Space members. Please register to confirm your attendance.
General Admission – $49
For non-members. Ticket includes full event access, catering, and complimentary coworking at Q Space for the remainder of the day.

About Q Space
Q Space is Lower Hutt’s flexible coworking community — designed for focus, flexibility, and connection. We believe strong communities create stronger businesses, and this event reflects our commitment to supporting both.

We look forward to welcoming you!

events.long_description:
Elvis has left the building and he's now in Titahi Bay, running a wedding chapel just for you. This is a truly unique experience where you can either live out your dream of getting married in a Las Vegas wedding chapel either just for fun, or legally. Arrive in a convertible Cadillac with bubbles in hand, step into a sanctuary where the King himself is celebrant, MC, photographer, and musical send-off rolled into one gloriously ridiculous evening.

After the ceremony (legally binding or symbolic, your choice), newlyweds are seated for a refined take on Elvis's signature sandwich: peanut butter mousse, candied bacon, and banana on brioche. Tater tots on the side. A slice of red velvet wedding cake to cut and share. Elvis karaoke and Elvistravaganza festivities carry the night forward, because what else would you do after getting married in a chapel run by the King?

Wedding packages vary; you can have an Elvis commitment ceremony just for fun, a vow renewal for a bit more seriousness (with a bit of help beforehand) but also
an Aoteroa New Zealand Legal wedding/union is available - ticketholders will be contacted to make confirm all the details are just right - and will get extra pre-time with the celebrant to get all the proper legal details done.

Wedding tickets are per couple and include: transport in the Cadillac, glass of travelling bubbles, wedding ceremony and certificate, breakfast, wedding cake, photographs, wedding album, sandwich, sides, cake slice, and one cocktail or non-alcoholic alternative. Additional alcohol is available for purchase.
