events.id: Create Your Own Coffee Blend-Mojo Coffee Roastery, 37 Customhouse Quay-WOAP
events.name: Create Your Own Coffee Blend
events.venue: Mojo Coffee Roastery, 37 Customhouse Quay
events.long_description:
Join the Mojo team at their roastery on Wellington’s waterfront for an immersive, hands‑on coffee workshop. Their team of experts and coffee roasters will take you on a journey where you’ll taste and learn about coffee origins, and watch the transformation from green beans to flavoursome roasted coffee beans in a live demonstration. 
Then it’s your turn! At the end of this workshop, you’ll create your own coffee blend from your favourite varieties of the session, and you will be able to take home 200g of your personalised blend.
There will be platters served at each session.

events.id: Elvistraganza-The Beer Engine, 31 Whitehouse Road-WOAP
events.name: Elvistraganza
events.venue: The Beer Engine, 31 Whitehouse Road
events.long_description:
Elvis is in the building, and he's brought the whole neighbourhood with him. The Beer Engine is turning the Titahi Bay Mall into a laid-back Elvis festival, running alongside their drive-thru Vegas wedding chapel, where every ceremony is officiated by The King himself. This is a free, all-ages event with a scheduled programme of karaoke, lip sync, and lookalike competitions throughout the day. Expect plenty of pompadours, big ballads, and friendly rivalry, a hum of voices and laughter echoing across the mall. The Beer Engine team and local celebrities will judge the contests, and performers and spectators are equally welcome. 
Food will be available to purchase from The Beer Engine and other local Titahi Bay businesses including TBay Café, Kams Fish and Chips, the Mariner, and TBay Bakery, with drinks available separately. Come to compete, or simply pull up a seat and enjoy the show.

events.id: Unwrapped-Pātaka Art + Museum, 17 Parumoana Street, Porirua City Centre-WOAP
events.name: Unwrapped
events.venue: Pātaka Art + Museum, 17 Parumoana Street, Porirua City Centre
events.long_description:
Unwrapped: Whittaker’s since 1896 invites Chocolate Lovers behind the scenes to discover how a local maker became a global name. Set within the wider history of cacao production, the exhibition traces Whittaker’s story—from J.H. Whittaker’s founding vision and the iconic products that followed through to the company’s ongoing commitment to quality, beans-to-bar manufacturing and ‘Good Honest Chocolate’ values.

The exhibition unwraps the story of New Zealand’s Most Trusted Brand and features objects to delight all the senses. Visitors will encounter nostalgic memorabilia and classic advertising, a scent station that reveals chocolate’s layered aromas and artworks made in collaboration with contemporary creatives.

2026 marks 130 years of Whittaker’s. What began as J.H. Whittaker’s small family business in 1896 has grown into a world-class chocolate manufacturer, still proudly family-owned four generations on and based in its original Porirua factory.

events.id: Kumeu Single Vineyard Launch-Boomrock Lodge, 292 Boom Rock Road-WOAP
events.name: Kumeu Single Vineyard Launch
events.venue: Boomrock Lodge, 292 Boom Rock Road
events.long_description:
Be among the first in the country to experience the 2025 Single Vineyard Chardonnay releases from multi-award-winning Kumeu River, produced in strictly limited allocations each year and rarely tasted on release. 

Hosted by Michael Brajkovich Master of Wine against Boomrock's spectacular coastal backdrop, the afternoon begins with canapés and a guided introduction to Kumeu River's history and philosophy, followed by a comparative tasting of their acclaimed Chardonnays as each wine is presented by the winemaker. A long lunch then follows, featuring a matched three-course menu created by Head Chef Vic Hughes and paired with a selection of additional Kumeu River wines. Expect pan-fried snapper with warm tartare and oyster fritter, chicken roulade with spinach, mozzarella, and prosciutto, and a finale of infused éclairs with candied lemon mascarpone and stone fruit salsa. 

Your ticket includes return bus transport to Boomrock, canapés, wine tasters, a three-course lunch, and matched wines throughout. Maximise your visit to Boomkrock and add on one of their most popular activities, extreme golf for an additional $10 per person.

events.id: Alan Davies — Think Ahead Tour-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Alan Davies — Think Ahead Tour
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Critically acclaimed stand-up comedian, actor, writer, and TV personality Alan Davies is heading to Wellington in 2026. His new show, ‘Think Ahead’, marks his first visit to Aotearoa in a decade. Like the latest instalment of his memoirs, White Male Stand-Up, the performance covers his extensive career, and the highs and lows he’s experienced. Alan says the show is about a late middle-aged stand-up comic. He thinks he’s Marty McFly, but he’s older than Doc Brown. He spends more time in the pharmacy than the gym and is subjected to relentless eye rolls from his kids. 
Davies is a staple of British television. He’s known for his performance as the eponymous hero in the long-running TV series Jonathan Creek, and as a permanent panellist on QI. His talk show, Alan Davies: As Yet Untitled, recently reached its seventh season. He is also one of the UK’s best-loved comedians.

events.id: Nurse Georgie Carroll: Infectious-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Nurse Georgie Carroll: Infectious
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The delivery is razor sharp, and the comedy is not so much deadpan as bedpan. Nurse Georgie Carroll is back with a brand-new show. For Infectious, Georgie turns 20 years as a frontline surgical nurse into a stand-up that’s full of laughs, shocks, and heart. She is known as a natural storyteller whose style is authentic, fearless, and deeply human. It blends personal anecdotes, healthcare absurdities, and everyday family chaos into sharp, relatable observations. It’s big-hearted, no-nonsense, and riotous. 
Beyond the stage, Georgie Carroll is also an author whose writing captures the same warmth and humour that define her stand-up. Her stories celebrate resilience, laughter, and the humanity within healthcare, inspiring audiences across professions.

events.id: Madeira My Dear-Noble Rot Wine Bar, 6 Swan Lane-WOAP
events.name: Madeira My Dear
events.venue: Noble Rot Wine Bar, 6 Swan Lane
events.long_description:
Madeira has been too often overlooked. It sits in grandmother's cabinet waiting for cooking or forgotten entirely. But Madeira is built to last, it's a quietly complex wine that deserves discovery. Noble Rot will spend an afternoon proving it to you.
The afternoon begins with a welcome cocktail before settling into a three-course long lunch served sharing style at the table, in the traditional Mediterranean spirit. Generous plates are designed to encourage conversation and a slower pace. Across the meal, guests will taste six Madeiras, exploring the spectrum from dry and saline expressions through to richer, caramel-toned styles, with insight into grape varieties, ageing methods, and the oxidative character that gives Madeira its unique flavour profiles and remarkable longevity.
Your ticket covers a themed arrival cocktail, three generous sharing courses, and six Madeira wines paired throughout the afternoon.

events.id: Divine Spice Masterclass-Apostle HQ, 3C Magrath Avenue-WOAP
events.name: Divine Spice Masterclass
events.venue: Apostle HQ, 3C Magrath Avenue
events.long_description:
Step into the world of chillies, spices, and bold flavours with team Apostle and craft your very own hot sauce. 

The evening begins with a short introduction to how their products are made, then you'll sample the range from Apostle alongside selected sauces from across New Zealand and Australia. From there, it gets hands-on: you'll experiment with fruits, vegetables, chillies, and spices to develop your signature blend, with guidance from the team and optional recipes for those who prefer structure. It's collaborative and fun, groups are encouraged, and there's plenty of sharing ideas and flavours. 

Your ticket includes around six bottles of your own handmade sauce, a kitchen notebook, grazing platters, drinks from Duncan's Brewery, and other treats. One alcoholic drink is included; non-alcoholic options available throughout.

events.id: Rodney Scott: Pitmaster-Boneface Brewing Co., Unit 14, 27 Blenheim St-WOAP
events.name: Rodney Scott: Pitmaster
events.venue: Boneface Brewing Co., Unit 14, 27 Blenheim St
events.long_description:
Rodney Scott has been cooking whole-hog barbecue over coals since he was eleven years old. By seventeen, he was working the pits full time. What began as a kid helping out at his parents' roadside business eventually became one of the most celebrated careers in American barbecue. In 2018, Scott was named Best Chef: Southeast by the James Beard Foundation, the second pitmaster ever to win a James Beard chef award. He has appeared alongside Anthony Bourdain in Parts Unknown, and in 2020 was profiled on an episode of Chef's Table: BBQ, showcasing his South Carolina whole hog style. 

Boneface Brewing in Upper Hutt will be transformed for the occasion: American cars out front, whole hogs slow cooking over a fire pit, long trestle tables set up beer-hall style inside, live music, flash tattoos, and a specially brewed Boneface collaboration beer made for the day. Scott will be cooking his signature whole hog alongside a second protein, two hot sides, two cold sides, bread, and condiments. Staff will be dressed for it. So should you!

Your ticket includes food and entertainment. Beverages and flash tattoos are payable on the day.

This International Chef Collaboration is proudly supported by the New Zealand Government’s Events Boost Fund.

events.long_description:
Jewellery Making Intensive - Beginners' Workshop
Learn the essential skills and techniques needed to make your own creations. The morning will begin with simple design examples and techniques using specialist jewellers’ tools, working with copper or brass. In the afternoon you will form and craft your own ring from sterling silver.
 All materials included in course fee.  Please bring pen and paper and wear clothing and footwear suitable for a workshop environment.  Limited to nine participants. Please note you cannot be late for this one-day course, you must be there from the start of the course when health and safety matters are covered.

By the end of the course, you'll be able to:
• Create a silver band 
• Demonstrate techniques for working sterling silver 
• Perform metal forming, piercing and texturing skills 
• Use essential jewellery-making tools accurately

Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Overcoming Imposter Syndrome for Creatives-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Overcoming Imposter Syndrome for Creatives
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Overcoming Imposter Syndrome for Creatives
There is a widespread ‘lack of confidence’ epidemic among creatives, and if you have ever felt like your work is not good enough, hesitated to submit your art to competitions, or doubted your skills when applying for creative jobs, you are not alone! Creatives often feel imposter syndrome most acutely. Unlike other industries, those in the creative field produce something new every time, and sharing this with the world can be daunting, even for established artists.
 This pervasive self-doubt, known as imposter syndrome, affects countless artists and creators, often holding them back from realising their full potential. This concise workshop is designed to help you uncover the roots of this inner voice of insecurity and provide you with effective tools to overcome it. Through engaging activities and practical exercises, you will learn how to transform self-doubt into a source of strength, empowering you to confidently share your unique talents with the world.
 What you won't find in this masterclass: This is not a session about general self-growth or self-confidence, and it does not include positive affirmations or self-talk. All the information provided is based on scientific research and is applicable to all creative sectors.
Bring along some examples of your work on the day (eg a painting, a photograph of your work, etc).
 By the end of this workshop, you’ll be able to: 
Identify and challenge the origins of your imposter syndrome.
Apply practical techniques to silence your inner critic.
Share your creative work with increased confidence.
Turn self-doubt into a motivational tool.
Recognise and celebrate your achievements as a creative professional.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Pelvic Power Workshop-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Pelvic Power Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Pelvic Power Workshop
Apart from assisting with supporting internal organs, and controlling elimination, the pelvic floor is an important part of a woman's overall stability and posture, so you will gain improvement in this respect as well. You'll learn far more than conventional pelvic floor strengthening. 
You'll learn to differentiate different parts of the pelvic floor, and also how the pelvic floor functions in conjunction with breathing and spinal movements.
By the end of the workshop, you’ll be able to:
• Recognise the anatomy of the pelvic floor, where the muscles are and how they work
• Coordinate the pelvic floor muscles with breathing, and with the spine, and recognise its role in overall good posture
• Discover what habits you have which work against your pelvic floor muscles working well
• Practise exercises designed to help you rehabilitate and strengthen your pelvic floor
Please note that instructions are given verbally rather than by demonstration, so good hearing and understanding of English is required. For women only.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

 Power BI is a powerful and accessible tool for data analysis and dashboarding, widely used across many industries. This beginner friendly course is designed to be practical and hands on, covering subjects such as:

events.id: Retirement Reinvented-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Retirement Reinvented
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Retirement Reinvented
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
In your middle years, working, retired or somewhere in between? What’s next? Life is about much more than work or leisure, but most of us have given little thought to what “retirement” might mean.
It’s difficult to speculate what may happen in the future – but we can be proactive and take control.
This course offers insights into how to make the most of our time once we’re less focused on career and/or childrearing. It covers many of the issues people face when considering a move from current employment to a different lifestyle that includes more time to do the things we’ve always promised ourselves we’d do – “when we retire”.
After the workshop, you’ll be able to identify a number of common issues and potential ways to manage them when considering and planning your “rejuvenation”, rather than a retirement. We won’t cover financial planning, but will focus on some of the personal growth opportunities for living and making a conscious contribution to your community.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
By the end of the day, you’ll have:
Developed your own perspective on ageing and retirement in the 21st Century and identified what’s important to you
Completed a personal life audit exercise and considered your values and your strengths
Considered options to include a sense of purpose and explore choices that can provide you with that sense of purpose
Explored possibilities in the major aspects of your life and identified potential challenges and strategies for overcoming them
Begun to develop your blueprint to maximise the best years of your life and have considered a “future proofing” downsizing plan.
Bring pen and paper.
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Sculpture - Intro to Creature Sculpting-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Sculpture - Intro to Creature Sculpting
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Sculpture - Intro to Creature Sculpting
Join us for a fun day of creature making! Design and sculpt a one-of-a-kind character in your own style from polymer clays in a full spectrum of colours!

Working with oven-cured clays and wrapped wire armatures, this workshop offers a variety of modelling methods and approaches - a great opportunity to turn sketches & ideas into tangible 3D forms. Modern clay inventions mean we can create translucent, glittery, semi-flexible, UV reactive or even glow-in-the-dark elements to add more realism or a touch of the surreal into each creation!

Students will be coached through designing and creating a small-scale creature sculpture, beginning simple, and building to a more complex final form as we get familiar with materials.

The class offers beginner students a fun introduction to sculpting organic forms from scratch, and for those with some hands-on creative experience, it offers a great opportunity to experiment, hone and advance your making skills to new levels.

If you can, bring along some design ideas, sketches or reference images to help kick start the sculpting process on the day.

Please wear closed-in footwear suitable for a workshop environment. bring a work apron if you have one or wear clothes you don’t mind getting a little grubby. Remember to bring your reading glasses if you have them.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: The Digital Minefield: AI, Misinformation, and Detox-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: The Digital Minefield: AI, Misinformation, and Detox
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Together, workshop participants will demystify how artificial intelligence actually works, acknowledge some of the harms of this new technology, learn about the characteristics of mis/disinformation, build resilience against fake news, and explore why our brains weren’t built for the infinite scroll. You will walk away from the workshop with a better understanding of the hidden forces shaping your screen, equipped with real-world tools to cut through the digital noise.
  Dr. Andrew Chen is a leading New Zealand specialist at the intersection of digital technology ethics and public policy. With a PhD from the University of Auckland in Computer Systems Engineering, Andrew brings a deep technical understanding and applies it towards systemic issues and interventions. With extensive experience advising public agencies and private companies on governing emerging technologies, Dr. Chen translates complex technical concepts into clear, accessible insights for the community. He is passionate about helping people understand technology's societal impacts, and thinking more deeply about what’s happening behind the scenes, so we can all navigate today’s complex digital landscape.
  Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Nishi-dham Hatya Rahasya-St Teresa's Parish Karori,Karori Road, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Nishi-dham Hatya Rahasya
events.venue: St Teresa's Parish Karori,Karori Road, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Nishi-dham Hatya Rahasya
2026 Natokbaaj production coming up – mark the date in your calendar!

We’re back again this year with something new! For the first time, Wellington Natokbaaj is going to produce a murder mystery, Nishi-dham Hatya Rahasya (‘Murder at Nishi-dham’).

The plot is adapted from a novel written by the Queen of Crime, Agatha Christie. We have transported the story to the small town of Birpur in the pre-mobile, pre-Internet era of the 1980s. Our two quirky detectives – Tuni and Bulu – solve a murder occurring at the dilapidated mansion ‘Nishi-dham,’ owned by the enigmatic young woman Billie Mullick.

True to the Natokbaaj pledge, we have aimed to blend pure comedy into the narrative; hoping to bring to you an enjoyable experience of fun and excitement with a dash of fear. Come clue-hunting with us!

events.id: It's All Greek to Me-Boulcott Street Bistro, 99 Boulcott Street-WOAP
events.name: It's All Greek to Me
events.venue: Boulcott Street Bistro, 99 Boulcott Street
events.long_description:
We’re throwing a Greek-style long lunch and you’re invited to do absolutely nothing quickly.
Expect big shared plates, bold Mediterranean flavours, plenty of olive oil, lemon, herbs, and dishes made for passing around the table. Mezze will keep coming, mains will land when you’re ready, and dessert… well, you’ll make room.
The wine will be flowing, the music will be lively, and the only plan is to stay a little longer than you meant to.
Grab your friends, loosen the belt, and settle in. This is a long lunch, Greek-style.

Your ticket includes four courses and wine and beverages throughout.

events.id: MOKSHA • 5 Year Anniversary Gathering at Valhalla-154 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011-All Events In
events.name: MOKSHA • 5 Year Anniversary Gathering at Valhalla
events.venue: 154 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011
events.long_description:
MOKSHA • 5 Year Anniversary Gathering at Valhalla
❤️ TEAM MOKSHA PRESENTS ❤️

MOKSHA -5 Year Anniversary Gathering
08 AUGUST 2026 | VALHALLA, WELLINGTON

Five years.

Honestly, it's hard to believe.

What started as a small dream between a few friends has grown into something far bigger than we ever imagined.

Over the last five years, we've shared dancefloors, sunsets, sunrises, laughter, hugs, friendships, and countless unforgettable moments together. We've watched strangers become friends, friends become family, and a community grow around a shared love for music, connection, and freedom.

Our recent gathering, AARANYA , reminded us once again how special this community truly is. Seeing so many beautiful souls come together, dancing under the open sky, sharing smiles and creating memories, filled our hearts with gratitude.

To every artist who has played our stages.
To every volunteer who has helped behind the scenes.
To every person who bought a ticket, shared a post, invited a friend, or danced with us until sunrise...

THANK YOU !

Team Moksha wouldn't exist without you.

As we step into our fifth year, we wanted to celebrate this milestone together.

On Saturday, 08 August, we return to Valhalla Wellington for a special anniversary gathering.

No egos.
No expectations.

Just good music, good people, and the energy that happens when our community comes together as one.

Expect a carefully curated journey through sound, immersive visuals, and the unmistakable atmosphere that has always made Moksha feel like home.

Whether you've been with us since day one or you're about to join us for your very first event, this night is for you.

Let's celebrate five years of music, friendship, growth, and community.

And let's create a few more memories together.

We can't wait to see your faces on the dancefloor again.

With love,

Team Moksha ❤️

Five years later, the music still calls us together.
Welcome home. ❤️✨


📍 Valhalla, Wellington
📅 Saturday, 08 August 2026
🕗 8PM – Late


🎟 Tickets coming soon via PrizmaTix.
events.description:
MOKSHA -5 Year Anniversary Gathering
We've watched strangers become friends, friends become family, and a community grow around a shared love for music, connection, and freedom.
To every person who bought a ticket, shared a post, invited a friend, or danced with us until sunrise...
On Saturday, 08 August, we return to Valhalla Wellington for a special anniversary gathering.
Let's celebrate five years of music, friendship, growth, and community.

events.name: Wellington Salsa Ball 2026
events.venue: Harbourside Function Venue,6 Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Wellington Salsa Ball 2026
The Wellington Salsa Ball returns in 2026 to bring together live music and dancers from across the region for the city’s biggest Latin dance social of the year!

Social dancing and community is at the heart of this event. With live music, an open dance floor, and a warm, inviting atmosphere, the Ball serves as a mid-winter celebration to foster connection, joy, and a sense of occasion.

Whether you’re a beginner or seasoned dancer, this event is for you! Your ticket includes:

🎺 A 90 minute live Salsa set from Wellington’s own C-26 Salsa Band
💃 4+ hours of social dancing
🎧 DJ Panda playing Salsa all night, with touches of Bachata and Kizomba
✨ A beautiful venue and stunning wooden dance floor
📷 Professional event photography to capture the memories
🍹 Access to the in-house bar

Tickets are limited - and be sure to grab a cheaper ticket while they are still available!

https://events.humanitix.com/wellington-salsa-ball-2026

Dress code:

The Ball is absolutely an opportunity to dress up! However, we encourage you to wear whatever you are most comfortable social dancing in.

Event schedule:

8:00pm (doors open + social dancing begins)
9:30pm - 11:00pm (C-26 live Salsa set)
11:00pm (social dancing continues)
1:00am (party ends)

This event is made possible with support from Salsa Magic and the wider Wellington latin dance community.

Follow us on Instagram @wellysalsaball for the latest updates!

*Refunds: Please note that we are unable to provide refunds on tickets. If for some reason you are unable to attend you may resell or transfer your tickets, which will remain under the name provided for the booking.

events.id: Daily Dose of Pies-Doughnut Wagon, 242 Fergusson Drive-WOAP
events.name: Daily Dose of Pies
events.venue: Doughnut Wagon, 242 Fergusson Drive
events.long_description:
Find Dough Bakery's wagon, wherever it appears across Wellington, guided only by word of mouth.  Each day the wagon settles in a different location (maybe even your own suburb) and serves a single pie flavour inspired by locally sourced ingredients and a spirit of experimentation. Once you've tracked the cart down, enjoy your pie on the beanbags or take it away. Location details will be seeded out carefully to pillars in the community of each suburb and shared informally. Check social media for its location, and even campaign for the wagon to visit your 'hood.
One week only, a different pie every day using local Wellington suppliers.

events.id: Pravda's Scone Workshop-Pravda Café and Grill, 107 Customhouse Quay-WOAP
events.name: Pravda's Scone Workshop
events.venue: Pravda Café and Grill, 107 Customhouse Quay
events.long_description:
There’s a reason Pravda’s scones have a following. This hands-on class lifts the lid on what makes them so good, and gives you the chance to make your own.
You’ll begin at your table with a hot drink or juice and a short introduction to Pravda’s story before heading into the pastry kitchen. The pastry chef demonstrates each step, sharing practical tips and answering questions about baking — not just scones. Then it’s your turn to roll your own, choosing from plain cheese or adding extras like jalapeño, chilli flakes or bacon before they’re baked fresh.
Your ticket includes: two scones (one to eat and another to take home), two rounds of hot or cold drinks, and the top-secret Pravda scone recipe.

events.id: In the Know-Graze Wine Bar, 95 Upland Road-WOAP
events.name: In the Know
events.venue: Graze Wine Bar, 95 Upland Road
events.long_description:
Kyle Street, owner and chef of the two hatted Culprit in Auckland, joins the Graze team for two nights. The two kitchens have been recommending each other for years, based on a mutual appreciation. Now it's time to show what happens when they cook together. 

Expect eight courses fusing both their approaches, classics with special twists that only those in the know will get the reference. 

Your ticket includes eight courses and a welcome drink on arrival (non-alcoholic option available). Additional wine and beverages are available to purchase.

events.id: Talk of the Table-Glou Glou, 17a Allen Street-WOAP
events.name: Talk of the Table
events.venue: Glou Glou, 17a Allen Street
events.long_description:
Delivereasy is bringing together a range of Wellington On a Plate burgers in one place, so you and your mates can try multiple festival burgers without the usual logistical mission. Add free delivery, Double Vision festival beer matches and bar snacks, and you've got a very strong case for cancelling your other plans.

Then things get interesting. Some people trust online reviews, some people trust their mates. At Talk of the Table, you'll be trusting a stranger who sat in your seat 30 minutes earlier. The night turns word-of-mouth into part of the menu. Each diner passes down their burger recommendation for the next guest to judge, trust, or completely ignore.

Trust the recommendations. Challenge them. Become part of the table talk. Featuring burgers from Lucky, The Tasting Room, Burger Liquor and more!

events.id: Spooky Tour of the Parliamentary Library-The Beehive & Parliament Buildings, 1 Molesworth St, Thorndon, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Spooky Tour of the Parliamentary Library
events.venue: The Beehive & Parliament Buildings, 1 Molesworth St, Thorndon, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join us for some spooky fun, with our ghost tour of the Parliamentary Library.

Hear the dark secrets and spooky stories that lie within the walls of the oldest building on the Parliamentary precinct, plus, get a behind the scenes look at some of the spaces you don't usually see. Not for the faint hearted.

This event is full of scares and numbers are limited, so register now!

Please note, some areas of the route are narrow, and dark. There is also limited accessibility on this tour, and some parts are only accessible by stairs. Due to the content of some of the stories, this tour is for visitors aged 14 years+.

Please get in touch with private.tours@parliament.govt.nz if you have any questions.

events.id: Visa Wellington On a Plate--Wellington NZ
events.name: Visa Wellington On a Plate
events.long_description: Visa Wellington On a Plate (Visa WOAP) is the largest food festival in Aotearoa. It takes over the capital for the entire month of August, every winter. The festival sees hospitality venues across the wider Wellington region offer a feast of culinary delights. Expect everything from immersive experiences, to creative pop-up restaurants, and the hotly-contested Burger Wellington.

events.id: Bellbird Sings at The Dowse-Bellbird Eatery, The Dowse Art Museum, 45 Laing's Rd-WOAP
events.name: Bellbird Sings at The Dowse
events.venue: Bellbird Eatery, The Dowse Art Museum, 45 Laing's Rd
events.long_description:
A musical journey through art, stories, and seasonal food, with a touch of opera. 

Bellbird invites you beyond traditional dining into a sensory experience shaped by music, storytelling, and contemporary art. Inspired by the haunting song of the korimako (bellbird), you'll explore purakau (stories) through art and opera. Two-time Burger Wellington winner, Chef-owner Chetan Pangam crafts a menu celebrating Aotearoa's seasonal abundance: thoughtful integration of native ingredients, local produce, and refined Indian influences, all presented with an artistic lens. 

You'll arrive at Bellbird with a welcome drink and specially selected canapés, then move to the Dowse Art Museum for a private, behind-the-scenes guided tour with the gallery director, unlocking unique stories on display. A five-course sit-down dinner follows inspired by the art, matched with wines from Mishas Vineyard. Conversations flow from table to table, and each course reveals a new layer of meaning tied to land, memory, and identity. 

Your ticket includes a welcome drink, canapés, a five-course dinner, and five glasses of matched wine. Additional drinks available for purchase.

events.id: The Grower's Champagne-Noble Rot Wine Bar, 6 Swan Lane-WOAP
events.name: The Grower's Champagne
events.venue: Noble Rot Wine Bar, 6 Swan Lane
events.long_description:
Champagne takes on a different voice when it comes from the hands of the grower. In conjunction with Truffle Wine Imports, Noble Rot presents a premium degustation menu dedicated entirely to Grower Champagne. These are wines crafted by the families who farm their own vineyards and bottle under their own names, often in very limited quantities.
The evening unfolds over five courses, each paired exclusively with Grower Champagnes. Expect individuality, texture, and precision, with some bottles rarely seen beyond their cellar doors. These are wines shaped by site, season, and personal philosophy, offering a clear expression of place rather than a large-scale blended house style.
Throughout the evening, the Noble Rot sommelier team will guide each pour, exploring what sets grower producers apart and why their Champagnes have become some of the region’s most compelling, coveted and sought-after wines.
Your ticket includes an amuse bouche on arrival, five courses, and seven grower champagnes paired throughout the evening.

events.id: The Secret Garden-Homewood, 50 Homewood Avenue-WOAP
events.name: The Secret Garden
events.venue: Homewood, 50 Homewood Avenue
events.long_description:
Homewood will open its gates and invite you into the world of The Secret Garden, revealing spaces once hidden and locked away. At the heart of the night is a five‑course menu created by Chef Bet Cabrera, each course crafted to enchant and served like chapters in a story. Drinks will flow throughout the evening, inviting guests to indulge as the magic unfolds.

This evening will be nothing like the shadowed corridors of Misselthwaite Manor. Instead, Homewood’s warmth, elegance, and gardens will unfold in a flourish of gentle British charm. For this fleeting moment, the hidden corners will be opened and brought to life, thanks to the careful touch of our very own 'fairies'.

Join us for an evening where hidden doors open, tall tales become real, and Homewood transforms into its very own secret garden of elegance, flavour, and enchantment, available only for these special nights.

events.id: Atsuko Okatsuka — The Big Bowl Tour-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Atsuko Okatsuka — The Big Bowl Tour
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
She’s been described as having one-of-a-kind humour, boundless energy, and infectious charm. Comedian and actress Atsuko Okatsuka is heading to Wellington with her latest show, ‘The Big Tour’.   
With podcasts, television and film acting, and standup specials on streaming platforms, Atsuko has gained a huge following. Her previous tour, ‘Full Grown’, sold out over 200 shows across 20 countries. Her latest standup special, ‘Father’, premiered on Disney+ and has made it onto its Top 10 list. 
Atsuko’s viral moments are as legendary as her stand-up. She has ridden a Jeepney (a type of public utility vehicle) in the Philippines and started the #DropChallenge, which helped bring Beyoncé’s ‘Partition’ back to the charts. She’s become a true queen of the internet with clips from her live-filmed show, The Intruder, racking up over 100 million views. Fans even turn up to her shows wearing wigs styled as her signature bowl cut as a form of homage. Humour has no borders when you’re that funny.

events.id: Ocean Feast-Coene's Bar & Eatery, 103 Oriental Parade-WOAP
events.name: Ocean Feast
events.venue: Coene's Bar & Eatery, 103 Oriental Parade
events.long_description:
Some meals are best eaten with your hands. 

The seafood boil arrives tableside and suddenly the table comes alive, shells crack, plates get passed hand to hand, and the conversation doesn't stop until the last bite. This is what Coene's does best: shared seafood, dishes designed so you're talking across the table instead of inside your own head. Three courses of pure shared eating, starting with an elegant entrée, moving into the main event, that seafood boil, golden with stock and packed with everything fresh, and finishing sweet. 

Your ticket includes a three-course shared dinner, a glass of Prosecco on arrival, and the satisfying mess that comes with a proper seafood feast. Additional drinks are available to purchase.

events.id: Wing X-Volco - Egmont Street, 11 Egmont Street-WOAP
events.name: Wing X
events.venue: Volco - Egmont Street, 11 Egmont Street
events.long_description:
Ten wings. Ten different sauces. Can you handle the heat? This is hot ones for real, a raucous evening where diners work through escalating sauces, each wing a new test. The pace is quick (roughly 90 minutes), the energy high, and the challenge is genuine. You'll walk out with stories and probably a few tears, but that's the point. After the final wing, a supersized banana split ice cream sundae arrives to soothe the burn. 
Your ticket includes ten wings and the sundae. Boozy milkshakes available to purchase on the night to wash down the tears.

events.id: France vs New Zealand-Noble Rot Wine Bar, 6 Swan Lane-WOAP
events.name: France vs New Zealand
events.venue: Noble Rot Wine Bar, 6 Swan Lane
events.long_description:
France has long set the benchmark for fine wine. New Zealand, by contrast, is young, confident, and still defining its place on the world stage. For this five-course degustation, Noble Rot places the two side-by-side in a focussed tasting of classic pairs that asks a simple question: has New Zealand earned its seat at the table?

Each course is a head-to-head. A French classic meets its New Zealand counterpart. The food is classic, designed to let the wines show their differences. Expect thoughtful comparison across the varieties, regions and styles that shape both countries’ reputations. The emphasis is on quality producers and wines that speak clearly of place. Five courses means five comparisons, each one letting you taste whether New Zealand's found its own voice or is still chasing France's shadow.

Your ticket includes an amuse bouche on arrival, five courses, ten wines matched head-to-head throughout the evening.
events.description:
France has long set the benchmark for fine wine.
New Zealand, by contrast, is young, confident, and still defining its place on the world stage.
For this five-course degustation, Noble Rot places the two side-by-side in a focussed tasting of classic pairs that asks a simple question: has New Zealand earned its seat at the table?
Each course is a head-to-head.
A French classic meets its New Zealand counterpart.

events.id: Lucky Ladies of Lyall Bay-Southward Distilling, Unit 35 Lyall Bay Junction Complex, 53/70  Kingsford Smith Street-WOAP
events.name: Lucky Ladies of Lyall Bay
events.venue: Southward Distilling, Unit 35 Lyall Bay Junction Complex, 53/70  Kingsford Smith Street
events.long_description:
Word of mouth leads you to the coolest spots in Lyall Bay at this behind the scenes tour.
This relaxed, story-driven evening tours the Lyall Bay Junction, giving guests the chance to meet the owners, taste what they create, and hear the stories behind their work.
Start at Southward Distillery with a welcome drink from owner Frankie, paired with nibbles from Maranui Café. Then move through the Junction to visit Jane Blackmore in her artist studio, Tymone Winter at Empire of Genius, Jane Fahy at Real Aotearoa, and Kate Necklen at Wonderland Chocolates. Each stop offers a short 15–20 minute experience, with a tasting, a demo, or a behind-the-scenes moment.
The evening wraps up back at Southward with gins, more bites, and time to connect with the women who shape this creative corner of Lyall Bay.

events.id: Kapiti Business After 5 with Grant Elliott-Kapiti Boating Club,Marine Parade, Paraparaumu Beach, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Kapiti Business After 5 with Grant Elliott
events.venue: Kapiti Boating Club,Marine Parade, Paraparaumu Beach, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Kapiti Business After 5 with Grant Elliott
Join NZ cricket legend Grant Elliot for some inspiration, a bit of networking and a drink.

About this Event

This month:
GRANT ELLIOTT is a renowned former cricketer, having played over 100 games for the Blackcaps. Grant enjoyed success on the international stage particularly with white ball cricket.
Widely regarded by his teammates as a hard worker and a reliable player who always put the team first, Grant was written into New Zealand cricketing folklore books when he hit the winning 6 which propelled the Blackcaps into their first ever World Cup final!
An entertaining and insightful speaker, Grant shares his knowledge of team culture, motivation and exactly what it takes to create a high performing team.
Currently working as partnership manager for Life Flight, he has had over 18 years experience in the corporate world. Merging these two sectors is his passion as he believes sports culture plays a big part into creating a high performing corporate culture.

events.id: A Pearler of a Lunch-Logan Brown Restaurant, 192 Cuba Street-WOAP
events.name: A Pearler of a Lunch
events.venue: Logan Brown Restaurant, 192 Cuba Street
events.long_description:
Logan Brown is turning 30 this year, and owners Steve Logan and Shaun Clouston are marking the anniversary by inviting thirty lucky guests for eight courses of the most memorable dishes from three decades of exceptional cooking.
Since it opened Logan Brown has welcomed more over 500,000 guests and employed almost 900 staff. The menu will reach back through those eras. The food will tell a story of how Wellington's appetite has shifted over thirty years. Steve and Shaun will be on the floor throughout, introducing each course and sharing the moments, good, bad and ugly, that shaped the restaurant.
With guests seated at shared tables of six, the room will be intimate and easy, the kind of lunch that will no doubt stretch on into the afternoon. The long lunch we all long for. 
Your ticket includes a welcome glass of champagne, eight courses, and six wine matches.

events.id: Mo'Dogs-Kisa, 195 Cuba Street-WOAP
events.name: Mo'Dogs
events.venue: Kisa, 195 Cuba Street
events.long_description:
Come celebrate the king of street foods, the humble hot dog. Mosaic Venues, the team behind Wellington faves Mr Go's, Kisa, Ombra and Parla launches a roaming cart with flavours shaped by the restaurants and the way they like to eat: bold, international, and built for grabbing in hand. 
At any one time, you'll find four dogs on rotation, inspired by global cuisines: a Korean dog, a Turkish dog, an Italian dog, and an all-American classic. New Zealand made franks in Wellington sourdough hot dog buns, loaded with toppings, plus a sauce stand so you can finish it your way. The cart pops up in rotating locations around Wellington throughout the festival. Hot dogs are $9–$14.

events.id: Roots of Ceylon-t Lounge by Dilmah, 22-24 Willeston Street-WOAP
events.name: Roots of Ceylon
events.venue: t Lounge by Dilmah, 22-24 Willeston Street
events.long_description:
From the irrigation kingdoms of ancient Ceylon to the smoked-meat traditions of the Veddā people, Sri Lanka's indigenous cuisine carries centuries of survival, ritual and ingenuity. Most of it passed down by word of mouth and nearly lost to modernity.
Each course of this meal tells a different chapter: royal chicken porridge, coastal family curries, festive millet sweets, and more. The menu drawson local ingredients, ancestral techniques, and the ayurvedic principles that shaped how Sri Lankans have eaten for generations.
Your ticket includes five courses and tea-based beverages throughout the evening

events.id: Classical by Candlelight-The Runholder, 89 Martins Road-WOAP
events.name: Classical by Candlelight
events.venue: The Runholder, 89 Martins Road
events.long_description:
Candlelight is the only way to experience centuries-old music properly, and The Night Watch, Wellington's premier early music ensemble, know this. So for one night only, in collaboration with National Candles, in a concert steeped in candlelight, this is your chance to go back in time. Keep your phones pocketed and let the modern world recede for a few hours.

Bringing together Australasia's top specialists in early music, The Night Watch perform on period instruments: harpsichord, viola da gamba, and others from a tradition that predates the modern orchestra entirely. The evening features countertenor Austin Haynes and soprano Carleen Ebbs, two of New Zealand's top vocalists. Heard live in a candlelit room, you'll experience this music beyond anything a recording could do. The candles themselves are courtesy of National Candles, one of New Zealand's oldest candle makers, handcrafting their wares for over half a century.
At The Runholder in Martinborough, a three-course family-style menu built for sharing and unhurried conversation. The food is warm and generous: rich winter flavours.

Your ticket includes a welcome drink on arrival and three courses. Additional drinks are available to purchase on the night.

events.id: Yoga Nidra 30hr Training-Yoga Corner,21-23 Andrews Ave, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Yoga Nidra 30hr Training
events.venue: Yoga Corner,21-23 Andrews Ave, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Yoga Nidra 30hr Training
Kia ora,

I’m thrilled to share that Inspire’s Yoga Nidra Training is coming to Wellington this August @yogacornerlowerhutt

This is your invitation to dive deep into the profoundly restorative art and science of Yoga Nidra, the timeless practice of conscious rest that calms the nervous system and guides others into deeper states of relaxation and awareness.

Join Sandey Hoskin to explore the scientific principles that underpin Yoga Nidra and learn how to integrate this practice into your teaching and personal wellbeing routine.

Training: 30-hour Yoga Nidra Teacher Training
Dates: 14–16 August 2026
Location: Hot Yoga (The Yoga Corner), Lower Hutt Studio
Format: ~25+ hours in-person over 3 days

Early-bird cost until June 30th: $799

This training will also be in Manchester, UK (Oct 30–Nov 1 at @shala.sage. Limited spaces—Yoga Alliance Certified and eligible for 300-hour requirements or CE credits.


Aroha nui, Sandey ♥️

Acme Cupco are pleased/excited/over the moon to announce that we're doing it again!

If you're keen to give the Aotearoa Aeropress championships ago then you're in the right place.

events.long_description:
Atomic! - Aotearoa's Ultimate Femme Rock Supergroup - Wellington
After sold-out shows across the country, Atomic is back - louder, bolder and ready to light up stages across Aotearoa this August.

Led by powerhouse vocalist and Musical Director Julia Deans, Atomic brings together a who’s-who of Aotearoa’s most celebrated musicians for a high-voltage celebration of women in pop and rock.

This all-star lineup features Boh Runga, Dianne Swann, Vera Ellen, and Jazmine Mary, backed by an all-femme band: Karen Hu (drums), Rebel Reid (guitar), Louisa Nicklin (bass), and Ilayda Tunali (keys).

Together, they take on an iconic catalogue spanning generations - from Blondie, Eurythmics and Pretenders to Kate Bush, Florence + The Machine, Madonna and more - reimagined with the kind of energy only this lineup can deliver.

events.id: Tulsa Meets Martinborough-Union Square, 10 Memorial Square-WOAP
events.name: Tulsa Meets Martinborough
events.venue: Union Square, 10 Memorial Square
events.long_description:
From cattle country to oil fields, from dive bars to dinner tables. Tulsa's food tells the story of America's heartland. General Manager Adam Cruz continues his "meets Martinborough" series by bringing the bold, smoky soul of Southern cooking to the Napoleon dining room's intimate fireplace. 
Five courses trace a culinary exploration: start with a Reuben egg roll (sauerkraut, corned beef, Russian dressing), move through fried pork ribs that legend is made of, followed by biscuits swimming in sausage gravy, and then slow-smoked BBQ pulled pork served with cornbread, fried okra, and black-eyed peas — sides that honour the agricultural plains. Strawberry pecan pie closes out the night. Each course pairs with a Martinborough beverage: hand-crafted brews from Martinborough Brewery, award-winning Pinot Noir and Chardonnay from Palliser Estate, a botanical gin from Margrain, and a Noble wine to finish with pie. 
Your ticket includes five courses and four standard drinks (low and non-alcoholic options available).

events.id: Microsoft Excel - Beginners-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Microsoft Excel - Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Microsoft Excel - Beginners
Learn how to create and use spreadsheets, as well as identify elements of a work book, enter and edit data, generate basic calculations and use built-in spreadsheet functions.
Windows skills essential - this is not a course for absolute beginners to computing.
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
Note: All computer courses are run in the school computer labs and you are required to use the school desktops for the duration of the course (you are not able to use your own laptop).  Some of the courses may require you to login onto the network. 
By the end of the course, you'll have had a basic introduction to using MS Excel, including how to:
• Identify elements of a workbook
• Page setup and printing
• Enter and edit data and basic formatting
• Generate basic calculations
• Use cell references and built-in functions 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Our grandmothers would never be caught without a selection of home-baked biscuits, a slice or two and at least one type of cake or loaf. Today many of those skills are no longer being passed on.
In this workshop you will get back to basics with some of the Kiwi baking classics which, made well, are delicious and widely loved.

A light and moist Banana Cake,
A pretty, delicate Louise Cake and
melt in the mouth Afghans.
All ingredients included in course fee - bring some tins to fill!

Memory Mastery for Confidence
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Learn to retain names and lists, learn vocabulary, deliver presentations without notes, and boost your professional efficiency. Discover how to create vivid mental imagery, use spatial memory techniques, and leverage the power of association to revolutionise your recall abilities. Whether you're a busy professional, a student, or simply looking to sharpen your mental acuity, this course offers practical, immediately applicable skills for enhanced memory performance in both personal and professional settings.
Bring pen and paper if you wish to take notes.
Tea and coffee provided, please bring your lunch and snacks.

events.id: Sound Healing Experience at Lotus Yoga Centre-Lotus Yoga Centre,91 Ruapehu Street, Paraparaumu, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Sound Healing Experience at Lotus Yoga Centre
events.venue: Lotus Yoga Centre,91 Ruapehu Street, Paraparaumu, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Sound Healing Experience at Lotus Yoga Centre
We invite you to a special evening of Sacred Harmonic Sound with multi-instrumentalist Claudio Escutia. This immersive sound journey offers a deeply restorative experience that invites you to settle into stillness, reconnect with the heart, and explore a sense of inner spaciousness through vibration

About the event
Claudio skilfully layers the sounds of gongs, crystal and Tibetan singing bowls, Native American flutes, and other traditional instruments, enhanced through high-quality audio equipment so the sound can be both heard and felt throughout your body.Harmonic sound and vibration offer the nervous system a chance to truly rest. In this state of relaxation, the thinking mind softens, and we find connection to citta — the heart-mind. From this place, silence arises naturally through the vehicle of sound.This experience is open to everyone. No previous yoga or meditation practice is needed.


What to bring:Bring your own yoga mat or camping mattress, small pillows, eye pillows and blanket to assure comfort.

Booking is essential — spaces are limited and this event often fills quickly.

About ClaudioClaudio is a multi-instrumentalist musician originally from Mexico City and now based in Wellington. Born into a family of musicians, he began playing music at a young age and later studied classical guitar. His journey led him to Montreal, where he explored jazz, world music, and spiritual sound traditions.

Extensive travels in Asia and Europe deepened his connection with Indian music and yogic philosophy. He studied Tibetan singing bowls in Kathmandu and Varanasi, and has since worked with a wide range of instruments including the sitar, esraj, bansuri, harmonium, flutes, gongs, and voice.

Claudio’s work blends technical skill, deep presence, and a profound respect for the healing power of sound.

Contribution:

Early bird: First 10 bookings $40
General admission : $45

Learn more buy tickets and listen to Claudio’s music here:

Website and Event link:
https://www.claudioescutia.com/events/sound-healing-experience-at-lotus-yoga-centre-2

SoundCloud https://soundcloud.com/claudioescutiasounds

Instagram
https://www.instagram.com/claudio_yoga_sound_healing

Facebook
https://www.facebook.com/soundhealingyogamusicclaudio

events.venue: Lane Park Church Cafe,22 Lane Street, Upper Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Taste of Myanmar
Fundraising dinner. Live storytelling, photos by refugees and a wonderful taste experience. Proceeds to Myanmar refugee education.

About this Event


Taste of Myanmar
The rich flavours and culture of Myanmar at our lively in-person event... Food and fun and storytelling by refugees who've resettled in New Zealand. You'll be moved by the photos from refugee camps and your ticket price goes to the 25 Bridges charity, (formerly Chin Humanitarian Aid International), who run online English classes for kids in Myanmar and in refugee camps in North East India.

We also fund education scholarships for ambitious, community minded teenagers.

We also fund education scholarships for ambitious, community minded teenagers.

events.id: Plini | ‘An Unnameable Desire’ Australia & New Zealand Tour-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master

events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
Plini returns with a breathtaking new chapter, headlining in support of his latest album "An Unnameable Desire."

Known for his genre-defying blend of technical precision and emotional depth, Plini’s live show is an immersive journey through intricate soundscapes and soaring melodies.

Joining him are UK math-rock favourites Delta Sleep, celebrated for their intricate rhythms, shimmering guitar work, and emotionally resonant songwriting. With a reputation for explosive live performances and a devoted global following, they bring both intensity and nuance to the stage.

Australia’s hot newcomers, nightdive round out the lineup, adding their atmospheric edge to an already compelling bill.

Experience an unforgettable night of inspired, powerful performances, and moments that linger long after the final note.

San Fran holds a Supervised License, meaning minors are welcome to attend events at San Fran so long as they are accompanied by a legal guardian at all times whilst at the venue. Minors are of course not allowed to purchase alcohol under any circumstance.

Join pastry chef Maxine Scheckter of Sugar Flour for an art class like no other. While being tutored by Inverlochy’s talented artists in a medium of your choice, you’ll enjoy a range of sweet treats that have been inspired by the artists and their food memories. events.name: Prophetiae Sibyllarum [Paraparaumu]
events.venue: 1 Langdale Avenue, Paraparaumu 5032,1 Langdale Ave, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Prophetiae Sibyllarum [Paraparaumu]
The Tudor Consort celebrates its 40th birthday in 2026 and we have an incredible season planned to celebrate. Collaborations with five different ensembles, a debut in Auckland, three premiere performances, and of course a performance of Thomas Tallis’ 40 part masterpiece Spem in alium. Make sure you visit our website to get all the details and secure your tickets!



We will be taking part in our third Aotearoa Festival of the Arts with SoundCathedral on Sunday March 1, an incredible re-composition of Orlande de Lassus’ Prophetiae Sibyllarum by Michael Norris, written especially for our anniversary and the 25th birthday of Stroma contemporary music ensemble. We celebrate going ‘over the hill’ in style in our May 2 concert XL, featuring two 40 part works by Tallis and Mäntyjärvi. We have two concerts with our friends Orchestra Wellington - Leonard Bernstein’s energetic Chichester Psalms as part of W:Ellington on 30 May, and Victoria Kelly’s brand new opera Ocean on 28 November. A debut in Auckland with NZ Barok sees us performing Lotti and Handel, and we finish the year with Messiah with Australian virtuoso harpsichordist and director Erin Helyard.




This is one of the most exciting seasons in our history, and I hope very much you can join us as we celebrate!
events.description:
Prophetiae Sibyllarum [Paraparaumu] The Tudor Consort celebrates its 40th birthday in 2026 and we have an incredible season planned to celebrate.
Collaborations with five different ensembles, a debut in Auckland, three premiere performances, and of course a performance of Thomas Tallis’ 40 part masterpiece Spem in alium.
Make sure you visit our website to get all the details and secure your tickets!
We will be taking part in our third Aotearoa Festival of the Arts with SoundCathedral on Sunday March 1, an incredible re-composition of Orlande de Lassus’ Prophetiae Sibyllarum by Michael Norris, written especially for our anniversary and the 25th birthday of Stroma contemporary music ensemble.
We celebrate going ‘over the hill’ in style in our May 2 concert XL, featuring two 40 part works by Tallis and Mäntyjärvi.

events.id: The Dire Straits Experience-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: The Dire Straits Experience
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
From sold-out arena performances across Europe, the celebrated Dire Straits Experience now heads to Wellington. Personnel for the Long Highway World Tour has been assembled by Dire Straits veteran Chris White, who toured with the original band in the 90s. Prodigious singer-guitarist Terence Reis is also on board, adding further clout to the musical genius on stage.
Delving into Mark Knopfler’s platinum catalogue, this world-class ensemble reignites Dire Straits’ greatest hits with skill and showmanship. Hear classics like Money for Nothing, Walk of Life, So Far Away, Brothers in Arms, Sultans of Swing, Tunnel of Love, Telegraph Road, and plenty more. A seven-piece ensemble will take you on a two-hour odyssey with full concert production values.
Formed in London in 1977, Dire Straits was active until 1988 and then again from 1990 to 1995. Across six studio albums and five live albums, they have sold more than 120 million units worldwide.

events.id: WHO’S THAT GIRL?: KATE CAMP AND NOELLE MCCARTHY-Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa,Red Gates te Papa, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: WHO’S THAT GIRL?: KATE CAMP AND NOELLE MCCARTHY
events.venue: Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa,Red Gates te Papa, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
WHO’S THAT GIRL?: KATE CAMP AND NOELLE MCCARTHY
For one year in 1986, Kate Camp kept a daily diary. In her new book Leather & Chains, she writes back to that 13-year-old self. Encountering the pages from the distance of 40 years, Kate finds iconic ‘80s touchstones, swaggering bravado, vulnerability, and the linguistic fireworks of the proto-writer.

Noelle McCarthy’s new book, Stakes, brings her Irish Catholic girlhood obsession with Dracula into collision with the world of the 2020s, 'MeToo', and the horrors emerging from Irish soil.

These two memoirists join Kate Duignan for a conversation about hungry girls, and the ones who get invited through the door.

Writers on Mondays is presented by the International Institute of Modern Letters and The Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa. The 2026 series will run from 12.15—1.15 pm each Monday from 6 July to 28 September at Te Papa and Circa Theatre, with two special evening sessions at Meow. Admission is free and all are welcome. The series is supported by the Letteri family.

The full programme can be viewed here: https://www.wgtn.ac.nz/modernletters/about/events/writers-mondays

Come celebrate Aotearoa writers with us at Writers on Mondays – we look forward to seeing you there.

These two memoirists join Kate Duignan for a conversation about hungry girls, and the ones who get invited through the door.
Writers on Mondays is presented by the International Institute of Modern Letters and The Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa.

events.id: Pub Choir — Just Think About It-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Pub Choir — Just Think About It
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
You’ll definitely feel better if you come and have a sing with a few thousand strangers. That’s what New Zealand-born vocalist, conductor, and composer Astrid Jorgensen thinks. Astrid is the brainchild behind the touring show Pub Choir — Just Think About It. The premise is simple — come to the Michael Fowler Centre on 19 August and learn one popular song in three-part harmony. What began as a small gathering in a pub has now grown to fill concert halls around the world. No prior training is required — just bring your voice and a sense of humour. 
Astrid established Pub Choir in Brisbane in 2017. Her work associated with the show has gone on to win her several awards. This includes one of the 40 Under 40 Most Influential Asian-Australians by the Asian-Australian Leadership Summit in 2021 and a Medal of the Order of Australia (OAM) in 2023.

Lucas & Arthur Jussen - CMNZ Series, Wellington
Lucas and Arthur Jussen are among the most sought-after piano duos of our time, having performed in recitals across the globe and with leading orchestras including the Boston, Chicago, and Philadelphia Orchestras, The Royal Concertgebouw Orchestra, Royal Stockholm Philharmonic, NDR Elbphilharmonie Orchestra, and Konzerthausorchester Berlin.

The brothers will play a programme of exquisite beauty and invention, featuring four cornerstones of the piano duet repertoire.

“The two Dutchmen are formidable storytellers who captivate listeners with their spellbinding craft for light-and-shadow effects” -- Der Tagesspiegel

PROGRAMME
- Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart, Sonata for 2 pianos D-Dur KV 448 (375a)
- Franz Schubert, Fantasie f-Moll for piano Quatre Mains D. 940 Op. 103
- Maurice Ravel, La Valse, Poeme choregraphique for 2 pianos
- Igor Stravinsky, Le Sacre du Printemps - Version for 2 pianos

events.id: Emerson's & Cardrona Evening-The Tasting Room, 2 Courtenay Place-WOAP
events.name: Emerson's & Cardrona Evening
events.venue: The Tasting Room, 2 Courtenay Place
events.long_description:
Five beers, five spirits, five dishes. Each pairing presented by someone who has spent decades perfecting their craft: Richard Emerson from Emerson's Brewery. 
This warm and intimate evening unfolds with bold, flavour-driven combinations: distinctive beers matched with carefully selected spirits and liqueurs, each accompanied by a dish designed to bring out the best in both. 
Richard, the Cardrona team, and The Tasting Room Head Chef will guide you through every match, sharing insights into their craft, the inspiration behind each creation, and the art of pairing flavours. Expand your knowledge, discover new favourites, and experience an evening designed to delight the senses.
Your ticket includes five 200ml beers from Emerson's Brewery, five 15ml spirits or liqueurs from Cardrona Distillery, and five food pairings.

events.id: Entre Calles-Cuba Street Tavern, 172 Cuba Street-WOAP
events.name: Entre Calles
events.venue: Cuba Street Tavern, 172 Cuba Street
events.long_description:
Cuba Street Tavern owner Rich spent eight trips wandering Barcelona's backstreets, finding treasured and memorable venues, not because they were advertised but because someone leaned in and said, "trust me, you need to try this place". Entre Calles recreates that feeling through shared tapas, Spanish-inspired drinks and music. 

Tapas arrive in waves across shared tables while DJs spin Spanish tracks. Nobody's ordering from a menu, plates just come, each one designed for passing and picking at, the kind of eating where the conversation flows as easily as the Spanish-inspired drinks. Expect pinchos that hit hard, dishes that layer and surprise, and the feeling of being somewhere because someone recommended it rather than because you planned. It's tight, vibey, and a little chaotic in the best possible way. 

Your ticket includes eight courses of shared tapas and three paired Spanish-inspired drinks.

events.id: Dinner With Dionysus-Noble Rot Wine Bar, 6 Swan Lane-WOAP
events.name: Dinner With Dionysus
events.venue: Noble Rot Wine Bar, 6 Swan Lane
events.long_description:
For this five-course degustation, Head Chef Nicolas Mentzas draws on his Greek heritage to present the flavours and textures of home through a refined, fine-dining lens. Often perceived as relaxed and informal, there is a quiet depth to Greek cuisine. It is shaped by season, coastline, mountain herbs, and generations gathered around the table.
Dinner unfolds as a carefully composed menu that honours tradition while embracing precision in one of the world’s oldest wine cultures. Rather than a shared feast, expect elevated dishes rooted in Greek culinary history, reinterpreted with elegance and detail. Each course is paired with Greek wines, highlighting distinctive indigenous grape varieties and the growing confidence of the country’s modern producers.
Throughout the evening, the Noble Rot sommelier team will introduce each pairing, exploring how Greece’s food and wine traditions complement one another in perfect harmony, at the highest level.
Your ticket includes an amuse bouche on arrival, five courses, an arrival wine, and seven Greek wines paired throughout the evening.

events.id: Cyanotype Printing - Beginners-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cyanotype Printing - Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cyanotype printing is a fun way to create artistic images. Discover the magic of cyanotype printing. Explore how you can create blue and white prints using natural materials, light and simple techniques. You will be guided by experienced artist, CJ and you’ll select composition, layering and exposure, while making your own unique art works to take home.
All materials provided.
 You will:
- Use botanicals to make stunning prints
- Understand how to use light and create the exposure and print
- Explore composition and layering
- Take home your new art!
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Pub Choir - Wellington, Aotearoa - Michael Fowler Centre (Just Think About It Tour)-Michael Fowler Centre-All Events In
events.name: Pub Choir - Wellington, Aotearoa - Michael Fowler Centre (Just Think About It Tour)
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre
events.long_description:
Pub Choir - Wellington, Aotearoa - Michael Fowler Centre (Just Think About It Tour)
There are many facts in the known universe. For instance, did you know a giraffe is 30 times more likely to be struck by lightning? I guess because they are so tall?

Another fact is that Pub Choir is the world’s biggest, best, funniest, hottest music lesson. This fact has been verified by independent* sources (*we paid a guy on fiverr to say it).

An actually true fact is that Pub Choir is an opt-in experience. You don’t HAVE to buy a ticket and you are allowed to rot at home like you always do. But you PROBABLY should come and have a sing with a few thousand strangers. You would DEFINITELY feel better if you did. And hasn’t your sister-in-law been asking for years to go with you? It couldn’t be that bad could it?!? Everybody keeps bloody going on about it, maybe it’s worth a look. Anyway, just think about it.

What I'm saying is that Pub Choir will be touring across the world this year and Astrid is brewing some truly great arrangements, which I can claim with some authority, because I am her. We'll start in July in Australia, then head to NZ, Europe, the UK, and Asia. More info to come! In the meantime, the tour is called ‘Just Think About It’. And if you could, that'd be great.

You’ll learn one song in 3 part harmony at the show. That’s it, that’s the whole promise! Just think about it! Please come and have a sing! No worries if not!

This show is 18+ Please be aware that this event will be filmed & photographed. By purchasing a ticket and attending this event, you are giving your consent to be filmed & photographed during this event.

Tickets on sale here 2nd April 10am Local: https://www.ticketmaster.co.nz/event/24006471EC0A482C

Doors 6pm
Show 7pm

events.id: Agave Odyssey-Superfina, 232 Cuba Street-WOAP
events.name: Agave Odyssey
events.venue: Superfina, 232 Cuba Street
events.long_description:
Mexico doesn't fit in a single sentence, and neither does mezcal. This guided tasting dinner travels through the regions and agave varietals that define the spirit. Each course is inspired by the landscape, ingredients, and stories of a different area.

Mezcal maestro Oscar will guide you through the evening with genuine knowledge born of passion. As dishes unfold, you'll taste how terroir shapes flavour in Mezcal just as it does in wine: the smoke and earth of one region bleeding into the spice and brightness of another. Every course tells you something new about what's possible in a bottle.

Your ticket includes five courses, mezcal tasters throughout the evening, and matched cocktails.

events.id: Feast of Five Fishes-Noble Rot Wine Bar, 6 Swan Lane-WOAP
events.name: Feast of Five Fishes
events.venue: Noble Rot Wine Bar, 6 Swan Lane
events.long_description:
The ocean sets the tone for this five-course degustation, where fish and seafood take centre stage from first course to final bite. Drawing loosely on the spirit of the Italian Feast of Seven Fishes, this is a communal appreciation of the ocean's jewels, exploring the breadth and finesse of premium ocean produce with precision and care.

Each course reflects a thoughtful approach to seafood, drawing on seasonal catch and sustainably sourced ocean produce from around New Zealand. Expect considered technique and full use of the ingredient, treated with respect and imagination to reveal both delicacy and depth. The sommelier team has selected nine wines from around the world, fine Burgundy, savoury European whites, the occasional red, chosen to complement each expression of the sea.

Your ticket includes an amuse-bouche on arrival, five courses, nine wines throughout the evening including an arrival wine.

events.id: Gossip Club-The Library, Level 1, 53 Courtenay Place-WOAP
events.name: Gossip Club
events.venue: The Library, Level 1, 53 Courtenay Place
events.long_description:
Welcome to The Gossip Club, a candlelit, phone-free evening where stories are written, tested, twisted and passed on. 
You'll arrive to a cocktail and a sealed envelope of Gossip, Rumour and Confession cards. Throughout the night, you'll use these cards to tell stories, embellish the truth, and share previously untold tales. Running all evening is the Confessional Chapel, a velvet-draped corner for stories that feel more like sins. They are never revealed. Four themed cocktails and decadent dessert canapés are served throughout. 
This is a phone-free experience. No photos. No recordings. No evidence. What happens at Gossip Club stays at Gossip Club.

events.id: Beervana-Hnry Stadium, 105 Waterloo Quay-WOAP
events.name: Beervana
events.venue: Hnry Stadium, 105 Waterloo Quay
events.long_description:
Beervana is the Hoppiest, Tartest, Fruitiest place on Earth. The most delightfully weird beer festival takes place in Wellington every August. This is no ordinary tasting event, it’s a full-blown sensory adventure where the brewers get wild, the food gets fancy, and the unexpected is around the corner and always on tap.​

Expect more than 60 breweries pouring over 350 unique beers  - from hazy IPAs and fruited sours to pastry stouts to beer cocktails and non-alcoholic surprises. Add in live music, mind-blowing brewery stands (vote for your fav!), entertainment around every bend, and you’ve got a festival where you'll come away with a greater knowledge of great beer, belly full and memories to share for years to come!

events.id: Amuse Your Bouche-Amuse Snack Bar, 178 Willis Street-WOAP
events.name: Amuse Your Bouche
events.venue: Amuse Snack Bar, 178 Willis Street
events.long_description:
In many parts of Europe, the late afternoon snack is a daily ritual. In Wellington, it is finally getting its moment. For one Friday night, Amuse Snack Bar leans fully into the tradition, turning its pintxos offering into a lively, snack party celebrating kiwi tapas.

Expect a rotating spread of 10 to 15 pintxos built on Amuse’s sourdough baguette, topped with combinations like bacon and egg tortilla, pork belly with persimmon relish, seared tuna with kawakawa aioli, and classic gildas. Snacks are served quickly from the yellow window, ready to eat, with sticks tallied at the end.

Drinks stay close to the theme, with New Zealand vermouths, Spanish reds, and house cocktails designed for grazing. Drop in, eat well, and stay as long as you like.

events.id: The Rumour Lounge-Whisky & Wood, 60 Cambridge Terrace-WOAP
events.name: The Rumour Lounge
events.venue: Whisky & Wood, 60 Cambridge Terrace
events.long_description:
At The Rumour Lounge, you'll arrive to the sound of jazz and step into an intimate speakeasy-style dinner party with performances by one of Wellington's premiere drag performers. The night unfolds in three phases: roaming canapés as guests settle into the space and mingle; a move to luxuriously styled long tables where generous shared dishes arrive, rich, warm, enhance flavours designed for conversation and passing plates; and finally, dessert canapés circulating through a lounge-style setting as the space softens into something even more relaxed. 
Between courses, your host returns with moments of performance, theatre, and playful storytelling, keeping the energy alive and guaranteeing your dinner companions will no longer be strangers by the end of the night. 
Your ticket includes a welcome signature drink (choice of edible cocktail, mini martini, or non-alcoholic option), canapés, shared mains, sides, and roaming dessert canapés. Additional cocktails available to purchase.
