events.venue: Manawa Ora,236 Middleton Rd, Churton Park, Wellington 6037, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Manawa Ora Workshop - The Foundations of Māori Healing and Bodywork
Manawa Ora - The Foundations of Māori Bodywork and Healing.

Now - more than ever - there is a focus on self-care and a revitalisation of our traditional healing practices.

This workshop will be held at The Manawa Ora whare hauora (clinic) in Glenside, Wellington.

Date: Saturday 22-23rd August
Please note spaces for this workshop are STRICTLY LIMITED.
This is a 2-day workshop only - not a noho-marae (sleepover).
Hours are 9am-4pm, Saturday and Sunday.
Please note this workshop will NOT qualify you as a practitioner.

You will be introduced to Mirimiri and Romiromi; basic protocols and the philosophies that underpin practice, cultural concepts of health and wellbeing and the importance of selfcare.

If you are travelling from out of town please ensure you have made your own accommodation arrangements. Morning teas and lunch provided

The cost of this workshop is $650. Please note spaces are strictly limited. You will need to pay in full when registering.

TO REGISTER: Please email:
You will need to include information about yourself, where you are from, where you currently live and work and what you do for a living. Please outline the reasons why you want to attend, and what you hope to get out of attending.

FYI if you work in community, education, healthcare or social services you may be eligible to have your registration fees paid for under professional development or cultural training. Please enquire with your employer.

Jolie Davis (Ngāti Kurī /Te Rarawa) is founder of Manawa Ora - one of the leaders in the Rongoā Māori space, with a team of practitioners, full time clinic in Glenside, Wellington and weekly clinics in the Hutt and Masterton. Jolie is a member on the ACC Rongoā Māori Advisory Panel.

For more information about Manawa Ora please go to the website: www.manawaora.org

events.name: Adobe InDesign - Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Adobe InDesign
This introduction to Adobe's InDesign program will cover: setting preferences; understanding the frame-based approach through exploration of frame creation, properties and use; placing and editing text (with styles); placing graphics; defining and applying colour and print considerations.
By the end of the workshop, you'll have been introduced to InDesign. You'll be shown the program and the main features of its desktop, and then be taken through how to create and work with the main publishing items (text and graphics) in order to create your own publication. 
Note: All computer courses are run in the school computer labs and you are required to use the school desktops for the duration of the course (you are not able to use your own laptop).  Some of the courses may require you to login onto the network. 
By the end of the course you'll be able to:
• Describe the features of InDesign and how to work with them
• Create text frames, connect text frames and format text
• Create shapes and frames and format them
• Import pictures into publications
• Use rulers, guides and the pasteboard to lay out pages
• Finish off, check and then print 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Getting the Most from ChatGPT: Practical AI for Everyday Use-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Getting the Most from ChatGPT: Practical AI for Everyday Use
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Getting the Most from ChatGPT
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $29 with ID, all others pays $120).
AI tools like ChatGPT are everywhere, but how do you get the best out of them? In this hands-on, phone-based workshop, Jon Bell will guide you through practical techniques to make ChatGPT a more useful, reliable partner in your work and life. Whether you’ve tried it a few times or use it regularly, you’ll learn how to write better prompts, refine responses, and avoid common pitfalls without needing any technical background.
We’ll cover real examples, answer your questions as they come up, and spend plenty of time experimenting together so you leave feeling confident and ready to put your new skills to use.
Please bring: A phone with the ChatGPT app installed, and enough battery to last the day.
By the end of the workshop, you’ll be able to:
Write effective prompts that get clearer, more useful responses
Use ChatGPT for research, brainstorming, and creative work
Recognise the tool’s limitations (and how to work around them)
Apply practical strategies for using AI in your personal or professional projects
Confidently troubleshoot when ChatGPT gives unexpected or unhelpful answers
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Women's Health and Aging Workshop-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Women's Health and Aging Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Women's Health and Aging Workshop
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
An interactive workshop presented by experienced physiotherapist Sara Dore, focusing on how movement and function can be challenging for women as they age. Particularly for women, there is more to the core than abdominal muscles and strength and we explore these broader aspects.  Most importantly, you will come to understand that movement is medicine and we can make a difference.
You will be able to make a positive difference for yourself! The objective of this workshop is to give you knowledge and understanding about how you can manage the effects on your health and wellbeing as we age as women.  Knowledge is power!
In this one-day workshop you will discover:
- The base of the pelvic floor. Anatomy of the pelvic floor, and the common conditions urinary incontinence, and pelvic organ prolapse will be discussed. Education on basic pelvic floor advice and management as well as appropriate exercises will also be covered.
- The middle deep abdominal muscles. Trunk stability versus core strength as we age and the impact on our other core muscles and pelvic floor will be discussed. The principles of Clinical Pilates versus traditional Pilates will be presented together with the key principles of movement is medicine.
-The importance of the diaphragm and breathing well will also be discussed to enhance optimal movement and function for women.
Please wear comfortable clothes and socks as this is an interactive workshop, and bring a yoga mat or beach towel.
Tea and coffee provided, please bring your lunch and snacks.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Zentangle® Method - Beginners-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Zentangle® Method - Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Zentangle® Method - Beginners
The Zentangle® method is an easy-to-learn, relaxing and fun method to create amazing images by drawing structured patterns.
In this course you will learn to make beautiful art, following the 8 steps of the Zentangle® method from a certified Zentangle teacher.
Discover your creative side, even if you think you don’t have one. You might surprise yourself!  If you can hold a pen, you can do this.
Creating Zentangle patterns is super relaxing and a creative form of mindfulness. Increase your awareness and focus, get absorbed and feel more confident while making your own unique and original art.
Learn about borders, strings, and shading. Learn your first tangles and discover the world of Crescent Moon, Hollibaugh, Printemps, and Florz. It feels great to have creativity flowing. Anything is possible one stroke at a time. There are no mistakes to make here!
After a step-by-step introduction you will take home your amazing images and have discovered a new way to relax.
All art materials are included in course fee.  Tea and coffee is available but please bring your lunch/snacks.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
-    follow the 8 steps of the Zentangle® method.
-    make beautiful structured black and white drawings.
-    explain why creating Zentangle patterns is a relaxing form of creative mindfulness.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: A Day in the Heart - One Day Silent Yoga and Meditation Retreat-Lotus Yoga Centre,91 Ruapehu Street, Paraparaumu, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: A Day in the Heart - One Day Silent Yoga and Meditation Retreat
events.venue: Lotus Yoga Centre,91 Ruapehu Street, Paraparaumu, New Zealand
events.long_description:
A Day in the Heart - One Day Silent Yoga and Meditation Retreat
An invitation to press "pause" for a day, and just Be.

To drop from the busy mind into the silence of the Heart.

And to remember the freedom of who you are, when you're not in a role.


This lovingly crafted offering is a day immersion into practice, providing a glimpse of what the experience of silence can offer.

Suitable for complete beginners in meditation and yoga, as well as for advanced practitioners who'd like to unplug and enjoy retreat-time.


The schedule will include:

- Multiple seated meditation sessions with guidance

- Walking meditation

- Mindful Hatha Yoga and Restorative Yoga sessions

- Yoga Nidra

- Cacao to open the Heart

- Time to journal, walk in nature, or just be


Cost for a whole day program + 2 meals: $195


Food:

breakfast and lunch - nutritious vegan meals prepared with love + tea and cacao


Location:

the Lotus Yoga Centre in Paraparaumu


Date: 22nd August 2026 Saturday

Duration: 7.30am - 5.30pm

please arrive at 7.15am to settle in and start on time


To book, please purchase your ticket below or email Radha for more info at


*WHY IS THIS A SILENT RETREAT? A silent retreat means that everyone participating observes mauna, noble silence (apart from the teacher/ the guide). This means no casual chats and conversations, but also no use of phone or any devices during the day. The practice of silence allows the the mind to quieten down significantly, it is a very unique, deeply purifying and enlightening experience. It creates space for a deep insight and a proper reset, even in the short span of one day.


*WHY MEDITATION? Meditation is known for thousands of years as one of the most powerful practices, within and outside of spiritual context. It is good not only for creating a state of calm or relaxation, but also is the best known path to self-knowledge and self-love. When we retreat into silence beyond mind, there is an unmistakable feeling of being back home, in the heart of being, a sense of connection and purpose.


*WHY "A DAY IN THE HEART"? The Heart/ Hridaya in Sanskrit is a synonym of awareness or presence. According to the great yogis and the non-dual philosophy, the Heart is a place to centre in our meditations, but also an organ of knowledge and the seat of our true Self. When the mind is quiet and we centre within, the Heart can awaken - bringing a sense of being back home, back to what truly matters, back to our Self.

As Rumi said, "Close your eyes, fall in love, stay there"

events.id: Dark Sky Trek Wainuiomata-Wainuiomata Recreation Area,10 Whitcher Grove, Lower Hutt, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Dark Sky Trek Wainuiomata
events.venue: Wainuiomata Recreation Area,10 Whitcher Grove, Lower Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Dark Sky Trek Wainuiomata
We’ve teamed up with the Wellington Astronomical Society to bring you this exclusive FREE behind-the-scenes event.

Walk deep into untouched native forest, through some of the oldest trees in the Wellington region as darkness falls. You may even hear the calls of kiwi and rūrū (morepork). Explore an area not normally open to the public, including a glowworm-filled tunnel that links to the Orongorongo Valley.

The Wellington Astronomical Society will share insights on Dark Sky awareness and preservation and provide a telescope for viewing the Moon and the Southern Cross. Talk to Remutaka Conservation Trust volunteers about their Kiwi release programme and nest monitoring efforts.

Wainuiomata Regional Park is working towards dark sky reserve accreditation, one of only three in the Southern Hemisphere. Light pollution from the city washes out most stars, but the park offers one of the best stargazing experiences in the region.

What to bring:
• Torches/headlamps (red lenses preferred)
• Sturdy waterproof footwear
• Warm and wet weather clothing

Note: This event includes a steep hill walk in the dark. A moderate level of fitness is required, and it is not suitable for children under 15.

This event is strictly limited to 30 people. Registrations for this event will be on a first in first served basis and opens at 10am on Tuesday 14 July via this link:
events.description:
Walk deep into untouched native forest, through some of the oldest trees in the Wellington region as darkness falls.
You may even hear the calls of kiwi and rūrū (morepork).
Explore an area not normally open to the public, including a glowworm-filled tunnel that links to the Orongorongo Valley.
The Wellington Astronomical Society will share insights on Dark Sky awareness and preservation and provide a telescope for viewing the Moon and the Southern Cross.
Talk to Remutaka Conservation Trust volunteers about their Kiwi release programme and nest monitoring efforts.

events.id: Mediterranea-Cicio Cacio Osteria, 167 Riddiford Street (entrance from Wilson Street)-WOAP
events.name: Mediterranea
events.venue: Cicio Cacio Osteria, 167 Riddiford Street (entrance from Wilson Street)
events.long_description:
At Cicio Cacio Osteria, the Mediterranean comes to the table in an intimate dinner shaped by memory and place. Inspired by the owner's childhood on a Mediterranean island, this dinner traces a path from Italy to Spain, across Greece and into the Middle East, with dishes from each region reflecting the shared spirit of the Mediterranean: the sun, the sea, the music, the nostalgia, the being in the moment. The food is uncomplicated and entirely enjoyable, simple, fare focused on freshness and simplicity, letting the ingredients speak for themselves. Expect the most perfect hummus with grilled pita, the best Spanish tortilla, uncommon seafood like octopus and sardines, couscous, lamb, and goat, pastas, and colourful salads, a true insight into the Mediterranean spirit. 

You'll be seated at a long communal table in front of a real fire for a progressive nine-course menu, each plate paired with a matched wine selected to reflect its origin and character. Throughout the evening, the chef shares stories behind the dishes, offering insight into flavours, traditions, and personal history. You're invited to come into the kitchen and share the secrets of each recipe, cooking alongside the chef. A final musical touch ends an evening dedicated to the beauty, spontaneity, and simplicity of the Mediterranean. 

Your ticket includes nine courses, nine matched wines, storytelling, hands-on cooking, and recipe sharing.

events.id: Hidden Roll-Dragonfly, 70 Courtenay Place-WOAP
events.name: Hidden Roll
events.venue: Dragonfly, 70 Courtenay Place
events.long_description:
The spring roll is represented across Asia in many forms - fresh, fried, delicate and indulgent.
For this special blind tasting event, Dragonfly remove the visual cues and allow flavour, aroma and texture to lead the experience. You'll sample 5 thoughtfully crafted spring rolls, each representing a different style, region or contemporary Dragonfly interpretation. Can you guess the hidden ingredients in each wrapper? Whether you're a food connoisseur or just curious about new tastes, this immersive experience promises to be full of surprises and delicious discoveries. The person who guesses the most correct ingredients will receive an $80 Dragonfly voucher and bragging rights as the ultimate flavour detective.

An exploration of contrast; crunch and softness, spice and sweetness, tradition and innovation.

events.id: George Motz: Burger Scholar-The Opera House, 113/111 Manners Street-WOAP
events.name: George Motz: Burger Scholar
events.venue: The Opera House, 113/111 Manners Street
events.long_description:
Anyone that fancies themselves a smash burger expert knows who George Motz is. He has dedicated his life to burgers - he has written about them, made films about them, and cooked them for the masses. This has rightfully earned him the Burger Scholar title. Now he is bringing that knowledge to this burger loving city, at the bottom of the world, to share with all of you.

On stage, Motz will present his world famous "History of the Burger" starting from his research into the origins of the sandwich from 13th-century Mongolia to its rise as an American staple and now a favourite all over the world. You’ll then get a full demonstration of the burger that gave him a global following - the Oklahoma Onion burger. The demonstration will be projected overhead live to capture all the detail and he's holding no secrets back. The night will conclude with a Q&A

Your ticket includes the presentation, live cooking demonstration and Q&A. No food will be served. A cash bar and merch will be available to purchase on the night.

This International Chef Collaboration is proudly supported by the New Zealand Government’s Events Boost Fund.

events.id: Cooking: Japanese Home-Style-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: Japanese Home-Style
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Japanese Home-Style
We will prepare simple, traditional Japanese home-style meals consisting of rice, soup, and a main dish (Ichijyu-issai). In each class, we'll make a different-flavoured miso soup along with a popular Japanese dish -- for example, teriyaki chicken or tonkatsu (crumbed pork cutlet).
  We'll introduce you to Japanese food that's easy to prepare, tasty, and made with simple fresh ingredients plus we'll show you how easy it is to incorporate tasty and healthy Japanese food into your daily diet.
All ingredients are included in the fee. You just need to bring an apron, a dish cloth, a tea towel, and a container to take your food home in. Don't forget your smartphone if you want to take photos!
By the end of the course, you’ll:
become more familiar with using Japanese ingredients
feel more confident to cook authentic Japanese-style dishes at home 
have the know-how to make delicious, well-balanced, and nourishing meals for the whole family.
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus. 
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: A Night on the Orient Express-The Botanist, 219 Onepu Road-WOAP
events.name: A Night on the Orient Express
events.venue: The Botanist, 219 Onepu Road
events.long_description:
All aboard this culinary exploration travels across Europe via the legendary train route, stopping at five countries and sampling what each kitchen does best. 

Expect refined plant-based interpretations of classic dishes from France, Germany, Austria, Hungary, Serbia, Bulgaria, and Turkey: German maultaschen, Viennese schnitzel, Hungarian langos, Serbian sarma, Bulgarian kashkaval, Turkish lokma. The atmosphere shifts with each course as live music carries the evening forward. 

Your ticket includes five courses with optional drink matches available to purchase.

events.id: Wines of Poland-Noble Rot Wine Bar, 6 Swan Lane-WOAP
events.name: Wines of Poland
events.venue: Noble Rot Wine Bar, 6 Swan Lane
events.long_description:
Poland's wine scene barely appears on most people's radar, which is exactly the point.  For this special evening, Noble Rot sommelier Maciej Zimny turns the spotlight toward his homeland’s emerging wine scene and the wider momentum of Eastern Europe.

Four courses of refined Polish culinary tradition will be served, each dish interpreted with precision to complement the wines and let them shine. Maciej shares his personal story throughout, the vineyard sites he knows, the producers finding their voice, the momentum building across a region that's changing in real time. This will be a wine education that feels more like conversation rather than lecture. Guided by Maciej’s passion for Pinot Noir and Chardonnay, alongside varieties long enjoyed across Eastern Europe, guests will be the first in New Zealand to taste bottles imported specifically for this event. Expect cool-climate purity, bright acidity, and distinctive expressions from a region finding its voice with growing confidence. 

Your ticket includes an amuse-bouche on arrival, four courses, six matched Polish wines, and Maciej's personal guidance.

events.id: Love Bites-Beach Babylon, 232 Oriental Parade-WOAP
events.name: Love Bites
events.venue: Beach Babylon, 232 Oriental Parade
events.long_description:
Modern dating is a minefield, so Beach Babylon and local comedians Mike Fowler and Anya Rzhevitskaya have turned it into a laugh-filled dining event not to be missed. 

Love Bites is a three-course comedy dinner centred on the chaos of modern relationships: red flags you clocked and ignored, situationships you still can't explain, and texts that should've stayed in drafts. Across the night, Mike, and Anya will take very different angles on love and dating as the courses land, from early sparks and suspicious charm through to full-blown hindsight. The menu mirrors the arc perfectly: entrées spark connection (think love bombing fondue and I'm hooked prawns), mains explore the situationship (steak with main character energy or fish soft launches), and dessert brings the happily ever after. It's loose, social, and just self-aware enough. 

Your ticket includes three courses and a welcome cocktail. Additional drinks available for purchase.

Delicious traditional Indian dishes (not based on ghee and cream!) are the focus in this four-session course where Manjula will share with you the recipes, flavours and aromas she grew up with.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:

You'll be able to make several pieces of mosaic to your own design and after the completion of the course, will feel confident to carry on working on your own projects.

events.id: Field to Feast-Mangaroa Farms, 298 Whitemans Valley Road-WOAP
events.name: Field to Feast
events.venue: Mangaroa Farms, 298 Whitemans Valley Road
events.long_description:
Set on a working regenerative farm just outside Wellington, this long-table dinner brings you as close to the land that produced your meal as possible. The vegetables and pasture-raised meat will come directly from Mangaroa Farms and other local suppliers, shared in a cosy winter-dinner setting designed to spark conversation, curiosity, and connection around food. This is an interactive dinner experience that blends food, conversation, poetry, live music and inner-enquiry into one cohesive experience.
You're welcomed with whakawhanaungatanga, a warm welcome drink and canapés to acquaint your taste buds to the land. You'll gather in the yurt for a multi-course regenerative meal, with stories from the farmers and producers who grew the food. Woven through the evening will be live music from Casual Healing, poetry and reflective questions to open the table. Designed to foster a single, focused conversation, this Jeffersonian style dinner invites conversation across the table. You'll talk with strangers and leave with friends. 

Your ticket includes a snack and drink on arrival, a multi-course farm-sourced dinner complete with fresh baked bread, local sides and dessert with non-alcoholic beverages throughout the evening.

Prepare to enjoy a front-row seat to world class drag performances from the likes of beloved performers like Slay West and Poneke's petulant pinup, Altra Violette. Paired with a seasonal and generous multi-course menu and gorgeous service from the team at Floriditas, this is an evening of elegance and escapism you won’t forget.

events.id: Social Media Marketing Secrets for Small Enterprises: Visual Impact-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Social Media Marketing Secrets for Small Enterprises: Visual Impact
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
This course helps small business owners and entrepreneurs unlock the full potential of social media. Each week we’ll cover practical steps to review your current channels, set up strong profiles, and create a tailored content plan that works for your business. You’ll learn how to choose the right platforms, use hashtags effectively, create engaging posts, select visuals that connect with your audience, and understand the role of AI tools. By the end, you’ll walk away with a clear and actionable plan to grow your social media channels and attract the right customers.
Whether you’re starting out or already running a business, a strong social media presence is no longer optional. The challenge is knowing where to focus your efforts when every platform seems to demand something different.
This course is designed to cut through the noise and give you a roadmap that fits your business. Together, we’ll:
Review the main platforms and choose the right ones for your goals
Tailor your profiles so they look professional and trustworthy
Build a realistic content plan you can actually stick to
Learn how to use hashtags and trends to boost reach
Explore how AI can help (and where it can’t)
Understand what visuals work best and how to avoid common pitfalls
Discuss tips for creating engagement and growing a loyal following
The focus is practical and hands-on. Each week you’ll apply the ideas directly to your own business, so by the end of the course you’ll have a solid social media strategy ready to put into action.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Fight Club-The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Fight Club
events.venue: The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Four comedians face off in one of the most brutal and radical fighting styles: Structured Debate. Welcome to Fight Club!

A gladiator-style comedy showdown where hot takes replace punches and the audience is judge, jury, and executioner.
Four comics battle across three rounds of ruthless, ridiculous debate. Two rounds in the first half, a desperate losers’ bout for the coveted booby prize, then a final face-off between the victors. The ultimate winner earns a spot in the Grand Final in November and a shot at the Fight Club belt.

They’ll argue life’s most important questions, like “Which comic would be the best date to your ex’s wedding?” and “Who could talk their way out of a speeding ticket?” No facts, no data, no mercy. Expect sharp jokes, fake confidence, real egos, audience voting, and maximum tomfoolery.

This isn’t just comedy debate, it’s trial by charisma.

Expect egg sandwiches on pillowy Japanese milk bread, rice bubble slices stacked with unexpected flavours, and a shelf of nostalgic goods done properly. Walk in, browse the shelves, hand-pick what you want. No booking, just lit food with a familiar feeling. Last year's pop up was popular so get in early, once it's gone, it's gone.

events.id: Dita Von Teese-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Dita Von Teese
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
With her signature mystery and old-Hollywood glamour, Dita Von Teese is a global icon in the world of burlesque. She’s bringing an exciting new show to The Opera House in August. ‘Nocturnelle’ is a sensual spectacle inspired by the golden age of magic. It blends sophisticated striptease with the art of illusion for an unforgettable evening. Dita is accompanied by an ensemble of hand-picked neo-burlesque artists, so expect to be dazzled. 
Dita Von Teese is considered to have set the benchmark for American burlesque. The exotic and provocative art form has advanced under her unrivalled artistry and flair. The performer, creative director, and choreographer has sold out some of the world’s grandest venues, from Shanghai to New York. She is also a designer, collaborator, and entrepreneur. She’s forayed into fashion, beauty, music, and publishing.

events.long_description:
Dilly Dally's Bryn and Jesse wanted to share their favourite Auckland restaurant with Wellington, and Pici's Jono, Gemma and Kaz were happy to make the trip south.

Pici has built a devoted following on handmade pasta and trattoria energy: simple, generous food with no pretension, and good music. So a collaboration between the two restaurants makes sense, they share the same philosophy and vibe, and the menu will embrace that shared spirit: snacks, antipasti, two pasta courses, and dessert. 

Your ticket includes four courses. A selection of New Zealand natural wines is available to purchase on the night.

events.long_description:
Sugar Flour and Glou Glou have teamed up with Laser Kiwi, the world's only surreal sketch circus, for a five-course dessert tasting menu with a twist.

Inspired by Laser Kiwi's famous Sketch Game, the evening is built around a team quiz woven directly into the food, and have a created a menu that both tastes great and has whimsical prompts, sensory riddles, and cryptic challenges buried in the flavours, textures, and presentation of the desserts and drinks. Your ticket includes five dessert courses, matched beverages throughout, and the quiz experience.

events.id: LIVE MUSIC - Donal Gunning , Irish Nutrition-Get Fixed Bicycle Cafe and Panhead Bar,16 Parumoana St, Porirua City Centre, Porirua 5022, New Zealand, Porirua-All Events In
events.name: LIVE MUSIC - Donal Gunning , Irish Nutrition
events.venue: Get Fixed Bicycle Cafe and Panhead Bar,16 Parumoana St, Porirua City Centre, Porirua 5022, New Zealand, Porirua
events.long_description:
LIVE MUSIC - Donal Gunning , Irish Nutrition
Dónal Gunning is a singer songwriter from Dublin, Ireland. He draws on rock, folk, indie and punk influences.

Now based in Wellington, New Zealand his debut solo single Homesick released in October 2023. Homesick is a song dedicated to the masses who have emigrated their motherland for opportunity and adventure. It tells the story of the struggle in leaving friends and family.

His debut EP Born to Perform released in May 2024. The EP was inspired and written while travelling New Zealand, Portugal and Ireland.

Donal released his sophomore EP Zero Bucks Given in August 2025 and is currently touring New Zealand. He knows how to work the crowd and always brings fun, energy and Irish charm through his thought-provoking lyrics and formative sound, he is taking a break from his Spring Zero Bucks Given Tour to play a solo set this Friday.

Dita Von Teese: Nocturnelle 2026 [WELLINGTON]
The ever-reigning Queen of Burlesque, Dita Von Teese, returns to Australia and New Zealand with her most opulent show yet, the all-new Nocturnelle.

With Nocturnelle, Dita unveils a bewitching new vision - an exquisite blend of old-Hollywood allure, theatrical magic, and the high-art “Stripscapes” Dita is known for. Inspired by the magicians of the 19th century, this production conjures a world of wonder where sensuality meets magic.

Following her sold-out GLAMONATRIX tour in 2019, Dita is back to dazzle once more. Joined by a hand-picked cast of celebrated neo-burlesque artists, this is your chance to witness an icon in action. Prepare to be mesmerised by the glamour, the mystery, and the magic of Dita Von Teese.

Don't miss out - secure your tickets for an evening filled with glamour and true showmanship.

events.id: Escarpment Launch & Long Lunch-Boomrock Lodge, 292 Boom Rock Road-WOAP
events.name: Escarpment Launch & Long Lunch
events.venue: Boomrock Lodge, 292 Boom Rock Road
events.long_description:
Be one of the first in the country to taste the 2023 vintage single vineyard wines from Martinborough's Pinot Noir superstars, Escarpment. From the gravels and windswept terraces of Martinborough, Escarpment has built a devoted following through word of mouth, shared by locals, championed by sommeliers, and remembered long after the last glass. 

Presented by winemaker Tim Bourne against the wild outlook of Boomrock's coastal position, you'll sample their wines paired with locally sourced wild ingredients including wild boar, Cook Strait snapper, tītī (mutton bird), venison, and Wairarapa cheese. 

The afternoon opens with Rosé on arrival and canapés of wild boar arancini and ceviche, then moves to a three-course long lunch. Expect leek, tītī, and Mānuka honey pasta with kawa kawa butter; tart cherry venison loin with purple kūmara gratin and Kikorangi Blue; and a Wairarapa cheese course with dark chocolate and blackberry jelly. Each course is matched with Escarpment's range, celebrating the magic of Wairarapa. Your ticket also includes return bus transport.

events.id: Curry Journey-Dragonfly, 70 Courtenay Place-WOAP
events.name: Curry Journey
events.venue: Dragonfly, 70 Courtenay Place
events.long_description:
Warm spices, layered flavours and regional traditions come together in a five-course exploration of curry across Asia. Each dish draws inspiration from a different place or technique, alongside  contemporary interpretations from the Dragonfly kitchen.

Courses are served one at a time, allowing guests to experience the range of styles, ingredients and spice profiles that define curries across the region.

The ticket includes a five-course curry menu, with accompanying sides and condiments where appropriate. Drinks are not included, but guests may purchase a specially created drinks match or other beverages on the day. Each guest also receives a curry-inspired treat to take home, created by Dragonfly’s friends at Asian Food Republic.

events.id: Orchestra Wellington — Kiri and Lou In Concert-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Orchestra Wellington — Kiri and Lou In Concert
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Award-winning animated children’s series, Kiri and Lou, follows the adventures of Kiri, a feisty little dinosaur, and her best friend Lou. The New Zealand made show explores the world of feelings through laughter, song and play. Now, the songs from the show come to life in delicious orchestral colour thanks to the players of Orchestra Wellington.
Presented by Liv Tennet, the voice of Kiri, this concert brings the music of one of Aotearoa’s favourite TV shows to life. Beautiful visuals from some of the episodes will be projected during the concert. This is a family-friendly event and is recommended for children aged 3 and above.
This handcrafted stop-motion animation show is now in its third season. Kiri and Lou shows in many countries around the world including Sweden, Czech Republic, Canada, Mexico, United States, and Latvia.

events.id: Makers and Shakers-Cuckoo Emporium, 57 Customhouse Quay-WOAP
events.name: Makers and Shakers
events.venue: Cuckoo Emporium, 57 Customhouse Quay
events.long_description:
Get ready for a full sensory experience as makers and shakers come together to showcase craft, flavour, texture, and design. You’ll enjoy three one‑of‑a‑kind curated cocktails, each crafted by the talented team at Cuckoo Emporium and served in a unique ceramic vessel handmade by local potters.
As you sip, savour, and share your impressions, you'll hear the makers talk through the shapes they chose, the clay they selected, and the ideas that guided their hands. Then the shakers reveal the stories behind each serve — how they matched flavour to form. It's the kind of collaboration you'll remember for years to come, each time you reach for your new favourite drinking vessel.

Light canapés will keep things grounded, and a market stall will offer additional handcrafted wares straight from the makers' studios. The buzz from the last two sell-outs came purely from word of mouth. Come along, and you'll understand why.

Your ticket includes a welcome drink, three bespoke cocktails, light canapés, and three handmade ceramic vessels to take home.

events.id: Keiko Kuwakino + Joe McLeod-Pipitea Marae, 55 Thorndon Quay-WOAP
events.name: Keiko Kuwakino + Joe McLeod
events.venue: Pipitea Marae, 55 Thorndon Quay
events.long_description:
For one night only, two forager-chefs from opposite ends of the Pacific will bring you - Kai meets Shoku - as they cook together at Pipitea Marae, for an all inclusive six course dinner that could only happen in Te Whanganui-a-Tara.

Pipitea Marae has always been a place of gathering and welcome. For generations, Te Āti Awa ki Te Whanganui-a-Tara has offered manaakitanga to those who arrive by sea, by land, and now, from across the Pacific.

On 29 August, that tradition continues with a dinner bringing together two chefs whose life's work is rooted in the same conviction: that honouring culture and history is best done via the puku.

The story of this harbour is one of guardians, pathways, and the meeting of worlds. Te Āti Awa have long understood that the sea connects rather than separates. What began as a relationship across the Pacific between Te Āti Awa and Japan finds its expression at the table, where two Indigenous cultures share their stories through food.

Chef Joe McLeod (Ngāi Tūhoe) brings more than 40 years' experience as an international chef, alongside six decades spent researching, recording, and reconnecting Mātauranga Kai Māori, returning traditional food knowledge to marae across Aotearoa. One of the foremost authorities on traditional Māori cuisine, Chef Joe's kaupapa is grounded in the belief that food carries whakapapa, and that restoring a people's relationship to their kai is an act of cultural survival.

Chef Keiko Kuwakino travels from Satoyama Jujo in Niigata, Japan, where her Michelin-starred restaurant reflects a practice rooted in wild mountain vegetables, fermentation, and the rhythms of Japan's snow country. Her cooking begins with the land she forages. For Keiko, cuisine starts with the land itself, her role simply to listen carefully to nature and translate its story through food.

Hosted by Te Āti Awa at Pipitea Marae, Kai Meets Shoku is a partnership between Wellington Culinary Events Trust, Te Rūnanganui o Te Āti Awa, and E Kai Māori, supported by MBIE.

Your ticket includes five courses, matched beverages for all courses, and Māori and Japanese entertainment,
events.description:
For generations, Te Āti Awa ki Te Whanganui-a-Tara has offered manaakitanga to those who arrive by sea, by land, and now, from across the Pacific.
What began as a relationship across the Pacific between Te Āti Awa and Japan finds its expression at the table, where two Indigenous cultures share their stories through food.
One of the foremost authorities on traditional Māori cuisine, Chef Joe's kaupapa is grounded in the belief that food carries whakapapa, and that restoring a people's relationship to their kai is an act of cultural survival.
For Keiko, cuisine starts with the land itself, her role simply to listen carefully to nature and translate its story through food.
Hosted by Te Āti Awa at Pipitea Marae, Kai Meets Shoku is a partnership between Wellington Culinary Events Trust, Te Rūnanganui o Te Āti Awa, and E Kai Māori, supported by MBIE.

events.name: Songs from the Deep
events.venue: Little Beer Quarter, 6 Edward Street
events.long_description:
LBQ drops anchor for one night, trading its usual warmth for something saltier. Long communal tables, low light, rope and timber, and the Pōneke Shanty Club leading the room through some rousing singalongs.
Sea shanties were never written down. They travelled port to port, voice to voice, and that's exactly how the evening works. By the second set the room is carrying the chorus. Between the music, anonymous dockside confessions are read aloud and become part of the night's folklore.

Food is hearty and built for winter: slow-braised beef cheek and dark ale pie, or roasted kūmara and caramelised onion pie to start. Char-grilled market fish or a smoked mushroom and lentil wellington to follow, and a spiced rum sticky pudding or dark chocolate and sea salt tart to close.

Your ticket includes three courses and three drinks. Choose from curated beer, rum, or non-alcoholic ginger beer.

events.id: Hidden Ferments-Chaat Street, 171 Willis Street-WOAP
events.name: Hidden Ferments
events.venue: Chaat Street, 171 Willis Street
events.long_description:
Indian fermentation has long lived in home kitchens and ancient texts, yet rarely takes centre stage in restaurants, especially on the global stage. This collaborative event invites you into that quieter, largely unseen world as chefs Saavni Krishnan and Sriram Aditya from Melbourne's Saadi restaurant, and chef Vaibhav Vishen, from Wellington's Chaat Street, bring a fine-dining lens to India's deep fermentation traditions, set against Wellington's wild, coastal produce. Across an intimate evening, the menu combines age-old techniques and new terroir: cultured grains, pickles, kanji-style ferments, and lacto-preserved fruits.

For both chefs their cooking is rooted in memory and comfort but expressed with restraint, clarity, and precision. Think fermented rice and lentil batters reimagined as delicate snacks, vegetables transformed through lactic fermentation to echo both Indian aachar and Wellington's coastal flora, and broths built on aged, cultured bases for depth and length. The journey traces a loose path from the grain and pulse-based ferments that underpin everyday Indian eating, through preserved vegetables and fruits, to experimental applications responding to Wellington's climate and producers. 

Your ticket includes a five-course tasting menu with thoughtful beverage pairings designed to echo ferment-forward flavours. Non-alcoholic options feature pickled, infused, and cultured elements without overpowering the palate. This event has shared seating.

ABOUT THIS EVENT: BirdBox coaching development is a CrossFit® approved two-day educational course that expands understanding and applies coaching principles to coaches, personal trainers, practitioners, and leaders.

The Birdbox level 1 seminar is an applied literature review of everything we feel a coach should know to be transformational. events.long_description:
Cooking: More Tastes of Singapore Street Food
Come and try some delicious new recipes - you don’t have to have done Mei-Fen's first course to join in!
Singaporean cooking is characterised by being mildly spiced, but you can always adjust to your preference.
By the end of the workshop, you’ll be able to:
• Follow recipes using correct procedures
• Create a selection of tasty Singapore Hawker dishes, which may includes recipes such as Chicken Rendang and Nasi Lemak.
• Feel more confident to cook Singapore Hawker Food at home
Some ingredients supplied but a list of additional ingredients needed (approximate cost $35) will be sent out when course numbers are confirmed. NOTE: Nuts and gluten will be used in some recipes - this course is not suitable for vegetarians.
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Getting the Most from AI: Practical AI for Everyday Use-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Getting the Most from AI: Practical AI for Everyday Use
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Getting the Most from AI: Practical Skills for Everyday Use
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $29 with ID, all others pays $120).
AI tools are everywhere, but knowing which one to use — and how to use it well — makes all the difference. In this hands-on workshop, Jon Bell will guide you through practical techniques to make AI a genuinely useful partner in your work and life. Whether you've tried it a few times or use it regularly, you'll leave with real skills you can apply straight away, with no technical background needed.
We'll start with a quick tour of the main tools — ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Copilot — so you understand what makes each one different. (They're less different than you might think!) Then we'll spend most of the day going deep on Claude, which Jon considers the easiest to use, while also being the most powerful.
We'll cover real examples, answer your questions as they come up, and spend plenty of time experimenting together.
By the end of the workshop, you'll be able to:
- Write effective prompts that get clearer, more useful responses
- Use AI for research, brainstorming, writing, and creative work
- Understand the differences between the major tools and when to use each
- Recognise the tools' limitations (and how to work around them)
- Apply practical strategies for using AI in your personal or professional projects
- Confidently troubleshoot when AI gives unexpected or unhelpful answers
Please bring: A phone or laptop with ChatGPT and Claude installed and signed in to both. Enough battery to last the day.
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Sound Healing Experience at Tusk Yoga with Claudio-TUSK Yoga,Knight Frank House, 23 Waring Taylor St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Sound Healing Experience at Tusk Yoga with Claudio
events.venue: TUSK Yoga,Knight Frank House, 23 Waring Taylor St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Sound Healing Experience at Tusk Yoga with Claudio
We’re so excited to bring this deeply restorative sound journey to Tusk Yoga for the very first time — an immersive evening of vibration, stillness, and heart-centred connection with multi-instrumentalist Claudio Escutia.

Let yourself be held by the layered tones of Gongs, Crystal and Tibetan singing bowls, Harmonium Native American flutes, and other traditional instruments, all delivered through high-quality audio so the sound is heard *and* felt throughout your entire body.

In this state of deep rest, your nervous system softens, the thinking mind quiets, and you naturally arrive at the heart-mind. Here, silence rises gently through the vehicle of sound itself. 🌿

No yoga or meditation experience is needed — absolutely everyone is welcome.

**What to bring**
Bring your own yoga mat if you like, though studio mats are available. We’ll provide bolsters, blankets, and eye pillows so you can settle in completely.

🎟️ **Booking is essential** — spaces are limited and these events tend to fill quickly. Don’t miss this special first gathering at Tusk Yoga.

Contribution:
Early bird: First 10 bookings $40
General admission : $45


Learn more buy tickets and listen to Claudio’s music here:

https://www.claudioescutia.com/events/sound-healing-experience-at-tusk-yoga-with-claudio

---

**About Claudio**
Born into a family of musicians in Mexico City and now based in Wellington, Claudio began playing music as a child and later studied classical guitar. His path led him through jazz, world music, and spiritual sound traditions in Montreal, and extensive travels across Asia and Europe deepened his connection with Indian music and yogic philosophy.

He trained in Tibetan singing bowls in Kathmandu and Varanasi, and his work now embraces instruments including sitar, esraj, bansuri, harmonium, flutes, gongs, and voice. Every session is a weave of technical skill, grounded presence, and deep reverence for the healing power of sound.

🎧 Listen & connect:
• SoundCloud: soundcloud.com/claudioescutiasounds
• Instagram: instagram.com/claudio_yoga_sound_healing
• Facebook: facebook.com/soundhealingyogamusicclaudio

Be part of the very first Sacred Harmonic Sound evening at Tusk Yoga. Reserve your space now — we can’t wait to share this with you. 💫

events.id: Edible Idioms-Lustre Kitchen and Dining, 7-13 Seaview Road-WOAP
events.name: Edible Idioms
events.venue: Lustre Kitchen and Dining, 7-13 Seaview Road
events.long_description:
Language and food belong together, add history and the story deepens into something altogether different. Lustre's taking the words we use every day and turning them into dishes. Cool as a cucumber becomes a palate cleanser, a piece of cake arrives as something entirely new, and every course speaks to the way culture lives in how we talk about food.

Seven courses of interpretation, each one teasing out a different idiom from around the world. There's a German one about sausage and indifference, an Indian one about something fishy in the pulses, moments where the kitchen challenges and entertains in equal measure. One surprise dish waits mid-evening, your job is to guess which idiom it is. The menu shifts with the seasons and the whims of Lustre's local farms, but the game never changes: food as language, language as history.

Your ticket covers the seven-course Lustrous banquet menu and an idiom-inspired arrival drink. Alcohol is available to purchase.

events.name: Winemaker's Long Lunch
events.venue: Noble Rot Wine Bar, 6 Swan Lane
events.long_description:
The return of an annual favourite. Each year, the Winemaker’s Long Lunch brings friends of Noble Rot together around a shared table, and rather than focussing on one region, this time they're welcoming legendary names from across all corners of the New Zealand wine community.
Settle in for an afternoon shaped by conversation, generous hospitality, and wines poured by the people who made them. Winemakers will join you at the table, sharing bottles from their own cellars and the stories behind them. Such as the significant vintages, the vines they grow, and the decisions that define their style. The atmosphere is relaxed and intimate, with time to taste, and ask questions over a glass (or three).
A relaxed three-course long lunch will be served sharing style, thoughtfully designed to complement a line-up of outstanding New Zealand wines poured throughout the afternoon in this fitting celebration of wine and friendship.
Your ticket includes three courses and wines poured throughout the afternoon by the winemakers themselves. This is an afternoon of genuine hospitality.

events.id: From the Fisher's Mouth-Graze Wine Bar, 95 Upland Road-WOAP
events.name: From the Fisher's Mouth
events.venue: Graze Wine Bar, 95 Upland Road
events.long_description:
Tom and Cole of Strait Speared have been supplying Wellington's best venues with spear-caught butterfish for four years using methods that have minimal impact on the ocean. Knowing your fisherman matters, and tonight they're coming to the table to talk you through why. 

A nine-course degustation using all the parts of the butterfish: fillets, collars, roe. Each preparation different, each one revealing something new about the fish and the selectivity it takes to harvest responsibly. You'll chat with the fishermen about their work, with the chef about different butterfish preparations, and with the people beside you as course after course unfolds. This is what transparent seafood looks like when the person who caught it is sitting across from you. 

Your ticket includes nine courses and one welcome drink on arrival. Additional alcohol can be purchased.

events.id: Hiatus Kaiyote tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Hiatus Kaiyote tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Live Nation
Genre-bending future soul visionaries Hiatus Kaiyote return to celebrate their 15th anniversary with an electrifying night of funk, jazz, hip-hop, rock and progressive R&B as only they know how.
Led by singer-guitarist Naomi “Nai Palm” Saalfield, the Melbourne funkateers –featuring bassist Paul Bender, keyboardist Simon Mavin and drummer Perrin Moss –exploded internationally with their 2012 debut Tawk Tomahawk, which drew praise from the likes of Prince, Erykah Badu and Questlove. They landed their first of three Grammy nominations for their Q-Tip collaboration Nakamarra, received their second for their polyrhythmic record Choose Your Weapon (2015), and had their music sampled by no less than Kendrick Lamar, Beyoncé and Drake (with whom Nai Palm also collaborated on 2018’s monster hit Scorpion).
The band’s kaleidoscopic 2021 album Mood Valiant sent them into the national top five and earned them another Grammy nod, while their latest, 2024’s Love Heart Cheat Code, landed them an ARIA nomination for Best Group. Wizards of the shapeshifting live show, Hiatus Kaiyote have built a formidable reputation for their spectacular stage performances – a high-wire mix of musical mastery and thrilling, edge-of-your-seat chaos.

events.id: Burn The Floor — Luminous-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Burn The Floor — Luminous
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Ballroom dance has never been more thrilling. For over 20 years, Burn the Floor has been revolutionising the fast and furious steps of ballroom style. Groundbreaking moves and rebellious energy are brought to the stage every night to create an electrifying experience. For its latest show. Luminous, twenty-six dancers, musicians, and vocalists will take to the stage. You’ll get to witness romantic waltzes, a futuristic foxtrot, and the passion of the paso doble and tango. You’ll also marvel at the footwork of the cha cha, samba, jive, and a smokingly irresistible Rumba. With lights, costumes, and eclectic live music, you’ll want to leap out of your seat to join in.
Director Jason Gilkison sets an uncompromising standard and comments, “From the very beginning, we set out to shatter every expectation of what ballroom dance can be. Burn the Floor has always been about pushing boundaries — and that hunger to evolve is what has kept us alive and thriving.”
Burn the Floor has grown into a global phenomenon, selling more than four million tickets across theatres in over 30 countries. The show is also seen by more than one million people each year through its four permanently installed productions with Norwegian Cruise Line.

events.id: Chris Parker — Take a Good Hard Look at Me-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Chris Parker — Take a Good Hard Look at Me
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
When comedian Chris Parker was young, he liked attention. He’s always endeavoured to make people look at him. In his latest show, he continues the theme of a lifetime. Take a Good Hard Look at Me displays the Kiwi comic’s sharp wit and clever timing. Chris says of his hour-long show — “There’s no secrets about to be dished, no valuable lesson to take away. I’m just a 35-year-old gay man, who was told there was something special about him as a kid and has been grappling with that ever since.” 
Chris is an actor, comedian, writer, and podcaster. He gained fame on TV’s Have You Been Paying Attention and Thank God You’re Here. He has written and performed extensively with Tom Sainsbury on live shows such as The Opening Night Before Christmas, Camping, Giggly Gerties, and Hauraki Horror. He also created and performed several sketch comedy shows with fellow comedian Hayley Sproull, including Tighty Whiteys, The Outsiders’ Guide, and Milky Bits.

events.name: Wellington Home & Garden Show 2026
events.venue: Hnry Stadium,105 Waterloo Quay, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Wellington Home & Garden Show 2026
The iconic Wellington Home & Garden Show is back at Hnry Stadium. Join us for a tonne of inspiration for the home, garden & lifestyle!

About this Event

The entry ticket is non-refundable, but it is transferable so you if you can't attend for whatever reason, you can give the ticket to a friend.
It’s all about ideas at the Wellington Home & Garden Show 2026. A wonderful range of exhibitors, 'Ask an Expert' sessions, latest trends, innovations and exclusive show specials it’s the place to be for inspiration for indoors and out.
For more information visit homeandgardenshow.co.nz or 'Like us' on Facebook.
We are working very hard to keep our events safe and we ask that you please stay home if you are unwell.
Show opening hours: Friday & Saturday 10-5pm, Sunday 10-4pm
FAQs
1. What are my transport/parking options for getting to and from the event?
Parking is available at the venue, at a cost of $10. Please note parking is card payment only (entry to the Show is card or cash).
2. What is the best time to attend?
Peak visitor times are normally between 11am - 2pm so the crowds are less outside of these times.
3. What days is the ticket valid for?
General Admission entry tickets is for one entry only and is valid for any one of the 3 event days.
Gold Card Special 1/2 price tickets valid for Friday only.
4. Do I have to bring my printed ticket to the event?
A printed ticket or showing the barcode on your phone to the entry staff is required.

events.id: Yoga Nidra-Awhi Yoga and Wellbeing Centre, 15 Johnston St., Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Yoga Nidra
events.venue: Awhi Yoga and Wellbeing Centre, 15 Johnston St., Wellington
events.long_description:
Unwind from the stresses of the week and start your weekend in a state of relaxation. Yoga Nidra, often referred to as "yogic sleep," is a deeply restorative practice that promotes physical relaxation, mental clarity, and emotional balance. It's the perfect way to rejuvenate your mind, body, and soul: your hinengaro, tinana and wairua.

Yoga nidra is a powerful technique that directly works to heal your mind by removing samskaras - untying knots and mental impressions. This practice of learning to relax consciously is well regarded as an antidote for stress, anxiety, poor sleep amongst other health benefits.

Everyone is welcome to experience the profound benefits of Yoga Nidra, and no prior experience is required. For some people, however, Yoga Nidra’s prolonged stillness or internal focus can bring up strong emotions, memories, and/or sensations. You are encouraged to listen and respond in a way that feels safe for you — adjusting your position, keeping your eyes open, grounding yourself, or stepping out of the room at any time.

Yoga Nidra may not be suitable for everyone. If you are experiencing acute mental health challenges, unprocessed trauma, or conditions that make stillness or internal focus difficult, we recommend seeking guidance from a qualified healthcare provider or speaking with the teacher before participating.

events.id: Mel Parsons — Castle Hill Tour-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Mel Parsons — Castle Hill Tour
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Expect soaring harmonies, a dynamic live show, and feel-good vibes when Mel Parsons performs live in Wellington this September. With a style that ranges from indie folk to alt-country, the Kiwi artist has established a reputation for moving songwriting. 
The tour repertoire is based around songs from her latest album, Castle Hill. Named after the remote mountain town in the Southern Alps where it was recorded, Castle Hill was recorded live with Mel’s longtime band members, Aaron Stewart on bass and Jed Parsons on drums. It also includes Ben King (guitarist from Goldenhorse and Grand Rapids). They will support her on stage too. Singles from the record that you’ll hear include alt radio chart toppers, Post High Slide, Brick By Brick, Be Here Now, and Grain by Grain. 
Since her first album in 2009, Mel has established herself in New Zealand’s musical landscape. Awards include Best Folk Artist (2020 New Zealand Music Awards) and Best Country Music Song (2016 New Zealand Music Awards).

events.id: Rhythm & Dance - Wellington-Michael Fowler Centre,111 Wakefield St, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Rhythm & Dance - Wellington
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre,111 Wakefield St, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Rhythm & Dance - Wellington
Stamp the beat and feel the heat in this concert of pan-American dance, jazz and pizzazz!

Programme
John Adams The Chairman Dances
Copland Clarinet Concerto
Gabriela Ortiz Antrópolis (NZ Premiere)
Bernstein Symphonic Dances from West Side Story

Gemma New’s signature “strength, clarity and grace” (WTTW News) invigorates this 20th century American classic, Bernstein’s Symphonic Dances from West Side Story. In depicting a New York gangland Romeo and Juliet, Bernstein’s score captures an irrepressible American swagger. Rhythm & Dance also infuses John Adams’ The Chairman Dances, an outtake of Adams’ opera Nixon in China, which imagines the young Chairman Mao dancing the foxtrot with his mistress.

Aaron Copland’s Clarinet Concerto melds Latin American popular music with jazz in this work commissioned by “The King of Swing” Benny Goodman. And we proudly present the Kiwi premiere of Gabriela Ortiz’s Antrópolis. Grammy Award-winning Ortiz is one of Mexico’s hottest contemporary composers, and this work refers to el antro, Mexican slang for a nightclub.

In association with New Zealand Herald.

In depicting a New York gangland Romeo and Juliet, Bernstein’s score captures an irrepressible American swagger.
Rhythm & Dance also infuses John Adams’ The Chairman Dances, an outtake of Adams’ opera Nixon in China, which imagines the young Chairman Mao dancing the foxtrot with his mistress.

events.id: Bob Ross Landscape Painting Workshop (held in Seaview, Lower Hutt)-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Bob Ross Landscape Painting Workshop (held in Seaview, Lower Hutt)
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Bob Ross Landscape Painting Workshop
If you don’t already know who Bob Ross is then Google him, and you will be amazed at how he creates fantastic paintings using simple tools.  You can too! 
Please wear old clothes and bring your lunch.  You'll go home with an oil painting completed 100% by yourself.
All materials are included in course fee –  a 16x20 inch professional canvas, Bob Ross oil paints, brushes, and all equipment, as seen on NZ TV and YouTube.
This is a unique technique of wet-into-wet oil painting, which is ideal for those who have never painted before, as well as more experienced artists.
Please make sure you have a method to transport your wet oil painting home at the end of day.  Each workshop focuses on doing a specific set landscape painting - the whole class group does the same painting.
(Please note there is no wheelchair access to this venue - held at NZ Art School studio at 61 Seaview Road, Lower Hutt).
Painting for 17 May
Painting for 30 August
Painting for 15 November
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Bike Maintenance for Beginners-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Bike Maintenance for Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Bicycle Maintenance for Beginners
Have you recently started riding a bicycle and want to make sure you can take care of it properly? Or have you been riding for ages, but have always been scared to fix anything yourself?
Are you unsure of how to pump your tyres, or how much oil your chain needs? Find yourself fumbling for words when trying to describe that clunking sound to the guy in the bike shop?
This one day course lays bare the nuts and bolts of bicycle mechanics for absolute beginners, as well as those with a little experience. Through practical experience working with your own bicycle, you will find out what needs to be done to keep it running smoothly. We’ll cover what to look out for when things start to wear out, and how to check for problems, so you can be confident your bicycle is safe to ride. You will also learn how to fix some of the most common mechanical problems, and learn about the correct terminology and tools to use.
Those who feel intimidated in bike shops are especially welcome in this course. No question is a stupid question!
Bringing a bike to work on is essential, preferably one that you are riding regularly, because the best way to learn is by building familiarity with your own ride. E-bikes are welcome, although the extra weight can mean they are more challenging to work on. Regardless of whether your bike is in pristine condition, or desperately in need of TLC, it is welcome on the course – bikes showing a bit more wear can be useful for the learning of the whole group.
Bring your pushbike along on the day.  If you have the following, please bring them as well:  A multi-tool and pump; chain oil and rag; lightweight close-fitting gardening or rubber gloves.
Note: A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
(Please note this course is not exhaustive and is not a professional training course).
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Name most parts of a bicycle correctly
- Perform a quick safety check on any bicycle
- Fix a flat tyre
- Adjust your bike to fit your body
- Oil and clean your chain
- Identify and fix some common brake problems
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cake Decorating Workshop-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cake Decorating Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cale Decorating Workshop
Join cake expert, Lina, for a hands-on cake building and decorating workshop where creativity meets craftsmanship!
With over 20 years of experience in the culinary and hospitality industries - from managing restaurants across Europe and Asia to running her own cake design business - Lina brings not only technical expertise, but also a genuine passion for sharing the joy of decorating.
Known for her friendly, engaging style and eye for detail, Lina will guide you through each step of the process - from building cakes and decorating including palette knife and  gum-paste techniques, to using buttercream and creating a centrepiece.
Whether you're a complete beginner or simply want to sharpen your decorating skills, you'll leave with new techniques, confidence, and of course, your own edible masterpiece.
Let’s make something beautiful - and delicious - together!
You will leave with a tall 6” decorated cake in its own cake box to take home and enjoy. All ingredients included in course fee - just bring an apron and a couple of teatowels. 
Not suitable for those who are gluten-free. 
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cooking: Lentils – Types, Recipes and How to Cook Them-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: Lentils – Types, Recipes and How to Cook Them
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Lentils – Types, Recipes and How to Cook Them
The lentil is perhaps the world’s most versatile and indestructible food. It has significant health benefits from lowering cholesterol to supporting weight loss – and lentils are delicious when cooked well. Enjoy this half-day course that will show you how to cook lentils properly and make the most of this nutritious staple ingredient. Discover spices to complement and enhance lentils and create some mouth-watering dishes.
All ingredients included in course fee.  Please bring your apron, a couple of tea-towels, a sharp knife and leak-proof containers to take your food home in.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Recognise some different types of lentils.
Cook some delicious lentil dishes.
Understand some spices that will work well with lentils.
Follow simple recipes.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.long_description:
Creative Writing - From Idea to Page
“Just write!”
That’s the advice given to every newbie writer… but what happens when you
struggle to get words on the page?
In this one-day course, we’ll brainstorm ideas, complete writing exercises and
discuss strategies for keeping up writing momentum.
Please bring pen and paper.  Tea and coffee available for your breaks, please bring lunch and any snacks you may need.  
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Identify different brainstorming methods
- Complete writing exercises to develop ideas
- Describe your own writing process, and identify what works for you
- Apply strategies to keep up writing momentum
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Zine Making - Introduction-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Zine Making - Introduction
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Zine Making - Introduction
In this workshop you will be guided in making a classic cut and fold 8-page zine from a standard A4 sheet of paper - this is a clever design you can easily photocopy and reproduce later! You will be introduced to a range of techniques and approaches such as cut and paste photos and text, collage and drawing on paper.
If you’re keen to make more zines and share them with the world, there's a broad and growing community of Zine-makers and traders, as well as the well-attended annual Wellington Zinefest!
Paper, newspapers, magazines and basic drawing materials will be provided. We recommend you bring along a sketch book and any favourite drawing materials, newspapers, magazines or other interesting paper material to cut up and collage with in class.
Tea and coffee provided, please bring your lunch.
 By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Cut and fold a classic 8-page zine format from a single sheet of paper
Brainstorm content ideas for your personalised zine publications
Arrange & compose text and images to fit a classic 8-page zine layout
Combine cut & paste magazine content with hand drawn/ written content
Create a draft zine of your personal interests
  Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: The Spring Fling Express-Paekakariki Railway Station,Paekakariki, Paekakariki , New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: The Spring Fling Express
events.venue: Paekakariki Railway Station,Paekakariki, Paekakariki , New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Spring Fling Express
🌼🚂 The Spring Fling Express – A Heritage Rail Journey to Central Hawke’s Bay’s Biggest Spring Celebration! 🚂🌼

Saturday 5th September 2026

Celebrate the arrival of spring aboard Steam Incorporated’s Spring Fling Express, a special return rail journey from the Kāpiti Coast and Manawatū to Waipukurau for the opening weekend of Central Hawke’s Bay’s much-loved Spring Fling festivities. Travel behind heritage diesel locomotive Da 1410 through the spectacular Manawatū Gorge and some of the North Island’s most picturesque countryside aboard our beautifully restored heritage train.

Once in Waipukurau, choose how you'd like to spend your day:

🌼 Taniwha Daffodils Experience
Enjoy coach transfers to the famous Taniwha Daffodils, where thousands of blooms create a spectacular spring display. Wander the gardens, soak up the atmosphere, enjoy a packed lunch, and even pick your own daffodils to take home.

☕ Explore Waipukurau at Your Own Pace
Spend the day discovering local cafés, shops, gardens and attractions before re-joining the train for the journey home. Perfect for those wanting a relaxed day out in the heart of Central Hawke’s Bay.

🚉 Boarding from:
Paekākāriki, Paraparaumu, Waikanae, Ōtaki, Levin and Palmerston North.

🎟 Fares from just $119 Adult / $99 Child
🌼 Taniwha Daffodils packages available!

Whether you're chasing spring colour, exploring a charming country town, or simply enjoying a day aboard one of New Zealand’s most unique heritage rail experiences, the Spring Fling Express promises a memorable day out for the whole family. Seats are limited and advance booking is essential. 🌸🚂

events.venue: Ivy Bar and Cabaret, 63 Cuba Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Are you ready for the hottest new drag queens, drag kings & drag things that Wellington has to offer?

Drag has exploded here in Aotearoa with new performers emerging every week and we're giving them the chance to show us their stuff.
This stunning resurgence of drag has arrived with thanks from powerhouses in the Wellington drag scene, stars of RuPaul's Drag Race, The Boulet Brothers Dragula, and many more!

From kings, queens, and all the beautiful theys in-between, get ready to cheer these newbies on their debut night, scream their name and say SHANTAY, you stay!

Please contact Olivia Dreams or Ivy Bar and Cabaret if wheelchair access is required.

*On purchasing tickets please contact the Bar if you wish to book a table for your group. Please arrive earlier to get a seat before the show kicks off (very limited seating)
GA tickets are the same for seating and standing. Venue seating is essentially first in, first served.

events.id: Suzanne Vega-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Suzanne Vega
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Widely regarded as one of the foremost songwriters of her generation, Suzanne Vega emerged as a leading figure of the folk revival of the early 1980s. Four decades later, she is still writing and singing great songs. In her Wellington show, Suzanne will perform a career-spanning set. See her on stage at St James Theatre performing favourites like Tom’s Diner, Luka, Marlene On The Wall, and plenty more. There’ll also be new songs from her 2025 album, ‘Flying with Angels’. Joining her on stage will be longtime guitarist, Gerry Leonard, and cellist Stephanie Winters. 
Since the release of her self-titled, critically acclaimed 1985 debut album, Suzanne Vega has played to sold-out concerts across the world. She is a masterful storyteller who, according to one The New York Times critique, “observes the world with a clinically poetic eye.” Her song lyrics often focus on city life, ordinary people, and real-world subjects. They are notably succinct, understated, and immediately recognisable.
