events.id: Greg Davies — Full Fat Legend-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Greg Davies — Full Fat Legend
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
He’s six feet and eight inches tall and has a towering sense of humour. After a seven-year break, comedian Greg Davies is back on tour, and he’s heading to Wellington. His fresh new show, Full Fat Legend, promises a wild walk through the beloved UK comic’s mind. Expect a run-through of the more embarrassing situations he finds himself in, plus memories from his childhood in the 1970s. This will all be delivered with his characteristic wit, along with a healthy dose of toilet humour.   
Greg hasn’t been on the comedy circuit for a while — he’s been busy. You are likely to have seen him hosting Taskmaster, presenting Never Mind The Buzzcocks, or starring in his sitcom The Cleaner.

events.id: Creedence Clearwater Collective-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Creedence Clearwater Collective
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Take a walk back in time to enjoy some of the most famous music of the 60s and 70s. Creedence Clearwater Revival was a juggernaut of phenomenal proportions. From their album debut in 1968 to their last album in 1972 the band produced some highly memorable songs. Many of them are still regularly played today. The Creedence Clearwater Collective recreates the success of the original band. The St James Theatre stage in Wellington will come alive with a six-piece tribute band. There’ll be a guest saxophonist and a vocalist who emulates the distinct sounds of original vocalist John Fogerty. 
The night promises to be full of iconic tunes including songs they wrote and songs they covered. Listen to hits like Have You Ever Seen The Rain, Fortunate Son, Bad Moon Rising, and Proud Mary.

events.id: Fly Fishing-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Fly Fishing
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Fly Fishing
An introduction to fly fishing for trout, which includes choosing gear, finding the fish, fly tying and casting and a practical fishing session.
By the end of the course, you'll be able to: 
• Apply the basics of fly fishing
• Choose the appropriate tackle
• Have an appreciation of the entomology associated with trout
• Demonstrate practical aspects of casting, knot tying, tying of flies and stream craft
• Present an appropriate fly to the water with a reasonable chance of catching a trout
There are three sessions at the high school, plus a session at the Capital Trout Centre and a choice of two river sessions (times and dates to be discussed at the class). Tutors from Wellington Flyfishers Club.   There may also be additional opportunity before class (from 6pm) for casting practice before sessions 2 and 3.
[This is a physical activity that involves an element of risk.  While all possible measures are taken to prevent accidents or injury, please note that participation is at your own risk]. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Oh The Horror 2.0: Horror and Gothic Market (PG-13)-Whirinaki Whare Taonga,Upper Hutt I Site, Fergusson Dr, Upper Hutt Central, Upper Hutt 5018, New Zealand, Upper Hutt-All Events In
events.name: Oh The Horror 2.0: Horror and Gothic Market (PG-13)
events.venue: Whirinaki Whare Taonga,Upper Hutt I Site, Fergusson Dr, Upper Hutt Central, Upper Hutt 5018, New Zealand, Upper Hutt
events.long_description:
Oh The Horror 2.0: Horror and Gothic Market (PG-13)
Darker, edgier, sexier than before....

Step into the eerie world of "Oh The Horror! 2.0" - a spine-chilling extravaganza awaiting all horror fans, goths, and Halloween enthusiasts alike. Embrace the dark side from 10:00 AM onwards at this specially curated market presented by Flea Bag Events.

Are you ready to be mesmerized by a macabre display of handpicked items designed to cater to your eerie tastes? Join us for a hauntingly good time as you browse through a selection of peculiar and unique offerings guaranteed to send shivers down your spine.

Immerse yourself in the ghoulish atmosphere while indulging in the company of fellow enthusiasts. Music by On The Record will set the mysterious tone, heightening your senses as you explore the market. Additionally, vintage Halloween cartoons will be playing in the backdrop, adding a touch of nostalgia to the event. (While we have taken great care in selecting media, some scenes may scare young, vulnerable children)

While this all-ages gathering welcomes everyone, we must warn you - there will be spicy items available for the brave souls. Feel free to bring your younglings along, but beware of the thrills that await you!


Dare to step through the shadows and embrace the horror that awaits you at "Oh The Horror! 2.0" - a market where nightmares come to life.

If you are interested in being a vendor, please have a look at our non-committal expression of interest form:

Don't miss out on this opportunity to experience a market like no other, where the bizarre and the beautiful collide in a symphony of darkness.

events.name: Amped Up 2026
events.venue: Brewtown, Upper Hutt, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description: It’s going to be an unforgettable day of live music. Six of New Zealand’s premier rock and metal acts converge on Brewtown in Upper Hutt for a Labour Weekend celebration. The epic lineup at Amped Up 2026 includes headliner Devilskin. The four-piece alt-metal band will deliver precision and power accompanied by searing vocals from Jennie Skulander. Alternative rock heavyweights Villainy, Kiwi rock legends Tadpole, and industrial-metal pioneers Head Like A Hole also make an appearance. Modern rock powerhouse Written By Wolves, and rising favourites The Boondocks, round out the stellar lineup.

events.id: Get Rhythm — The Johnny Cash & June Carter Show-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Get Rhythm — The Johnny Cash & June Carter Show
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
It’s more than just a tribute concert — it’s a live theatrical experience bringing to life the heart, humour, and deep connection of a famous country music couple. Get Rhythm — The Johnny Cash & June Carter Show is coming to Wellington for the very first time in 2026. 
 
Based on the award-winning movie ‘Walk The Line’, Get Rhythm stars real-life married duo Mark and Joanne Caligiuri. Together, they capture the look, sound, and spirit of Johnny and June with remarkable authenticity. Between songs, heartfelt storytelling and charming onstage chemistry, you will be taken on a beautiful musical journey. Authentic, emotional, and unmissable — Get Rhythm stands as a powerful and respectful celebration of Johnny Cash and June Carter’s legacy.

events.name: Amped Up 2026
events.venue: 25 Blenheim Street, New Zealand 5014,25 Blenheim St, Maidstone, Upper Hutt 5018, New Zealand, Upper Hutt
events.long_description:
Amped Up 2026
AMPED UP 2026
Saturday, October 24th | 2PM 'til Late
Brewtown, Upper Hutt.

New Zealand’s loudest night of mean as rock & metal mayhem is locked and loaded.

Join us here at Brewtown for AMPED UP 2026, featuring SIX of NZ’s heaviest hitters!

DEVILSKIN
Headline this epic line-up, delivering precision, power, and the searing vocals of Jennie Skulander.

WITH

VILLAINY
Hard-hitting, hook-driven rock with an alternative edge—big riffs, soaring vocals, and stadium-sized energy.

TADPOLE
They’ve re-emerged with purpose, sounding sharper, heavier and more certain of who they are than ever before.

HEAD LIKE A HOLE
High-octane Wellington rock legends, hitting the stage with their raw, chaotic energy and unapologetically outrageous live shows.

WRITTEN BY WOLVES
Unleashing cinematic, genre-bending rock—blending huge anthems, electronic textures, and explosive energy into a powerful modern sound.

THE BOONDOCKS
High-energy riff-rock duo, delivering punchy riffs, raw attitude, and crowd-moving anthems. Loud, gritty, and built for sweaty, full-throttle live shows.

Six bands. One stage. No second chances.

As always, Brewtown will be serving up heaps of unbeatable beverage options from our festival bars ft. craft beer, bourbon, wine and non-alcs. And we’ve got a line-up of serious feeds from the best local foodies.

Pre-Sale – Wednesday 3rd June, 7pm.
General Release – Thursday 4th June, 7pm

Score your ticket. Feel the volume. Get AMPED UP!

New Jersey hell-raisers Lorna Shore make their first-ever appearance in New Zealand this October. For the past 15 years, the band has pushed beyond the confines of the heavy band genre. Since forming in 2009, Lorna Shore has evolved into a powerhouse of intense brutality and melodic sorcery. To the mainstream, their signature brand of deathcore has become infamous. Their 2020 album ‘Immortal’, along with the guttural vocal acrobatics of vocalist Will Ramos, has catapulted the group beyond cult status and into global renown. 
Lorna Shore offer the ultimate meeting point between unbridled catharsis, raw emotion, searing sonic aggression and cinematic beauty. Their fifth studio album, 2025’s ‘I Feel The Everblack Festering Within Me’, continues to galvanise a significant fanbase. For the Wellington show at Shed 6, they will be joined by fellow American heavy hitters in The Acacia Strain and local New Zealand death metallers Organectomy.

events.id: New Zealand Cricket: Summer Season-Hnry Stadium and Cello Basin Reserve, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: New Zealand Cricket: Summer Season
events.venue: Hnry Stadium and Cello Basin Reserve, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
It looks like New Zealand Cricket (NZC) has smashed it for six. The 2026-27 international home season of cricket promises an exciting few months of action across the country. Wellington has scored six major opportunities to see the BLACKCAPS and WHITE FERNS in action. Three formidable opponents are travelling to New Zealand’s shores. The Indian, Bangladeshi, and Sri Lankan teams have booked their tickets for Aotearoa and will play tests, ODIs, and T20 matches against the Kiwi women’s and men’s teams. 
The Indian men’s team will be particularly busy during their visit. With twelve matches booked against the BLACKCAPS, it will be the most internationals scheduled for an Indian men’s tour in NZC history. Fans will get their chance to see them duke it out in all three formats across the capital, beginning with a T20 at Hnry Stadium on 27 October. 
Make sure you don’t miss the WHITE FERNS. They host Bangladesh in Wellington in a T20 and ODI, during their only home tour of the summer. The Wellington schedule finishes with a visit from Sri Lanka for an ODI against the BLACKCAPS on 19 January at the Cello Basin Reserve.   
With games split across Hnry Stadium and Cello Basin Reserve, the city will be bouncing with life. The 2025/26 season saw bumper crowds, and our men's and women's sides are set to build on this exciting momentum.

The Wellington schedule finishes with a visit from Sri Lanka for an ODI against the BLACKCAPS on 19 January at the Cello Basin Reserve.

events.name: Wellington Zinefest Weekend 2026
events.venue: 139 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011
events.long_description:
Wellington Zinefest Weekend 2026
Wellington Zinefest is hosting their annual two-day zine festival to celebrate the thriving zine scene here in Te Whanganui-a-Tara.

We're open to the public from 12 til 4 on Saturday October 31 and Sunday November 1 at Te Aro Campus of Te Herenga Waka Victoria University of Wellington - 139 Vivian street.

Each day we'll have sixty+ unique stalls, loaded with self-made zines, comics, prints, and trinkets. We'll have our beloved community table and trading post running too!

Wellington Zinefest markets are free to attend, make sure you bring cash to buy zines tho!

From 11am to 12pm each day we'll run 'accessibility hour'. This is a chance for any visitors who can't attend when Zinefest is crowded. We'll have no music and try to maintain a low-stimulation environment.

Cooking: Masalas and Breads from India
Take a day and create a range of delicious and different Indian masalas, using both Manjula’s tried-and-true recipes but also some of the new spice mixes now available in Indian shops. You’ll also learn how to make a variety of breads (naan, roti and parathas) to serve with the curries.

events.name: Dare to Be Seen – Overcome Stage Fright and Performance Anxiety
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Dare to Be Seen – Overcome Stage Fright and Performance Anxiety
The skills you will practice may be applied to presentations, interviews, appraisals, business meetings, networking, performing on stage and more. Prepare yourself to deliver your best self under pressure. Discover the art of being seen and heard.
 Learn how to banish stage fright and performance anxiety. Communicate from a place of confidence and energy. Hold the room and speak with authority. Come across authentically, with maximum personal impact.
In this masterclass we will work with the fundamental toolkit of each person’s body, breath, voice and mindset to increase communicative power, gravitas and influence. You will leave with the skills to master any kind of performance you need to give.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
-  Understand how your physical, mental and vocal habits help you or hinder your effectiveness
-  Practice simple repeatable exercises that will transform your capacity to make a powerful entrance
-  Learn how to manage nerves, take up space and land your message with impact.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Posture Perfection is Possible!-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Posture Perfection is Possible!
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Posture Perfection is Possible!
Are you a computer slouch, feeling stiff and tense at work? Do you find it difficult to maintain a good posture?  Does your posture cause back, neck or shoulder tension?  Would you like to enjoy more freedom in everyday activities?
Under the guidance of an experienced teacher, we'll explore movement in ways that help you reduce tense unhelpful habits.  Movement lessons are done on the floor or sitting on a chair and are based on the pioneering work of Israeli physicist and engineer Dr. Moshe Feldenkrais.
This workshop is suitable for all ages (over 16) and fitness levels.
By the end of the workshop, you'll be able to:
• Identify and reduce muscle tension
• Move with more lightness and freedom
• Improve your posture
• Describe some basic anatomy
• Have tools to use at home and at work
Bring two blankets (or a mat and a blanket) and wear loose, warm and comfortable clothing. Tea and coffee provided but bring your lunch. Please note that instructions are given verbally rather than by demonstration, so good hearing and understanding of English is required.
Feel free to contact the tutor, Sue Field on 027 466 7123, if you have any questions. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Nau Mai Haere Mai, meet you at the Market. 🌞

Discover a vibrant outdoor market nestled on State Highway One in Ōtaki, just across from New World. Join us to greet our regular stallholders and special guests from the community.

PARKING
There is plenty of free parking available at the Otaki train station.

CASH / ATM

events.id: Killswitch Engage tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Killswitch Engage tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: SBM Presents
Killswitch Engage return to New Zealand in 2026, bringing more than two decades of genre-defining metal to the stage with their biggest Antipodean headline shows yet, joined by special guests Sylosis. The Western Massachusetts heavyweights helped redraw the map for modern metal in the early 2000s, fusing blistering European-inspired guitar work, East Coast hardcore energy and emotionally charged, uplifting lyrics into a sound that changed the game for a generation of bands and fans.
Across a landmark career, Killswitch Engage have earned three Grammy nominations for Best Metal Performance, multiple gold and platinum records, billions of streams and a reputation as one of the most powerful live acts in heavy music. From classic albums like Alive Or Just Breathing, The End of Heartache and As Daylight Dies through to chart-topping releases Disarm the Descent, Incarnate and 2019’s Atonement, they’ve consistently raised the bar while packing out venues and festival main stages around the world.
Fresh from celebrating 25 years as a band and the release of their latest album This Consequence – featuring the blockbuster single “I Believe,” the biggest radio hit of their career – Killswitch Engage are set to deliver a career-spanning set for their New Zealand fans.
Expect a night that runs from era-defining anthems to new favourites, all delivered with the intensity and heart that has seen them share stages with everyone from Iron Maiden and Slayer to My Chemical Romance, Mastodon, Slipknot and Parkway Drive.

events.long_description:
Upper Hutt Night Market
The Upper Hutt Night Market returns to Trentham Racecourse for an unforgettable evening.

About this Event

This vibrant community event promises an exciting evening of entertainment, artisan crafts and delectable food options for attendees of all ages.

Over 80 artisan craft stalls will showcase the talent and creativity of local artists. Attendees will have the opportunity to browse and purchase a wide range of unique handmade products, including artwork, jewellery, candles, home décor, plants, clothing and much more.

To satisfy the appetites of hungry visitors, the Upper Hutt Night Market will also feature 30 carefully selected food trucks offering a diverse range of culinary delights. From international cuisines to local favourites, attendees can indulge in a variety of mouthwatering dishes, ensuring there is something to satisfy every palate.

At the heart of the event will be live music. Lively melodies and infectious tunes will grace the sound stage, ensuring a memorable night filled with music and joy.

FREE ENTRY

events.id: Cheesemaking - Brie and Queso Fresco-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cheesemaking - Brie and Queso Fresco
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cheesemaking - Brie and Queso Fresco
The ultimate class in practical cheese making!   Learn all you need to know to replicate making NZ style Brie and Queso Fresco at home.  You will be guided through each step of the cheesemaking process while making your very own round of Brie. Then, while the Brie is ripening and setting (don't worry, all terminology will be explained!), we will make Queso Fresco together.  
All ingredients are provided as are recipes and detailed handouts to take away with you. Bring an apron, a dishcloth, 3 or 4 tea towels, a pen to take notes and a 2 litre container and plastic re-sealable food bags to take your cheese home.
During the workshop, the following topics will also be covered: Milk and where to source it, hygiene and sanitation, equipment required and the origins of the cheeses you are about to make.  You will also receive comprehensive instruction on how to mature your Brie cheese over the next few weeks.
A small break will be provided and you will have the opportunity to purchase equipment and cheesemaking ingredients after the workshop should you wish to replicate the experience at home.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
-  Make Brie and Queso Fresco
- Recall the best milk to use and where to source it from
- Name and identify what equipment and ingredients you need to make Brie and Queso Fresco
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Crochet - Christmas Special!-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Crochet - Christmas Special!
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Crochet - Christmas Special!
Join Monique in this half-day crochet workshop where you will learn how to crochet a range of little projects suitable for the holiday season.  Choose from stars, icicles, mini wreaths and Christmas jumpers, Christmas trees and a mini Santa hat!   There will be a booklet of patterns for you to take home, to enable you to complete in your own time the projects that you didn’t have time to make during the workshop.
  You will also learn how to crochet and decorate Christmas baubles!  While you might not finish all the patterns this day, the aim is to have a go at making as many as possible!
  You will need to know some basic crochet stitches such as single and double crochet. You’ll also need to have some understanding of how to read a pattern and work with diagrams, as taught in the beginners’ crochet course.
  Please bring yarn that you enjoy working with (cotton, acrylic or wool, 4ply or 8 ply) in a variety of colours (red, white and green, a neutral colour for the mouse, and glittery yarns are all good options for the Christmas projects) and a matching crochet hook. You’ll also need a pair of scissors and a darning needle.
  Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Harakeke (Flax Weaving) Workshop-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Harakeke (Flax Weaving) Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Harakeke (Flax Weaving) Workshop
Gain an introduction to the Māori art of flax weaving in an intensive one-day workshop, suitable for beginners and those with some experience.   
By the end of the workshop, you'll be able to:
•  Identify commonly-used types of harakeke
•  Describe how to harvest and prepare harakeke for use 
•  Describe the tikanga (the protocol) surrounding the use of harakeke and weaving 
•  Demonstrate some basic weaving techniques  
•  Construct and complete a small project 
Materials provided but please bring a 'bread and butter' knife and 8 spring-loaded clothes pegs. Tea and coffee provided but please bring your lunch.
[A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend)
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Sir El Tom-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Sir El Tom
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Here’s a chance to hear Sir Elton John’s music performed live by someone who cares about authenticity. Tom Cridland is an Anglo-Portuguese musician from London. The St James Theatre hosts his alter ego, Sir El Tom, in homage to one of the great living musicians. Sir El Tom performs Sir Elton John’s music in an exhilarating concert full of famous hits. You’ll hear songs like Can You Feel the Love Tonight, Don’t Go Breaking My Heart, I’m Still Standing, Goodbye Yellow Brick Road, Crocodile Rock, and many more. 
Whether it’s singing at a grand piano or with a full band, you’ll hear as authentic a performance as possible. Sir El Tom is known for extraordinary attention to detail, where the music always comes first and everything is live and natural.

events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Thai Food with Aranya
Thai cuisine is an exciting explosion of tastes and textures. This highly aromatic food has become popular around the globe. Each week you will be shown different Thai dishes and you will get to make these. This course will provide you with tips, tricks and knowledge and it will be fun, easy and yummy!
Not suitable for vegetarians.  All ingredients included in course fee – please bring to each session your apron, a couple of tea towels, a dishcloth and containers to take leftovers home
By the end of the course you'll be able to
Make a range of Thai dishes at home with more confidence
Use ingredients spices and herbs like Thai people do!
Be able to explain some simple Thai eating etiquette
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.

Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Cooking: Indian Vegan
Indian cooking expert, Manjula Patel, has assembled a range of vegan Indian dishes for you to learn and enjoy making and eating. In this practical cooking course, you’ll observe dishes being made by Manjula and then work in pairs to create most of these dishes yourselves.
Please note this is a practical cooking class that will not cover vegan philosophy.  Not suitable for people allergic to nuts.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Discuss the flavours and spices used in Indian cuisine
• Know where to purchase ingredients for a selection of vegan dishes and finger food
• Read and follow a range of recipes step-by-step
• Create some classic dishes to try again at home
All ingredients included in course fee. Bring an apron, dishcloth, and a couple of tea towels and containers to take any leftovers home.

events.id: Cheesemaking - Farmhouse Cheddar Cheese Workshop-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cheesemaking - Farmhouse Cheddar Cheese Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cheesemaking - Farmhouse Cheddar Cheese Workshop
During this hands-on workshop, you’ll be guided through each step of the cheesemaking process while making your very own round of farmhouse style cheddar.
The fee includes a cheese press (value $95.00) which can be used every time you want to make a hard cheese. It holds up to 6 litres of milk and this is what you’ll be taking your cheese home in to press for 12 hours after the workshop. All ingredients, recipes and a full set of course notes are provided.
You’ll also receive comprehensive instruction on how to wax (if you choose to) and then mature your cheese over the next five weeks. Alexis will also demonstrate how to make halloumi cheese which will then be grilled for you to enjoy! There’ll be a short lunch break and cheese making supplies such as wax will be available to purchase.
By the end of the workshop, you’ll be able to:
• Describe the basics of sanitisation and hygiene
• Recognise the difference between raw and pasteurised milk and know where to source them
• Make a round of cheddar cheese using a cheese press
• Describe how to wax and mature the cheese
• Describe how to make halloumi, following a demonstration 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: FLYTE-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: FLYTE
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
Acclaimed indie band Flyte bring their signature blend of heartfelt lyricism and melodic craftsmanship to a special run of New Zealand shows this November.

Flyte have earned widespread acclaim for their refined songwriting and emotionally resonant sound, with a catalogue that showcases their evolution from classic British guitar pop to more expansive and introspective arrangements. Their releases have been celebrated for their lyrical honesty and rich harmonies, cementing their place as one of the UK’s most compelling contemporary bands.

Formed in London, Flyte are led by singer-songwriters Will Taylor and Nick Hill, whose close creative partnership underpins the band’s distinctive identity. They broke through with their debut album The Loved Ones and continued to build momentum with subsequent records, including This Is Really Going to Hurt and Flyte, earning praise for their timeless influences and modern sensibility. Their work captures themes of love, vulnerability and connection, delivered with a stripped-back elegance and attention to detail that has resonated with audiences worldwide.
For audiences in Aotearoa, these shows offer a rare opportunity to experience Flyte’s intimate and immersive live performance, bringing their finely crafted songs to life with warmth, precision and emotional depth.

events.id: Jesus Christ Superstar-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Jesus Christ Superstar
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
It’s a 70s rock musical of biblical proportions, and it’s back. Jesus Christ Superstar is heading to St James Theatre, and Wellington audiences are in for a treat. This production won the 2017 Olivier Award for Best Musical Revival and came fresh off an acclaimed 2023/24 tour of the United Kingdom. 
Jesus Christ Superstar explores the biblical events leading to the death and resurrection of Jesus Christ as seen through the eyes of his betrayer, Judas Iscariot. The story, told entirely through song, explores the personal relationships and struggles among Jesus, Judas, Mary Magdalene, Jesus’ disciples, his followers and the Roman Empire.
The 1970s rock score features lyrics and music by Emmy, GRAMMY, Oscar, and Tony winners Tim Rice and Andrew Lloyd Webber. Well-known numbers include I Don’t Know How to Love Him, Gethsemane, and Superstar.

events.long_description:
Presented by: Plus1 + The Sound
Hoodoo Gurus announce hit-packed ‘Rewind – All Killer, No Filler’ NZ tour.
Kiwi crowds in November get a chance to celebrate 45 years of Hoodoo Gurus, with the legendary Australian band announcing Auckland, Wellington and Christchurch shows on their HOODOO GURUS Rewind Tour - All Killer, No Filler.
For the tour – proudly presented by Plus1 and The Sound – Hoodoo Gurus will only be playing singles! No b-sides, no album tracks - only A-sides.
Look forward to hit after hit, like What’s My Scene, Bittersweet, 1000 Miles Away, Come Anytime, Death Defying, My Girl, Miss Freelove ’69, I Want You Back, and Like Wow – Wipeout!
Says frontman Dave Faulkner: “We’ve often been described as a "singles band” so for this Rewind tour we’re gonna embrace that notion by only playing songs that were released as singles. No b-sides, no album tracks, we’ll just be concentrating on the hits - and quite a few misses as well!

events.id: Saint Etienne tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Saint Etienne tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Destroy All Lines
One of the UK’s most beloved and enduring pop groups, SAINT ETIENNE, are heading to Australia and New Zealand for the very last time. Following the announcement that their 2025 album International would be their final studio release, the iconic trio will take their celebrated catalogue on a farewell tour across the region this November and December.
Released in September 2025, International was met with rapturous acclaim, returning Saint Etienne to the UK Top 10 and reaffirming their status as one of Britain’s most treasured musical institutions. Now, fans across Australia and New Zealand will have the rare chance to experience the band’s shimmering, genre‑blurring sound live, one final time.
With a career spanning more than three decades, Saint Etienne’s songbook is rich with classics including ‘Only Love Can Break Your Heart’, ‘Spring’, ‘Nothing Can Stop Us’, ‘You’re In A Bad Way’, ‘He’s On the Phone’ and ‘Wood Cabin’ tracks that helped define an era of British pop and continue to resonate with audiences worldwide.
Formed in 1990 by Croydon natives Bob Stanley and Pete Wiggs, Saint Etienne struck gold from the outset with their debut single, a transformative cover of Neil Young’s ‘Only Love Can Break Your Heart’, which quickly became a club anthem. The arrival of vocalist Sarah Cracknell in 1991 completed the definitive lineup, her debut on ‘Nothing Can Stop Us’ and the Mercury Prize‑nominated album Foxbase Alpha cementing the group’s place in UK pop history.
Their follow‑up album So Tough soared into the UK Top 10, leading to a landmark moment in 1994 when Saint Etienne became the first band ever broadcast live from Glastonbury Festival.
Now, after decades of innovation, reinvention and irresistible pop craftsmanship, Saint Etienne prepare to say goodbye to Australian and New Zealand audiences.
A final bow from one of Britain’s most cherished groups which is not to be missed.

events.id: An Introduction to Self-Hypnosis - Unlock Your Inner Power-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: An Introduction to Self-Hypnosis - Unlock Your Inner Power
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Self-Hypnosis - Hypnotise Yourself
Self-hypnosis is a fantastic self-development tool. It can also be used for controlling pain, accelerated healing, letting go of limiting behaviours and a number of other applications. This workshop will provide you with a good insight into the world of hypnosis, demystify it and dispel the myths that surround it.
You’ll learn a structured method of taking yourself into hypnosis (informed by scientific research), some of the classic applications of self-hypnosis and evidence-based skills that will convince you without doubt of your ability to hypnotise yourself.
By the end of the workshop, you’ll be able to:
• Define and discuss hypnosis
• Practice self-hypnosis
• Create and write hypnosis scripts for personal change
• Use the language of self-hypnosis
• Relax hypnotically to deep levels
Tea and coffee provided but bring your lunch, pen and paper.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Beekeeping - Next Step-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Beekeeping - Next Step
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Beekeeping - Next Step
This is a practical and hands-on experience so bring your beekeeper’s outfit and your lunch. Please check your course instructions for more information on what you need to bring on the day.  The tutor can lend you a veil if you don't have one, please let the CEC office know a week before the course is due to run so this can be arranged.
The workshop starts in the classroom and is followed by a visit to a local apiary.  (Please note that you will working with live bees during the visit so there is a chance of being stung by one.)
If weather isn't suitable on the day, the morning session will still go ahead but the visit will be postponed to the next available Saturday.   Please note that if you can't make the catch-up visit, it's not possible to join in later courses due to number restrictions (a part-refund will be made). You must have done the Beginners' course first.
By the end of the workshop, you'll be able to:
• List the key steps in setting up a hive
• Describe how honey is extracted
• State what causes swarming and some ways of preventing it
• Describe what needs to be done to manage Varoa and American Foul Brood 
• Construct kitset frames for a honey super
• Open up and inspect a hive 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.name: Cooking: Thai Home-Style
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Thai Home-Style
Thai cuisine and cooking has a key secret - the secret is that authentic Thai food balances key flavour elements. Combining sweet, salty, bitter, sour and spicy, Thai cuisine creates sumptuous and exciting flavour combinations. Come along to this half-day cooking course and learn to make some home-style Thai dishes.
Not suitable for vegetarians. All ingredients included in course fee, you just need to bring your apron, a couple of tea towels, a dishcloth and containers to take leftovers home in.
 By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Make some  Thai dishes at home with more confidence
Use ingredients/spices/herbs to match with dishes
Apply some aspects of the Thai eating style at home or when visiting Thailand
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Courtney Barnett — Creature of Habit Tour-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Courtney Barnett — Creature of Habit Tour
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Australian singer-songwriter Courtney Barnett is heading back to New Zealand five years after her last sell-out tour. The ‘Creature of Habit’ tour coincides with the release of her fourth studio album of the same name. The album is emotionally resonant and explores the central question: how to get out of your own way so you can truly feel your life. With singles Stay in Your Lane, and Site Unseen’ setting the tone, the album and concert mark a decisive new chapter in her musical evolution. Along with the latest music, she will delve into her extensive back catalogue. 
In addition to a Grammy nomination, Courtney has won multiple ARIAs (Australian Recording Industry Awards). She is known for a style that incorporates intimate storytelling with indie rock and bursts of raw grunge-like energy. Her blend of moody guitar, witty, anecdotal lyrics and laconic talk-singing has cemented her as “one of the most distinctive voices in indie music” (The Mancunion).

events.id: Richard Dawkins — 50 Years of The Selfish Gene-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Richard Dawkins — 50 Years of The Selfish Gene
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Richard Dawkins is a world-renowned evolutionary biologist. When published in 1976, his book, ‘The Selfish Gene’, reframed our understanding of life, behaviour, and human nature. Now, 50 years later, he is coming to Wellington to reflect on the book’s revolutionary legacy. He explores what fifty years of science has confirmed or challenged and explains why its core questions are more urgent today than ever before. 
‘The Selfish Gene’ has remained a cornerstone of modern science. It introduced the gene-centred view of evolution and coined the now-ubiquitous concept of the ‘meme’. The ideas in it ignited global debate and five decades of subsequent research. Richard Dawkins is one of the most consequential scientific communicators of our time, so don’t miss the rare opportunity to hear him speak in person.

events.id: The Sweet Caroline Tour — A Tribute to Neil Diamond-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: The Sweet Caroline Tour — A Tribute to Neil Diamond
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
One of the top singer-songwriters to come out of the United States’ 60s music scene was none other than Neil Diamond. With a career spanning five decades, Neil is behind many hits that have stood the test of time and still woo listeners around the globe. Now you get to hear a tribute to him at The Opera House this November. 
Imbuing long-loved hits with new energy, The Sweet Caroline Tour recreates the feel of the famous man with careful attention to detail. It focuses on recapturing the sound, look and charisma of one of the world’s greatest entertainers. 
At the concert, you’ll hear songs that made numerous top ten lists, won Grammy awards and earned an induction into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame. Zac Coombs steps into the shoes of the original performer and brings his own musicality and showmanship to the stage. Don’t worry, you’ll hear all the timeless hits like Sweet Caroline, Song Sung Blue, Red Red Wine, Cracklin’ Rosie, Cherry Cherry, Crunchy Granola Suite, Shilo, Solitary Man, Kentucky Woman, I Am… I Said, and more. 
Relive an era when songs were written with heart, soul and honesty, and melodies stayed with you for a lifetime.

Imbuing long-loved hits with new energy, The Sweet Caroline Tour recreates the feel of the famous man with careful attention to detail.
It focuses on recapturing the sound, look and charisma of one of the world’s greatest entertainers.

events.id: Cat Power: The Greatest on Tour-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Cat Power: The Greatest on Tour
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
20 years on from its release, Cat Power’s album, The Greatest, still retains its mesmeric pull. A late-night classic, it still feels as intimate and raw as it did in 2014. Now you get the chance to hear the album in its entirety live at The Opera House in Wellington.   
The concert won’t just showcase this seminal album — it’ll sit alongside favourite songs from Cat’s catalogue over the past three decades. The concert will drift through heartbreak, survival, longing, and grace with the kind of restraint that only makes the emotional weight hit harder. From the slow-burn sway of Living Proof to the bruised beauty of The Greatest, these songs endure because they tell the truth softly.   
The support act for the Wellington concert is Lawrence Arabia. He is known for his sophisticated songwriting and distinctive blend of indie-pop, chamber-pop and psychedelic influences. Lawrence has toured extensively across New Zealand, Europe and North America.

events.id: Dermot Kennedy: The Weight of the Woods Tour-TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Dermot Kennedy: The Weight of the Woods Tour
events.venue: TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Singer/songwriter and multi-platinum recording artist Dermot Kennedy has confirmed he’s coming to Wellington, New Zealand, as part of his international The Weight of the Woods Tour. The tour follows the release of his new album, also called The Weight of the Woods. Poetry and music from the album’s fourteen tracks draw inspiration, energy, and strength from Dermot’s native Ireland. You’ll get to hear those songs plus more from his previous albums. 
Known for his passionate and powerful live performances, the gig will feature songs like Honest, Refuge and the anthemic lead single Funeral. Dermot Kennedy created UK chart history as the first Irish solo artist ever to reach Number 1 with their first three studio albums. His highly successful songwriting continues with this latest album. The best way to experience it, of course, is live.

events.id: Yīn Yīn - New Zealand Tour | Wellington-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Yīn Yīn - New Zealand Tour | Wellington
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
Dutch groove-explorers YĪN YĪN are bringing disco, funk, psychedelia and dance to Wellington’s San Fran Friday 27 November and Auckland’s Tuning Fork on Saturday 28 November. 
Made up of Erik Bandt on guitar, Kees Berkers on drums, Jerôme Schere on keyboards and Remy Scheren on bass, YĪN YĪN are the kaleidoscopic instrumental quartet from Maastricht, Netherlands, renowned for their genre-bending records and transportive live experience. 
Emerging out of an experimental jam session in a remote ballet school village in 2017 that resulted in the making of their debut album The Rabbit That Hunts Tigers, the band have since created another three critically acclaimed albums and spent the better part of the last decade chasing rhythm across continents. 
With relentless international touring and performing everywhere from Fuji Rock Festival and Lowlands to North Sea Jazz Festival and Clockenflap they have been collecting sounds, textures and musical flavours like souvenirs, drawing from a multitude of influences such as ‘70s Thai Funk, Japanese City Pop and crate-dug global disco. Each project has evolved alongside the eager exploration to immerse their worldly experience into a sonic universe of their own.
Their latest of which,Yatta!, arrived in January 2026. A defining influence on Yatta! Is the cosmic allure of Italo Disco - the psychedelic futuristic dance music that emerged in Italy during the late 1970s and flourished throughout the 80s. “It has something of a mystique,” the band explains. 
“Because all the producers were using new recording techniques and effects and stuff like that, but there are not many pictures or videos of how they were creating things in the studio.You have to use your own fantasy and create your own story about how that music is created.”
This one sees the band into their most realised selves yet, translating into an unmissable live experience. “We tried to make a mix of songs that are very energetic and danceable and are party starters, but also have songs that take you on trips and are more easy. People can also just chill and relax and listen to the music and enjoy it.” - YĪN YĪN 

Aotearoa, get ready to move. YĪN YĪN are set to take on Aotearoa for the first time for two shows this November.


San Fran holds a Supervised License. Minors are welcome to attend events at San Fran so long as they are accompanied by a legal guardian at all times whilst at the venue.
events.description:
Emerging out of an experimental jam session in a remote ballet school village in 2017 that resulted in the making of their debut album The Rabbit That Hunts Tigers, the band have since created another three critically acclaimed albums and spent the better part of the last decade chasing rhythm across continents.
With relentless international touring and performing everywhere from Fuji Rock Festival and Lowlands to North Sea Jazz Festival and Clockenflap they have been collecting sounds, textures and musical flavours like souvenirs, drawing from a multitude of influences such as ‘70s Thai Funk, Japanese City Pop and crate-dug global disco.
Each project has evolved alongside the eager exploration to immerse their worldly experience into a sonic universe of their own.
Their latest of which,Yatta!, arrived in January 2026.
YĪN YĪN are set to take on Aotearoa for the first time for two shows this November.

events.name: Marton Market Day
events.venue: Follett St, Marton 4710
events.long_description:
Marton Market Day
Marton Market Day is one of the biggest annual events in Rangitīkei, one of the best days on our district calendar.. and entry is free!

The town will come alive with over 200 stalls, great entertainment, and plenty to see and do throughout the day.

Marton Park will be transformed into a mini inflatable world, bringing fun for little kids, big kids, and everyone in between. There will also be an amazing line-up of food trucks, with flavours from all around the globe.

It’s the perfect place to get your Christmas shopping sorted, with something for everyone. Bring the whole whānau along for a fantastic day of shopping, food, entertainment, and festive fun in the heart of Marton.

This kind of event promotion aligns well with Council’s marketing role of encouraging residents and visitors to attend local events and take part in community life.

events.name: Microsoft Excel - Advanced
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Microsoft Excel - Advanced
For those who have completed the Intermediate course or equivalent - you must be competent using Excel formulas and formatting.
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
All computer courses are run in the school computer labs and you are required to use the school desktops for the duration of the course (you are not able to use your own laptop).  Some of the courses may require you to login onto the network. 
By the end of the course, you'll have had an introduction to the more advanced features of MS Excel, including how to:
• Manage lists: sort and filter
• Create a pivot table from an existing set of data
• Create custom date formats
• Analyse data: goal seek, data tables and scenario manager 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cooking: Singapore Nyonya Food-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: Singapore Nyonya Food
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Singapore Nyonya Food
Create some classic mouth-watering and flavourful Nyonya dishes. Come and discover how to blend ingredients with distinct spices and cooking techniques. You can easily replicate these dishes at home. Singaporean cooking is characterised by being mildly spiced, but you can always adjust to your preference.
Some ingredients supplied but a list of additional ingredients needed (approximate cost $35) will be sent out when course numbers are confirmed.
Please bring an apron, dish cloth, sharp knife, tea towels and containers to take food home.
Target audience: Adventurous food lovers!
NOTE: Nuts and gluten will be used in some recipes - this course is not suitable for vegetarians.
By the end of the course, you’ll have:
• Made a selection of Nyonya dishes to take home.
• Become more familiar with using the various spices.
• Understood more about the Nyonya cooking.
• Become more confident to cook Nyonya food at home.
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Aldous Harding: Train on the Island-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Aldous Harding: Train on the Island
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
By the time indie/folk singer songwriter Aldous Harding hits Wellington, she will have travelled across the UK and Europe with her Train on the Island tour. By the time the tour reaches the Michael Fowler Centre, it will be a finely oiled machine.   
Named after her fifth studio album Train on the Island, all ten of the album’s songs will appear in the concert, including One Stop, and Venus In The Zinnia.   
Aldous Harding captivates listeners with what Martin Pepperell describes as an ‘elastic and shape-shifting voice’. This, combined with eccentric performances, has catapulted the Lyttelton-born singer to fame. Her second album ‘Party’ won the Taite Prize in 2018, and The Barrel, from her third studio album ‘Designer’, won the 2019 APRA Silver Scroll award.

events.id: David Nihill: Taking Tangents Tour-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: David Nihill: Taking Tangents Tour
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
DOORS OPEN 6:00pm
SHOW START 7:00pm

Leaving Dublin, Ireland where he was born and raised until the age of 22, David got drunk on travel and never quite hit sobriety of it. He has lived and worked in twelve countries and visited over eighty. His shows draw on his wide travels, cultural observations, attempts at language study, (occasional) international drinking session(s) and being a mildly confused immigrant. 
David is the winner of the prestigious annual San Francisco Comedy Competition, (previous finalists include Robin Williams, Ellen DeGeneres and Dana Carvey) and runner-up in the Moth’s largest US Grandslam storytelling competition. Also a bestselling writer, David recorded his debut special Documented for Dry Bar Comedy in 2019, his follow up Cultural Appreciation in 202, and his award winning show Shelf Help in 2025. He has been featured on TED.com, Sirius XM, Just For Laughs, Netflix is a Joke Festival, RTE’s Late Late Show, The Irish Independent, Inc, NPR, and the Huffington Post among others. A festival favorite, David's videos have also gone viral many times over with over 800 million views and a social following of over 2 million people. More info: davidnihill.com

events.name: The ABBA Day Party: Wellington 💃🕺
events.venue: Eva Pub - Wellington
events.long_description:
The ABBA Day Party: Wellington 💃🕺
Calling all Dancing Queens and Super Troupers! 🎉
Get ready for a glitter-filled afternoon of pure pop perfection as we celebrate the iconic music of ABBA! Whether you're a lifelong fan or just love a bit of feel-good disco, this is your chance to sing, dance, and party like it's Eurovision '74!
What to Expect:
🎶 Wall-to-Wall ABBA AnthemsFrom “Dancing Queen” to “Gimme! Gimme! Gimme!”, enjoy all the greatest hits and hidden gems that made ABBA a global sensation.
✨ Disco, Funk & 70s ClassicsWhile ABBA takes center stage, we'll also be spinning a fabulous mix of other dancefloor-fillers from the disco era—think funky grooves, 70s gems, and all the classics that’ll keep you moving from start to finish.
💫 ABBA-Inspired AtmosphereThink shimmering lights, tinsel wigs and more sequins than you can shake a tambourine at! Step into your own mini-Mamma Mia moment!
🌈 Feel-Good Vibes All Day LongCelebrate friendship, freedom, and unforgettable tunes with an afternoon of singing, dancing, and joyous nostalgia.
Whether you’re a Fernando or a Chiquitita, this is your Waterloo! don’t miss it! 🎤✨FAQ’S🗣️ Why do the tickets come in different price tiers?Whilst there is no physical difference. There is only a limited amount of tickets we can sell. Therefore we operate a festival style ticketing model. The earlier you buy your ticket the cheaper it will be.🗣️ Do you do group discount? Stag/Hen etcYes, we offer a discounted ticket price for groups of 10 or more. These tickets can be found listed as the “Big Group Package!” above, orders for these tickets are restricted to a minimum of 10.🗣️ Will there be more tickets available if the event is sold out?Once this event is sold out, there will not be any additional tickets released. In addiction to this we will be unable to sell them at the door due to maximum capacity and health and safety regulations.🗣️ Is the venue accessible?Our events take place at a variety of locations around the world. As each varies please ensure to check the specific venue's website for accessibility information.🗣️ Can I resell my tickets?Yes, if you are unable to attend an event you are welcome to sell your tickets on. We do not use names on tickets as a condition for entry.🗣️ Are your events 18+?All of our events are strictly 18+. A valid photographic ID may be requested on entry.🗣️ What do I do if I cant find my tickets or confirmation email?We use Fatsoma to sell tickets for our events. If you’re struggling to find your ticket, you can access their support channels via https://support.fatsoma.com/support/home🗣️ Are there tickets available on the door?We always recommend booking in advance as we cannot guarantee on the door entry. However, in certain instances there will be a limited number of tickets on a first come first served basis for sale on the door.🗣️ I have more questions?If you have additional questions and wish to contact us directly, don’t hesitate to drop us a DM on either Facebook (Daytime Disco) or Instagram (@daytimediscoevents)Refund Policy:In-line with normal consumer standards for ticket purchases, tickets for Daytime Disco events are non-refundable, on the condition that we not moved the event or canceled it. If you are no longer be able to attend the event, you are of course welcome to gift or sell your tickets onwards as we do not use names on the tickets as a condition of entry.

events.name: Thorndon Fair 2026
events.venue: Tinakori Rd and Hill Street, New Zealand 6011,241 Tinakori Rd, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Thorndon Fair 2026
Wellington’s biggest and oldest street fair is back 💛

Join us on Sunday 6 December 2026 as Tinakori Road and Hill Street come alive with more than 250 stalls, incredible food, local makers, entertainment and community spirit.

Held on the first Sunday of December each year, Thorndon Fair has been a Wellington tradition since 1977 and attracts more than 20,000 visitors from across the region.

Whether you’re searching for handmade treasures, delicious food, unique gifts, art, plants, fashion, books or simply a great day out, there’s something for everyone.

Entry is by gold coin donation, proudly supporting Thorndon School.

Follow this event for stallholder announcements, event updates and behind-the-scenes content as we count down to fair day.

events.id: The Reytons Australian & New Zealand Tour-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: The Reytons Australian & New Zealand Tour
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
UK music’s biggest underdogs, indie rock juggernauts The Reytons, keep charging forward on their unstoppable rise. Having flirted with overseas territories successfully over the past 5 years, the band are now heading out on their very first World Tour, and have announced their return to Australia and New Zealand this November and December

Renowned for their raucous live shows, and touted by many as the best live act of their generation, the British 4-piece pride themselves on their honesty and pure energy. Having taken their native UK by storm, with number one albums, sold out park shows and major festival headlines, the lads from Rotherham are taking on the world with their upcoming world tour.

In 2025 alone, they toured the UK, headlined Tramlines Festival and the iconic Wembley Arena. The upcoming world tour is a huge statement for a band who have hurdled every obstacle with nothing other than blue-collar grit and determination. It cements them as a band at the top of British music. The Reytons also recently announced their fourth studio album A Love Letter To A Broken Town - due for release in July.

Rooted in the rough around the edges humour and honesty of everyday life, The Reytons continue to channel their origins into everything they do, and A Love Letter To A Broken Town sees them staying true to those roots while pushing their sound forward with confidence and intent.

“I honestly feel like this album is the best we’ve ever done!” explains frontman Jonny Yerrell. "A lot of bands and artists like to change direction later in their careers, but that’s just not us. We absolutely love the style of music we make, the social commentary aspects and the people who turn up to jump around with us. Why would we change? Expect more, just bigger and better with a few surprises on the side! "

San Fran holds a Supervised License, meaning minors are welcome to attend events at San Fran so long as they are accompanied by a legal guardian at all times whilst at the venue. Minors are of course not allowed to purchase alcohol under any circumstance.

events.id: Uncle Acid and The Deadbeats AUS / NZ Tour-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Uncle Acid and The Deadbeats AUS / NZ Tour
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
UK psychedelic heavyweights Uncle Acid & The Deadbeats return to Australia and New Zealand this December, bringing their dark, hypnotic live show to stages across the country.
The brainchild of Kevin Starrs, Uncle Acid & The Deadbeats have spent over a decade carving out a singular space within the global heavy music landscape. Since emerging in 2009 from the depths of the British underground, the band have built a reputation for their unmistakable fusion of warped psychedelic pop, downer blues and doom-metal intensity.
Too bold and idiosyncratic to be easily defined, Uncle Acid’s sound is steeped in haunting melodies, layered vocal harmonies and eerie, cinematic arrangements, offset by crushing riffs and a persistent sense of unease. It’s a sonic world that feels both deeply nostalgic and completely otherworldly, cementing Uncle Acid & The Deadbeats as one of the most compelling live acts in contemporary rock.
With their last visit to our shores in 2020, Australian audiences can expect a set that traverses the band’s cult catalogue, delivered with the immersive intensity that has made them a must-see worldwide.

events.id: Urzila Carlson — Fatty On a Yacht-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Urzila Carlson — Fatty On a Yacht
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
She’s known for her deadpan delivery and understated punchlines. Fan-favourite comedian Urzila Carlson is back in time for Christmas with a brand-new show — Fatty On a Yacht. Ever felt like you don’t belong? Like everyone else got the memo — and you turned up in the wrong outfit, on the wrong day, on the wrong yacht? According to Urzila, Fatty On a Yacht is for anyone who’s ever tried to squeeze into a space — physical or social — and thought, “This can’t be right”. 
The multi-award-winning South African-New Zealander is one of Australasia’s biggest comedy stars. In 2025, she won the prestigious People’s Choice Award for most tickets sold at the Melbourne International Comedy Festival for an incredible fourth time, after wins in 2019, 2022 and 2023. In 2019, she was presented with the Rielly Comedy Award from the Variety Artists Club of New Zealand for her contribution to New Zealand entertainment.

events.id: Troy Hawke ’Never Stop, Never Change!’-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Troy Hawke ’Never Stop, Never Change!’
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The comedian’s show, Never Stop, Never Change! will play the St James Theatre. It’s part of a 2027 world tour. Troy Hawke is the creation of Milo McCabe. He’s the Errol Flynn of the high street — educated, sophisticated, but utterly clueless. He tackles modern issues head-on in his uniquely untimely manner. The character has found fame for viral videos online where he pays compliments to strangers in the street. 
In this new show, angry security guards, barked compliments, and crossed wires abound as Troy navigates various conversational minefields, both online and in real life. 
A previous show, Troy Hawke: Sigmund Troy’d won Best Show at the Leicester Comedy Festival Awards 2022. He has twice been nominated for the Amused Moose Best Edinburgh Show award and was listed as GQ’s Top Ten Comedy Shows at the festival.

events.id: Paul Smith: Happy-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Paul Smith: Happy
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
It’s billed as the biggest and boldest show of his career. Paul Smith’s show Happy is set to visit Wellington’s Michael Fowler Centre in February 2027. Happy takes Paul’s trademark crowd work, razor-sharp observations, and laugh-out-loud true stories to an entirely new level. This is stand-up at full throttle: fast, fearless, and relentlessly funny. 
The show is packed with outrageous audience interaction and stories pulled straight from real life. It’s about finding joy in the chaos, laughter in the everyday, and comedy in absolutely everything. 
Liverpool-born Paul Smith is known for his quick wit and unrivalled ability to roast audience members. With over 5,000 videos online, he holds the unofficial record for the most online stand-up content and has amassed a massive global following. Happy will be his sixth stand-up tour.

events.id: The Human League — Generations: The Greatest Hits Tour-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: The Human League — Generations: The Greatest Hits Tour
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
A fresh and influential sound dominated the charts in the 1980s — synth-pop. The Human League were one of the first bands to find success with this new sound. Now they are bringing decades of catchy hits to Wellington fans. Expect an unforgettable night with timeless classics like Don’t You Want Me, Human, Love Action (I Believe in Love), The Lebanon, Mirror Man, Being Boiled, and more. 
Long before electronic music became mainstream, the band was successfully using computers and sequencers to create rhythm and melody. In the process, they redefined modern pop. Their stylish, forward-thinking sound remains relevant today as ever. In 1982, The Sydney Morning Herald described them as “one of the first futurist bands.” Join founding members Philip Oakey, Joanne Catherall, and Susan Ann Sulley on an electrifying journey through four decades of stylish synth-pop brilliance.

events.id: Drax Project & Friends-Te Whare Whakarauika Wellington Town Hall-Wellington NZ
events.name: Drax Project & Friends
events.venue: Te Whare Whakarauika Wellington Town Hall
events.long_description:
Homegrown Wellington band Drax Project are booked for a very special gig — the grand reopening of Te Whare Whakarauika Wellington Town Hall. After a 13 year hiatus, the building is back online and who better to christen it than a local band with an international reputation? 
Drax Project fuses pop, R&B, hip-hop, and jazz. They formed in 2014 after meeting in Wellington (members studied Jazz Performance at Victoria University and Whitireia Polytech). After capturing local audiences busking on the streets, they launched a professional career. Since then, they’ve released two albums, toured extensively, and opened for global heavyweights Christina Aguilera, Ed Sheeran, and Camila Cabello. 
Joining Drax Project on the bill is one of the city’s most beloved artists. Oscar-winning Bret McKenzie will open the evening alongside his eight-piece Wellington-based band, The State Highway Wonders. 
When Te Whare Whakarauika Wellington Town Hall officially reopens in February, it will be 13 years since the iconic venue closed for refurbishment and strengthening. This landmark concert, full of Wellington talent, is the perfect way to honour the city’s rich cultural and musical legacy.

events.id: Simple Minds — V Tour-TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Simple Minds — V Tour
events.venue: TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
One of the world’s most popular and acclaimed live bands, Simple Minds, is heading to Wellington in February. With music styles spanning synth-pop, new wave, and art rock, Simple Minds commanded the stage in the 1980s. The band’s reputation eventually propelled them to stadium-sized gigs, which they filled with anthem-like choruses. Head to the TSB Arena and delve into their enduring catalogue live. You’ll hear global hits like Alive and Kicking, Promised You A Miracle, Waterfront, Don’t You (Forget About Me), Belfast Child, and Sanctify Yourself. It won’t take long before you understand why the band has sold over 60 million albums worldwide. 
The tour includes a very special guest — Gary Numan. Rising to fame with hits like Are ‘Friends’ Electric? and Cars, Gary pioneered a distinctive sound that helped define the future of electronic music in the 80s. His sound has inspired artists across rock, electronic, and alternative music.

events.id: CubaDupa--Wellington NZ
events.name: CubaDupa
events.long_description:
One weekend every March, CubaDupa takes over some of Wellington’s central city streets for an epic party. The free annual festival brings a carnival vibe to the end of the summer. It offers live music, street theatre, dance, parades, and other cultural experiences. 
CubaDupa is New Zealand’s largest outdoor arts and music festival. It takes place over two days on Cuba Street and the surrounding roads, right in the heart of Wellington. The festival is suitable for the whole family with performances and activities running throughout the day and night. Multiple performance spaces showcase New Zealand’s emerging musical talent alongside established artists. The lineup usually includes over 140 local and international acts.

It offers live music, street theatre, dance, parades, and other cultural experiences.
CubaDupa is New Zealand’s largest outdoor arts and music festival.
It takes place over two days on Cuba Street and the surrounding roads, right in the heart of Wellington.

events.id: The Traveling Wilburys Collection-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: The Traveling Wilburys Collection
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
There’s never been a group, let alone a supergroup, in rock history quite like it. Now you get to see a tribute to their huge success. In 1988, a group of five rock legends joined together to form The Traveling Wilburys. They were Bob Dylan, George Harrison, Jeff Lynne, Roy Orbison, and Tom Petty. 
Across four years, they produced two mega albums, a slew of hits and won a Grammy. They never toured. Now, a tribute band, The Travelling Wilburys Collective, is on tour and it’s visiting the St James Theatre in Wellington. 
You’ll get to hear some of their biggest and most celebrated hits like Handle with Care, End of the Line, Tweeter and the Monkey Man, and She's My Baby. Along with these original hits, you’ll get served classics from each individual artist and the groups they were in. That includes The Beatles, The Heartbreakers, and ELO. 
The Traveling Wilburys Collection bring together the collective talents, excitement, and magic that the original quintet had to offer. Join them for a night of fun and nostalgia.

events.id: New Zealand International Comedy Festival--Wellington NZ
events.name: New Zealand International Comedy Festival
events.long_description:
Every May, the capital comes alive with laughter, thanks to the New Zealand International Comedy Festival (NZICF). New Zealand’s homegrown talent shares the stage with some of the world’s most exciting comedians. You could catch a big-name international artist, or discover a new local rising star. 
Since it began in 1993, the aim of the festival has been to champion the craft of comedy so everyone can share a laugh. Every year, a huge group of comedians descend on Wellington from local shores and beyond to deliver three weeks of merriment. 
The festival offers a diverse range of high-quality laughs. It showcases the best of local comedy and gives audiences a taste of some of the world’s best acts. Catch over 100 comics performing at The Opera House, BATS Theatre, Fringe Bar, San Fran, and more.

You could catch a big-name international artist, or discover a new local rising star.

events.id: Kia Mau--Wellington NZ
events.name: Kia Mau
events.long_description:
Kia Mau is an arts festival that showcases innovative Indigenous performances. Held every two years, the festival lights up Wellington's winter. See contemporary performances by leading Tāngata Whenua , Tāngata Moana, and global Indigenous artists. The two-week festival includes dance, theatre, music, and visual art. Audiences can expect to be challenged and thrilled by new work from Aotearoa and beyond.
Kia Mau was first held in 2015, founded by two leading Aotearoa creatives. Hone Kouka (Ngāti Porou, Ngāti Kahungunu, Ngāti Raukawa, Kai Tahu) is the Artistic Director and Chief Executive. Mīria George (Ngāti Kearoa, Ngāti Tuara, Ngāti Awa, Ngāti Kūki ‘Āirani) is the Executive Director.
Hone and Mīria describe themselves as innovators. They push boundaries, redefine forms, and forge new paths. They say the festival is not just a platform for presentations, it’s a hub for kōrero and whanaungatanga . The festival has a strong influence on many artists and plays an important role in shaping New Zealand’s contemporary arts scene. “Kia Mau has continued to thrive because of the imagination and commitment of our artists. And of us to them,” says Mīria.

events.id: Black Ferns vs British and Irish Lions-Hnry Stadium, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Black Ferns vs British and Irish Lions
events.venue: Hnry Stadium, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The first-ever Howden British and Irish Lions women‘s series is coming to Aotearoa in 2027. With five games across the country, Wellington is to play host to one of three Test games. The competition is sure to be fierce and the atmosphere electric. 
Sitting at the halfway point between Rugby World Cups, the tour will offer high-calibre clashes between two rugby powerhouses. Ben Calveley, CEO of The British & Irish Lions, said the tour marks “something truly special for the women’s game.” With the first-ever tour title on the table, you can expect both teams to leave it all on the field. Let‘s fill the cake tin with stomping feet and edge-of-your-seat screams. The Wellington game is Test number two of the series, so pull on your jersey, warm up your pipes and get ready for Black Ferns fever to sweep the city. 
Tickets go on sale late 2026.
